text:
Been getting better with greeting since my last review and I do enjoy their craft cocktails. 

We bought many bottles of bourbon for gifts.

Great lunch spot for a slice, coke and fries.  I have been known to go here a couple of days a month for a slice.  Service is always great and they make sure everything is OK a few times while you're there (even when you just get a slice).  Great job, guys.  Keep it up!

My friend and I went to Jackson's while she was in town. We arrived for dinner at 8 PM and were seated outside. The restaurant view was gorgeous of Tampa and great for someone who is coming from out of town. We ordered the crab ravioli and 4 different sushi rolls with sake. The crab ravioli was delicious and the corn emulsion was tasty. Our rolls were delicious, the Tampa roll with grouper was amazing and the green onion gave a little kick. The slammin salmon was very flavorful as well. For dessert, I had the s'more bar and my friend had the peanut butter torte. As much as I love s'mores, the torte was delicious.

I know there are plenty of places to get a great cheesesteak in Philly. You've gotta say you tried it at least once in your lifetime right? I think so. 
I liked it a bit better than you know who's . A bit less greasy and hit the spot! Peppers were yum. Wiz with  enjoy! Went by at like midnight and I was greeted and the gentleman that took care of us was super friendly (I've heard otherwise ‍)

I found this place in one of the back corners in West Edmonton Mall.  They were having this huge sale and I was interested to see what they had considering I've never even heard of this store before.  Personally I don't think it's in the best location of the mall.  Anyways, I have to warn you that if you have any large bags or backpacks you may be asked to store them behind their counter; I guess they've had a lot of theft, which is really too bad.  

The look of this store was pretty cool.  They have most of their clothing items on clothing racks against the wall, but they also had a huge amount of stuff neatly folded on low stylish tables.  I really liked the stores appearance; it was very classy or stylish/modern. 

They have a huge selection of jeans for both men and women.  They have different styles and cuts and jeans with different designs on the back pocket.  If you are looking for skinny jeans this is the place to come to.  They have a whole slew of different styles, most of them I've never heard of.  They were having a sale on some of their jeans, about 30% - 60% off, but I do have to warn you that their jeans are pretty pricey.  The ones that I checked out were in the $100's or $200's range!  I was really surprised to find that they cost that much, considering some their styles were not totally unique.  Oh well, if you want cool expensive jeans you can find them at High Grade Clothing Store.

I guess the name of the store, High Grade Clothing Store, really reflects their clothes.  Their clothes are high grade at a high price.  I guess if you really want to look cool or sexy there is a price to pay.

The staff there is very friendly and nice.  They were busy helping people carry their items to the change rooms.  The guy behind the counter was also very nice and happy.

If you like to look at what the latest styles are but don't have the money to pick them up you can window-shop at High Grade Clothing Store.
It was an ok experience.. vibes were nice but shame there's no outdoor space. Food was very tasty.. drinks were unique. Bit overpriced and service was just fine.

I am in Tampa quite a bit and I tend to stay away from Starbucks just because it can be insane, however, I was one of the designated coffee-getters for an event and Starbucks seemed the most classic. We came in with a 21-drink order and the baristas handled it like champs. They messed up on a few things but made up for it with amazing attitudes in the face of our Starbucks swimming pool challenge. The atmosphere is typical earthy tones and people sitting on their laptops. Comfy... if you have a Mac. (;

It was Sunday around 8:30 when I landed at this place. The place looked in decent part of town but something did not feel like a traditional eatery. Well I settled down and the waitress (very young) who was awesome in service and smiled all the  time attended me. I looked at wine menu and ordered La Crema Pinot Nior. She came back and informed me they are out of it and she selected the wine for me. I don't want to criticize the place but here are some suggestions if they do want to thrive from someone who has owned dozen Indian restaurants in last 30 years.

1) Don't cook or advertise it as tandoori if you don't even have tandoor and you going to advertise it as tandoori dishes. I was so offended that I came in order the tandoori chicken, keema naan and glass of La Crema and they were out of wine and chicken was not fully cooked inside. 

Rest do keep the girl who served me otherwise I would not even taken my food to go and pay and leave. By the way my tab was $37 and I still tipped $5 and paid and left like gentlemen. 


Work on improving or you will be another one in flames. From someone who knows your business too well and been doing it since you 1986.

Okay, to be honest, I went by the Center on a bitterly cold day because I was in town to visit my boyfriend's parents, and I wanted to see the place where my bf has worked as a teenager (so romantical!) But I did check out the place as far as ice skating was concerned and here's my take.
Nice warm break areas with tables, lockers and a good elevated view of the rink, which is standard size and well-kept. The staff was sweet. The rink is outdoor, so if you are going to stay on the ice for a long time, consider the elements. Rental skates seemed fine, but I usually bring my own. They had steady bars for little kids, hooray!
Overall, fun!
I walked there, so I can't speak about parking.

I tried to stop there this morning at 10:15 AM and they were closed.   The sign on the door, as well as the voicemail message indicated that they open at 10 AM.  I had my bike on the back of my car which I cannot take to work with me and have nowhere to store while at work, so I was dependent on them being there when I arrived.  If your sign says that you open at 10 AM, please open at 10 AM!  I am now late for an appointment because I had to go to Montgomeryville Bike Line to drop the bike off.   With the upcoming MS 150 city too short bike ride this weekend, you would think that you would want to make yourself available for potential customers.   Don't be a participating bike shop if you're not going to be available to inspect the bikes for the MS 150 bike ride.
The service was absolutely impeccable. Food delicious!  Fried green tomato, oysters, red fish!!!!
Crasher Barrel refused to honor a reservation at 10:15 we made yesterday for 12 people. Please note they do need money for large groups Beware

Positives: clean, quiet, available machines (washing: -+6 small, 3 med ($4.50 +), 2 lg ($5.00 -$7.00); drying: 8 $.25/6min)
Tennis balls for dryer ( or play catch while u wait).
Amazon Lockers!
Customer service promptly addresses issue - ...see below 

Negatives: just ONE power outlet by the dryers (which was broken); can't charge devices if waiting for laundry.

A positive experience.... 4 stars!
Yes, the change machine worked however it's always best to BRING QUARTERS!!
I used to bring my laundry to this facility over 20 years ago when I lived at 10th and Spruce. It was no big deal then like many self-service laundromats it had its share of problems but being less than a hundred yards from the front door it was the only option in the neighborhood at the time and pretty much still is. Recently, I needed to bring some items in to be cleaned and prior to arrival read the reviews on Yelp and other websites to see if it was the same old place that I remembered. I saw a few bad reviews regarding customer service and some amenities that were lacking but again with very few options in the immediate area, decided to get give it a try. I believe the prices quoted in earlier posts were pretty accurate. I needed the medium sized washing machine which starts at $4.50 and increases depending on whether or not you need an extra rinse or if your clothes are more soiled or even if you need to use hot water. The increase was roughly $.25-$.50 depending on which option you chose.  
The issue came with the drawing. One of the dryers did not heat up and despite only having one item in the dryer, I repeatedly got the E15 error which meant the vent was being blocked. I adjusted the item and started to cycle again. Unfortunately, the dryer never got hot. I checked the settings several times repeatedly got the E15 error but continue to dry the item, since I had already put $1.50 into the machine. There is a sign posted what the E15 error meant and suggestions to remedy the issue. I ended up throwing about 4 tennis balls into the cycle with the hope that I would no longer get the error this was not the case. There is a sign posted on the wall for contact should there be any issues and I sent an email with a picture indicating which dryer was causing the problem. To my surprise later that evening I received the email with an apology and then offer to refund my money and said the dryer would be repaired. I thought this was very good customer service.
So went here tonight because the reviews were good.   Absolutely rocked it!  Best Bloody Mary ever.  And what they said about Carlos?   Right on. Going back tomorrow night for sure!

Ok, so the price of happy hour martinis have gone up...  But they're still super strong!  Make sure you check the speciality drinks menu, as the martinis change on what seems to be a seasonal basis.  When the weather is warm, try to sit outside in the courtyard!  It's a beautiful little patio decked out with plants, water features, and cool outdoor objet d'art.  The staff is fun and cheerful.  And the customers are a mix of interesting characters!  Go early and go often!  It's lots of fun!

The absolute BEST smoke shop in the city. Knowledge staff, amazing selection, and good prices. The store is MUCH larger than it seems from the outside (it's like a TARDIS). They have a TON of work by local artists, and sometimes you can even catch the artist at the shop! There are plenty of people to help you find what you need! Make sure you don't say anything that can make you be asked to leave or refuse a sale (and if you don't know, point and grunt). Definitely go here for all your smoking needs!

Side note *there are no cigarette sales at this location, but nicotine vapes are available*

I gave this restaurant 2 stars, only because I think the pulled pork would be good if it was warm.
Don't ask a server for a recommendation unless you want to hear, "whatever you like".

This is in the shopping plaza by Kroger. Ask for the foot massage for an hour and they do your whole body.
The rooms are in the back and very dark. The music is very calming and the mood is very relaxing. 
They come and lay you down on a table and place your feet in hot water. They will take care of your head, ears, face, neck, shoulders, arms, legs, back, feet, and massage them all 
They use some sort of oil that is very mild. I have very sensitive skin and am allergic to many products but this was fine for my skin. You start on your back for half the massage and then they dry your feet and after they finish your foot massage, flip you in your front to do your back side.
Anytime I need a relaxing massage to wind down I come here. At the end of the massage I feel great and so relaxed. I almost don't want the massage to end. I've come very close to falling asleep because it was so relaxing. Make sure you wear comfortable clothing, like shorts and a loose t-shirt. I definitely recommend coming here to get a massage. I'm

The place was okay. The fried rice could be better, but I definitely enjoyed the chicken. It is a good price for a large portioned meal. The line wasn't bad and I got my food quick enough for my 30 minute lunch break. I would probably eat there again. Definitely better than panda express, but still isn't anywhere close to PF Chang's. Good quick cheap Chinese food.

Not good enough...

Stopped in for dinner on-the-run last night around 630.
Store was empty.
Staff was friendly and helpfull enough.
Decide upon a Big Mary combo with Tater poutine and a bottle of water. Total was just shy of $14.
Sandwich was smaller than I expected and mediocre at best. Coleslaw side was pretty bland. Poutine...
Well let me tell you...
I enjoy a good poutine...
And this was FAR from good.
I ate a 1/4 of the dish at best. Couldn't be bothered to make a bigger dent...even after the smaller than expected sandwich.

I really doubt I will be back.

I always order eggs Benedict when eating breakfast out, and this was THE BEST I've ever had, hands down. So
often the poached eggs are over cooked and hard. This was the runny, gooey egg that make eggs Benedict! I would have liked to tell the server how good it was, but she never returned except to drop off the check. None of the staff seemed happy to be working there -- from the gentleman (owner ??) handing out menus and seating us, to the server with no personality, to the checkout person. Not a single "how was everything?"  Thankfully the good food made up for the lackluster service.

I just finished eating here and couldn't finish my meal. My daughter wanted some of the green pancakes she had been seeing on tv. She only ate two bites. My wife's omelette had a brown chunk of avocado on top. That was an extra $1.25. Yuck. I could not eat my eggs or hash browns. So I just spent $39 on a meal that we wouldn't eat. Also after my wife took a few sips of her tea you could see the dirty ring around her glass. 

Nope! Nope! Nope! No more IHOP for us.

1 star for the atmosphere(not the people just the decorations and music) and the other for the variety and cleverness in the drink menu...

soo... they act like this is some top notch place that everyone is just dying to get into. the dude that checks your id at the door acts like you trying to get into the White House or something. After you get past his ass, you can sit where ever you can. Fridays are pretty cool..complimentary happy hr snacks, tacos, chips etc..other than that the food sucks.. (if your lays chips are better than your menu items i think we have an issue).

yes there are a lot of drinks, but honestly after awhile you cannot tell the difference and all you feel is a stomach ache. service is eh' nothin special either.

all in all i feel like this place is somewhere you can gon once try the damn scorpion and go on with your life.

The novelty of this place is 5 stars. I'm really glad I went and would encourage everyone to go once as it's a very interesting and unique place. I was a bit disappointed with the flavor of my ice cream in the end especially because it is pricey, but it's a great spectacle!

What a disappointment. The last time I ate at Sketch was 2009 and  it seems to have gone downhill rapidly.

The staff seemed to be slammed the day I came and didn't really seem to care. I watched as two different tables left after not even being seen by the server.

Having a server doesn't make any sense since they just turn in a checkbox list into the cook. The service (when they did anything) was rude, short and not understanding.

On the plus side the fries were good. So was the burger. For the money and time it's not worth it, unfortunately.

Disappointed last night.  Took forever to place order and receive food.  Appetizers came out piece meal.  I ordered sides and they came out 10 minutes before the meal.  I questioned the waiter about it and he said he thought we wanted them as appetizers. I said, OK but we have no plates or side dishes as you cleared them. LAME.  Meal was average.

My friend and i walked into this restaurant. Wasn't greeted until someone from the kitchen area came up, seated us, and gave us the menu. When I look down the table decor there were symbolism which seems to be racist and insensitive. If you have any conscience at all, think twice about where your spending your money. My friend got up and left IMMEDIATELY after seeing that!
Beautiful salon and the staff is friendly and attentive. I've been there twice since I  recently moved to the area. I always have walked away pleased by their services.

Always a fun time. Tonight we celebrated a fellow foodie's 25th birthday at her favorite spot. Hotel Tango debuted their Summer Cocktail menu and it did not disappoint! The "Razzario" and the "Temptation Island" were both yummy. Both sweet and strong! They had the large barn doors open and it was the perfect, breezy summer evening. There will be many more summer nights like this to come in our near future, I'm sure!

So, I ended my last review of Sandpiper with "I would stay here again". And, I did! This time we had a family suite, as Gramma was along. I felt safe, the place was spotless, and the front desk staff was friendly and accommodating. Good work, Sandpiper!

Came here last night we started with the wj poutine fries which were very good. We order the two slushie drinks that souned good but were nasty as well we had to choke them down.  My husband ordered the fried chicken sandwich which was good and very big and had alot of flavor.  I ordered the perminto grilled cheese sanwhich with tomato soup.  The soup came out luke warm! Who brings out luke warm soup to a table.  So asked them to heat it up, which they did.  Didn't taste that great even after they heated it up. A little bit later I was puking my guts out and it was only tomato soup so something about the soup wasn't right.

I wish I could recommend this place for the two dishes we did like and because service was good but the fact I got sick off the tomato soup here means I can not.

I am so disappointed by how terrible the service was here! I wasted over 20 minutes in a mostly empty room to get a cup of mostly milk. I was excited to get the new macchiato, which I tried in NY while I was home for the holidays. 

 While the interior was nice and exceptionally large, that's the only positive I have about this location. Even the bathroom was dirty.

We have had so far a great experience with this furniture store.  We purchased some lamps and a bar. I did not give it a five star due to we haven't received furniture yet because our home won't be built for a couple more months. Susie has been helping us and she has been wonderful. I will re-rate once furniture is delivered. So far it has been a pleasant experience.

eh, it's alright. 

Nothing is terrible, nothing is great except for their butter cake dessert. 

The pizzas are ok. Some better than others. I would avoid the Greek pizza, and get the Jerk. Don't get the thin crust as you could probably eat two of them in one sitting and still be hungry. 

It's also a little pricey. Not my favorite, but certainly don't hate it.

I went to this place to grab a beer and eat dinner. No body at the front to greet and I had to wait for 2-3 minutes. I ordered a beer but didn't have the ID so I was refused that I understand. The manager came and sat on the opposite side. Bad attitude!! 
Secondly, she explained me about the policy that they check IDs for people below the age of 30-40 years. Really shocking!! The policies should be same for all and how does she knows if a person is over 30 or over 40 years of age. At the end, she said if you show me your ID once, I would not have to bring my ID again as she would remember it. She also said if you would like to go out and dine somewhere else you're most welcome to do. That pissed me off.

The law is that person taking the order must request for your ID and checks if it is a photo ID and also checks your age whether it is 21 years or not. (legal drinking age in U.S) 
No straw with the first drink, had to remind server to get it.  

Simple solution was to politely say no- and tell me that I do not have a ID and I cannot be served alcohol. Secondly, she should not have sat opposite me disrespecting me as the guest. Really, I had to remind the server to get a straw on a slow night? This took place at Uno, Providence.

I was reluctant to review this place after my first time eating here but I've given it several tries now (breakfasts, lunches) and the verdict is in: PB's is out. The food (eggs, potatoes, toast coffee; pancakes and coffee; burgers, fries, and beers) always manages to be underdone and overdone all at once! Also, the food was stone cold on every visit. The servers we had were gruff and it took a painfully long time to get our orders or to get water or meal accompaniments like hot sauce, or even the check after we were finished.  I understand slow service during busy times but when staff far outnumber customers there's a problem.  Last, everything felt sticky, as though our table wasn't properly wiped down. We really try to visit local businesses but the bad has outnumbered the good every time we've eaten here, so unfortunately, we won't be back again.

I live in Creve Coeur and this is  really one of the go-to places for quick and healthy food, be it breakfast, lunch or dinner. I love bagels and they have a great assortment of fresh bagels which I get bread sliced. Their coffee is good too. I like that they have a lot of vegetarian options. I like they sandwiches, salads, pasta in pesto sauce. My favorite is black bean soup in a bread bowl. 
the missing star is because the tables are not always clean.

Ok so where's the "country" in this country bar?  - there's a bull and maybe one or two songs every 10 min intermixed with rap and pop songs which were really poorly mixed. Just your everyday mediocre dance club ala 1991 ... Had an ok time when we accepted the place for what it was but just know it's just a dance club / pick up joint.

This place is awesome, everyone here is great. Mike in service is excellent, we brought our Ranger Rover in for service twice and he made the whole process simple and easy. Quality people make a business.

Totally unimpressed with this restaurant's ability to serve customers.  We waited for 5 minutes before even being acknowledged.  There were several servers standing around, avoiding eye contact.  The hostess was on the phone dealing with a reservation.  There seemed to be no plan in places as to who helps the hostess when she is busy and people need to be checked in.  

LaScala's was recommended to us by one of our co-workers, who came with us on this evening.  She apologized profusely, as she had not had any problems on her two previous visits.  We had to ask one of the three servers talking to one another near the kitchen if they could seat us.  One did offer to seat us, but one kinda shot us a dirty look for bothering them.  

Two of us received dinner menus, the third a lunch menu.  When asking our server what "ricotta saluda" meant and was included in one of the dishes, she didn't know and had to ask another server.  She was then shadowed the rest of the evening. 

When the food came, we were not offered pepper, which was not on the table.  The server did not follow up to see how the food was, but eventually the shadow server did.  

The potatoes were bland, the asparagus was good and the meatloaf, their Monday special, was overly salty.  

The interior was starkly decorated, with large flat screens tuned to ESPN.  The audio over the speakers was a distracting mix of Christmas music and snippets of television programs that were disjointed and unentertaining.  

We may go back for lunch, which our co-worker was raving on, but not for dinner.  She could not apologize enough for recommending this restaurant to us.

Ordered 3 milkshakes for dine-in and Waited over an 1 1/2 hours for them to be served after we paid for them.  No sense of urgency  on behalf of the staff to get shakes served.  They had plenty of staff members as I counted 8 staff members standing around the ice cream bins.  

Their cashier was telling people that the wait was around 1 1/2 hours.  The shakes were good but in my opinion not worth the wait, nor price MOL $13.99 a shake.

I had a leak into the basement from my bathtub. I called my home warranty and they assigned my claim to PK Plumbing. The "plumber" Steve, came out and took a five minute look at it and said it can only be repaired by ripping out the wall and the warranty will not cover any of the tile work to replace the wall once the leak is fixed. The plumber said he'll fix the leak but leave the hole in my wall for me to have repaired. I was dumbfounded and not sure how he could come to this conclusion without touching or looking at anything at all so I posted on a handyman Facebook group with many plumbers who all agreed this didn't sound correct with the video I uploaded. They all believed the leak to be coming from the drain. I had a different plumber come out and give me a second opinion and he actually fixed my leak in 5 minutes!! No need to rip anything out as it was just a seal around the tub drain. PK Plumbing is shady and Steve is dishonest and out to rip off the warranty company (and the customers along the way). My warranty company agreed to waive the service call fee and will be looking into the shady practices of this company. I would never allow them to step foot into my home again.

You really have to be terrible for me to give a restaurant one star, because as a Chef I know anybody can have a bad meal due to various situations (hence two stars) but the food here was just not good. 

Now I know this place has been a local favorite for years (and the place really looks it) but retro decor aside I just don't see the attraction. 

My Manhattan clam chowder looked like it literally took the trip down the turnpike to our table, tasteless and "full" of potatoes. The steamed clams were gritty and dry. 
My entree left much to be desired as my fries were way over cooked and my coleslaw was warm. At least the butterfly shrimp were good. 
My guest had flounder which served up a decent portion but otherwise Ehh!
Service was fair, but if you ask me they would do well to give the place a real face lift both in and out. 
I knew there had to be a reason I held on to this gift certificate so long. Sorry but never again.

The whole process was easy. They did the estimate to re-coat my roof and came when scheduled. The quote was detailed and addressed my concerns. Other quotes didn't address those concerns, so be sure to examine quotes carefully, I say. Since they don't do full roof replacements (or at least they didn't in the past), I feel they will only recommend work you need.

I was always curious to see how the food was because I work near by. I can actually say I'm not impressed for the crowds I see heading out for brunch with family/friends every weekend . I ordered the farmers platter, don't get me wrong, for me to have barely finished my meal I was disappointed at the quality of the food. Ordered my food a certain way and it wasn't received as such, but while being at work and unable to to leave I ordered for delivery. The menu doesn't have a nice variety selection either. I will not dine in, I think I'm sold with my first experience....very nice establishment for both locations !

Nice building/location - but service is always awful.  Been bad for years now.  This morning - no line in drive through - so figured would stop for coffee.  Placed order, said pull up to 2nd window.  When got to window - they didn't have any record of my order.  Pulled right out and went to Dunkin Donuts across the street.

This Waffle House is top notch! I have been to a lot of waffle houses in my days and the staff and unit manager were on point. The food was great and the service was even better. August the unit manager knows how to
Keep customer happy and coming back. There were many regulars in here and the staff new a lot of people by first name. I give this place a 5 star for service and food.
Tuna was good.  Filet was ok, needed zip sauce. Kids tenders were  good. Waiter was friendly but did go missing for a little bit but very friendly.

We ordered the Rockefeller and the 801 oysters, they were ok... the broadway oyster bar does them way better, 3/5. The scallop potatoes were sooo good 5/5. My filet mignon was cooked to perfection one of the best I've had. The grand marnier soufflé was mediocre. The service was exceptional. If you go I'd say stay away from the seafood and dessert. Stick to the entrees.

We took our cat Gypsy to the groomer and she did an amazing job!!! Perfection!! We had a not so great experience prior to brining her here and this has restored my faith for sure!! We are so happy with the result!!!!

Went here for dinner before a Cardinals game. Sat at the bar. It was early on a weeknight so there weren't that many people. Somehow the fact it was early didn't seem to stop the guy next to us from getting slightly pissed (drunk) and insisting we order the tenderloin tips... We resisted. 

We split a burger/fries. Burger was decent... Good size bun... average tasting burger. Had good char. It was obvious that even though we said no onions, one had been put on and then removed. Because the lettuce tasted oniony. That disturbed me. I'm SO not picky about food, but I allow myself to hate one thing. Onions. 

The fries? Good Heavens they were magnificent! The skins still on, some crispy, some soft, loaded with seasoning and salt... I want to go back and eat a whole basket. 

The bartender alternated between being friendly and looking irritated by our presence. Hmmph.

Came in Easter Sunday for breakfast and got seated right away. The owner was greeting people at the door which was a pleasant surprise. I got the croissant French Toast and it was amazing. This place reminds me of a small town diner. Service was great will definitely be back again

While the french windows and tiny tables give the ambiance of being in Paris (?), the service was super slow and I was starving by the time my food arrived. How long does it take to make french fries and scoop soup du jour into a bowl? The food is good but I do think it's over priced. The waiters were nice but super slow.

Three stars. 

My first delivery order was prompt and still hot. Exactly as ordered. 

Second order for delivery was average. I SPECIFICALLY asked for extra spicy for all orders. None were made with extra heat. 

Please make orders as requested!

New place but truly not worth the time and money.. waiter extremely rude.. they made such a big deal because my husband was wearing sandals and a dressy shirt... this is florida!! It's hot!! You're in a side walk.. but at another table 3 girls wearing shorts and bikini tops and sandals were ok??...when I asked the host he said.. they were here since brunch, mind you we came for dinner at 5pm... I would never come back here..

This place sucked!!  Got 6 oysters and sent them back. 4 of them where so small and at $3 each no way was I eating them. 4 barley the size of a nickel. Also had the kale salad. It was OK but nothing to write home about. Had a tuna platter that I had to get after they told me that they were out of the crusted tuna. Forgot the name but it was also nothing to write home about. Was still hungry so I ordered a $16.50 burger. I have to tell you Applebees burgers are ten times better. I normally don't write bad reviews but this place was not good at all. I will never go back to this place. The only good thing about this place was the drinks and our server was very nice. I'm only giving this one star because of the drinks and the server.

Burgers are great, wait time is long (because burgers are great).

However.... it never fails to surprise me how many kids people have in IN (and bring to Bub's).
We just moved from out of state and my wife and I started discussing how families at Bub's probably think we have fertility problems (with just 1 kid).

Also, I once came with two gay friends and, I must say, the entire restaurant was staring at us.
I felt weird.

Anyways, welcome to Carmel IN, where the neighbors will call the cops asking them to 
"check out" a suspicious car (AC repairman in this case) parked on the street late at night (8:15pm).

Prices were not as advertised. When we showed up in person the price was $20 more for weeknights and almost double for the weekend, than what was posted online. We had no place to stay, because our previous reservations were at a prostitute and Roach infested hotel, and booked this place in desperation 2 days before we needed it and when we found a better deal and tried to cancel our reservation they would not refund our $113 deposit, because "we did not cancel a week in advance." We can't cancel 1 week in advance if we didn't book a week in advance. They also never told us about their cancelation policy. So much for "southern hospitality".

I moved to Nashville not too long ago and hadn't found a doctor yet, because I didn't need too.
I came down with a sore throat and horrible cough and decided not to self medicate with over the counter stuff so I walked into the closest urgent care clinic I could find.

I walked in at 5:00 pm on a Thursday evening and was told that there was an hour and half long wait. So I decided to put my name down and come back at 6:30. Fair enough, it was my own fault for not booking an appointment online.

I go back at 6:30 and they make me sit in the waiting area for 15 minutes. Nothing wrong yet.
Finally the nurse takes me in to check the regular stuff and says the doctor will be just in, in a few, he says.
45 minutes later I'm still in the room and the doctor isn't here.

They could've asked me to wait in the waiting area, I would've at least had the company of my boyfriend. I'm just sitting in this room enclosed between 4 very sanitized white walls!

I'm sure they are really good healthcare providers, when they actually get around to providing it!

I might just stick to self medicating with over the counter stuff for minor ailments next time!
Absolutely delicious food. We enjoyed every bite. Definitely expensive but if you're looking for good, creative pizza and pasta, you can't go wrong.

I really wanted to give this more than 2 stars. I ordered through a delivery app, so I could not send food back. Just do you know. I ordered  hot chicken and waffles, with the spiked honey sauce. So I love waffles and I immediately ate those first. The waffles were wet and soggy but I still at them. I even tried adding the butter and the maple which wasn't maple syrup. That did not help. So then it was time to eat the chicken. I always break my wings apart, something we were taught growing up. And as I broke the first wing open a I saw was pink, and I was disgusted. I then opened the second wing and I saw more pink and I was very disappointed. I said ok third times a charm and yup it was more pink chicken. This meal overall was a disappointment. I never got a chance to even taste the chicken. Until you get your Temps right with cooking your chicken since this seems to be a common complaint I will not be back. I was looking forward to some hot and tasty chicken and this was not it.

Very disappointed our experience here.  We were told they were closed on Nov. 23 at 11:58 PM.  There were people in the bar and all doors were open  Hours on their website showed they were open until midnight. Reviewed them on Facebook and the manager pulled the wrong day and time and called us liars and that he didn't want it business anyway. I wish I had read the negative reviews before choosing to eat there.  I've donated items to Villa Lobos, walked dogs and fine the tour.  The shop is also cool.  Still a fan, just will help the rescue in other ways.

The mgt. DId send me $10 in the mail.  Why not take care of it at the time can't say.  Want to give credit for doing that though.  Has been a great place for a long time, and hopefully was just an isolated incident.

Overpriced.... but remember that it does give student discounts. I'm usually at the Westshore AMC but the occasional IMAX movie / off time gets me here. Remember to validate your chip coin and it's on 50 cents for 4 hours.
Don't be like me if you get your tires done and have a ton of loose change in your ashtray ‍ but someone might of needed it more than me ‍ great work and easy breezy experience!
Great food.  Really friendly service!  Would recommend as a good neighborhood brunch spot.

Can't get much closer to the convention center than this!  Right on the same block as Reading Terminal, if you aren't planning on doing any driving and just want a central place downtown, this works great.   The hotel was very clean, staff friendly, and the gym is small but good.  My biggest complaint was the pool...not terribly big (about 8 strokes long)...the first night I was there it was way too cold (and I swim in lakes), then after that it was like bathwater...ug.

The best Nachos ever!  That's what the waitress said, and she was absolutely right.  The chips are unbelievable!  

The fries are fresh and "real", but not too real that my picky 10 year old wouldn't eat them .  We also shared a wonderfully fresh and tasty rib eye.  

The service and patrons (older crowd) were all super nice.  They have TVs for games, but it's not for the rowdy loud crowd.  

I've heard they're breakfast is phenomenal!

Whether it is or is not good pho, this is about a matter of appropriating asian food and culture. You can replicate dishes all you want and it will taste the same, but you cannot change a culture and something vietnamese people have been doing for generations. You cannot say whether their customs or wrong or not because it is NOT your place to say. A white person telling us how to eat foreign food is incredibly disrespectful. This is our people's food and should be eaten the way we like to eat it. If you're going to have a vietnamese restaurant, at least have the decency to keep vietnamese tradition and not disrespect their culture by saying you eat it wrong. 

There is nothing ever wrong with tweaking dishes, but you cannot ever change a culture that you did not grow up with. Cultural asian foods should not just be "trends". When you take away the authenticity away from pho, its not even pho. White entitlement never ceases to fail me.

Decent rolls but nothing to write home about. The edamami was very good as was the green tea. We enjoyed the birthday roll although it was rather small and the shoya roll. There are many other places that are less expensive and tastier. Overall impression a bit overpriced for the quality and quantity.
no bueno...annoyed I couldn't get any service and the prices are a joke.
This is a good non chain coffee shop off of the main drag in Lambertville. Good bulk coffee lots of interesting coffee gadgets as well.... worth a visit.

Hooray for happiness! The team working the grill and counter at Spataro's Cheesesteaks (in the always popular Reading Market Terminal) were the happiest people I met in Philadelphia. They seriously seemed to love their jobs. 

I just wish I loved their cheesesteaks as much.

Oh, I'm not saying my Cheesesteak with Peppers from Spataro's was bad. It was fine, ok, and alright. It was also meaty, cheesy, filling, and pure Philly. It just wasn't lovable or the best.

Regardless, huge props to the crew at Spataro's Cheesesteaks for the happiness and joy on display behind the counter. You made my day even though the cheesesteak didn't.

I ate here two mornings while visiting from out of town. The first morning was excellent, it was my first time. The menu was extensive, food was delicious, and covid precautions were well managed. There was a 45 minute wait time which was accurate and they texted me to let me know when my table was ready. Great server as well. The second day was not as great, we were seated on the patio directly under the heater. The temperature was set on hell. We had to ask the take out runner if she could adjust the temperature before I melted into the floor like a drippy egg. Food was still good. The waiter we had was ok, seemed frustrated and not as attentive as other staff. I chalk it up as a busy Sunday. This will become my go to spot when in cherry hill but I dont want to be seated on the patio again.....thats for sure

my experience dus far from Martinez to Reno was awesome! now my return is less desireable only because of the attendent behind the counter..#1 He was not busy, so when I arrived a 1/2 hour early he said window closed. will not check my bags as I go to leave he then decideds to assist a lady to check her bags and a stroller..as I stated no one in line he was not busy and to dismiss me was highly unprofessional,so Amtrak if you hire people to be the face of a company please let them know about the first impressions! can impact a business

The food and drinks here are horrible!  It's a shame because it's a great night out with a group of friends and they usually have good acts come through. But when you have to order 2 things per person at least have semi good options.

Food was delicious, service was ok. Server kept disappearing to the back. When she my card, I didn't see her for 10 mins. I had to go ask for my card back. I would definitely return for the food, but ask for a different server.

Breakfast was a waste of time and money. The food was practically plastic. 

Gorgeous view from a few tables. 

I wouldn't go again.

I checked out this establishment today for a late late lunch based on the reviews. In addition, they have additional options for vegetarians. 

The space is cozy with seating both outside and inside. The atmosphere is definitely eclectic. 

The service was good/average. However, I will note that they we were brought the wrong pizza. However, a few minutes later our server quickly realized the kitchen made a mistake. Our server was apologetic and quickly got another pizza made/served. 

However, unlike other yelpers loving the pizza. I did not find the flavors of the pizza tantalizing. The ingredients were fresh, but I felt though the pizza was average, nothing special.

I always forget why I don't like ordering from this place. My most recent delivery reminded me why. 

The wonton soup that I ordered had some veggies in it. In the first bite into the bok choy, I bit into a rock! Owie! A simple rinse somewhere along the way would have gotten the dirt out of the veggies. *

I also ordered the mixed vegetables with seafood fried rice. The seafood fried rice is just a few pieces of shrimp mixed in with rice and soy sauce. The mixed veggies had a good assortment of veggies, but drowned in a brown sauce. ick! 

Another disappointment? In addition to the food being pricey, they charged me twice (total of $4) for delivery. 

* wash your veggies. Farms usually don't rinse the veggies because the shelf life of rinsed veggies is shortened.

We were 4 Vegetarians, two kids...many of the dishes are spicy so ask your server if that's challenge for you. We found the dishes inventive and quite good. Maybe a little too inventive. I found myself longing for something a litte more hardy, a little more filling and perhaps... Dare I say it, predictable? We sat at the Chef's counter which was fun and energizing. Our waiter was perfect and the ambiance of the place is just right.

Come to Speedway Dennys where the call in customers are fed first today on Christmas before the sit in customers.  A lot of people are sitting in waiting over an hour just for their food to be cooked but sat down with drinks to keep them full.

Went into this maggianos because I went to others in Florida.  Ordered and everything went find until they passed around our food to another table and tried to then give it to us. Manager remade it an chef came out and apologized.  I got the rigatoni D.  Shredded chicken on it was not great.  Food was overall mediocre. Then they tried to charge me for an extra meal.  Lol.  Oh well.  Won't be going back here.

This place has potential but very over priced for the quality. Just your typical salon with an extra $50 tagged on and not for the friendly service. Ashley is fresh out of beauty school and any of my friends could have done even better than her. If you go don't go to her!! Looked like I didn't even get my hair done for a very expensive price tag. I would recommend  looking elsewhere.

So disappointed. Restaurant is overpriced for the service and quality. The restaurant is beautiful with great artwork. This is the only good thing about it.  New Orleans is about hospitality and the service and management have neither. Snapper was salty. Two people order same entree and both came out with different portion sizes. Server tried to add automatic tip with a table of 5, but policy states table of 6, sneaky. Our water was never refreshed and we were never offered bread.

There are many restaurants in New Orleans which you don' mind paying top dollar, but this one is not one of them. It was Friday, the 13th. Unfortunately, 1st impressions are lasting impressions.

Let me start by saying that my husband and I loved The Garden on Grand, but something has changed lately. Normally I would give this place top marks, but the recent changes have made me suspect the place. 

The change in hours is one thing--no more weekend breakfast. But gone are the friendly guys we were welcomed by during our first five visits before. The two times I've been there recently, they weren't open but the front door was standing open. The music has changed and flashing disco lights have been added. 

I will probably give them one more chance, but the Garden feels like a different place these days.

This wonderful restaurant feels like home. The food is made with love, and is so delicious...I can't stay away! I love their (always changing) soups, and gluten-free paninis & desserts (you won't miss the gluten!) The service is warm (and supremely accomodating for dietary restrictions) And the atmosphere is cozy & comfortable.  You will feel better about yourself and life when you walk out of Adama :)

I hate my boss. I mean, I hate him.  Not for the ordinary reasons that people hate their bosses; in fact, he's one of my favorite people out there (I love you KSB). No, I hate my boss because, in addition to the other benefits of being a super badass who has earned enough to enjoy the finer things in life as a matter of course, more often than not on any given Friday afternoon, he can be found at Ruth's.  I have had the good fortune not only to have the opportunity to learn from the master, but to have been  taken to his famous Friday afternoons at Ruth's a few times.

Ruth's cuts the best steak you can eat at a restaurant.  They cook it precisely to order (even obeying my admonition: "take off the bell, wipe her ass, and walk her through a warm room"), lavish it in garlicy buttery ooey gooey goodness, and serve it up sans accompaniment.  I love that they prize their steaks enough to serve them solo - that volumes.  Their side dishes can stand up to the steaks, though - I ADORE the creamed spinach, the mashed potatoes, and the stuffed mushrooms.

The service is stellar, the windows are blocked out (very convenient for day drinking with the boss), and the whole ambiance is pure elegance.  I would recommend Ruth's to anyone.

Pretty good! I think this will be my new go-to spot. I ordered a chicken salad and an iced coffee. Coffee was good and they have plenty of non dairy options. I have to take one star off bc although the salad was made quickly on the spot, it wasn't a tossed or chopped salad; it really just reminded me of a prepared salad you'd buy at a supermarket. It just didn't really taste incredibly fresh and made on the spot. I really want to go back to try their sandwiches. The ambiance also seems perfect for studying!
You got be kidding me. The food is trash. One of the two stars I gave is for the drink I got, which is actually good.

Oh Cabo's whatever did you do?! You have painted the once lively and colorful place grey (who wants to sit in drab grey at the beach/), raised your prices and you have changed your staff so drastically that I am not sure I even want to return to this once favorite place I frequented while living there and visiting throughout my 40 years! Debbie made this place buzz with energy and she was always greeting people with a kind and friendly smile which made up for a lot that had been lacking. The service now is forced and semi unkind. The amount of ice put in your margarita pitchers is excessive - Note to consumers - go for the medium, as there is less ice and actually more margarita! My friend and I were able to barely get two each from the large and over two each from the medium. The al pastor tacos were very good, but the carne asada lacked flavor. Where did you put the seasoning salt for the chips? The tri tip is till my go to and the chicken is still delicious. The outside table are an improvement from the old and very shaky plastic ones. And what happened to the jukebox? I just don't know if I was even in the same place! I had to keep looking around like it was a bad dream! I remember coming here back when it was Chuy's and believe me, I would take peeling paint, rough floors and the old school feel any day when there were servers like Debbie and others like her that made this place really special. Its still Cabo's and it is still Carp and that may be the only reason I would go back.

My husband and I went here for lunch while visiting New Orleans as we heard great things. We waited in a line outside the restaurant for about 15 minutes or so. We thought it must be amazing for there to be a line, but we were not too impressed to be honest. We had the fried crawfish tails as an appetizer as well as a dozen of raw oysters. We shared a peacemaker po boy which had fried oysters and shrimp on it (it was big enough for two people). I liked the Tabasco infused mayo on the sandwich. With the being said I've had much better po boys in the past. My favorite of the entire meal was the fried crawfish tails. The oysters were huge, but not briney enough for me- but It seems that is how all oysters are in New Orleans? Later, a friend had told us that Acme was known for their grilled oysters, maybe next time we will try those if we were to come back to New Orleans.

The music was so incredibly loud that it literally gave me a headache! The band was good and live music is usually really enjoyable, but the volume was absolutely insane. 
The chips and salsa were okay. Nothing great, but not terrible. It was the same for the fajitas and veggie burrito. Just okay. There are better Mexican restaurants around, so I probably won't be back.

With beer such a big part of you experience it is disappointing that no attention is spent how the draft beer  is being served take a look at your draft beer no carbonation or foam beer is totally flat either the soap detergent you are using to clean your beer glasses is affecting the carbonation or your co2 air handler is effecting the beer. Get with any of your beer suppliers and they can assist thanks for having Stormbringer what a great band

This is an interesting score for me, I usually try and visit an establishment a few times to get a baseline reading so I know what to expect. Every single one has been so widely different than the others it's really hard for me to give a 100% accurate score. Im stuck between 3/4 and giving the benefit of doubt by listing as a 4. 

Overall the food quality has been swingy. The food at times is literally the best sandwich I've had in a long time. Other times the meat is dry and bland. I Personally order the cheesesteak so my opinion of food is limited to that one sandwich for now. Every time I order I hope that I get to experience the great tastes, but it's hit and miss.

The staff is mostly pleasant. I've witnessed a few interactions with other customers that I would have corrected if I was a manager on staff. There are ways to be compassionate and still express your point. 

The App is good for ordering but the single time I used it for delivery it took over an hour to get delivered and my sides were not included. The driver (who I believe is doordash) said something I couldn't comprehend about not having everything, but I was never notified prior from Jersey Mike's. Maybe they could have called me and asked for a substitute item rather than leaving me with something I didn't ask for a s still charging me.

I'd like to say I'll go here again, but I'd like to see some stability.

Ordered from doordash so I should start with that caveat; however when "toastpoints" are essentially a slice of baguette thrown in the oven for 20 secs and slicing the bangers and cooking them the way you would cook freshly expired meat are your best first impression it leaves me saying "meh". With $20 spent between a side salad and the entree the pricing is not crazy, but don't be fooled by flowery language in the menu; the cooks have the same skill and passion as your local fast-food joint.

I'd never had Provel before, so when reading about regional pizza, I was intrigued. And later when knowing we'd be spending the night in STL, I knew we had to try Imo's.  Since this location was a couple blocks away from out Airbnb, I called in a pick up order. The STL Special Box Or Whatever was a good way to try all the side nonsense like fried ravioli (chef boyardee meets alpo but still kinda good), provel bites (fried cheese cubes get cold fast), and cinnabuddies (they should probably be called that and are breadstick bites with the obvious sauce, obvi) along with a large two topping pie. 

Pie: THIN CRUST! CUT IN SQUARES! MINIMAL SAUCE! See, I hate Pizza King (MAF), but this pie was very nice. The provel is pretty much white american. Processed cheese is a little wack, but this works. Something about it just crushes. And unlike PK, Imo's uses real whole slices of pepperoni. Toppings were ample. They hooked the olives up.  

Service on the phone was whatever and it seemed like the kids in the store didn't really want to be there, and that's the lost star. Coulda been a couple bucks cheaper too. Maybe I didn't have to tip for carry out.  

I don't know when I'll be here again, but I will definitely speak highly of this delicious mutant pie.

Went for breakfast this morning-service was very, very slow. Only one cashier and she was running dining room orders as well as drive thru. A gentleman complained about the slow service, another employee laughed at him and then to the entire dining room:"you people bitch about it being slow, don't come in at 7am. Come at 7:30. This is shift change, I've been here since 11pm, you don't see me being miserable." She continued to laugh and make faces at the man behind his back. Very rude and unnecessary.

I have been going to this car wash for a good 8 years now and have watched it progressively get WORSE and worse.  It's at a low point right now. I'm not a angry customer or anything - I'm just over paying $14 for a terrible job. It's basically the machine they have does all the work and then they wipe wet towels around afterward. No care shown for your car at all and the management is terrible - speak broken english - rely on their son to talk. If there is a problem, they just get defensive. Failure of a location, honestly.

We really enjoyed the French decor and atmosphere. Great place for date night. Excellent bread to start any meal. We had the escargot for our appetizer and it was excellent. My wife had the scallops and I had the white sausage special. They were both good. Excellent coffee and port to finish our meal. Over all one of our favorite places to dine in the central west end area.

My boyfriend brought me here for our 2 year Anniversary dinner. It was a pretty nice place for dinner and in downtown Santa Barbara. The food is overpriced. But, what we had was pretty good. I liked the Pretty in Pink & The Pro Roll was pretty good as well. The Miso Soup was kind of bland though. the service was great. Our server was very nice and polite. It's very crowded, I guess because it has a 4.5 star. But, the wait isn't that bad, at least it wasn't that bad for us. We just gave them our phone number and they called us when our table was ready. So, while we were waiting, we walked around downtown. The atmosphere was nice. The restaurant was 2 stories. We sat upstairs in a cute little corner. Most romantic table in the joint. It's way too busy downstairs and has very little walking room. The door was really crowded with people coming in and going out. The parking isn't that bad at all either. you get 75 minutes free if you decide to park in a lot. But after 75 minutes it's only $1.50 an hour. We ended up paying 1.50 more because we walked around after dinner. Overall, I would go back and recommend this place to friends who visit Santa Barbara.

Sadly, we had a pretty not great experience at Alma. We visited on Sunday, Dec. 12, 2021 around 1:30pm for a late lunch after shopping. It was a long wait at the other restaurants near Alma but Alma was only 30 minutes, so we got a drink at the bar and waited for our table, which technically only took about 20 minutes. Sadly, after we were seated, no one ever came.... For over an hour! We actually got impatient and went back up to the bar for another round, but still, no one ever came to serve us. We had menus and everything so I'm not sure why we were never served. We saw the reviews online were mediocre so literally while we were still waiting for a server at Alma, I walked over to Whiskey Cake, waited 20 minutes for a table there, and we just left. Sadly, we never got to try the food at Alma because no one wanted to bring us anything, but the drinks and bar service were good. Wouldn't go back, just seemed a little chaotic and unprofessional.

Service Department: Scheduled an oil change for my Jeep Wrangler, when I arrived I was told about 90 minutes, Jeep was completed in about 45 minutes which included the exterior wash. Second time visiting this dealer and was satisfied both times.

I really don't see what all the hype is about Geno's or Pat's.  I've had better cheesesteaks at hole in the wall pizza joints around Philly.  Maybe it's South Philly preference but I lived in the Philly area for 6 years and everytime I had Geno's I was not impressed.  Go to Gooey Looie's instead - gooey and delicious!!

Came here for the hibachi though my daughter had the all you can eat sushi. Luckily we had reservations. It got crowded fast. The food was quite good and the price was very reasonable. You get soup, salad, hibachi and ice cream. The performance by the hibachi chefs were great as usual. For my daughter's sushi, she got an appetizer and all the sushi she could order from the menu. She was charged the kids price (17.95) though she ate like an adult. We will definitely be back next time we're in Reno.

You definitely get your money's worth here and what you are getting is absolutely delicious.  It's a little hectic when its busy but overall great food and friendly staff.  It may be obvious by the name but the hummus is the best I've had.

I was really excited to try out the sister restaurant to the Fork. This restaurant has a ton of hype surrounding many saying one of the premier "Italian" destinations in Boise. It is definitely a trendy place; wasn't an empty table in the house you will need reservations in order to get a table most nights. The customer service was top notch!! My water glass was never more than half empty always being refilled. The waitress was on point the whole night; she knew the menu like the back of her hand. She was very knowledgeable of the menu and helpful in finding you the right dish. For me; the food is where it fell flat. I don't know I ordered a few different things to give me a sampling of their menu. The appetizers ordered I thought was a pan fried mozzarella cheese ball; more like a pan fried rice ball with marinara sauce. I wasn't a huge fan plus it didn't even really seem very Italian a pan fried rice ball with marinara sauce. The salad was very fresh; the house dressing the waitress raved about an Italian dressing they make in house didn't really taste much of it; it was very fresh yet lacked flavor in this case the taste of Italian dressing. Lastly I ordered the rigatoni with short rib. I am still scratching my head on the Italian classification of this restaurant. They make all their pastas in house that part I get. This dish has absolutely no flavor/no pop to it at all. It was just rigatoni and "shredded beef". It literally was just an overpriced bowl of noodles with shredded beef. For me, the flavor didn't pop at all in any of the 3 dishes I ordered. I found the food bland and not even close to Italian. I walked away impressed wit the ambiance and customer service yet the food isn't going to be be bringing me back anytime soon. Maybe I missed out on it somewhere to me a northwest spin on Italian is now just high priced house made noodles and flavor wise isn't anything close to what one would expect in an Italian restaurant.

My wife and I recently traveled to New Orleans for Mardi Gras.  The first place we went after getting off the plane and the last place we went before returning was Kenner Seafood.  The food and service was great.  I'm going to miss the crawfish.
Are you kidding me? $12 for 2 single cones??  And, of course, the price isn't posted so you don't know until it's scooped & you're  at the register. And it's not quality ice cream.

I am borderline vegetarian and rarely eat red meat; however, the burgers here are delicious and I can almost NEVER turn them down when someone suggests we go here. These are the best burgers I have ever had. The superior quality is consistent and their customer service is unmatched. Please, do yourself a favor and try this place. You will NOT be disappointed.

I seriously debated before leaving this review, because I love Brugge's food. I always leave waiters 20% tips even when I get bad service because I figure my pay doesn't get docked if I have an off day, so why should waiters? I finally decided to leave this because this place could SO EASILY be a five star place if they would just work on their service. Their food is amazing, and that's why I keep going back-- I've gone a few times per year since they opened, and service has been a consistent problem for them. I'd go more often if the service was even just okay, but it's always bad. Their servers seem like they mostly work the kitchen or bar and just kind of show up to tables in between when they can. They usually are borderline rude. I'm not one of those people that expects or cares about "service with a smile," but these guys seem vaguely annoyed every time you ask for anything. One even cut me off with a "Yeah yeah yeah I know" when I started to ask for a water refill! Every time I go, there is at least 1 thing (usually more-- last time it was 3) I have to ask for multiple times. You basically have to tackle a waiter to get one water, let alone a refill. This kind of service would make sense at a place that was a little lower budget and casual, but Brugge's prices and ambience make you expect something nicer. And what is truly troubling is that the food is five star food! The mussels and frites are AMAZING. But seriously, whenever we go there, I remind everyone with me, "just remember we come here for the food." It's kind of a joke amongst my circle at this point. Brugge could easily be the classy nice restaurant in Broad Ripple if they just worked on this one thing.
No complaints. Great price! Great staff! Great service! I'm seeing 20/15.. Pretty dope.

Enjoy this Mexican restaurant especially on Taco Tuesday where you basically get a free soda with two tacos and a pile of nacho chips.  Service is decent, including bringing out your meal.  When they aren't super busy they are usually very attentive on refilling your beverages, unless you sit outside and then not so much.  If you run out of chips (which is rare) you can ask for more.  On Thursdays you can get a burrito with basically a free soda as that day's promotion. Their multi-sauce bar is also pretty god with a wide selection.  And the food is good beyond the taco and burrito I mentioned.  Prices are very reasonable and there is a lot of tables to sit and enjoy the couple TVs.

I have used wright's for take out lunch many times. I have not had a sandwich or salad that I have not liked there. The fruit and cheese platter is really nice for a group. Also they are known for their cakes. You can get an entire cake or just a slice. Either way it's AMAZING!

Ugh! Nice space, pleasant people, but terrible almond latte. Felt like I was drinking the coffee grounds, but with an unpleasant coffee taste. No balance, not smooth, not good. 

Cannot speak to anything else served there.

We visit Scape about 6-8 times a year. Up until this visit I went to lengths to extoll the positives about each and every visit. Each and every is pretty darn good in my book! Four of us dined there last night and were somewhat startled at the first piece of information provided by our server, Phillip; Chef Kelly left a few months ago. Wow, that's huge! 
The evening just didn't click this time. I'm trying not to blame in on Kelly's departure but some restaurants, especially the really good ones, are affected by a principal players departure. The Caprese salad and Gazpacho were spot on. My friends fish and my wife's items also hit their mark but my ribeye was terrible, both in quality and preparation. The menu states Cowboy, i.e., bone-in but it was boneless. Half went back to the kitchen as gristle and fat. It was medium well instead of medium rare, unforgivable. ($39) In all fairness, Phillip offered to take it back as he did our guests pasta (cold) but who wants to hold up a dinner for a steak to be reworked. Desserts were compted because of "the previous issues". As a final note, they took their signature banana dessert off the menu? I recall Kelly telling me once that it was their most popular dessert. Gosh, I hope this was just a glitch!

I had the Tuna Salad from their new seafood menu. Very disappointed. The asparagus was bad & the salad was mostly willted & sone brown to black. Won't be going back anytime.  At least the spinach bowtie paata was good.

Dr. Speyer is awesome! I went in for severe allergy's and horrid nose congestion. He very clearly explained everything and quickly got me setup for a procedure that immediately improved my comfort of living. 

And the allergy department is the sweetest group of ladies, and I actually look forward to seeing them every week for my allergy shot, which has helped tremendously with my day to day allergy symptoms.

I'd rate this a 3.5 if I could. 

We went for dinner around 8:15pm. There was a band playing on a Friday night that was asking for a $5 cover. Since it was our first time, we didn't want to pay a cover just to try out the food, so we said no thanks and walked out. A few seconds later, the door man came back out and said the manager told him to go ahead and let us in with no cover charge.  We took a seat at a back booth since the band (a blues band, that were actually pretty great) were pretty loud. We ordered a beer and looked over the menu. It was small in , but had plenty of BBQ options! I chose the brisket with beans and slaw. My buddy chose the pork steak and the same sides. We shared, of course. I found the brisket to have great texture. Not too soft, but it still was tender. It had a dry rub and came with a side of sauce. The sauce was really unique-a little sweet with a little spice! They mentioned they have a great hotter version but we didn't ask for any. The pork steak fell apart with a fork! It was also a dry rub with sauce on the side. It had a good smoke flavor as well. 

So, the food was good for the price, the ambiance??? Well, it's a restaurant connected to the Econolodge... so it appeared to have seen better days. But all in all it was in decent shape and the staff was very friendly. 

Would I go back? I think so. But it would not be my first choice for BBQ in the Lou.

The best person I interacted with was their internet / intern manager Daniel. Great guy. He was very professional, knowledgeable. I visited 5 Honda dealers on the day of my visit looking at used cars (great experience everywhere except here) and had a bad experience with the used car manager at this location. He was a bit abrasive overall, following me out of the place telling me how his price was final on the car and they would not take any money off their internet price and how their prices were better than anyone else around. 1st, I never tried to or intended to start a discussion about price on this visit, that was ALL HIM.  2nd, their prices were not all that great in my research and you really need to look out for the dealer fees / reconditioning fees on used cars in general. I ended up deciding to buy a new Honda, I got my 3 Truecar prices (Tampa Honda was not one of them) and even then called around some more and had 3 dealers beat the Truecar price. I decided since Tampa Honda was my local dealer, I'd give them a shot at my business. Even with all the great quotes I had from around town (I emailed them a few of them) , they came back with a price on a brand new 2018 Accord, Get this, at least over $2,000 higher than everyone else; they said that was the best they could do. I ended up buying a car from Kuhn Honda the next day. (Top notch dealer, look for my next review on Yelp about them).  So if they don't need a sale that bad, fine, but to stick to a quote $2000 higher than 6 other dealers in the Tampa area, that's crazy in my book. Coupled with the brush in with their used car manager, probably will never bother with them again.

Me encanta esta pizzeria, diría que es una de mis favoritas en todo el vecindario, usan real queso mozzarella, los mejores ingredientes y todo súper fresco, definitivamente es una pizzería que recomendaría a todos probar. 

I love this pizzeria, I would say that it is one of my favorites in the whole neighborhood, they use real mozzarella cheese, the best ingredients and everything super fresh, it is definitely a pizzeria that I would recommend everyone to try.

So much miscommunication!! Do your employees even know how the wellness plan works? If so then why would you schedule a visit under the wellness plan that isn't covered and then call me 30 minutes ahead to say oh by the way this will be out of pocket. This has not been the only time this has happened. Please learn your products for the health of the hundreds of pets served here.

If you've ever traveled the roads on any of the islands in Paradise, then you surely have had plate lunch - the staple mid-day meal that consists of two scoops of rice, a scoop of macaroni salad and a choice of meat. Often served at a roadside stand or more likely from a food truck, it's the island go-to for Kam'aina (local or resident; aka child of the land).

L&L is the real deal and it was quite a surprise to find a coupon advertising it in a local coupon mag. We made a bee-line for the franchise and found it to be just like the ones we visited on Oahu and Kauai. Affordable lunch served by congenial wait staff.

The Kalua pork was tender and moist--relatively bland contrasted to Tennessee barbecue; the generous portion proved to be enough for two meals (or to share). The Chicken Katsu was spot on: golden brown, crispy coating on a tender chicken breast. Be sure to dip in the accompanying Katsu sauce...but don't count the calories. They're pretty high.

A big disappointment was the Poke. I am a Poke aficionado and I've tasted everything from commercially made Poke to (my) Hawaiian family's recipe--literally prepared on the beach with fresh-speared fish. Both the Shoyu (bland) and the Spicy (ugh mayo-like dressing hiding any true flavor) fell far short of the mark. The Spicy did have a mild and lingering heat, but that's the extent of it. Where's the Limu, bruddah?

I am anxious to return and try some of the other offerings. The Spam Musubi (which we have made at home) and the Loco Moco looked good. (For some people anything covered in brown sauce is good.) My guess is, they probably are. For now...(3.5) 4 stars for an inexpensive plate lunch.

I've been here a few times over the past year and the service continues to get worse each time. This trip we sat for 10 minutes before anyone came to talk to us, our drinks came out in waves and only 3/4 full and our server was trying to get the attention of other employees (to check on other tables) while she was taking our order. Our food also came out in waves. Some people got their burger, others got their side, no one got both. The food quality was mediocre. The only real attention we received was when it was time to pay the check. While it was lunch time, it was not crowded. Very disappointing.

This is one of the busiest Wawas that I have seen. On many occasions, the bar with the condiments for the coffee has been totally disgusting. There seems to be only one cashier for all of the people in line waiting to pay. I have to go there since there are not many Wawas in the area.

We booked a 2 hour city tour with VIP.  The driver was friendly and informative and even worked in personal stories into his tour. Overall an interesting tour especially the Katrina and cemetery portions.  We took the 4pm, but this time of year it gets dark so its hard to see the spots at the end of the tour.  So keep that in mind when choosing your time.  It's a great way to get an overview of the city and hear some history behind it, you even get to see a couple of celebrity homes.
Don't bother making an appointment; it isn't any faster. Just show up as a walk-in if you have to go.

Smoothie was delicious, don't get me wrong. But their inconsistency with enforcing mask rules is why this place is getting 3 stars. There are 2 or 3 signs on the front door saying that masks are required to enter the establishment. Yet, a woman walked in with no mask on and no employees batted an eyelash or asked her to wear one, and she was promptly served. Everyone else in the store had a mask on. If you're going to place signs on your door requiring masks but don't enforce it, that is not fair to the patrons who are following your rule. Too bad the smoothie was so tasty.

The most amazing boba joint in reno! I definitely recommend the monster boba latte and the signature milk tea. The drinks are so delicious and refreshing. I'm never going to another boba place in reno.

The best bang for your buck for apartments in Germantown. The apt is seasonally priced and the apts seem to be bigger than the others in the area. There is plenty of parking for guests in the garage or on the street. You get the benefit of being in Germantown and 1.5 miles from downtown but feel like you're in a great neighborhood. Better than being in the congestion closer to Jefferson.

Terra Nova did a awesome job with outside dining. Very enjoyable. However, the menu has been the same there for many years. Needs a make over on the menu. Nothing that really is different. Love the sushi
Rolls. Love the happy hour when we could have a happy hour.

Absolutely our favorite place for Mexican fare.  Always good service, always great food and atmosphere.  Never, EVER get tired of this place!!!  Lunch prices are a great bargain and after lunch stroll a few doors down to the authentic Mexican bakery and have a drool fest there....  Las Palmas -- We LOVE YOU!!!

BE CAREFUL - ER DOCS MAY NOT TAKE YOUR INSURANCE!

Had to go there twice.  Service and speed were fine, but their billing for the ER docs is a mess.  The hospital billed us right away and everything for the facility was as expected.  

But - both Physicians didn't bill until a half a year later, and the hadn't even submitted to my insurance.  So, after calling and asking for that, it turns out that the ER docs practicing there aren't on our health plan (BCBS, which is extremely widely accepted).  So it seems that this is another case of "you don't have a choice of doctor, so we'll screw you and bill you full price."  

So, be very careful and ask first (like that's an option when it's an emergency ...), you may be in for a steep price!!

My night at Killer Shrimp was out of this world. I came in with a few friends and got a variety of appetizers: calamari, popcorn shrimp, and the ahi tuna poke. Every dish exceeded my expectations and I enjoyed the atmosphere. The manager ended up buying us a few of the specialty drinks: the westside which was by far the best drink on the menu! I will definitely be going back to this place!

This food truck place has the cutest set up ever and was very much worth of a trip to get some drinks. I got the Return of the King, Mango Tango and Sticky Sticky Mango. Return of the King has a slight durian taste but it wasn't overwhelming however the smell is still there so try not to get this if you're going to talk to somebody later lol. Overall, it was a nice drink to try once in a while. The Mango Tango was EXTREMELY sweet and I literally thought I was drinking straight up sugar. Even though, it looked appealing, I would probably not get it again due to the extreme sugariness. The Sticky Sticky Mango does not look like the picture on the stand they have and it definitely was not worth the price it was. I was disappointed to see how measly the portion was and how hot it was. Since it's a dessert, I expected it to be cold but that's just my expectations.

Burt food, bad service, dirty this restaurant is everything bad!! To top it of one of our servers assistants a young man, was extremely rude laughed when my husband told him how bad the service was, when my husband got up to find the manager he got in my husbands face and started yelling at him he needed two other employees to come pull him away!! Unbelievable never in my life have I ever had such a horrible experience in a restaurant!

I went in with a blood pressure spike, back and jaw pain and a history of DVT and PE.  Everything was quick and efficient getting checked in, but then it all came to a stop.  I was there six hours and never saw a Dr. until the end.  Heck, I barely saw a nurse.  I did have bloodwork, an ekg, a chest xray (all done in the first 30 minutes), and thanks to the nurse, a CT scan to rule out a PE.  After five hours, and me asking where the Dr. was, I finally saw a D.O. who said everything was great and as long as my BP looked good, I could go.  It took another hour to get a nurse to take my BP.  It was not good...168/91, but the Dr. said I could go anyway and to go see my Dr. the next day.  Instead, I looked up my bloodwork on the hospital portal and saw four tests were not ok and all of them pointed to DEHYDRATION!  I drank five bottles of water and by that evening, my blood pressure was back to 112/68 which is normal for me.  All it took was Google and water.  If they had hung a bag of saline, it would have been fixed.  I already had a needle in my arm.   If they can't figure this out, what are they doing with life threatening emergencies?

Update:  Largo Med billed almost 18K for this worthless visit.  Thank God I have Medicare.  This is why we need single payer in this country!  It's obviously working in countries like Canada, England, Sweden, etc.  So why can't we do it?  Oh that's right...Republicans.

I like the restaurant but on a recent Friday night they were closed for a private party, so I was left standing in the rain looking for alternative restaurants.
