They don't take reservations and their website, that I had reviewed that afternoon, didn't warn me about the closure. I guess it's ok if you are a neighborhood resident, but it's a little awkward if your Uber dropped you off and now you are left wandering around in the rain and dark. I probably won't try again because it's too much trouble if they happen to be closed. I prefer restaurants that take reservations so I at least know if they are open.

I used Mark Hill and Tandem Realty for six years to rent out two of my properties.  Initially, I was pleased and recommended them to a friend.  He had issues with them early on and left.  It took me a while to catch on.  I could never get anyone on the phone, they didn't do a good job of screening applicants, they used handyman companies that I could have called and found on Google, and some of the repair work was really bad and there was no quality control.

Sad but true..this place certainly didn't pull through.  The service was crappy, and kept giving us the wrong rolls.  They ran out of high chairs.  They argued us when we said what we ordered.. They need to learn all about service and pleasing custumers. Won't be back here again!!

After my last visit (and review), I wanted to give A Sense of Serenity one more shot. I visited around 2pm on a weekday and the store was empty, leaving me ample time to browse. 

The shop, once again, had me feeling uneasy, and the gentleman behind the counter had an off-putting vibe about him, which didn't help.

They offer a wide selection of Angel and Tarot cards, many of the decks being at least $5 more than the same decks purchased at a similar store downtown. 

While I can't speak to their other book and  gift prices, I can speak confidently about their stone collection.

Since I frequent many shops for stones every few weeks, I am able to gauge whether certain crystals and gems are in line with pricing around town. I couldn't begin to fathom the exorbitantly high prices listed for the stones in ASoS. Most stones were AT LEAST $5 - $10 more than you could purchase them anywhere else and when I found pieces of optical calcite (comparable to a specimen I purchased for $4 at another store the previous week) labeled with  $80.00 tags, I made up my mind. I will NEVER shop at a store that so blatantly takes advantage of it's clientele. It's places like A Sense of Serenity that give new age/metaphysical shops a bad rap. 

I ask ANYONE looking for stones and crystals to go to ANY other shop in town. You will find friendlier, more knowledgable staff, pleasant vibes, and fair prices.

Pleasant atmosphere, okay food. It's just glorified bar food (nothing wrong with that), but it was not priced like bar food. The waiter was trying his hardest to upsell some stuff we didn't want. I understand, it was lunch and quite slow, but you will not create repeat customers by being pushy. Long time customers are what sustain businesses, of which we will not be. First and last time visiting this establishment. Sorry restaurant, just being honest.

I love that Costco sells things wholesale.  I understand the reasoning for needing a card to get in, but as someone who doesn't have a family yet, it makes no sense to get a card of my own (although, I hope one day to have one).  I usually go with my mom and we could spend hours in there looking around.  While you can usually go for certain items, there are times they will just stop carrying whatever item you are looking for with no idea when they might get it back in stock.  What I can tell you?  Don't go here on the weekend during the day.  The best times to go are during the week or evenings about an hour before they close.  However, if you are looking to sample items, then I guess you better brave the crowds over the weekend!

I really was looking forward to having First Watch--it's definitely been a few years--and my first at this particular location. I remember liking them in the past, but after today's experience, I might choose a different breakfast spot. We ordered online for pickup.

There were so many good-sounding items to choose from, I had a hard time deciding. I opted for the chickichanga since it sounded flavorful and something different than you find on other breakfast menus. My husband ordered the pumpkin pancake breakfast. We also got a side of the million dollar bacon.

When it arrived, it looked great...except the fruit "bowl" had two blueberries and three small pieces of pineapple. The potatoes were extremely salty, and that's all you could taste. They were cooked perfectly, crispy on the outside, so it was really disappointing. The main entree was good. Nothing special enough for me to order again.

The bacon was okay. It was spicy and maple syrupy :)

But all in all, it wasn't good enough for me to choose again when we have other breakfast options in the area.

Romeo & Juliet was well done. The lighting , stage presence from the dancers and music made a worthwhile performance. I would recommend people to visit once more to see the Black Swan or the Nutcracker.
The shrimp tacos were A+++ and my whole family LOVED the tortillas. Try this place when you're in Tampa.

So impressed with Dr. Soli  and highly recommend him as a very knowledgeable veterinarian. He spent a long time with me and my dog - we were first-timers - and through simple tests, and a solution he mixed up himself, he managed to heal my dog's chronic ear infection without antibiotics. Also, I didn't have to wait at all - they took us back immediately and They promptly called me with test results. So happy to have discovered this vet!
Nice clean place but no coffee choices.  Says coffee on the sign but just a carafe of regular coffee.

I love this little place. I lived in Sierra Vista and would drive all the way to Tuscon just to visit. My husband and I loved the hookah and we especially enjoyed their house flavors (Ice Flower being my personal favorite). They don't offer fruit bowls or the ice hoses but honestly the shisha options they have more than make up for that. The staff was very attentive even when it was busy and would frequently check in and bring fresh coals. It's also very affordable. 
The bar had great drinks and the whole atmosphere was just overall pleasant. On weekends they have themed parties, karaoke, or a DJ so it's very lively. During the day the outside patio is also a nice place to relax and smoke with a coffee. 
We just moved to North Carolina and we have yet to find another hookah spot that even holds a candle to this cafe. We miss this place a lot so if you have the opportunity to go I highly suggest it!

I've eaten here several times! Generally the food is really good. Lately the food has been ok. This morning we went and I ordered a white chocolate mint cold brew. When I received it it was 3/4 of the way. So when we went to leave I went to order another, of course will to pay. That was upon asking them to not put so much ice in the cup the employee said "we have to put that much ice in there for cost purposes". That is NOT something you tell a customer!

Our first time here just ordered the virgin margerita. Sour! Decor is very nice and festive. Husband had chicken enchiladas and I had mancho nachos. His choice was basic or soso sauce tasted like chef boy r d' s sauce but clean in taste and texture. My dish was delish with every vote. Must try!

Stay Away! I stayed in filth for the duration of my nights. Human hair balls all throughout my room. Complained to front desk (Zoti I believe her name was each time, brunette shoulder length hair approx 5'5") Three nights in a row was told no one could do anything at this time that would clean in the morning. Never the less when I got back in in the evening my Bed was made but hair balls still remained through out.  Front desk Joanna (blondish hair, 5'1") didn't care wouldn't help me in anyway upon check out, incompetence on many factors here. If you like dirty rooms this is the go to place for you.

Amazing brunch/breakfast spot in the heart of Center City. We started with an excellent bloody mary, and moved on to our delicious entrees.  Avocado toast with perfectly cooked eggs and Creole shrimp over cheesy grits with poached eggs.  The food was terrific, and the service could not have been any friendlier and more professional. Beware, the waits can be long on the weekends, and they only take cash.  But, this place is well worth the wait and the cash only hassle.

I use another bank/credit union someplace else, but learned of this bank through my spouse.
I have never had an issue with this credit union whether I'm going through the drive-through or coming inside.
 The people are usually helpful and if you have banking problems with your ATM card or anything they're quick to get in there and help you with that.
 I loved the rewards program up until this year when they stopped giving reward points towards the debit cards which I think is unfair...but I guess that is business.
Missy was amazing! She placed my party in a quiet little corner and hooked us up with our champagne!! Best bottomless mimosas for such a great price!

I had lunch here recently, and this sits squarely at 3.5 stars.  The front patio is great, right on St Charles and close to other neighborhood attractions.  The inside bar is nice as well.  There is a small, but good beer selection on tap.  Several NOLA offerings, always good.

The service is friendly, if a bit slow.  Though not a problem.  The food ranges from small plates and po boys to fillets.  I had the Shrimp and pasta plate.  Good flavor, not to garlicy, with some kick from the peppers.  The angle hair pasta was cooked well.  All in all a good lunch.  A place I will visit again if I get a chance, maybe for happy hour and try some more of the menu.

My food was fair.  I ordered a fried shrimp and oyster platter.  It was filling and tasty, though nothing special.  My friend ordered an etouffee.  He's polite and doesn't like to complain about food in public. But he only took two bites of his seemingly soupy plate of rice and decided he was done.  Our waiter, a young man with dreadlocks, acted like he didn't want to be there.  It felt a bit rushed and he didn't make himself approachable for questions or even desert requests.  I wish I hadn't given him the low tip I gave him because he was nasty.  

There are other fabulous restaurants to dine at that where the food is good and the service just as good.  Check out Huck Finns on Magazine Street.  Stay away from Gumbo Pot!
I was there with a group. Most people liked their entrees, which however were a bit small. Great fish and chips. Love the local beer selection. No one liked the olive oil ice cream.

Always had an good experience until now. I paid $180 for an contact exam, received a trial that I was unable to wear. Follows up but was unable to because I no longer had them. They wanted to charge me another $180 for another eye exam to get another trial. Customer service got ugly with me as I tried to explain myself.

Omg! The Spaghetti is amazing !!!! I definitely would recommend! I remembered this restaurant from  like 2yrs ago and it's definitely worth the money. Prices are  just right! I drove 30min just to eat lol that drive was most definitely worth the ride. I will be going back.

MehMehMexican
Only people who think Applebee's and Chili's are haute cuisine could like this place. That ssid, it is wildly popular. The Mexican plate wasn't awful. Nothing was awful. The margaritas were even good. I just don't see why anyone would wait 5 minutes for a table here. Bean dip is 80% cheese. Chips were pretty greasy. Beautiful area. Nice ambiance. Eat at Los Primos and then come here for a margarita. I have nothing against them. I simply have nothing for them. Meh.

Still not sure about this place after three pies. The flavors are good, no doubt...  But we feel like the pizza could be a little more cooked.. It was soggy. The slices are very limp, on every pie we had. I guess we just don't like it limp.

Four stars because Mad Donna's is really trying, and the owners are super friendly. I, too, miss the Radio Cafe even though I only went there a couple of times. The past is past, though.

Mad Donna's has become quite a hotspot in East Nashville, located just across the street from Lipstick Lounge. Quite a few of my friends go there quite often. 

I would avoid the fish tacos. I still haven't found any in town as good as Baja's, but I will keep trying. The sweet potato enchiladas were good, though. They also have solid nachos and other basics.

I've been for brunch one time and had a really good omelet. However, service was pretty bad. It was maybe the second weekend they were open for brunch, so I'm sure they were still working the kinks out. They also had plastic sheeting up outside which is a good idea to keep the bugs away, but the solid plastic sheeting had a sort of greenhouse effect. 

The bar inside is pretty nice, and there's also a bar/lounge upstairs with a dance floor. I think they have done a good job renovating the space, and once they get everything ironed out, this will be a favorite neighborhood haunt (and I'll cross the river to go there every now and then). And, like I said, the owners definitely seem like they want to get things right which is a big plus. Oh, and thankfully they have removed the table we were seated at right by the door one cold cold March evening.

Have gotten delivery from here many times. We like to get the lamb samosas, chicken tikka masala, chicken vindaloo or chicken curry. All white meat and spice levels can be adjusted-we like it pretty spicy. The onion and spinach naan are really tasty and arrive warm and fresh. Price points are reasonable. My only criticism would be I am not a fan of the biryani.  Definitely give the a shot. I believe the do a buffet at lunch.

Sky Cafe, I'm so sad that I'm moving away and leaving you. 

I came here on Saturday with my sister and it was like being transported back to Medan, Indonesia. We were the only non-Indonesians in the place. 

You MUST get the sate ayam (chicken sate), and the kwetiau goreng (fried noodles). Both taste just like the food that I used to eat in Indonesia. Sitting in the restaurant and listening to the bahasa Indonesia and stuffing myself with Indonesian food was definitely the highlight of my weekend. 

I also love how affordable this place is. Lunch for two came to $12.
The only place I go to get a slice of pizza when I'm in the city!    I've been a loyal customer and I've never been disappointed when it comes to pizza or cheesesteaks for that matter.

Not so good...

Popped by for a nice lunch on a Tuesday.  It was in between lunch and dinner - around 4pm.  But about 15 other people were eating so I stopped in.  Ordered a simple, small Jambalaya and a romaine heart salad with Caesar Dressing - quick and easy.  As a local I know jambalaya.  This was NOT a good example.  The portion was laughable - barley a large spoonful in a large round bowl.  Was it meant for a cup? Even so it would not have filled it.  Then to add insult to injury it was dry and over cooked.  Not an ounce of moisture.  The rice was dried out, the sausage hard and shrimp chewy.  BAD!  This is a staple dish in New Orleans! How do you get it wrong? A store bought mix would have been exponentially better.  Then the salad....what a sad salad.  About what you would expect from a Safeway salad bar - not a marble topped Vieux Carre establishment.  Each item was $8.  With  a drink and tip it was a $21 meal not worth $4.  And the waiter - uncomfortable and peculiar acting.  Not sure if he was in a rush to go home or simply over it all.  Will not be going back and I will be sure to tell all of my out of town visitors that despite the unbelievable location - not worth it.

We ordered two drinks, I always thought that I enjoyed matcha, however I was proven wrong this time, the coconut matcha latte was for me a bit seaweed tasting. The matcha was still slightly powdery which was unpleasant for me. Perhaps I'm not a fan of matcha but regardless this was not the drink for me.

However, the pistachio delight was soooooo good, I still dream about it. It was perfectly creamy, the pistachio wasn't completely broken up which left tiny pieces of pistachio heaven, the boba was a neat addition, the drink was so good honestly I wouldn't of noticed if the boba was gone. Definitely will go again and will try their other drinks, and maybe order a teatanic size of the pistachio.

When I read the reviews I thought this place was going to be delicious honestly it was just ok I went there the first time and it was a 45minute wait outside ...there was still a line inside. The next morning I came back and got breakfast. I'm glad I didn't stay in that long line there were 3 pieces of shrimp in my omelet way to expensive for that out the service was great

this car wash is ok if ur just are buying a one time wash , 3 times i tried to buy a package  or buy 2 get one free ,the machine never works,  So u have to get help. Sometimes help comes to u . Usually  U have to go to them . Sometimes u get a gift card   sometimes u get a receipt thats SUPPOSE TO SCAN .  All 3 times I bought ONE I HAD TO GET HELP   EVERYTIME  I returned & tried to redeem my washes  I had to get help .  The last time i tried to buy  a buy two  get one free the machine didnt work . Nobody came out so I went to them , When I get there  they are playing hacky sack . I get treated like im bothering them . Im rushed off & handed a receipt . I ASK NO CARD ?  Told no just scan this receipt .  Obviously  Next time i return it doesnt work .When i left today   I am told next time to just come in cause this receipt  wont scan . After  this next wash ive paid for I wont be back ,  I have 2 side notes to add .  1rst I want to apologize for losing my temper to the last guy trying to help me  Ryan (i think ) . It wasnt his fault .  2nd its a shame because I would have been a life time customer . When Pelican Point  built a car wash in the next blk I felt sorry for them. They r local been here for years & an out of town corporation sets up shop right next store. I had every intention of doing all my business with them .

I ordered a $100 tray of hoagies to be delivered for the holidays to my local doggy daycare. I was very specific about address, time and card. The delivery person called me 30 minutes after it was to be delivered and was lost.  He never called back but I got him an hour later and he told me he made it. Weeks later when I didn't hear anything from daycare I asked and they said they received with note or card. So they had no idea who they were from. I called primo to let them
Know of error and basically got told well I wasn't here that day.  I will think again before using them for tray delivery.  When I spring money for a service especially around the holidays I would hope for the company to get it right.

Came here on New Years Eve around 4 pm and they were able to fit me in for a manicure and pedicure with no wait. Not the fanciest salon, but I got 2 pedicures and 1 manicure for $47 - can't really complain. The woman doing my nails was polite and kind. She did a great job shaping my nails which is my biggest concern (polish is polish - it will eventually chip). The salon is clean and actually pretty spacious as it goes back pretty far with 4 pedicure chairs in the back and about 5 manicure stations in the front. They also do massages upstairs and have waxing available. Would come back here if in need of a manicure or pedicure in a hurry.

I was a happy man when entering this diner, but when I left, I was broken, down, and empty. I give charity, I help out my friends and family when in need, and I believe in caring for others. This experience at the diner ruined me and made me darker. The head receptionist is consistently rude to my friends and I. She gives an attitude about getting separate checks. She is very rude to us and it destroys my self-esteem. She makes me feel that I don't belong. What did I do to deserve this treatment? Or should I ask what should she do to be whole again? Why are we treated with such disdain? I wish her well and hope she becomes better again. Good luck, your guide.

First time for me, very nice decor really well done. Food is very good and staff was knowledgeable and attentive, place was clean and vibe was nice. Had the chopped salad w tuna, cheesesteak egg rolls, edamame and the fries. Everything was very much on point, quality was above average and prices were reasonable.
Looking forward to my next visit and exploring the drink menu..

Boyfriend and I came here for brunch on the Friday of Mardi Gras weekend. The ambiance is great, very casual, service is awesome, easy parking, and with the Yelp option to put yourself on the waitlist, we skipped a 30 minute wait and were seated immediately.

We also took advantage of the buy one get one free mimosa deal from Yelp. Mimosas were a 6/10 at best. The juice and champagne weren't the best quality, but not bad for a BOGO deal. Probably wouldn't order them myself though at the regular price of 10 dollars. 

The food, however, we were pretty disappointed by. We were really excited given the Yelp reviews, but the food was lukewarm when served, the Costa Rican Breakfast was extremely poorly seasoned aka not seasoned at all, and the Shrimp Boogaloo was meh. 

Perhaps the reason for our disappointing experience was not getting what other Yelpers recommended. Maybe if we got the Eggs Cochon or french toast special we would have a more positive review. Definitely take into account that the entire menu is not as good as some dishes may be.

Congress has a disease. On one hand, congress has restaurants with great interior decoration, nice service, environmentally-forward policies on straws and napkins, easy parking options relative to other metropolitan areas, though basically everything great about these restaurants disappears in the back of house. The Hub restaurant is one of these places. 

The menu is confused. I'm quite certain that only kids places or childrens menus are supposed to have food from varying cuisines--and, in my defense, usually that's okay when it's a fusion place or the flavors make sense. But this is an establishment that has all-American burgers w tater tots, but also pho, and ontop of that, fancy European braised pork belly on the same menu. My memory doesn't serve me well, so there's likely more contradiction in this menu, but at our table, the conflict gave off a really amateur vibe of the head chef. Is this all he can think of?

With that said, the food we did order was really well made. The pork belly was pretty tender, the burger and sandwiches were constructed solidly, the lasagna... was lasagna, the pho tasted like pho, though the mac n' cheese tasted like it had a Kraft single ontop... which it might have. Our table was inconclusive over whether it did or didn't. But that's kind of a problem. Lasagna can really only be so good. It's egg pasta, tomato sauce, cheese, and meat. There's not a lot of creative input there. This isn't a luxury burger place, these are just normal burgers. Pho can be super interesting, but it's played safe here. All of these things are pretty well done... for dishes that don't actually require an expertise to make or think of. 

With that said, this could be a great restaurant, the ice cream is a really good foot above other places. But we've gotta cut the TV's in the bar, sportsbar isn't a good look when the entirety of your restaurant is geared to look contemporary and modern. Service was nice and attentive, shout out to Robert, I think.

I'm gonna give Paul 2 stars instead of 1 because what he did do.... Came out great. He was easy to get ahold of, also, good about getting back to me. I've never used yelp till now... Thought I'd share about this experience. Paul just may fall into the category of what you call, "shyster"..... I'm totally aware that I could go to local hardware store and rent a tile and grout steam cleaner for about $50 per few hours. Thing is I'm a busy guy... Had a lot going on at the time like always and my SUV was full of stuff, I was in the middle of moving so I figured, hell, I'll call somebody, make my life a little easier and let someone make a couple bucks. I don't knock the hustle... Love to see people doing their thing. Thing about Paul is.... When we spoke on phone I let him know I was aware of going rate but when he came and gave estimate that rate went up. I reminded him about our conversation then the rate went back down a little. I still felt it was a little high being how he wasn't going to have to move anything really but I let it go. The day of the job he was here earlier than scheduled. I like that usually but he came inside when he saw me throwing a couple floor mats out front...before I was ready and then attempted to help move my Ottoman for me which happen to have 2 good sized mugs of coffee on it. How he didn't notice the coffee on the Ottoman, I don't know but it inevitably spilled. He said leave it cause he'll clean it up but had a weird little attitude about him. I pick up on these things. The guy was in his feelings about the coffee he just spilled all over my stuff, then disappeared outside. I left the house (small condo actually) to take my daughter to school with a bad taste in my mouth. I'll admit Sometimes I'm not a morning person. My daughter hadn't cleared her floor all the way the night before so I wasn't the happiest and I don't like grown men that spill my coffee and then get a little bitchy attitude with no apology.   The job was so small...it took him less than an hour, literally. I was just down the street when he called so arrived in minutes. Floor looked great from what I could see. I was very pleased.... While in middle of my inspection he let me know he had a busy schedule that day and needed to get going. He Was already packed up and trying to leave so I went ahead to square up with him. All of the sudden the rate went back up. I said something about it as he's starting his truck and trying to leave. Shows me some piece of paper that had numbers on it I've never seen that's supposed to mean something to me and was different from the numbers we spoke about. I already know what time it is. I let it go and gave him the money so he'd just leave. I had to because little did this prissy attitude having guy know, I felt like giving him a slap for trying me but I can't go that route these days and.. naturally he doesn't even have change.. so I really need this man to leave before something bad happens. Go back inside to finish looking things over.... Guess what's still on the other side of my ottoman, couch and floor going all underneath and behind my couch...... coffee. Thanks Paul. Paul is not what he seems. And if he's reading this... He knows where to find me. You guys... Go to home Depot and spend a fraction of the price that Paul charges. If you can't do that...Don't let anyone charge you more than 35¢ a square ft unless they're moving some serious furniture around to do your floor. I'm telling you... Stick to what I just told you. 35¢ per square ft at the most.. Even if your floor is real dirty. If they have a rebuttal to that, tell em that you'll go to Home Depot and spend 50 bucks then see what they say. I thought car salesman cornered the shyster market

We got a coupon and since I didn't have P.F in so long; I really wanted to avail it. I have had better tasting P.F Changs. Their lettuce chicken wrap starter was great and on point. 

However; their spicy sichuan beef and chang's chicken, the signature dish were small portions and the sweetest spicy mains I have ever had. Disappointing; especially compared to other franchises. 

It will take desperation or an involuntary social gathering to have me dine here. 

Just maybe, for the apps?

I've been going there for the past 10 years now, never disappointed!! Steven, one of the owner, is my go to person. He knows I have very high expectations about my haircut. Always takes his time and make sure everything is perfect! Highly recommend this place!!

I visited here to try a seared pork BBQ bahn mi. What a treat! The sandwich was a little dry, but otherwise tasty. I love that they have such a variety of drinks and snacks to choose from if you want more than just a sandwich.

Make sure to bring cash; they do not accept credit cards.

We stopped by after visiting Winterthur and ordered an assortment of pastries and cookies.  Everything was pretty good, but nothing special.  The employees should learn how to smile.  The prices were a little on the high side.  Next!

TIP: Make reservations, then come in complete company or you won't be seated!
We waited for awhile, since the rest of us are parking their cars.

We're having brunch here today.

I ordered Lancaster Three Egg omelette and my wifey ordered Green Meadow Double Cheddar Burger. 

Order took awhile on a Sunday, busy kitchen? 

The entrees we ordered are freshly made. Ingredients are organic, cage free chicken eggs and seasoned beef patties. Its delicious. 

Serving staff are courteous and helpful. Serving are in moderate portions.

Clean, very tight neutral gender bathrooms, four of them. No changing table. 

One of the best farm to table restaurants in Philly. 

No ramp access for wheelchair person in need.

There's a fine line between dumpster chic and dumpster. Unfortunately, this place falls on the wrong side of it. Popped in to work for a few hours based on positive yelp reviews, but was completely underwhelmed. My iced chai latte tasted like a strong black tea with some soy milk poured in. I'm not sure where the spice was. It looked like they had a decent selection of vegetarian fare, but after seeing the absolutely disgusting bathroom (presumably used by employees), I wasn't willing to touch the food.

Not a great spot to read or get work done, owing to the general dark and gloomy atmosphere. Also, cash only, so if you do stop in make sure you have some on you.

This review will not matter since people are going to flood this place regardless.  Maybe an off night but the ribs are very dry with rub applied after smoking.   The atmosphere is nice and the sides were good.

Chef Rio has brought real sushi to Nashville.

I complained that there was no good sushi in Nashville, so my friend took me here for a true omakase experience. We called ahead because Rio only serves one group at a time in the restaurant (COVID-19 regulations). I was suspicious, since it's a 'mongolian bbq' place... but for those 3 hours, my mind was blown. It's a shame I didn't have a camera on me, because there were so many dishes and fish that I had never eaten before - and all were incredibly fresh and well-prepared. My friend who is a regular customer there mentioned that every time he's had the omakase, there has never been a repeat in the menu. Basically, Rio is a genius.

I will say that if you don't know your stuff, it'll be harder to appreciate. But if you want high quality sushi, get it from Rio. Order takeout platters for a big group, or if you want quality but don't have time/$$ for the omakase, the chirashi is BEAUTIFUL. He packs a ton of fish onto a special mushroom fried rice... you have to try it.

Do not depend on this taxi service. I scheduled the taxi 3 days prior, plus called to confirm 3 hours prior to pick up. All attempts to have this service to be on time has proved to be useless.  I have now missed my appt. because of their poor service practices.
Overrated, overpriced and underwhelming.  Pass.

$5 dollars for a bottle of domestic beer!! $5!!!!! This is Nashville and you should be ashamed of yourself Lime!  I ordered a Miller Light because the mojito you made for me was a nasty color, tasted like sweet tarts, and conatined no rum only to find out that you would try and rip me off in the "cheap" beer department also. The bar constantly smells like bleach because the glass washing thing is right in your face, and the girl bartender can't make a drink. I've been back 3 times to give it a fair shot and every time she effs it up and the guy bartender has to remake it for me. He normally just shakes his head at her and looks at me sheepishly.

I've never had the food, but it looks overpriced . The design is cool, except that there is not a lot of dancing space and this girl salsa'd her stilettos right into the top of my foot which was bruised for weeks.

I like new, fusiony, trendy places, but they have to be able to deliver. Lime does not deliver for me; not in the prices, or the drinks, or the layout of the space. I will give it an extra star for being open and airy and full of pretty people, but the whole thing just screams rip-off to me. Sorry Lime.

We have been seeing Dr. Jeff Peppard for over 20 years.  He was so good my 3 year old son came out with a smile.  I worked in the dental profession for many, many yhears and he is so professional and goes over and above in the hygiene of his office.l  Every is sterile and immaculate.  I live in the Ventura county and the dentists there, even with my PPO dental insurance, were so bad, I had to come back to Dr. Peppard.  He is the BEST. FRANK is
the man.

This is a very nice place. Masks are required to enter and sanitizer is provided upon entry. We visited 2 days in a row while visiting Tampa for spring break. The waitstaff were excellent. The first day we had Pam and the next day we had Dericka. They were both very attentive and got both orders right! My son had the Mr and Mrs crab seasoning mild and I had the garlic butter seasoning hot. They were both really really good. Being from Texas, I appreciate the fact that we are able to get fresh crawfish. That was the only downside BUT the waitress did advise me that they were previously frozen before ordering. I took my chances and they were not very good. Nonetheless, everything else was seasoned to perfection and very filling. We will definitely be back when visiting Tampa again!

We were visiting Reno for a few days and wanted something other than casino food. On a recommendation from our hotel, we decided to give Sabrina's a try. We were thrilled with the quality of the food and the service. We liked it so much we went back the next night. This restaurant is must try!

I feel that my review can't be complete cause we only stopped in for a beer during the day to kill some time. Though it became two beers cause it was a happy hour promotion. But they have Old Style, in Nashville? Yes and a few craft selections.  The people there were friendly. We'd like to check it out at night. Don't sleep on this place, it's like an old school road house.

the service is really good here and the inside is really clean.  
I would come here late nights with some people from my church and the employees were always courteous and friendly.   
i know sometimes you can get cranky mickie d employees, but it is not the case here :)

the only annoying thing about this place is turning a left out of the restaurant -_- stupid ridge pike.
Lukewarm-to-cold fries once more.  Forget this place.  I won't stop by there again.  Pass.

I was very much anticipating my night out at Fond, but unfortunately I was very disappointed with the experience. We were sat at a table near the door and by the windows. There was a very perceptible draft coming from the windows and a freezing breeze every time the door opened. There was a space heater next to me but nothing for my husband. The wait staff kept going in and out, I think they were trying to track down a cab. It was extremely unpleasant. Everyone was perfectly nice, but we sat for a while without being greeted and the service was generally slow. The food was not great and certainly not worth the price tag. At the end of the meal, the server sensed we were dissatisfied and tried to make it up with some sparkling rose, but that didn't take away the sting of spending $130 on a sour experience. If you go in the winter either request a table far from the door or bundle up.

So what can I say about this place. 
I have had lunch and breakfast here a few times. The burgers are pretty good and breakfast is very good. Service is a little slow but they get super crowded. Quite possibly the worst coffee I have ever had in the city, but is a big deal for me in a breakfast spot. I would recommend bring your own coffee when going for breakfast

We went to Karaoke. When we entered the restaurant, waiter/waitress just looked at us. Took about 3min to get hosted to Karaoke. We had fun. However, when we were about to pay for the Karaoke, they charged two times more than we did. They didn't keep the time. They added one more hours to our bill. Gladly we didn't pay for what they billed, but be careful if you want to go for Karaoke.
i mean, it's just not my style.  i like danceable music and mixed drinks, but i try to remain objective.  if you like college crowds, live bands, and beer, this is a great place for you.

First things first: The French onion soup was superb! I love French onion soup, and therefore, have tasted a lot of different versions. I can't remember tasting anything better than this - does that mean it's the best I've ever had? Or does that just mean I have a faulty memory? Perhaps the latter...but nonetheless, it was freaking delicious! It was so rich and flavorful. The crouton, instead of disintegrating into the broth, stayed intact and delicious - the crouton definitely pushed this soup into the upper echelon of French Onion Soup-dom.

I've read reviews that the service is really poor - I did not experience this. Even with the busy weekday-lunch crowd, the service was not bad. Our water was refilled promptly, our food and check were both brought within a reasonable timeframe. 

I have nothing bad to say! I will definitely go back when there's room in my tummy for dessert!

I used to go here years ago for sushi and the kimchi fries. Honestly this past time I will refuse to go ever again. The curry was sad, the sushi was so ugly and poorly made. There was no direction for how to order, when you scan on the table QR code it sends you to the mobile ordering website. Apparently you had to just look through the menu for what you want and then get someones attention to order what you want. 

The place forces you to use hand sanitizer when you come in then sits you so ridiculously close to the next tables all in on side of the small restaurant, separated by these hanging plastic screens that don't do anything for covid.

Yet the majority of the staff were not wearing masks and definitely not wearing a mask when getting so close to me especially the hispanic guy, and I'm pretty sure when I was paying he selected a higher tip FOR ME right before letting me sign on his mobile payment terminal, which was really ridiculous, but I didn't say anything. I'm just never going to come here again.  

Bathroom is disgusting. And honestly the covid precautions here are just hypocritical once you don't have the staff wearing masks, and the way the service and food is, it's just honestly really sad. I'm very disappointed I showed my friend here that day.
I opened the lid to my Sierra Mist I ordered and there was a ripped apart fly in it. I'm glad the straw they gave me was broken because I would've used it and ended up swallowing that.

Not bad for a Tues night
Prob one of more packed places...
Has Nice outdoor seating

Waiter took our drunk order and forgot water (which i asked for) until I asked again...

Fish tacos: interesting... Different than what I'm used to... Marinated in a dark sauce and shredded

Chicken fajitas wrap: what is indeed a wrap but what was weird is that it had cheese in it. Normally he does don't have cheese. It wasn't bad at all just not what I expected. The fajitas came with either chips french fries or sweet potato fries but the fries were $2 extra.

I was however thrilled when I saw that they had rogue Chocolate Stout on tap...delicious!!!!!

I also had the sangria which was decent.

Overall not a bad place.   Prefer over the "branded" food restaurants out there.

I went here many many years ago as a little kid and went back for the first time the other week. My intention was to see the mum mountain for fall. The display was beautiful and inside the store they had plenty of pumpkins for fall and tons of plants - like tons! You can get lost in here. The prices seemed reasonable and they have an extensive variety. 
This is definitely a unique spot in Montco.
Meh. Coffee was fine. Pastries were dry, decent taste. Donuts were great. Music was screamy death metal shit.

I knew when I bought my 70 year old house that the sewer line was ... sensitive. That compromises would have to be made for pipes well past their expiration date. But this year it became clear that it was time to call in the professionals to take care of a problem I had hoped to avoid - a complete replacement of the sewer line.

I was referred to A1 Plumbing, and from start to finish, they couldn't have been better to deal with. Their estimate was more than fair (and less than I had been dreading). The team arrived on time (a rarity these days). They excavated four deep holes with a care for the existing landscaping. They did the pipe bursting and connections (fascinating!). And then they put everything back together so well that it's hard to tell at first glance that they were even there. All of this they did in two days with me watching over their shoulders, asking questions about the process and taking pictures. They turned what could have been nothing more than a depressing necessity into a fascinating demonstration of skill and knowledge.

No one wants to have to replace a sewer line. But if you find yourself in that position, know that the people at A1 Plumbing are the ones to call, full stop.

First time on bourbon street and we decided to stop here to eat and use the restroom. I recommend the alligator po boy and red beans and rice. I do not eat seafood so We had the alligator po boy and the hot sausage po boy. The alligator po boy was delicious! The meat was well seasoned and in fried nugget form. I was not a fan of the sausage po boy. I expected it to be links but it came out in thin patties and it gave me really bad gas and indigestion. I also had a margarita that was made well. And I had a side order of red beans and rice, which was delicious and well seasoned!

Dunkin' Donuts... well I've been to this DD a few times before mainly in the early morning and never really a problem. But I went a couple of times in the afternoon/early evening and it's totally unacceptable. I should have gotten the hint by the empty parking spaces directly in front of the store. But Today I decided to stop in because it's a little chilly out and wanted a simple Dark Roast coffee and 2 ice coffees with a egg croissant. I get the ice coffees pretty quick and after about 6 mins or so i get the sandwich (in which he had to make himself) so I asked about the Dark Roast and he says "yeah I'm heating it up"?!?!.. I asked him what do you mean heating it up? He says to me he has it in a carafe on top of the brewer literally heating up the coffee on the burner. Who knows how long it been sitting there. He then tells me I can make a fresh pot but it's going to take a bit... REALLY?!?.. I honestly believe it's THE MANAGEMENTS FAULT because they only have 2 employees working so the one who takes the order has to prepare the sandwiches get coffees etc etc. The same goes for the one operating the drive-thru. Needless to say I just walked out with a reg coffee that was way too light which went straight in the garbage. It's a shame because it's a fairly new DD in a prime location. But there are many other DD on 301 so it's time to move on and give them a try.
WAKE UP MANAGEMENT!!

If I'm honest this is officially our favorite Indian restaurant in the Area and Philadelphia. D.P. Is easily one of the most attentive waiters, always greets you with a smile and always refilling the water. The food is absolutely prepared to order and always comes hot to the table. I haven't been able to find a better Indian restaurant and I won't even try. Can't wait to return!
The service was very good, as was the coffee. The breakfast sandwich and home fries were extremely greasy. Didn't eat the potatoes at all. You could've lubed a car with the grease.

I wish I could pack this place up and take it home with me. Great drinks, great food, and great staff. Atmosphere is kick-ass too! The food is basically modern interpretations of southern comfort food (and vegetarian friendly for my fellow veggies out there). Though it's a secret I kind of want to keep to myself, I can't give this place enough love.

With all of these negative reviews I wish this company would have just been shut down already. They towed my car from a parking garage in downtown Tampa after I was parked for only 1 hour. The document said "parked in front of mailbox". There was no mailbox, this is clearly a trap and they must have someone waiting to call as soon as anyone parks there. It was $205, cash only yet they give no change?? We waited at their sketchy lot for over an hour before the man could come let us in so I could retrieve my car. Unacceptable.

By and large the store employees are amazing people (5 star). Here's the big but:
The Hampton Bay cabinets are of substandard quality. I had numerous defects and went all through the procedures for rectifying the problems which included faulty drawer glides; however, the manager and the company and the cabinet rep did NOT stand by their products. I would also caution people against the Anderson 100 series windows the hardware for them are substandard as well. The higher-quality Anderson reputation is for the higher numbered windows.

Amazing!!!  The food is complex and delicious, really well-prepared. Service was friendly and efficient. Decor simple and bold, pictures of Angela Davis, Einstein, Lincoln, the guy in front of the tank at Tiananmen Square. And that was lunch! The dinner menu looks even better.

My name is Jerome and my friend name is Yvonne   We make a priority to stop by different pizza stores. We found out that this is a hidden gem in  South Philly  if you're looking for the best  look no further  you found the best .please check it out and try the pizza is great and the lamb is excellent .Bon appétit.

4 stars for vegetarian options!

I have twice gotten the mock sweet and sour chicken and avacado cuke rolls- both were delicious both times. Piping hot and when I called in and picked up, it was ready in exactly 15 minutes form call time. The guy at the counter was very friendly too.

However- called for takeout last week. I live about 15 in walking distance- can literally make it there in 5 minutes from the car, but I was feeling super lazy so we ordered in. I had to call them when it hit past an hour thinking they forgot about us. They likely did, but came about 20 minutes later with very hot food. Total time 1 hour and 20 minutes from call. Really decent food, great options for a vegetarian, but I would caution the delivery option.

The first few times I've ordered pickup from Ra was pretty great but the past few times I've ordered to pick up I've missed half of my order once or my miso soup a few times. I work in the mall so sometimes I don't have time to go back when I'm on my lunch time. Today I was pretty disappointed and had to go back in. I ordered the salmon poke bowl with rice but I got the lunch poke bowl version. So I kindly went back in and asked if it was the lunch menu salmon poke bowl and the girl in the front said that the menu had changed and I showed her the picture from the online menu of what I ordered and it was missing the sauces and green onions and the portion was a little smaller. And I've ordered the regular salmon poke bowl with rice and it did not look like this. I just felt like i was ripped off. And I think the online menu needs to be updated if it did change.

Visited Han Dynasty with great expectations and it turned to be just abive average. Dont get me wrong the food is v good but guess expected something to blow my mind. We ordered the famous Dan dan noodles and their spicy dumplings. Dan Dan noodles with great and taste it also good however it wasn't worth the hype, the dumplings were very good it was spicy level 7 but you really enjoyed it. The next thing we ordered was there a dry hot pot with fish as a choice of meat this was supposed to be spicy level 10 and I was scared that we won't be able to handle it but I think we did a very good job and really enjoyed it. Actually spicy level 10 could have been a little bit more spicier are probably something more to offer spicy crazy people like me. I had a dogfish beer but they didn't have the one they had on my note and I had to switch to Dogfish Ale which was good because it was Dogfish my wife order they're famous cocktail punch and she really liked it though it was on sweeter note. Videoder there Mochi ice cream for dessert, it comes with 3 different flavors and we tried by pistachio,  dark chocolate and coffee express. The dessert was good. We requested to do a take out for the leftovers and they put everything into cheap plastic boxes inside a plastic bag just like a regular take out OMG they could have done better than that. Also I would have expected the restrooms to be a bit more cleaner. Overall a good place to dine in and that's it
This Taco Bell is also a Pizza Hut as well.  Pizza Hut is served until 11 PM but they have a tendency to run out of Pizza Hut items well before 11 PM, so be aware!

Bulrush STL is a magical experience that must be experienced. This is one of those restaurants that is worth a special trip, even if that means you come into St. Louis for the day just to eat here. 

The restaurant has a wonder mirrored layout to it where the front bar mirrors the chefs counter in the back. Very comfortable place that makes you feel at home. 

The service is top notch from front to back, but the true delight of the service is chef Connelly and his friendly and Inviting personality. He is very engaging through the whole dinner service. He really takes his time and explains every meal in depth and gives you a true understand of where the meal came from. 

With tasting menus I always get concerned that I won't like some of the meals throughout the dinner. With Bulrush this was not the case at all as every plate was packed with flavors that worked so well together. The turkey thigh with corn purée and house made barrel aged hot sauce was definitely the highlight  of the meal with the pumpkin pie(not a dessert) a true honorable mention. In the end though everything was great, even the squash was delicious and that's not something I typically find myself liking. 

Bulrush STL comes with my highest recommendation, and can't wait for my next trip to St. Louis to dine with Chef Connelly and his team.
This place is great! Super cool vibe, server was awesome and I had the best scallops I've had in a long time. They oysters are on point with perfection as well! Highly recommend!

If you like watered down ice coffee or burnt hot coffee and not getting exactly what you order food wise you'll love this Dunkin. The other one down the street Id rate at 3stars it's bigger and cleaner yet both places make Krispy Kreme's coffee taste great

I had my order taken to my vehicle. They were prompt, friendly, and masked. I enjoyed my iced mocha; it was perfect. The croissant breakfast sandwich was tasty and done just right. We also got a variety of sweets to share and enjoy midday.  The tiramisu is traditional and, an average helping/price, and taste is on point - same with their cannoli. I took one star off because of the cookies. We got the sugar and the chocolate chip. Sad to say that they were very dry, like in-the-box-grocery-store-cookies dry. Some people may like a dry cookie but I expect a bakery cookie to be fresh and moist. However, they don't claim that their cookies are moist. Perhaps I should have asked. All the menu says is, "Large Marble Chocolate Chip Cookie" and "Large Sugar Cookie."  I'd go back for more specialty coffee drinks and to try other menu items like the NY cheesecake. Quite a few things were out because it's just a few days before Xmas.
Beers were good, they had 10 of there own on tap. Enjoyed the Brown especially (hickory smoke was strong!) good outdoor seating with cornhole and other games. Buff Chicken pizza hit the spot.

Came in at 830pm for a reservation for my friends bachelore party. The server was not the problem. The only problem was the two hour wait for our food. Our party was a party of seven and did not order anything out of the ordinary. Just ordered what was on the menu. The sever was calm and collective and brought us the right drinks. It was not his fault. When they finally brought out my food 230 hours later they brought me the cat fish instead of the aligator sandwich. Come on guys, almost three hours for you guys to not know what the fuck you're doing! Don't come here l. Nothing special, server was not the problem. The problem is the establishment.

I've been going to Amethyst salon for two years. This place is amazing. It's like my own hidden salon. 

The service that I receive is waxing. My services each time are phenomenal. I leave here happy and satisfied. After each appointment I plan to continue and go back. 

The staff, even though I have only worked with Mary, the other staff the greets me every time I walk through the door. They have all been courteous and nice. As for Mary, she is a wonderful person who is amazing. She has a passion for her job and her clients. She is the reason I continue to make appointments. 

The environment is comforting and bright. The waiting area is in with the single hair station in the salon. 

What they offer at amethyst salon is nails, pedicures, waxing and the newest hair. They have other products there like medusa makeup. 

The price for waxing is very reasonable. I have not found a place that matches the price of Amethyst Salon!!

I would recommend people to try this place! I for sure will go back!

Do not use this company if you are not a celebrity or some person with a little blue check mark for status. Five star treatment is far from what I received. Employees took my personal information that were not authorized and contacted me at 730am trying to rebook my move on moving day or asking to show up immediately. I asked them to bring extra large boxes to compile a few small items and they showed up with two small boxes and set them on the floor. To top it off movers were seen DRINKING ALCOHOL on the job by a property manger and taking advantage of a free happy hour and eating food -going back for seconds, meant for local residents while leaving my personal belongings in the hallway unattended. I was made aware the drinking the following day and because they had no uniforms on staff were unaware who they were. They broke and shattered a glass frame 15 feet high in the hall. Such an embarrassment to have my belongings strewn everywhere, unattended. I had sectioned off a basket of items I planned on taking to my car. I told the movers DO NOT take these items- several times !!! Later on I found the basket sitting out in the hallways unattended. When we arrived at my new home the lead parked he truck out front blocking a thru traffic area. I had told him that it was suggested we unload in the back. The mover insisted that we park in the front which wasted an hour. One of the movers became aggressive and hostile later in the day and I watched them drop & then kick a $400 piece of electronics that I specifically asked to be padded and wrapped.I asked to speak to someone on call as things were getting so uncomfortable and uncoordinated that I started carrying my own things down the hall. I understand that moving in some of the apartment complexes can be tricky but perhaps it would be helpful if you did your research before sending people and made sure of such things. Later, The two movers were arguing with each other and the third was dragging stuff down the hall and smelled like weed. 
It starts to rain and was getting so late that we had to finish the move the next day. Then one of the movers went on to confront me in a threatening voice asking  "did you tell my supervisor we were not doing a good job?"
Hands down I should have just called the police and had them arrested as 2 of them were clearly under the influence while working and the other smelled of weed. They proceeded to lose their phones in the moving truck and sat in the parking garage area until nearly 1am looking for their phones swearing, cussing with the truck door open with my belongings for 2 hours. Covid or not -drug screen your employees -I should not have to smell weed from your employee. Get your act together- have supervision around your employees. It took them over 10 hours to move a 1 bedroom apartment across town. Moving is one of the top stressful events and your people made it way worse that it should have ever been. I'm no superstar but I'm a strong person who felt very disrespected, and down right threatened by the punks that were working. A total embarrassment- you might want to start investing in damage control because you have definitely got a bad reputation with the apartments in town.

entered 5:30 and asked the two hostesses about 'happy hour'..they looked at each other quizzically, confessed ignorance and said to each other 'guess we should know that'...anyway it extends 'til 7 and all starters (a big choice) are half off, a good deal...whole place is bloody noisy, with a share of drunk regulars being greeted by an abundant staff...ahi tuna was excellent, quesadillas were packed with chicken, wings were disappointing and not helped by a poorly chosen 'maple' sauce...nice selection of micros scrawled on a board, six bucks a piece...they graciously allowed us to take out a fourth 'starter' at the same half price...the whole (limited to burgers, sandwiches, etc, nothing fancy here) menu was most reasonably priced, everyday and easily found pub grub you never feel particularly good after eating...it serves it's purpose but 50/50 i'll pull in next time passing...

I gave a rating of two because the collard greens and potato salad reminded me of my auntie.  The chicken however was greasy and fried really, did I mention really really hard with a lot of salt.  The concept is great, but the space is. It large enough to accommodate the wait.  As a person with handicapped needs, I literally had to stand 23 minutes for friend chicken legs - don't feel as bad as the woman in front of me who had already waited 30 minutes and was told it would take 7 minutes.  

I guess you live and learn.  Having read reviews and since I was staying next door in the hotel didn't want to walk too far tried this place.  I think I did make a mistake when I walked past the BBQ spot to find this place.  The mac and cheese was cheesy, but unseasoned if you could picture that. By the time they got the chicken out and I waked back next door to the Hilton the Mac and cheese was I iced cold telling me it had been sitting a while.  $19 later, a little disappointed.
Victor was a great help. Very courteous as he tested and replaced my dead battery for free.

I was wandering the FQ last weekend and happened into this shoe store since I was trying to find information on a closed store across the street. The staff person couldn't help me with that and since that was my initial question, she didn't really seem interested in helping me in the store. Perhaps we just got off on the wrong foot. (How could I resist?)

The shoes are off beat and aren't terribly expensive and they do carry a variety of styles with heels and flats and spectators, etc. The store is really spacious and the decor adds to the funky vibe.

I'm here on Saturday March 13 2021. The new place is horrible. So loud. Lacks the cozy fun Mexican feeling. 
Sadly I think they're understaffed that's why I asked for napkins three times and still don't have any. Salad served with no utensils.

This was the best Tampa surprise! Mathew helped entertaining us with the menu and did a wonderful job! We had shrimp toast and tuna with some mango sorbet to begin, then we had sea scallops and grouper: both incredible! The best part was desert, peanut chocolate bar and sugar cake with vanilla! OMG!! Simply loved the experience!

We went there for breakfast on a busy Sunday morning and the wait was average not too long.  The waitress was very courteous and quick in bringing out our food.  The food was ok for a diner.  My husband did not like the pancakes said that they were uncooked however my kids loved their eggs and bacon and omelets.   A lot of people seem to go for the buffet maybe we will try that next time.
the food is still good. menu getting old but why is the service gone to shit..? the staff stands around and looks right through your or doesn't acknowledge you at all.

If I could give this place a zero I would but I got a pedicure and the guy burnt me with the water when I said I wanted it colder he kept going hotter and hotter and I was getting a manicure done and the guy was talking about me In his language and he didn't know that I understood his language but he messed up my shape and did dip when I said that I wanted acrylic and my nails were 80 for plain white and he was very rude when I said that I would like another person to do my nails they said I couldn't get a new person because I had already paid him‍ it's just a little sketchy

Worse ever!  Don't go here.  It's a waste of money and TIME!!!  Unfortunately, Noodle City was closed so I had no choice but to go there.  The pho is horrible!  The beef brisket is way hard and the broth is lukewarm.  Sure, they were generous with the broth but what's pho if the broth is not piping hot??  Icing on the cake?  Almost halfway through eating, found a piece of clear plastic in my soup!  Informed the waitress and she was nice enough to replace it.  That should've been on the house!  Got the lunch special which came with dessert.  Waited 20 mins additional just for two freakin pieces of fried banana!  Overall, just not worth it!!!!  It's a miracle that place is still in business.  BTW, if i could give it 0 stars, I would.

We ordered a tire to put on the rim and they did call us when the tire came in. Then we dropped off the bad tire  so they could put the rim on the good one. They told us to come back in an hour and an hour turned into 2+. I would suggest just staying at the place if you have the time or else you will be skipped. Also if they can call you when yoir oder comes in then they can call you when they get backed up on work so customers don't have to change their schedule around them. It looks like the store has a large selection and they speak English and spanish.
I had a goblet here it was real amazing. The bacon Bloody Mary is very good as well!! N

I wish I'd looked on Yelp before buying a dishwasher at Goedekers.  
I am not a happy customer.
The delivery of for my dishwasher was quoted at 8 to 10 business days but actually took 20 business days.  This was 29 calendar days over the holidays that we had to spend without a dishwasher, washing all the dinner party dishes in the sink.
When the dishwasher finally arrived, it was somehow wet inside.  The manuals were soaked inside their plastic bags, and the drain hose had water inside.  How does this even happen?  A dishwasher is waterproof unless the door is open and a hose is pointed at the opening.  Was this a used dishwasher?
The styrofoam base the dishwasher was on was shattered into many pieces, the cardboard box ripped and torn.
I had NO updates on the delivery time until a day before it was to arrive.  No email communication and their website was useless.
After all that I missed out on a $50 manufacturer's rebate from Bosch because it expired before the dishwasher arrived.

Bottom line:  Pay the extra money to buy local.  Don't trust thier delivery quotations.

Honestly, it's a shame that we don't have a better yarn shop on the NW side. LYS are supposed to be friendly and inviting, but the customer service at this place is sorely lacking. Usually it seems to be that the staff are extremely friendly to... their friends, and anyone who buys a ton of overpriced yarn. As I usually come in to get one or two skeins, I'm not in their clique by any means. Would be nice to have a reasonable room temperature in there - as they also charge for water, it seems nasty to keep the shop so hot. 
I hate going there and will only do so when I can't get what I need anywhere else. As soon as I walk in, there's a chill if the owners/staff don't know you, like you walked into their living room. Not a great way to run a business.

MUST READ: Planned a trip to NOLA & Read all these reviews on yelp, so me and my friends decided to go. Our waitress was an older lady who immediately showed us she was very upset. Took our order & after our terrible sour mix margaritas we got some pretty decent onion rings so we were hopeful for our meal.  We got gumbo,jambalaya and she insisted that we get the 4 piece chicken wings so we could share but again very DISAPPOINTED. She came to ask how things tasted and we said we didn't like the Jambalaya &  asked if we could exchange or just have it taken off SINCE WE DIDNT EAT ANY OF IT BUT A SMALL SPOON PORTION. She laughed and said no, " the food is going to go to waste we didn't even eat it" in exact words she said " get a to go box and give it to the dirty people on the street" UM OK?! at this point I can't believe she even suggested that.. after her being very disrespectful, we just ask for our check ask if we could split it & before we finished asking she yelled no "all cash or one card". We paid and told her to keep the 12 cent change. As we head to the door to walk out she very loudly said "F*** you" and told us to hurry and leave. We told her it was a tourist driven economy & she should be nicer and she kept insisting to get out and continued to yell at us and said she didn't like tourist like us and said their was a Denny's close by. We did tell her we had way better gumbo and jambalaya at the market. And she yelled "I doubt it" . PLEASE don't go unless you want shitty service and want to be treated like nothing. Ps Denny's servers are way better at their job then you lady!!!!

What happened to circles? This place use to have great food. Last night, we ordered delivery. The noodles were mush, and unappetizing. The massaman curry dip Had soggy bread and barely any sauce and no chicken. The cheesesteak spring rolls were great in taste but you only got three tiny ones For $9. I won't be ordering from here again. $40 Down the drain.
I ordered a large cheesesteak Stromboli and the chicken sampler.  I wasn't pleased at all. I wasted my $25 bucks! Live and learn
Giant pizza is amazing here! Sandwiches and sides are also delicious. Have not dined in, only gotten take out on multiple occasions but food was always good. Online ordering is super simple

This is actually my first Yelp review, but I thought it was important to warn visitors and hopefully get the Sheraton management to fix things.

I stayed at the Sheraton the week of October 20 for 4 nights.  The room itself was fine overall, what you would expect from a Sheraton- comfortable bed with nice linens, large flat screen tv, etc...  The bathroom could have been a little bit cleaner, but I always bring Lysol wipes anyway to get the door knobs, light switches, remotes and other items that are always on the "dirtiest items lists".

During my 4 nights at this hotel I had only one hot shower, 4 lukewarm showers, and 2 cold ones.  After asking the front desk to look into this on my second day, I was told they came up to my room to fix things.  That night I took the most frigid shower I've every taken in my life!  I believe that the hotel simply does not have the equipment needed to support hot water for all the rooms.  I stay at about 15-25 hotels a year and have never experienced anything like this before.

My other complaint is the excruciatingly slow internet.  I even paid extra for the fast service.  Forget about getting any work done in the evening, the internet is so bad that it could not even load a full webpage without timing out.  

So if you are okay with no hot water and unusable internet, this is the hotel for you!

I don't normally complain about food, because I love food! But.... 

My boyfriend ordered me food at Quik Wok in Meridian for delivery at 5:05. All that was ordered was teriyaki chicken and crab rangoons. With tip total was 20 bucks. Website says 1hr delivery time and left a phone number to contact with any issues.

6:25 rolls around and still no food, so I call the number on the screen. I asked about my order and the guy could not locate it with name or phone number. So I give him the address and order content and he puts me on hold. A minute or so later he comes back and says he found that order and address but it has a name and phone number that isn't mine or Jay's. Then he tells me it's for cash, but I paid with card. He continues with an apology and says it's starting to cook and will be there shortly.

At this point I was upset because we are close to an hour and a half and it just started to cook so I wouldn't receive it until 7 PM. I asked about cancellation and he said it take 7 business days for a refund. I asked to speak to a manger at that point...

Manager answers and I explain my frustration. She apologizes and tells me they are very backed up and that's why the website says to call for delivery time after you place it. I still had the website pulled up and read to her what it says, which was nothing of the sort. It stated that its take approximately an hour. She then tells me I'm looking at the Boise website and the Meridian one says different. So I tell her I am on the Meridian website, because it says it on the page that I just read her and she continued to argue and tell me I am wrong. I decided I wasnt going to waste the time arguing over what I clearly can read and ask about my food. She said it will get to me when its ready. 

So after two hours of waiting and rude customer service I received my food (with no fork or napkins). The crab rangoons were pretty delicious, but the chicken was gross. I was able to eat 3 pieces, because the rest was fat. I picked it off and ate some of the rice before I discovered that most of was rock hard. 

Needless to say I got over the fact they were rude, and decided to wait because I was hungry and didnt want to wait 7 days for a refund. Food and service was awful and I do not suggest ordering take out from them.

I got the grilled shrimp poboy but I wasn't a fan of the flavors. The shrimp had a great juiciness to it but it was overly garlic-y? I don't really know if it was garlic but there was something overpowering about it that didn't mesh well with the prickliness of the carrots and radishes. I'm curious about their pork belly poboy though.

Check your bags before pulling out of the parking lot.   Recently multiple orders have been missing an item, and it's super frustrating.   Tonight's debacle  has us swearing off this location.     When 2 out of 3 of your children's kids meals are missing the main component (i.e.  a cheeseburger and nuggets) that is beyond ridiculous.

The partner that just got off work ordered five drinks thus causing a already slow team to slow down even further. They first made my hot latte that I ordered cold. I placed my mobile order at 4:28 and I have been waiting til now for my mobile order (the time is 5:08.) They need to get a grip.
Service is great.  Drinks are good.  I had the fried mozerella and it was amazing.  I was excited about th

I should have written this long ago and spared people some misery. The equipment is outdated and the xrays were painful. I don't know why they did them, since I had to tell the dentist where my fillings were. The worst was some technician named Victor who jabbed me so hard with the probe that my gums were bleeding extensively (plus it hurt). He said I was all '6's and needed a scale that I had done somewhere else the year before. Also, when I went to a real dentist a couple years later, I found out that I had a huge cavity that wasn't diagnosed. I had to get a crown.
Best airport I've ever been. Very easy to navigate and very good food and drink choices past the checkpoint especially granite city
Great coffee. Went for a walk and decided to get a coffee. It was perfect. Sandwiches looked very good and so did the bakery area. Nice little place.

I was initially very nervous to move. I only had about three weeks to prepare and being a single woman I couldn't lift all the furniture or totes of things. I checked out Yelp and saw that these great guys were near my house so I called to get a quote. Paul took the phone and was very patient while I scrambled and took forever to give him the new address and everything. An hour before the move the guys called to say they were on the way. When they arrived they were very easy going yet professional and careful with all of my fragile boxes. They even quickly helped me pack up the odds and ends! The guys were great and broke down my extremely complicated ikea bed and put it back together at the new place! They moved my 2 bedroom in less than an hour which is incredible! After unpacking today not a single item was broken so I'm very impressed! Rates are $100 initial booking fee and $100 an hour for two guys with a 3 hour minimum. I will recommend these guys to anyone!
Italian as pizza (not very).  Fernando - 9 bowls, beeeyatch!!!!!!!!!

Had brisket, chicken, and ribs, with sides of mac & cheese and baked potato casserole.  The brisket was very good.  However, the chicken was too dry, the ribs were dry and tasted very salty.  Mac & cheese way way way too much cheese  (could be good for children??) .  Would not return.  However, the servers are very nice, service was good, and the place is small but cute.

I was looking forward to the Big Cat Rescue as a self proclaimed "crazy cat lady". It turned out to be the closest I have ever been to a tiger (along with some other usual cat varieties). 

Our tour guide Nicole was very familiar with the Big Cat Rescue's history and their individual animals. I also liked learning that the volunteers are selected over time to give tours. Her knowledge and expertise showed her dedication to the organization.

I was not impressed with the facility. I had expected the animals to have larger living spaces. The facility was more like a zoo than a sanctuary. The cats seemed lethargic. (It might have been the time of the day.) 

The history of the organization also appears to be in a little hypocritical. The original owner acquired a large breed cat(s) prior to the discovering they don't belong in peoples' homes. She was the same kind of person the organization currently condemns. Overall worth seeing when in the area (but it wasn't a life changing experience).

Clean bathrooms on both levels & friendly store staff. Note that this location is where multi bus vendors depart- so be aware that certain times are busy with bus customers; And if a bus customer, they prefer u wait outdoors. I was there to catch a bus to Lewiston, & found it easy to move my bag thru the store as I picked up snacks for the bus ride. A definite recommend.

This is a beautiful gem nestled in Havertown. I shouldn't write a review because I am selfish and really don't want anyone else to know about it. 
Everything was exquisite.

This was probably one of the most disgusting meals I have ever had in my existence. Service was garbage, food was garbage, all in all... nasty. Food was cold, "clam sauce " was a jelly chowder .... literally horrific.
Excellent pizza and a good atmosphere! Definitely worth visiting if you like Real New York style pizza.

Review from my visit 6/6/17. FWIW - Small zoo so adjust expectations accordingly (I.e. Limited number/size of habitats).

Pros: Quaint little zoo not far from downtown Nashville. Very friendly/helpful staff. Lots of shade for hot & humid TN summers. Very reasonable entrance fees. 

Cons: NO maps available for distribution (seriously, someone "forgot" to order maps!!!). No zoo app either (get with the the times or you'll become extinct). Parking fee!!?! The layout requires a lot of doubling back thru things you've already seen in order to move on to other exhibit areas.

As a nurse I can provide a healthcare sided review to this office. The office is run by Dr. Parag Patel and Dr. Shah.  While the physicians are average skilled in assessment and treatment their bedside manner is unprofessional and demeaning.  The staff will "forget" or "lose" your referral numerous times yet continuously blame your insurance provider.  If you are fortunate your call will be answered by a respectable administrative assistant at best 10% of the time.  This office takes on PA students (docs receive a paycheck from the college/university) who herd in groups and yell your PHI so loud Dunkin Donuts employees can hear.  Finally the physicians are purely financially driven in your care, well truly lack of CARE. Worrying about CODING your visit for optimal reimbursement, and speaking about this in front of you (the patient). Then telling the staff that you will need an additional separate appointment to address your health complaints because they can't code and receive money for addressing it at this visit.  I understand medical coding and reimbursement, as a consumer you need to be observant and question how this deceptive office bills/codes you and your insurance. Easier to drive to Marlton, Voorhess or Medford! Convenience of local location isn't worth the trouble.
I realize I'm in the minority here but I've had better in gulf shores Alabama. Dry, cold, blah.! Not fresh. Disappointed.

I want to start out by saying that my massage was fine - by no means the worst I've ever had - but I likely won't go back for a few reasons.

1) Their technique for a Swedish Massage was not what I'm used to.  Rather than a fluid, slow, soothing experience with good pressure, my therapist moved VERY quickly, so fast that I felt I didn't have time to absorb the experience before she moved on to a different area.  I also felt more prodded rather than kneaded.  My therapist also didn't use any bolster pillows at all under my ankles or knees and I noticed their absence compared to past massages I have received.
2) I'm 30 and wouldn't consider myself especially shy, but I was a little taken aback by the lack of attention shown to keeping me covered up.  We started with me face down, and my therapist walked in the room and threw back the covers so that a good 3/4 of my rear was exposed.  In addition, when she worked on my legs, I expected that she would tuck the blankets to keep me covered up down below, but this was not done so I felt a little uncomfortable.  Same when I was turned to lay on my back.  My chest was barely covered and the same inattention was shown to covering me up when working on my legs.
3) My therapist tried to sell me on facials during my session.  I was there to relax, and the last thing I wanted to do was fend off a salesperson.  After I just stopped responding, she eventually dropped the subject, but it took me a while to get over the annoyance which of course impacted the relaxation I was hoping to achieve when I booked the massage in the first place.
