Bottom line, if a friend told me they were going to Day Spa by Zsuzsanna, I wouldn't tell them not to go, but I would definitely warn them about my experience.
The Pizza was wonderful and the place was super clean.  A win win all around.
Really good food at great prices. Nothing fancy but all around solid experience time and again.

I used to come here all the time for tires but the place has changed in a negative way. I came to get a run flat tire repaired or replaced if needed. Well this guy told me that the state of MO doesn't allow these to be repaired and it has to be replaced (odd b/c Dobbs repairs them) which was the first red flag to avoid. Then he started saying oh depending on treadwear on the others they may have to replace all 4....like wait what? I've never heard that before, ever and decided to never come here again. Go elsewhere, avoid here.

Prices are reasonable for food, only complaint is the rice and clam chowder were WAYYYYYY to salty.  Salmon and grouper bites were very fresh, bartender/staff in bar area are very friendly.  The girl taking names was a bit unwelcoming did not look like she enjoyed her job.

This place kind of sucks.

It usually smells overwhelmingly of sweat. Which is fitting for the title, but definitely not something the gym should be striving for. Many of their cardio machines are broken or make lots of clanking and other weird noises.

Nevertheless, I can usually get a pretty good workout here. Natasha's spin class and Brittany's yoga classes are the bomb.

Went on a Sunday afternoon & the place was empty. Atmosphere is nice. Nice outside seating. Food was ok. I had the chicken club which was very bland, nothing like the description in menu. Husband had a burger and Mac and cheese. He loved the Mac & cheese. Service was not good at all. Thinking maybe she was new. We had a 1.5 year old and try to get in & out. Waited forever to place our order and even longer to get the check. So long I had to take the baby to the car because he was getting cranky. Def wouldn't go back for dinner but maybe for drinks & apps.

Incredibly slow service, over an hour and still no food! This has to be one of the only times I have left a restaurant before my order came! 75 minutes after ordering still no food! Miso soup (the only thing we ordered and got) was unimpressive and somewhat flavorless.
Best New York strip in the city....hands down. Service was impeccable. Valet parking helps too

Foods good however employees walked in and out with no face covering and allowed patrons to walk in and around the inside with no face coverings. Employee was heard entering with another employee and when the other had no covering on he said "you know the fucking rules just put your shirt over your mouth for now". Nearly each time an employee came out from the back, no face covering. Doesn't make you feel too comfortable they're wearing masks in the kitchen where you can distinctly hear them yelling, likely spraying their spit everywhere. Can do better

This Panera location is decent and employees are always nice but they always mess up orders. I occasionally order breakfast from the app to go pick up in store and they always leave out ingredients. Is there not a guide on how to assemble breakfast sandwiches or are they just winging it every time? How do you miss the sausage in a SAUSAGE, Egg & Cheese sandwich? Lol. If you're gonna order form here make sure to check your order!

One of the worst restaurants in Chinatown. To start, my Rangoon Appetizer Favorites was greasy. My Chilli Seafood dish was too salty. My Mandalay Nungyi Noodle was room temperature and the noodles were over cooked.

Looked online for their hours, went there when they opened and was told that the website was not correct and they don't open for another 35 minutes so I had to wait in the parking lot. Website said they had an arcade with 30 games but when I got there the arcade is completely gone. my son went skating and they never played music, he was skating in complete silence.  they also served us cheese fries with cheese that was chunky and disgusting and had to be returned. The woman said yeah I didn't think it looked right either it was the bottom of the bag I'll give you a fresh one so my question was why would you even serve us something you knew was expired and not right? Not to mention they clean the tables with a dirty rag then went behind the counter to make food and did not wash their hands.  people at the front desk were very rude. I won't be coming back,  was completely disappointed and disgusted.

Good place to watch sports, listen to live music, or dance. The food is great and the drinks are good. This place has 2 outside patios and a restaurant inside. Good spot to bring people from out of town or locals.

Ca'Dario wouldn't honor our reservation last night. We had reserved on OpenTable for a 7:00 seating on a Monday outside. They had given away all the outside tables. Explanation: Outside reservations are just a "preference," not guaranteed. Unfortunately, that's what's called in restaurant parlance a "fib." The reservation system lets you choose outside, nothing about a preference. Nor did they attempt to make any accommodation (it was fairly clear some outside tables would be done in 15 or 20 minutes.) By the way, the hostess was polite (if unhelpful), so this is clearly a management problem not a staff problem.

A restaurant is certainly entitled to set any conditions on reservations they wish. Not honoring what they set up is not okay.

Too bad, we've been to Ca'Dario before and enjoyed it. This was for a minor special occasion and they blew it.

Oh, Sidney Street Caf! How I love you! Soulard has a ton of good places to eat, but most of them are just traditional American restaurants without a whole lot of interesting or innovative menu choices. I guess the people over at Sidney Street Caf decided they wanted to fill that void. The menu at Sidney Street Caf changes all the time, reflecting what kind of quality protein is available on any given day, so trying the specials is a must. I love the wasabi steak and oyster poorboys. Sidney Street Caf is dinner only and kind of pricey, so keep that in mind before you dine.

2/24/18. Celebrating my birthday with a dinner date with the my better half. Wait time 1.5 hours. Seating in the front in a small section. Large party was extremely loud and obnoxious and made two other s and our table very uncomfortable. The food was out in a timely manner but lacked proper seasoning. A little bland. The mojito was not properly muddled and vibrant. To say the least we did not enjoy our experience. We were very disappointed. Quick tip, make a reservation. ‍

Finally got around to visiting Yats in their new location. Same great food as always in a much bigger space, which I am still undecided on whether or not I like. Gone is the intimate feeling that the other location (of which I've been visiting since the late 90's) had. Four stars only because I'm still a little sad they've moved. 

Beer and wine are available in the 21 and over bar. You'll know you're in the bar when you a) see the bar stools and b) the handwritten "21 and over only"

A kick ass picture hangs next to the cash register. I want this photo.

Parking is much easier. Meters up front.. spaces in the back. Less competition with competing businesses for parking on the far end.
You're going to get full on this food because it's loaded with butter oil and bread. It's a diner don't expect anything else.

Review is for food only, no music performance while we were there. Completely underwhelming entrees. Both the steak frites and the shrimp and grits lacked flavor. Steak was a puny thin cut, not worth the over $30 price. 5 small shrimp in the blah sauce. Service was ok, with a very friendly and helpful waitress at first who couldn't be found for 10 mins after we finished. Also out entrees were brought out while we were still eating our appetizer. The one  shining star of the meal was the poke appetizer, which is huge in size and comes with tons of veggies in a tasty sauce. If you end up here for a music night suggest sticking to apps and small plates.

Really great French onion soup, really good prime rib French dip, really crappy service, under cooked fish, and long wait times, on a Tuesday night!
Had to flag our server down, every time, and then wait at length for responses. 
When fish was extremely undercooked server took to manger who then offered to re-cook even though at that point we didn't even want it, waited 45 minutes again, for the next plate, didn't even bother to add discount or adjust bill. Heck the manager didn't even come out at all. Totally not worth it, and FYI much older crowd this restaurant is definitely aging, no wonder no one I know comes here any more. Sad.

Great little "Irish" dive bar in the neighborhood that I return to for the great bartenders, staff and patron companionship. 

HOWEVER.............

Recently the owner has been making significant cut backs - kitchen doesn't open til 5 pm, several good menu items dropped, etc. I've seen several customers walk in and right back out after hearing of the new changes. I love this place and it is saddening to see how the owners are driving off customers/employees and running it to the ground.

If you do stop by make sure to ask for the curly-haired tatted bartender, Alex. He's the best! Hopefully they don't drive him away....

I want to still like this place, I really do. But it's gone rapidly downhill the past few years and seems to only get worse. I took my car to get vacuumed and cleaned (gold level) this week and they did such a half-assed job. Dirt was still on the inside of the doors ( I wiped it off easily with a towel, which tells me they didn't even bother to wipe down the doors), I had a huge sap mark still on my windshield, and my trunk was still full of sand! I don't think they even bothered to vacuum my trunk at all. I honestly don't think they care about their job at all. Waste of time and money. Such a bummer, as this place has been my go-to since I got my first car many many years ago!

I'm giving two stars instead of one because the guy directing traffic was working really hard and the girl at the counter was very nice.
Had the Super Quinoa Salad. It is OK for an airport grab-n-go, but severely overpriced at $15. The checkout staff is not friendly at all too.

They seated us together as close as possible to other patrons at the front of the restaurant where the waitress would not have to walk far I suppose. I thought maybe they might distance us from other folks...

Oh so NOT appetizing. Let's start with the "Salsa". It tasted like tomato purée. No spice, no flavor. I ordered the hot salsa, it was greener...Still watery with an unsavory heat flavor without being hot at all. I ordered the Beef Monster burrito, and beef nachos.

The burrito arrived like it had cheese on top at some point, but it melted into the bottom where it turned into a watery greasy mess. I opened it up, and greasy hamburger ran out. No spice, no cheese just a greasy, soggy mess.

The Nachos arrived with the worst presentation possible. Fried out super greasy hamburger on top of chips that used to have white cheese on them, but now resemble just hamburger, and not real good hamburger. Cheese was terrible. It melted into a greasy mess on the bottom of the plate.

Sorry folks, you guys need to taste your own dishes.

I asked for pickled jalapeños, and got what tasted like a pickled sweet pepper. Beans and rice were utterly a disappointment!!

I won't try this restaurant again, and I DO NOT recommend it to anyone.

One of the best restaurants in Brentwood TN by far. This place is awesome in every way, a bit pricey would be my only complaint but I am cheap. The food quality here is top notch, awesome atmosphere, great decor the restaurant is the best. Very friendly wait staff, they make you feel at home. The food is American standard fare but high class at the same time. I would recommend this place if you are in business in Brentwood its in the business distinct part of town and they cater to upper middle class to wealthy clients. I would recommend you try this restaurant out.
This is review is long overdue. 5 stars for quality of service & customer service.Thank you Nina  my cookie is nice and smooth
I got a good cleaning for a pretty cheap introductory price. The scheduling was a bit weird--had to make one for checkup and one for cleaning but everything else went well.
New to New Orleans and glad there's a Whole Foods, but man - slowest-moving lines I've ever experienced at a grocery store.  Ever.  Really bad.
First time trying Korean food and the food was awesome.. I wasn't sure if the server was the cook also from how long he took to take our order and pretty much never came back

Gross abounds. Totally average rooms, bathroom looks a little tacky - the extra toilet paper is front and center in the open counter storage cubbies. The towels are big though. The pool area is right beside the parking lot, which is open to the reception area. It's a really weird set up, and hard to describe.

No mini bar, no room service, and it's close enough to Bourbon that you can smell the wafts of urine. The pictures online are not so true to life. In short, spare yourself the disappointment.

Came here on a random whim while at the Westshore mall recently.  It was eerily quiet and we had our pick of pretty much the entire restaurant.  Since there was a Lightning game on we sat at a booth in the bar so we had a good view of the multiple TVs that were on.  The bartender was our order taker and only person we really interacted with during our visit.

Drinks came out quick and the flatbread I ordered came pretty quickly after that.  The flatbread was great, but for an app and the price it was a bit steep.  Most of their menu items were also priced pretty high and it seemed like this place wasn't very busy so I'm fearful how long it may last in this location.

excellent food and even better price. I was welcomed and love the personal touch they give to eat customer. we had a nice conversation while waiting on my order. I got the crab leg platter and I love the fresh garlic butter sauce. very nice! the shrimp add on is well worth it. they are huge and tasted great! I hope I have time to return here before leaving town.
Good place!  Take your time and browse, I picked up some christmas gifts and treats for my dog!

This is the worst fastfood restaurant I have ever been to. They have messed up the last three orders so badly that it nearly boggles the mind that somthing this simple as a burger could be botched.

I could understand if there was a line out the door and they were understaffed maybe being slow or getting somthing wrong.  But everytime I'm here it's dead and they STILL cant get it right!

 This is why the minimum wage needs to be abolished. These fools need to be fighting ditches by hand.

I will never order from here again. Placed an order around 8:00 on a Monday night, called around 9:00 they said my order had already left about 10 minutes before I called. 9:40 I called again only to be told that they didn't have an order for me, they couldn't find my order In their system. So I asked why was I told my order left in the first place when they never saved my order to be placed. Ridiculous, I've ordered plenty of times and occasionally have small issues but this time they have lost my business for good!

Honestly there was nothing special about this store from my experience. Yeah, they have a lot of inventory but most of it isn't what I was looking for. The man at the counter was abrupt and not willing to help you find the gun you are looking for. Seemed to me like he was distracted with trying to help several other people at once and it led to a very unpleasant experience in general. Their prices are also $150 - $200 more expensive than other gun stores in the area. I was really hoping for more here but sadly, they didn't deliver. Overall an average gun store experience by today's standards.

I love the chicken quesadillas here, their amazing!! Being a California native who knows real Mexican food I was surprisingly impressed!!  I have not had any other food from this place so I can't speak about that, but the chicken quesadilla with jalepenos..so delicious! The one down side is they are a bit slow, hence the three starts instead of four! Go owls!!

Having been to the original Lonnie's downtown a few times, I was interested to see what the Lonnie's oddly situated on West End had to offer. With its debaucherous Karaoke ambiance it actually ended up being a great time. Though I have always liked Lonnie's downtown, I have not once attempted to sing there. If you have ever been to the downtown Lonnie's you will understand why. Karaoke usually reserved for the tone deaf or inebriated, at the Lonnie's downtown is a showcase for local talent trying to make it big. Though it's impressive it's not really encouraging to those that are not so musically inclined.

Lonnie's on West End, however, not only encouraged my presence on the stage but also demanded it. With a whopping rendition of Sir Mix a Lots "I like big butts", I felt that I definitely did Lonnie's justice.  It's not the kind of place that I would wander into by myself (not usually a huge crowd), but accompanied by my own motley we managed to take over for the night.

A BBQ joint in the making....give them some time, and I'm sure they'll blow you away. 

I ventured into the modest establishment desiring to sample their best wares, and let's say some I was thoroughly disappointed in, and in others made me want to come back for a second take.   As a new resident of St. Charles, Mo part of what plays into me returning to a place is distance, and if the food + service is that compelling then I'll waste a half of tank of gas to enjoy the experience.   Let's just say it will be a minute before I make my way back down to Vernon's. 

I had a combo plate where I got ribs, wings, and pulled pork which include one side, which I ended up paying to get a second for balance, and honestly was extremely disappointed.   While waiting for my food, the service was warm and friendly, and so I mozied on to the sauce counter and attempted to sample them all.  I will tell you the THAI BBQ is out of this world, as there is nothing on this planet that taste like it.   What you will find it is simply either you love it or hate it....and the owner even stated that was the general consensus of what people thought. So onto my meal:

The ribs were cooked perfectly with good flavor, and chew.  They didn't disintegrate off of the bone, which was bonus, that the smoke master knew what they were doing.   The Chicken wing while it was only one, was a pleasant surprise, because they used mulberry wood it doesn't darken the skin at all, so I was expecting to have an undercooked tasteless piece of bird, and let me tell you it was cooked perfectly and this bird soared....my only complaint was that it was only 1 whole wing...which I personally think two should've been included. The pulled pork was moist, while not soaked in water or broth, which is a sign it came out of it's own juices, and needed a hint of salt, but aside from that was tasty.  

NOW THE SIDES....the original sides I ordered was the Brisket Beans and the Potato Salad, and I am sad to say when the owner came out to make his rounds a round the dining room I had already surrendered eating another bite.   The beans in the BBQ BRISKET BEANS were GYnormous and the sauce was less than palpable.   The Horseradish Potato salad isn't a mustard or mayo based....it's actually sour cream...and while this is out of the box for a BBQ joint, I would suggest salting it so that it popped, or used one of their great rubs....as it presented as extremely bland.  Upon conveying this to the owner, he saved the day...with bringing out literally every side they make.   So in this order these were the BOMB.....Tequilla Lime green beans....do it...do it.....do it..trust me!  The Coleslaw....the healthiest, Tastiest out of the box creamiest cole slaw you'll EVER HAVE! The Brisket Chili was delicious.   The candied apples were so good, my complaint was that they didn't put them in a pie and serve them up that way.   The cheesy scalloped potatoes were not bad, just nothing fancy.  

If you live anywhere near Vernon's please give them a try, they are worth you visit.  Next time I plan on attempting to tackle on of their mammoth sandwiches. I gave them 3 stars on the food really because of the waste I felt on the sides before the owner knocked me out with service recovery.  So please know that if I hadn't chosen those initial 2 sides, this would be a 4-4.5 because of the food and service.  

Vernon's I'm rooting for you all!

Three dishes here are five stars, and the rest are just three. I'm a little surprised this was rated the #1 restaurant by Philly mag, but I will say I'd definitely come back for these three dishes only:

- the babganoush: creamy, perfectly balanced in smokiness and sweetness, sprinkled with pomegranates, divine on the hot pita bread, it lives up to the raves

- the chai latte: creamy, foamy, sprinkled with rose petals, totally divine, order this the moment you sit down 

- the cheese-rose water dessert: I forget the exact name but this blew me away with its crispy, toasted edges, cheesy and warm interior, and pistachio-topped crust, impeccably made

The rest of the dishes were disappointing. That Fatteh dish everyone raves about? Yeesh! Such a bizarre, crunchy concoction with a confusing consistency and too-sweet taste. 

The lamb dish with the cinnamon was way too spiced and off-putting, and the hummus was forgettable. 

Also wouldn't recommend the tahini hot chocolate. The best part is the cotton candy they top it with (delicious!), but once that's eaten, you're stuck with an expensive Nestle-tasting drink.

Just stick with those three dishes and you'll have a five-star meal.

Root probably has the most amazing burger i have tried in a very long time. They are a little bit meat oriented and i am not a fan of the charcuterie boards but the burger here is amazing, the fries are crispy and the home-made ketchup like tomato sauce complements it perfectly.
Their other items such as appetizers are not as impressive. Instead of an appetizer you should order the chocolate dessert which has a weird yet quite intriguing presentation. (comes on a heavy plate with ice cream and other stuff. the portion is definitely not big though.)
The place is also so nice and cozy, if you live in NOLA a very good place to have a business lunch or a casual meeting or if you are visiting, you should not miss that spot that offers something way better and different than those tourist traps the french quarter is filled with.

Let me start by saying this review should have been done quite some time ago so maybe they have straightened up their act a little. This is a place that had a great view and can be a romantic location for a date or a night out. I wish I could rate it higher but the food was just ok and the service was terrible. We got the wrong wine however they managed to get the brand right, the bread guy had been missing in action for the first 15 mins we were there. And the guy sitting next to us with his two lady friends that looked like they were being paid to be there having inappropriate conversations made it hard to hear my wife. Definitely a sour night for what could be a great place to dine.

I have to say that the service itself was actually great. They were friendly and took my order right away, a d the food took a while to be prepared, but it was fresh. I also found the facility itself to be clean.  Now let's get to the food. I have always enjoyed LJS, but the food I bought here was nowhere near as high quality as I've been used to. We got the family meal with both fish and chicken, with onion rings and lots of extra hush puppies (our favorite side). I also got some cheese curds. The curds were the best part of the meal, they were just right. The fish, rings, chicken, and hush puppies were all too greasy. Keep in mind, we love fried foods, and like I said, I've been eating at LJS for decades. This was beyond great. I literally squeezed grease out of this food! Very disappointed, I will not return to this location.

Great vibe, the shrimp is amazing, and down-to earth staff. I had a great, romantic evening with my wife here. Cocktails were fantastic and the shareable plates were a terrific idea -- all while listening to an amazing singer and a terrific piano player.

Have heard much about this place so decided to give it a try. It was ok not great not bad. On a Wednesday evening there was a wait to get in.  Everything was Ok -service, food etc.    very very loud place can't hardly hear people at your own table.  I had the grilled shrimp with fries and coleslaw. Food was not hot but not cold -tap water cost $1 -I was like what ??? A dollar for tap water ?? One of the people at table had the catfish special and fries and the other shrimp parm. Again food was Ok service was Ok

This is for the tire center. They rarely answer the phone when you call and when I finally did get through I received the run around and bogus info from someone who was either new or poorly trained. I don't want to pay $200 more at Les Schwab but I'm willing to just for their customer service alone

Five of us dined at Bernie's this evening. The food was fine. The service was slow and inattentive. The drinks took forever to arrive. The food was delivered but there were no flatware settings. We had to go to the hostess to get silverware. The bill was delivered but, again, took forever to collect and return. The place seems to be missing management and efficient processes.
Decent little place off the beaten track. It was hard to wrangle a waiter sometimes for a refill on water, but breakfast was good enough. I'd like to come back for the scotch eggs!

Cool hipster work spot. Great vibe and excellent tea.

I wish they had more food options other than pastries. I had a meeting there, but we ended up leaving and continuing at another location with lunch food because we were hungry.
Great craft cocktails. Food was meh   Service was indifferent (except at the bar). Seems like place tried to scale back to deal with covid times probably pass on food

This a great place to go to enjoy the French inspired patio    Although I've been a few times and the food never really shines. The portions are small. Most of the time my items have been overlooked or under seasoned. My least favorite of this restaurant group.

Very disappointed in their lack of attention to detail. The toe stitching on one of my expensive ankle boots had come out. Lady at the counter said they would probably need to glue it and then run stitching over it. A week later, I picked my boot up and to my dismay there was glue all around the leather and the stitching was the wrong color and wrong gauge. I only hope I can find someone else to fix this because I'm certainly not trusting Joe's to touch these boots again.

They say they would reserve a place for my friends birthday party for a group of 20. When we arrived they said they had to many customers and canceled it.  Then they told us to get out.  Disappointed in how they handled the situation.

Loved the relaxed vibe, tasty, upscale yet affordable
2.14.14

Zesty. Crunchy. Tasty.
Caesar salad with warm Seared chicken breast (Romain lettuce, warm sliced marinated chicken breast, garlic croutons, Parmesan Chris, Caesar dressing $10.75) my 13-year-old son nearly licked his bowl

Chicken & waffles
Comfort food with a flavor infused flare
(Bacon cornmeal waffle, crisp battered chicken, time infused maple syrup, with butter, $13.25) my wife thoroughly enjoyed this and took home half for lunch tomorrow


Horseradish crusted salmon: 
Zip. Crunch. Flare.
(fresh grated horseradish and panko breadcrumb crusted salmon, sautéed spinach, roasted garlic smashed potatoes, red wine natural jus $17.95) winner!

Although the menu is thin, I think that contributes to quality entrées

I have been going to Lucky Dill since it was a much smaller shop over on Alt 19. Maybe 15 or 20 years? Back then, there was never a bad meal. Now, not so much. My last three meals have either been awful or just plain disappointing. 

One of those meals was breakfast. I ordered the blueberry pancakes. They were not served as promised on the menu (citrus butter, fresh blueberries and blueberry compote). There was a blueberry syrup but the pancakes were inedible. Worse than cardboard. For the very first time in my life, I returned the food. The manager came over after about 10 minutes and offered to remove them from my check. Well, I am glad she 'generously' offered because there was no way in hell I was paying for that.

We recently went back for dinner. After two disappointing meals, I wanted to give them another chance since I've been coming here for YEARS. I ordered a steak sandwich. There was so much fat that I ate maybe 1/3 of it. 

Their only salvation is the free cheesecake. Best thing on the menu and the only reason they didn't get one star instead of two.

Sad but this place is now on my 'never again' list.

The food is good, however I only suggest going there to eat. The lady who " works the counter" does not answer the phone and IF She does you are lucky...... sometimes she will answer and then just hang up on you. Anyways she speaks English well and seems nice but they would make much more money if they answered the phone! Sometimes when I go in after calling 10 tens or being put on hold for 15 min I will see others inside saying the same thing ( they had to drive up there to order because she wouldn't answer the phone )
Being a new mother with an infant and working full time it's hard to always make dinner and I do like the food but they make it EXTREMELY difficult....just my honest thoughts on this...if the food wasn't so good I wouldn't ever go back here so that sucks you know... they are just COMPLETELY under staffed

We came here early Sunday morning and arrived just before 8. We sat outside until 8:15 when they finally decided to open (they were supposed to open at 8) and their staff was rude and took the orders of people who had literally just walked up before those who had been waiting for 15+ minutes. Very poor experience, haven't seen such poor service and lack of attention in a very long time. Disappointed.

Let me first start by saying I've been getting brazilians for years and have gone to many places. I've never been made to feel so uncomfortable as I was here. Nayeli had great reviews so I went to her. My first time she nagged me the entire time about how I shouldn't have shaved in between waxes. The second time she was 15 minutes late and then shoved product at me. And I still would have came back, but the third time is what did it for me. The moment I laid down she asked me how long it had been since my last wax and I told her 6 weeks. Which is a normal growth cycle if you go anywhere else. But of course she started with saying that was too long and that now I would have more hair. Ok so what?...Then when I tried to make small talk she brought up AGAIN that I had too many baby hairs because I was in between cycles and it made it more difficult for her than it should have been. As she was finishing, she found a strip of hair that she had missed and had the nerve to say "oh it looks like you have a strip here. Since you're already here, I'll just go ahead and remove it for you"....as if she was doing me some type of favor by removing hair SHE missed in the first place. Then at the very end she made sure to reiterate how I shouldn't have gone six weeks and that that was he reason she left some baby hairs. Weird....every where else just plucks them and moves on. Then after she finished insulting me for 15 minutes she walked me out and proceeded to shove product on me as she always does. Needless to say, this is terrible customer service. Getting a Brazilian is already not the most pleasant thing to do. I don't need to be nagged and made to feel even more uncomfortable throughout the entire process. The only reason I'm giving this 2 stars is because the girl at the front is always really sweet and helpful. It might be a good idea for management to work with the estheticians on etiquette and how you treat a customer who is lying butt naked in front of you.

Cool hipster vibe. Loved the decor, the Meze Original was really good, and the iced tea was brewed to perfection. We were there on a Sunday so the brunch menu was our only option to order from.  We were slightly disappointed that their hummus was not available for brunch, especially since they have the award for the best hummus. We will definitely be back when we are in the St Pete area again.

Why 4 stars? Because these were the fanciest arepas ever!

   ---   ---   ---

Prior to my visit here, my arepa experience was usually a bit grungier - maybe arepas from a food truck, or a hole-in-the-wall spot. By Mais Arepas is a really nice and trendy restaurant in the heart of New Orleans. As I mentioned above, everything looked super fancy.

The only bad thing about this, is that there was virtually no difference in the taste of these arepas, but the total bill probably ended up being a few notches above what I'd normally pay.

But we did enjoy the experience. The maiz de la rueda is basically corn on the cob with all the fixins', and basically required eating anytime I see something like it on a menu. 

As for my arepa, I went with the mechada, was is skirt steak, maduros, sofrito and mozzarella, which ended up being solid. Solid is my typical word of choice for - "good, but not amazing."

We shared a pitcher of sangria, which was some of the better sangria that I've ever had. I'm not one to typically order sangria, but our local friends we were dining with, said it was wonderful, and they were not incorrect.

We had a bit of a wait for a table here, as it's a small restaurant, that's apparently really popular. The NoWait app would have definitely come in handy here, so if the owners of Mais Arepas are reading this, I hope you look into it :)

A cute small plant selection compared to Green Things across the way! The prices here are fair but don't expect a lot of options. There are no vegetables or compost soil, which is what I was looking for.

Brought my dog in for a wash and trim, they complained how matted the dog was. We take good care of our dogs, When we got home I noticed him biting at his leg. Looked like he was burned by the blow dryer, took him to the animal hospital and his whole leg was burned. Never going back

Not so sure I understand the buzz of this place :(. They always have a wait which does go pretty fast so that's not terrible.  This morning though I ordered a breakfast burrito and asked if possible they could put some pinto beans in it.. I do like their pintos .. the waitress came back to the table to let us know she asked the supervisor about the beans and they cannot put them IN the burrito... only on the side??? Doesn't make a lot of sense to me , seems a bit lazy
So delicious! The menu changes every time I come in so I always get to try something new! Ask questions about the menu and be willing to try something different.

We had a Groupon for Eats because we've eaten here before and the food was good.  The restaurant is not necessarily around the block from us and the reason for the 1 star is before even walking into the restaurant there is a note on the door saying that their contract with Groupon has expired and they do Not accept previously purchased Groupings.  
It's a shame because it turned us off to even going inside and we choose to eat elsewhere. As a business decision to not work with Groupon we have no problem. However, the business should have honored already purchased deals.

Great work on putting new heels on a pair of cowboy boots. May have been a little pricey but did a great job and quick. Can't speak for others experience with JAW shoe repair but mine wasn't bad. 

My recommendations to owner would be to organize and clean the front of the store to make it more customer friendly and treat customers items with more care. Lots of shoes just thrown everywhere gives a bad image to people trying to repair/care for their shoes. Attention to customer service could be used as well. A simple "welcome to my store" or "thank you for your service come again" would be nice. Also, revisit pricing to see if your in line with other companies. 34 bucks for two new rubber heels seem a bit high but then again I don't own a shoe repair shop. 

Overall, I would recommend giving these guys a shot. Hopefully, they will listen to constructive criticism because being one of the only shoe repairs in town they could easily become the go-to place.

This place is not worth the trouble.  I went today and tried to get take-out but it was just more than I cared to deal with.

So I went in, and asked to order take-out, I was told to go to the bar.  Maybe that's normal, but I don't like bars, so I was uncomfortable there just to begin with.  I then asked about what style pizza it was, and the girl gave no real answer.  She didn't know, and I don't know if it is really a style of pizza.  I like classifying things, so I can properly describe them, and compare with similar types.

So I order the a 8 incher with one topping.  I estimated what it would come to, and but the price they rang up was about 15% more.  I protested that that couldn't be right.  It's not possible.  The woman at the bar, accepted my argument, but had to track down the manager to get the price right.

They did fix the price, and they did give me a free soda while I waited, which was nice, but I was irritated at the whole issue.

The pizza came soon enough, but it wasn't outstanding.  It was edible, and the sausage was fine, but there wasn't much cheese or sauce.  Sorry, but I'd much rather stick with a good New York style pie.

This mall was dying a slow death, like several others in Tampa before it. First came International Plazza, which gave e it .more competition,  but also added the stores and restraunts that payed the higher prices in International Mall,  only about a mile away.
But, just the opposite happened,  International Mall gave Westshore Mall the shot of B-12 it had been waiting for.
Within a short a.period of time all the empty stores were full and new restaurants sprung up in abundance.
Actually I see no competition between the 2 malls, one is all high end and the other ( Westshore) is a regular town type of mall. 
I love them both for different reasons, stores and restaurants.  Instead of them being in competition, I think International mall brought Westshore mall back to life and revitalized it, and trust me it needed it.
All of the new restaurants just make it all that much better. I now bieve Westshore Mall Is here to stay gor awhile, I had my doubts, but not anymore.
What's a girl to do? High end, medium and low end stores and all have their positive points, so just shop them all!

I've always had an amazing experiences at Olive Garden and one of our favorite places to stop on the go. Always a go to for us! The Streak Gorgonzola is AMAZING. The only thing is that the waitress this time never came back and refilled our drinks and I had to ask another waitress to fill up our drinks as she walked by and we had to ask for her through that same waitress. But will continue to go here!

This was my "go to" location for decades - even way before it was located at this spot (when it used to be a HoJo's! ) and P.B. was in Abington, PA in the 1980s! It's now just simply pathetic. They redesigned this store recently - for some reason. It's now a VERY cramped area with limited supplies - which are never in stock no matter what the website states. 

About 70% of the inside space is now tires...rows and rows of tires alost reaching to the ceiling! The help is rarely helpful and downright rude. I did my research on spark plugs for my car before hand, I asked this guy for these spark plugs and he simply said, these fit your car right?Because if they dont your car is screwed and these are non returnable. Ok..fine I get that. 

He cuts me off and asks what year, model is the car..(like this was a big chore to enter a few entries on the computer) The plugs DID match and process to slide them across the desk and walk away. 

I rather drive another 8-10 miles out of the way to the Warminster or Montgomeryville location. I have MUCH better luck finding what I need there.

Not sure what's going on these days, but I've been getting super unlucky with food orders from my usual favorites, most recently this one. 

My 10-piece buffalo wings looked extremely sad. First of all, they looked and tasted old (as if reused from someone else's order), but they were also tiny and chewy, as if they came from unhappy scrawny underfed teenage chickens. I almost felt bad eating them, but was mostly annoyed that I did not get my money's worth ($8.50+). 

The greek salad was the same size as usual, but the level of freshness wasn't. The tomato and lettuce pieces had brown spots all over them. I also ordered a cheeseburger, and found that although the quality was roughly the same as usual, the beef patties were super greasy and the "cheddar" that came with it was more like waxy orange American cheese than cheddar. 

The lamb kefta wrap was probably the highlight of the lot, but it was a bit small and also super oily. It was hard to tell whether the filling was beef or lamb. For $5.99, it's not super expensive, but it's also not super substantial, with only ground meat, a few strands of red onions, and some parsley. 

The weirdest thing about this order wasn't even the food, though. The delivery guy parked outside of my building and called me, saying I needed to go outside to the car and get it from him. In the past, Zuzu's Kitchen has typically delivered to my apartment, which is why I've always included a generous tip on top of the existing delivery fee. But the delivery guy this time even made a fuss about dropping off my food at the security desk in my building, and the whole point of me ordering delivery was because I was experiencing some leg muscle aches, making going outside a bit tough for me. 

Needless to say, there are too many good places for food in the city to pay extra fees to get greasy food of declining quality not get delivered to one's home. It's sad because I used to really like this place.

The visit was pleasant, this new brewery is in midtown not too far from me. We came to check out the sour beers! The on the bright side -Raspberry was my choice and pretty much nailed it for the sours! The on the bright side pineapple /guava was really good as well. The atmosphere is nice there are big enough takes inside to play some games. The outside patio is really nice as well. They have boards set up for bags and even play movies out there! I love that they have the simple board games to do something. Their selection is all around up on the brewery charts as well as the atmosphere for the indoor vs outdoor choices. I'll be going back soon!

Been twice sushi is on point, but service is hit and miss one 5 star experience and one 1 star experience.  There are tons of AYCE in Reno and ijji is right in line with prices of others.  The service when the place wasn't busy was slow, and items ordered with are party of 4 never came (this is common when sitting down) but looking at what didn't come it was the obvious more expensive items.  REALLY ijji?  The 5 star visit was during a time when the place was packed and we (3) sat at the bar.  Incredible chef nailed down our taste and gave recommendations, glass never empty.
My advice, sit at the bar.
It's all about getting the right waitress. Betty is fantastic. The food is great and the wait moves quick.

I headed over here for lunch yesterday pretty jazzed about the idea of having a gourmet burger to go. The place was bustling, but I was able to order my meal in about 5 minutes. While waiting for number to be called, one of the staff members came up to ask how I was doing, what I ordered, etc. He was very friendly and I liked that they were willing to go the extra step to check on their customers. 
I ordered a medium burger with provolone cheese and mushrooms with a side of truffle fries. I could smell the fries through the bag as I walked back to my office and couldn't wait to dig in. 
Honestly, I wasn't all that impressed. The fries were pretty tasty but didn't have the extra pizazz I was looking for. The burger was surprisingly tasteless; some more seasoning was definitely necessary. I was also kind of disappointed by the mushrooms. Again, not seasoned well and also, very few on my burger! 
I can see this being a great option after a night out (it's open until 3 am) but I don't think this is a place I will be craving in the future.

More Poke in indianapolis is always a good thing! At Moar you fill out your custom order on a little sheet and turn it in at the counter.  I loved my passion fruit black tea with mango jellies. I ordered the rice bowl with spicy tuna, pickled radish, eel sauce, seaweed salad, and fried onions. My major issue was that the tuna was shredded instead of pieces like other poke I've had. The consistency was gooey. My other thought is that if you're new to poke and not sure what you like, this style of ordering could be intimidating and maybe result in a bad order. I wish they had a couple of house bowls that you could order and know the toppings went well together.

Charming little place with a French theme. Had a pastry and coffee that was good quality. Not so warm atmosphere with employees working there - the girl at the register seemed like she could care less and just took the order like it was a cattle call "we're out of this, we're out of that, here you go..."

I was looking to get my car detailed and I'm so shocked. I called several places. Called  The BodyWorks Auto Body Repair. They said they could do it 5/19/16. When I got there they told me it would be a full day job from $150-$250. I said I thought it would be a couple hours? Why did you tell me you could do it today? I asked if he could do the interior only then. He replied not today. The owner there, (Mel) also was not friendly at all. Well, I lucked out completely. I looked at yelp reviews and called "Keep It Clean Mobile Detail Service" at (727) 851-4349.  I'm blown away. Herb from "Keep It Clean Mobile Detail Service" told me Trust me, your car will look brand new when I'm done with it. My car was filthy from living on the beach for 6 years and never having it detailed. It's a 2006 BMW 10 years old. I was ready to just trade it in. It took him about 1 1/2 hours and I can't believe how it looks. It looks brand new. The stains on the carpet are gone and they were really bad. It's perfect!!! I'm still shocked how he did it. Plus he's the nicest guy I've met. He comes to you in this cool painted van that's fully equipped to do everything. I mean everything. I just can't explain how great he brought my car back to brand new. The cost was only $75.00. What? Are you kidding me? Plus when others at our condo seen my car they were also shocked and wanted his card and business. I was so completely satisfied I gave him a extra $ 50.00 tip. So, take my word for it, call Herb at "Keep It Clean Mobile Detail Service" at (727) 851-4349.  Plus he does more than just detail.  I'm 100% satisfied and he will do all our BMW detailing from now on.

I typically don't write reviews on yelp, usually on OpenTable, but I am compelled to warn potential guest from dining at Giuseppe's on Cowpath Rd.  First the food was good and if we didn't have the issues we had, iwe would probably go back. Service was not great, but it wasn't terrible either, just really slow.  We live fairly far from this restaurant, over 30 miles so this was a first time for us here. A few days later I received a phone call from my bank's fraud department about some recent activity on my card and turns out that someone had my credit card information and just happened to use it across the street at RiteAid in Lansdale for over a $1000 dollars! Conscience? maybe, but I think there is some shading business going on there and wanted to warn anyone to watch out.

It only earned 2 stars because the wait staff was nice and the view was nice. The food was very disappointing. I try to go to a restaurant twice before I leave a review so this was my second time here. The first time was the same as the second time.  

The peanuts hummus tasted like peanut butter. I'd think for the price , the shrimp would be peeled. It was 14 dollars for about 10 shrimps but you had to fee then yourself. The Mac n cheese was runny. 

The lobster bake tasted like chorizo and overpowered the taste of the lobster. The pealla was oily and it seemed as though it was just fried rice with scallops  and shrimp sauteed separately and put on top of it. The shrimp on top was super tiny. Did not taste like the rice was prepared with the seafood like a traditional Paella. The fish and chips looked over cooked and was bland. The potato wedges were not cooked thoroughly. They need to redo the menu and change some of the recipes.

Friday lunchtime at Christmas/Holiday time. I arrive and there is a podium like piece of furniture with menus on it. Waited and no one came so I waved to the one server I saw and he grabbed a menu and sat me.
One thing to ruin a meal is slow service. Another is your entree arriving lukewarm instead of hot. Both happened today. 
In busy Media during the holidays they had one server on handling about 10 tables, most with over 2 people per table so he did his best to keep up but as my entree was lukewarm it obviously had sat for awhile before being delivered. It also took over 10 minutes to get my check after I asked because he was busy seating people, taking and delivering orders. 
 Very disappointed in a restaurant that for years has been a Media staple but even though the entree was lukewarm it was good. I hope to return soon and have a better experience

Came here for breakfast.  Service is fast, and the food is awesome, but I seem to keep ordering the same generic egg dish.  The place is big and every time I've been, we always seem to get seated immediately.

Great place to have a party they have two different rooms that both hold a decent amount of people. They are pretty cheap as far as pricing for catering food and don't have a room charge which is hard to find. Staff is friendly and they were really accommodating. Will be back for sure!

Today, 8/31/20 I was scheduled for an x-ray and follow up for my injured shoulder at 8:50am. This appointment was made after my last appointment on 8/3/20. When I arrived I was told I was not in the system for 8/31 but for 9/14. This was frustrating because I received a call last week confirming the appointment. I also have on my refrigerator the business card given to me by the individual who scheduled the appointment which has 8/31 at 8:50am on it. I unfortunately did not bring the card with me to todays appointment because I did not think I needed it. The staff was not very helpful and I felt I was inconveniencing them and that they did not believe me. I was looking forward to this appointment to see the healing progression of my shoulder and the Doctor told me at the last appointment that he would be starting me on some sort of physical therapy at my 8/31 appointment. Now I have to wait until 9/14 for my appointment and my back to work date was set for 9/24/20. I am extremely frustrated with the level of service I received today and I was also supposed to follow up with my workmen's comp nurse and inform her of my progress and now I can't. Not to mention the hour and a half of my time Rothman wasted this morning.

Wild Garlic is tucked away so nicely I never would've known it was there if not for a friend. They have pizza by the pie and some by the slice specials that I thought was pretty cool, and they also have a special pizza of the month on top of the regular menu. Shout out to the October MacZilla! *drool* with its delicious balance of sweet and spicy with really creative toppings. Often crust on a pizza is totally hit or miss for me--but not here!. *chef's kiss* Good flavor and chew here, and don't forget to dip in the honey they provide on the tables!

Minus one star frankly 
Made Rez on open table 
Special request box - so I asked for a 'nice table' 
Last time I checked, this table is certainly not. We were bumped - I was bumped - the entire time as that was the superhighway  to the bathroom
Notwithstanding, food came out 
Blackened fish tacos were ice cold and certainly not black - pale white and cold - 
Even the taco shells were cold - it was gross.

Oh - and of only 2 wines by the glass of  Sauvignon Blanc  - Kim Crawford being one - "they didn't stock the bar" - so my wife waited 15 minutes for a glass...it's dec 23...what bar doesn't stock at the very minimum the wines by the glass at the bar...?
Place is shit - too bad as Palm Beach Gardens and West Palm provide a far superior experience.

They comped the meal but who really cares 
We'll never go back - ruined a holiday dinner -

Enthusiastic service is about all I can say that was positive. The buffet is small, and the dinner menu overpriced. My malai kofta was exactly as spicy as I wanted it, but it was also salty as hell and not at all creamy. Husband's chana masala was good, but watery. 

Bombay Palace has ruined me for any other Indian restaurant. :/

Still the best cheesesteaks in Philly. It's been a year since we have been back and it's still great. The whiz is not that gloppy canned stuff and perfectly melds with the meat and onions. Get the whiz with your fries as an extra treat. The sodas are like an old fashioned soda jerk style and you do get one refill. Grab a great hand blended shake on the way out for the perfect ending. It's not that far from Center City, give it a shot

I'm a fan of the soup but unfortunately they lack in consistency. My biggest reason for the 1 start review is due to the employees not wearing masks. Some were wearing their masks, while others only allowing it to hang from one ear leaving their noses & mouths completely exposed. I try really hard to support small businesses especially with the pandemic impacting them greatly. I will not continue to give them my money until they can show the same support & care that their customers show them.

Cute place. Sadly, the potatoes were terrible and found bacon in them which is not ideal for a vegetarian. The waitress did not check in at all so i was not able to even get new potatoes. Not air conditioned, lots of flies. Also saw a cake from giant in the bakery case ?

I stayed last night with co workers, we were all in separate rooms on different floors and had the same in room experience. The night of check in there was no hot water to shower and called front desk to ask if there were any issues with the plumbing.  The front desk sent maintenance to the room but he left saying he had to check the main water system agreeing there was no hot water in the room.  We went on with our night hearing nothing assuming the issue was being taken care of until I tried to shower this morning, still no hot water.   When my co workers and I  met up in the lobby we realized we all had the same complaint of no hot water and that our bathroom sink didn't drain causing one person to flood their bathroom after the front desk told them to keep the water running and it would eventually get hot.  The front desk had no apologies or even gave acknowledge there was an issue with 6 different rooms standing in front of them with the same complaint.  Poor Service!

I've been here twice. Here's how it went:
Happy Hour: crowded. slow service. but fries were yum diddly delicious. we got every single french fry offered. cheese did the weird gross thing when it gets cold and congeals. in like 2 minutes. that sucked. 

Random Friday night at 9pm: loud-ass music could barely make conversation which is weird because there were like 10 people in the bar, clearly not there to start a dance party. One waiter. So service was slow. Waiter tried to make up for it by being good looking.
Howl at the Moon can be a fun place. They put in a new patio and windows that open onto Georgia Street, so maybe it will be even better this summer.

In a shopping center, -this place is a bit hard to find. This is a nice space inside when you enter... sorta rustic feel. Montreal style wood oven bagels are special. However when you taste one of these bagels, you'll find that they lack flavor....just plain bland. They are also rather thin.... about the size of ring-toss rings. The best thing they offer is the La Colombe coffee. 
Oh...  and, by the way, if you want some butter for your flavorless bagel, cough up another 75 cents.

we went to have a few drinks and listen to the band on a saturday evening. we went ahead and set up a tab for my husband and i and so did our friends..all seperate tabs. we ordered 20 wings and i had 4 drinks and my husband had 5...our total bill was 98.00!!! they are way over priced on their drinks! One of our friends ordered a mich lite bottle and they charged 4.75!!  and then to top it all off we asked for a glass of ice water but they were only allowed to sell bottled water for 2.00 a bottle!!  the womens bathroom was dirty and musky and the vanity along the floor and wall had water damage and it looked like mold. the one stall i used did not even lock. On top of that, they also charge a cover charge! I will never go back to this place again!!!

Great ambiance and decor. Large selection of food. Maybe too large. I think they are trying to be everything and not focusing on maybe their best. I tried the fish and chips which was ok but fries came out cold. Bread was average. I guess I'm saying the food didn't live up to the atmosphere.

Nice quiet little park.  I took my toddler on a Saturday late morning and there was only one other family there.  I would say that the playground equipment would be best for preschool age but my 8 year old would have enjoyed the monkey bars.  The park does have wear and tear on the equipment and a few items were either broken or missing.  Don't let that deter you from the park though.  It was peaceful and quiet even on a Saturday morning.  It would be a great place to meet up with other families and have a picnic.  Lots of shade (though not on the playground), lots of nice shaded picnic tables and really quiet.   It would have been nice to have some information, as other Yelpers stated, about the "heritage" portion of the park.  I'm assuming that it was a former site of the water company since the current one is next to the park.  

It's a nice little gem of a park.  A little paint, a few repairs and some fresh mulch would go a long way.
This is a very pretty nail spa.  If you want to get a mani/pedi and not get your cuticles done and not know what you are going to pay when you walk out, this is the place for you.

I want to give four stars for the quality of food but the awful service prohibits me from doing so. This is my second visit here and I am still holding out hope they will hold charm school classes along with their already scheduled pie school before my return.

Where do I begin? I feel as if this business cheated me out of a poorly done service that I even paid for. From the beginning she was extremely rude and seemed as if she didn't even want to hem my curtains. After going back in forth on the original price I was quoted she finally agreed to do the job. When I came to pick up the curtains not only where they thrown into garage bags but not all of the curtains where even hemmed. From instant attitude coming in the door to being made feel as a complete inconvenience. Take your money elsewhere for better customer service and an actual job to be done.

If you have no other option of another restaurant and you go to this La Guanajuato, I recommend that if you get a waitress named TERESA, go running, you will lose your money. la verdad este lugar es bueno por la comida pero el servicio de las meseras es pésimo maneje 25 minutos para tener una cena con mi hija y la mesera fue  muy grosería  a ella no le importo que hubiera una niña menor con migo su actitud era pésima como si le estuviera pidiendo comida gratis  he estado en otros restaurantes y  los meseros son muy atentos y la verdad no recomendaría este lugar por que  la cajera y los meseros tratan mal a la gente piensan que estamos alli por comida gratis. si no tienes otra opción de otro restaurante  y vas en este La Guanajuato te recomiendo que si te dan una mesera llamada  TERESA sal corriendo perderás tu dinero.

Urban Brew and BBQ is an outstanding place to go to get your fix for some seriously delicious smoked meats and a wide selection of brews. Wife and I had a truly delicious meal on our first visit here.

The place is in the somewhat up and coming Grand Central district of St Pete, which mean that if you are not a hipster you might make a note that you will be invading their domain when you visit the area. As long as you have a beard or perhaps some strategically displayed tattoos you should be fine.

All kidding aside, the inside of the restaurant is cozy, basically a bar with around 10 seats and then a few high two tops along the wall... It is small and intimate, and smells absolutely terrific. Out back they have a series of picnic tables accessible through the restaurant. 

We tried a number of items, including the ribs and chicken, as well as some delicious sides consisting of Brussels sprouts and mac and cheese. Everything was truly great. The rub on the meats almost made the finishing sauce unnecessary, and the sides were very well seasoned and had a great smoky flavor. The sprouts in particular are amazing, although some may find them a bit over-seasoned. 

My only negative was although the place is small, only one person was working as server/bartender/phone answerer etc. She was fast and thorough but clearly a little frazzled as the restaurant filled up. This may have been an atypical problem, perhaps someone didn't show up to work, but she was noticeably a little stressed. 

All in all I am looking forward to a return trip not far in the future. This is an outstanding, non-chain establishment serving up excellent BBQ.

Probably the worst experience I have had at a restaurant in Boise. Went with a group of friends and it was 25 minutes of waiting and still did not receive our first round of drinks. They were out of multiple items. There was blasting rap music distorting the speakers. And when we went inside to look for a server they were on FaceTime making plans for the night.  So we left and spent our $300 elsewhere. We won't ever be back.

So I heard good things (piled high with succulent meat and huge portions) about this sandwich shop right down the block from the St. Luis convention center.  I'm always down for a nice sandwich in a new city! Oh boy, was I disappointed.  The only plus about the sandwich was that it was huge. I got the Smoked Brisket with BBQ and Swiss in the "pro" size.  Sounded awesome but frankly the Brisket was dry and kinda bland.  I wasn't a fan of the sauce either, which was flavorless.  I walked in right around closing time so maybe that was a factor.  I'm willing to give this place another try but maybe stick to the roast beef or chicken.

Let me start off by saying I came in here with a recommendation from someone for a shampoo Bar. I have my kids with me so I plan on being in and out. I entered and I was greeted and went about my way but went back up to saleswoman and asked for some assistance. Cassidy took me over to the area of what I was looking for and I told her a little bit about my situation of my hair I asked her what she recommended before telling her what was recommended to me and she explained the differences thoroughly she was patient and helpful and suggested honestly. After I chose what I wanted she recommended a sample of something that I can try and I really appreciated that as I did not want to just try something and absolutely not like it  
I went to pay and she made up my samples and on the way out The kid spotted a fun area of play fun which I never knew about its use for bathtime but can also use dry so we spent 10 minutes at the table playing with it and Taylor came over to introduce us to the product and was so kind as she listen to me talk about my experience with Cassidy and then I asked her about some skin care suggestions for my next time I visit at that point she went over and made me some samples and told me to try them so I know what I'm looking for for next time .The Energy both Cassidy and Taylor put into making sure that I had something that would fit my needs  and I never looked forward to taking a shower as much as I did tha lt night. I used my shampoo bar and my hair feels literally squeaky clean I haven't tried any sample products yet but either way my experience at the store and they're honest opinions and the time they took would make me go back I'm so glad I chose to go in there rather than order online thank you for giving me a great experience

I found this spa during a yelp search, and read all reviews and immediately  thought this place was worth it. I called ahead as I wanted to surprise my parents with a Valentine's Day couples massage. I asked for hot stones and then asked the difference between hot stones and was told Himalayan was better. As this sounded fancy I immediately chose this one. Again this was a gift so I wanted the best. The day of the appointment arrives and my parents are mid check-in when the receptionist walks outside to yell at a bystander for peeing on their sign. Studio has some nice wall decorations but cleanliness was below average. Now it could have been a bad day and my parents are still optimistic. Once they go in the back my Mother had the woman massage therapist who gave her a massage above the blanket the whole time. My mother explained that it felt like someone who was a novice practicing on her. Massage lasted not a whole hour about 30-45 minutes. Imagine 30-45 minutes of blanket rubs. As my mother calls I'm excited to wait on her review and ask about the fancy salts when she shares there was no salt, or stones or anything of that matter. At this point I'm extremely upset and I contact the shop and a really unprofessional woman answers the phone and LAUGHS the whole time and then states " we did not look at the schedule, we did Swedish instead". I'm furious due to the lack of unprofessionalism and for ruining my special gift. I made the woman on the phone aware of my review.
Sushi was really good and great for the price. One of the better places in philly for sushi delivery. If your hesitant you should do it.

Why has no one opened competition for them yet? Their snowballs aren't that great, prices are too high, and the service is atrocious. The line isn't long because it's good-the line is long because they are slow as hell and there aren't any other snowball stands in the neighborhood.  And someone is going to get killed on the corner of Carrollton and dumaine because their line is so out of control.

They are terrible with scheduling. Out of 5 appointment they canceled 3 of them. When I came in for my 3rd appointment the gentleman at the deck informed me that I was being charged a $50 fee for being a no show. When I explained to him that the appointment was rescheduled he informed me that he has faith in his employees and he will believe them over me. I asked him what my options were and he replied we don't give refunds for treatments not used.

After speaking with 3 others they had the same problem. One of which was charged $100 for appointments they rescheduled. THEY ARE A SCAM. The laser hair removal is 6 treatments long, that you are charged up front for. Once you pay for the package they will reschedule you than charge you for the rescheduling and if you don't pay the fee they will not reimburse you for the treatment you didn't receive. 

The 2 treatments I did received were great. If you can afford to pay $50 every time they reschedule you go for it.

This was really nothing special at all--and not worth the 2 hour wait for delivery.

Since this was my first time ordering from Gianfranco's I decided to go with just a plain pie.  Like I said, it took about 2 hours to get here, so obviously the pie was downright cold by the time it got here.  The pizza itself was very chewy and somewhat soggy.  The sauce had that out of the jar type taste, and the cheese consistency just felt, well...really off.  It wasn't just because it was cold.  I feel like if the pizza had been hot, it would have been very oily to the point where the cheese would slide right off.

I won't be ordering again.  I'll stick with Slice and Robert Chiarella's.

Yummy large serving of a chicken sandwich. Chicken is well cooked and crunchy. I like the choices of different chicken dressings. The bread for sandwich is alright, not the most fresh. I love the music themed decor of the diner. Breakfast, lunch, dinner is served all day.

I had a cheese omelet here for breakfast a few weeks ago.  Well cooked egg with kinda cheapish cheese.  Home fries were very good toast was fine, coffee was weak  but I like strong coffee...

Service was fine, price was just under 10 bucks and a 2 buck tip.

Nice homey place, not outstanding food but it's fine.

Did not act like Masters in this trade.  After reading the reviews I had high expectations.  I brought a bridesmaid dress in that needed alterations and the tailor immediately said no that he prefers to do menswear.  I then asked him to shorten a shirt dress from h&m (very simple) and he once again insisted on only men's clothing.  He sent me away to take my business elsewhere.

I had Scott make my website. He is a scientist when it comes to being number one on the search engines. Without that, you can offer free money, and if people can't find you, it doesn't even matter. He came up with a great campaign for me, and it has been uphill from there.

C. R. E. A. M.
(Chinese Rules Everything Around Me)
(Chinese food, that is)

Red's is that FIRE.
Straight up.
Unapologetically aggressive.
Unwaveringly unique. 
Unabashedly bold. 

Constantly impressed by the culinary technique and style.
Continuously blurring the lines between East, West and NOLA. 

So when you see that red square lit up on St Claude.
Know it's all over.
Tobias will have you seeing pink hearts, yellow moons, orange stars and green clovers.

Save yourself the trip and go to crimson crab. Sauce is runny and little flavor. Prices were high for what you get. Service was awful. Tripped ahead of time at the counter and no one spoke to us once. I definitely won't be back.

Very, very, very dirty.  I could've said very a couple more times, but it would've become repetitive.  Watched a homeless man open the deodorant, clean himself with it, then put it back on the shelf.  This Rite-Aid may only be good for their ice cream and closed foods/drinks.

This is definitely one of the better hooter's locations I've been to. Love that it's on the water and the outdoor seating. Our server was Jessica and she was absolutely fantastic. We shared some delicious fried pickles to start. She kept my parents' beers topped off and our waters full. We had original breaded and naked wings which were delicious as well. Overall the food was great and the service was even better!

Here's a typical car dealership where the sales team's goal is to make as much money off customers as possible. I bought a "certified preowned" vehicle from here... Before I could even cross the 30,000 mile mark with my new car, I had to take it back to them 3-4 times...

The main issues were pretty serious ranging from the car shutting down during a drive and smoking through the vents to the whole engine actually shaking when it was revved. The car also had 2 recalls in the one year that I owned it.. Now all my repairs here were covered by warranty and the service department was good with all repairs and transactions.. The service department was able to work with me and around my schedule.. 

HOWEVER, this review is a two star because of the SALES team.. They were unprofessional with me from the start.. Very pushy, I should have listened to my instincts turned away after my first interaction with one of their salesmen when I first came for a test drive.. His tone and demeanor completely changed when I said I simply wanted to drive the vehicle because I've never driven a fusion before.. He actually took a smoke break while I waited at his desk.. A week later, because I was in a rush to buy a new car l, I came back and was able to negotiate with another salesman who was did not budge on the price, the sticker price was decent and I bought the "certified preowned"... After a year of use I realized there were so many issues with the car and there was no way it would survive long term.. I brought it back asking for a deal on any of their other vehicles because the first one they sold me could possible be a lemon in a few years... The sales team was still trying to make a major profit off me and did not negotiate on any of the prices.. Note: I was planning on keeping my first car long term but all the repairs in one year of ownership were big red flags for long term ownership.. I could have written a letter to the manufacturer but I decided yelpers need to hear about this..

Long story short...If this is the "highest rated ford dealership in the area".. I WILL NEVER BUY A FORD AGAIN mainly because of the dealer's inability to work with their existing customers to make sure they are happy with their vehicle... . 
I now own a BMW, and I honestly still think the Ford Fusion titanium is an excellent vehicle but don't buy one from these guys..

Food was ok, drinks were not good and I had a margarita and had little to no alchohol in the drink and when asked about it they wanted to charge me for an extra shot. Good view is about all this place has going for it. Less than impressed.

Super charming; this is a gem that's worth revisiting. Pastries are homemade and top-notch. The owners are wonderfully attentive. Your coffee cup will never empty. The interior is petite and European. Prices are far less expensive than a plane ticket to France.

Decided to eat here on a whim. We rushed to stand in line on a Saturday evening. It was a relatively long line as we waited within the last 20min of the Reading Terminal closing. I got the cheesesteak with sweet peppers, mushrooms, fried onions, and american cheese. The cashier wasn't too friendly. It's CASH ONLY, fyi. The total was $11.45. I thought the cheesesteak was good but not worth the price. Not sure why it's that pricey. It may be because it's in the reading terminal. Probably will not return again because there are other good places to eat cheesesteak in Philly at a better rate.

I literally stood in line for 10 plus minutes waiting to fax  some papers. There were 2 other associates standing around talking the entire time doing absolutely nothing. The woman helping the customers in front of me were talking and talking and talking about personal things while myself and 2 other people were standing in line. The other guy talking walks up and joins there conversation asking about the customer's last name because it sounded familiar with someone in his family. That went on and on. The fact that I had enough time to take a picture of the customer experience email address and phone number is completely bad service. I'm pregnant and had to use the restroom as well. Your associates need to learn how to work while at work and multi task. Never once was myself or anyone else greeted or acknowledged for standing in line 10 mins while your associates held on their personal conversations. Complete horrible experience. The one associate that was actually working and not running his mouth was Rasheed B. 

Store: 2308 West End Ave Nashville, Tn 37203 
         06/01/2019 @ approximately 11:21 am
By far the best pizza place in the world. Nothing can top this restaurant and you all need to go to this restaurant, and this restaurant only. Believe me. This place Fabrica needs an award.

If you crave a Cake donut with Chocolate Icing, then this is the place for you! A few years back a coworker brought donuts to work from here and I was hooked on the cake one. It's not too dense, moist, not too sweet and a hint of butter in the mix. Balanced chocolate flavor, not too rich.  I'll say, I can't co-sign for their other donuts. I only get the cake.

Poor service! I asked for a 6 inch meatball marinara sub. The two guys helping me with the sandwich were asking all the appropriate questions, i.e. Vegetables? What kind of cheese? Toasted? All went ok until it was time to check out. I asked the lady if there were any deals for ordering a drink and a sub. She ignored me the first two times and than walked away from me to the soda machine. I asked again Are there any deals? She finally answered me but was a little ways away so I could not hear her. She couldn't hear me because I finally decided to get a soda anyways along with my sub (deal or no deal), I had to repeat my soda order again. It seems even with the difficulty hearing each other, she absolutely refused to take one step closer to me. And I was leaning as far as I could over the counter. They were not that busy. It seemed that I was the only customer that lunch hour on Oct 25th 2018. I thought that they could be more appreciative of the customers that you do get. Why o' why work in a customer service related job when you don't like providing a service to people. I would not order from THIS Subway ever again!

If I could give no stars to this place I would. It is filthy, falling apart and just down right gross. The employee who checked us in had no idea what he was doing. The two bedroom "suites" have no air conditioning in one of the rooms, barely working in the other. We were told that is how all of there suites are set up. Miserable is the only way I can describe our stay.
Great Place! Make A Reservation Before Hand! The wait can get up to 2 hours! Luckily we Made One!

Last mouth eat here when I traveled here. Really terrible place to eat and stay. If could give zero star, I will give zero. The short-haired waitress's attitude is like eating . I don't want to give any tips to her, but how crazy, she not allow me to leave until I leave the tips on the table. Never come here again. Ps: different attitude to different people.

I love that they have chilled beer glasses.  The service is always great. Can't stand the techno music.  It's not good for eating.  Makes me feel nervous.  Are we on the set of Lost in Translation. The menu is super gimmicky.  When the hipsters start to have to watch what they eat, those beef fat fries won't seem so cool.

Are there negative stars?  Although the docs and nursing staff are great, don't stay there.  You will be better off in a third world country.  Heat did NOT Work at night-  the nurses passed out those heat packs that you squeeze to generate heat.  For 4 nights, I froze.  I wore a nightgown, big fluffy robe, socks, 3 blankets and fur-lined gloves I had a family member bring from home!  And I was still cold.  The nurses told me that the room next to me also had no heat-  what was the purpose of that?  To make me feel better?????
