One night I thought I would take a hot shower to warm up-  and the shower did not work!!!!!!!  Never once was I offered an apology for the conditions.   I think a State Inspection is well overdue-  which they would fail.

I've been to Dr. BBQ on three occasions. The food is always pretty good. On the last occasion I visited it was Father's Day and we took our family out for dinner. After waiting much too long for a server to greet us, we were informed that they were out of ribs, beef ribs, and pastrami. I eat barbecue fairly infrequently, but when I do go I want ribs so that was disappointing.
The music was turned up way too loud and not good.  We could barely hear ourselves speak.
The restaurant was not very busy but our server appeared over extended. The service was sporadic and she actually gave us a check before clearing our table our asking us if we wanted anything else.  Feels like management might be slipping a bit. 
Also there is now a charge to self park.  We arrived in a drizzling rain and walked up to the restaurant. The valet attendants were nice and informed us that we either had to bring back the car to be valet parked or walk all the way to the other end of the block and pay for self parking. We did not want to valet so we had to walk in the rain to pay for parking. At a minimum they should move the self parking pay station closer to the restaurant. I understand it might not be the restaurant's parking lot, but they need to figure it out.

Great place to eat breakfast on a Sunday morning in SB, and easy to find.  About a 15 minute wait, but they have self serve coffee out front.  We ended up getting seated way in the back by the bathroom, at that point we might as well have been at IHOP.  It was so loud inside.  If its a beautiful day, I would recommend waiting for an outside spot, because without the beach view, the price premium is not worth it.  I overheard a local say that there are much better places to eat in SB, but they tolerate Sambo's when their friends visit.  The coffee was good, and I had the California Benedict.  It really was delicious, with fresh hollandaise.  It was pricey, about $13, but something I have not seen anywhere else, I would go back for it.  My wife was disappointed with her French Toast, and the syrup was watered down.  Home potatoes with the green onion was nice.  Service was prompt once we sat down, and the mini muffin appetizer was a great way to keep us happy while waiting for the food.  The water had a horrible aftertaste.
Food was ok but ambiance is perfect for the beach! Great spot overall!!! Definitely recommend

Went in to Pinewood last Saturday for the first time and had a really awesome time! Danen was our server on the patio and he really went above and beyond to make sure we had a great time. He  took the time to explain the menu to us and the outdoor games. After we had had guacamole and some drinks outside he also took the time to get us a table inside and check on us as well as the server we had inside. He really made the experience a lot better and makes us want to come back

I ordered a burger and fries were ice old , no condiments. I asked for new fries and the condiments I ordered, waiting some more. Mind you, this place isn't packed at all. THEN, my dad orders a water and there's a lemon stuck in his stray. The server, manager Christa & Scott were so cold and their story of the lemons being next to the straws was nonsensical! I could feel the sarcasm behind their "apology". There's many dining establishments at the French Quarter, don't go here.

I've been to Calhoun's twice now, but I can't really think of anything that stands out for me.

The food is fine, and I am rather fond of the way they make their Coleslaw.

The Prime Rib is just average. It comes with Mashed Potatoes, and its extra for a Baked Potato.

For the price point its worth it, but if I really want Prime Rib I don't feel myself drawn here.  I haven't had the Ribs so maybe I'm missing the main reason to come here.

I went here recently - The salsa was A-OK - I have had better.  They forgot about us at the table and forgot our food... luckily it was nice outside and they helped with our bill.  They ended up putting our tacos in a doggy bag because we were there for so long waiting.

The only reason I give them 3 stars is because they accidentally gave me a fish taco...BUT I LOVED IT!

Nothin' fancy, fer sure. But, it's a place to meet with friends, have a nice, cold beer, maybe some wings and enjoy the boats that cruise along the river. 

Service was quick tonight, even though it was very busy. My beer was cold. I was relaxed with friends.

Mission accomplished.

I came here tonight with my best friend and we had a great girls night out! The happy hour menu is amazing here. We each had a drink and we split 3 of the happy hour starters and it was plenty for our meal! We split the bill and all that, with tax and tip was only $16! It was a steal!

I had their draft cider, which was Angry Orchard cider and on happy hour for $4. I love Angry Orchard and it was great on a Fall night! We split the Mac and Cheese bites, margherita flatbread, and chicken tacos. I loved the Mac and Cheese bites! The sauce they came with was a little spicy and so addicting! We ended up pouring some of it on our chicken tacos because we couldn't get enough of it! The chicken tacos were great as well. I thought the margherita flatbread was just ok. The other two were definitely better and next time, I would probably try something other than the flatbread.

Overall, I definitely recommend this restaurant for happy hour! Their deals are amazing and the food and drinks are great as well.
Really enjoyed happy hour. Specially the sweet potatoes fries and the mini rib burger. Oh and the sangria was the best, after four of them I guess I liked them!! It's fun, nice and clean. So go!

Went to see if they could install a brake light.  They gladly took my car in and realized they didn't have the bulb in stock.  They said the parts truck would be here in 20 minutes and they would install it and provide the bulb for free.  I told them I could go buy the bulb and they insisted on me waiting and they would rather me drive safely then spend 20 minutes going to find a bulb.  Can't ask for better customer service than that.  Thank you!

Upstairs bar was cool. Good service. Good prices.

Downstairs looked so fancy we were afraid to attend in our jeans & t-shirt ensembles. After receiving our meals, we realized we were overdressed. Should have dressed like raccoons to match our garbage meals. Also drink prices surged. Just wanted a good piece of chicken. Not here. 2 stars are for upstairs bar & for warm bread.

So I have gone into this dealership a few times and have not been impressed, today my husband and I said ok let's go in with an open mind and give them a chance. So we made the 30 min drive over. Firstly for a high end dealership there showroom floor is rather interesting they have a desk located toward the back of the floor so you are not sure if that is for service or a reception desk for the sale department either way it made no difference since they NEVER, EVER look up to welcome you or ask if you need help. We walked around for a few mins before a salesmen came over to speak to us. I explained that I am in the process of having my current Benz totaled and I will need a new car. Generally we work with the MB of Princeton but due to a few problems with the service department I said let try and work with this dealer instead. 
Off the bat our salesmen was nice but not really helpful, we walked over to his desk which I have to say the seating arrangement was set-up more like a low budget dealer rather than a high end brand. There was only one seat in the cube so poor hubby was standing for a while before our salesmen even noticed he need a seat. All that aside let's get down to business, I explained to him what I wanted as this will be our 4th car with the brand so I have nailed down the must haves; he explained that they did not have it in stock and we discussed what was in stock and how long it would take if you had to have it custom built. The answer was 3 months to customize your car. Fine I get that but I also know that there are a few upgrades that can be added to cars at the beginning of the manufacturing process I have done this before. The green, yellow and red coding on the vehicle list will determine if I could change things. So he say hmm no there is no new productions lined up till Feb of 2019 (right). 
So we asked him to show us what he had in stock, he walks away leaving us hanging at this point I was not sure what was going on. He pulls the car up, does not offer a test drive walks us over and walks away to chat with a friend of his. I looked over the car and said to him I am not feeling this one. He says ok and walks away, my husband and I was left standing here with our jaws hitting the ground he walked over to speak to his friend and I walked over said good bye and ran. It's sad to say that in my option the salesmen felt like an order taker at a fast food restaurant.

Stumbled into Cunetto today as I was exploring The Hill neighborhood. 

Pros: 
Amazingly soft fresh bread from Vitale's Bakery. 
Delightful Italian server who was brimming with recommendations. 
The lunch portions are HUGE. I saved half for dinner. 
The eggplant spagetti is a flavorful treat with a hearty helping of sauce.  


Cons: 
Sadly, there are not many vegetarian lunch options. 
Salads are served with iceberg lettuce.  Meh. 
Trys to be upscale but doesn't really succeed. 

Since there are so many other places to explore on The Hill, I don't know that I'll be back any time soon.
had the hush puppies here  they were great golden on the outside just right on the inside  it was a great treat

Great food great service!! Food comes fast.  Wings are incredible.  Meatballs are great. Pizza isn't my style but still everyone i was with enjoyed it. 
Overall fantastic place with fantastic value.

Came here for lunch today. I had a chicken salad sandwich with side salad -both were very good.  The service was nice and prompt. I would definitely recommend this place for lunch if you are downtown for work or leisure.

Pretty standard Americanized Chinese take out.  I tried a dish that I haven't had before: Pork with eggplant.  It was good, I might order it again, but it could use a bit more heat to it.  

Wonton soup was fine, not the best I've had, there weren't shreds of pork in the broth and very few scallions.  

Service was quick enough for pick up.  

All in all, "meh, I've experienced better."  It's not something that I would seek out, but it's fine for what it is.  I don't think that it deserves all the praise that it is receiving here, it seems like the high reviews are just because it is close to people's houses.

Brandon our bartender was very attentive. Introduced himself when we walked in. Loved the music on Monday, May 14. Not sure who the band was but they were phenomenal. Everything from Merle Haggard to a Metallica. Best classic rock music on Broadway!  We had already eaten but the pizza smelled so good, we decided to order late night pizza. Pepperoni and  half chicken and pineapple.  Absolutely fantastic!
Pretty good Thai food! I'm impressed! Ordered pad Thai & soup. So many mediocre restaurants out there tbh. Great vibe restaurant also love the location!

I like Chase bank.
Clean and clear to the point.
No easy way to earn interest.
I do enjoy their credit cards. Their online banking is excellent.

They are great, very affordable,  honest,  and have been very accommodating.  They have no problem coming back out to do the inside if your not available on their scheduled day, but they still come out and spray the outside. Definitely recommend over the big name guys

This review pertains to the "Corn Creepers" Haunted Maze only and not the farm as a whole. We only had tickets for this and did not have time to do the other attractions such as the regular corn maze, "Zombie Paintball", and the pumpkin patch.

We pre paid for our tickets online and I highly recommend that you do as well. The line just to buy tickets was insanely long. Also keep in mind that parking is not only free, but well organized as well.

We made our way to the line for "Corn Creepers" and got our tickets transferred from digital to hard copy (that's something you need to do BEFORE getting in line). The line probably took around 30 minutes to get through (a "skip the line" upgrade would've been great). 

The maze itself was ok. It was a lot longer than I thought it would be so that's a plus. However, they didn't do a great job at spacing everybody out. There was a bottleneck of people throughout the whole thing and due to this, my party saw most of the "scares" coming a mile away. They did manage to get me a couple times, though. 

There was a solid effort on their part and the scare actors were definitely into their roles. I think with some additions and tweaks, this could be an excellent haunt for the Reno area. Space parties out better, create better hiding spots for the actors, etc. We've already decided that we'll be back next year, so hopefully there will be some improvements done.

At least they were open on New Years Day!  It was cold and miserable and its' surprising that so many places are closed on New Years Day with all the tourists in town.  So at least I'm thankful that Gumbo Shop is open. 

Service was fast and OK.

We had the Blackened Catfish and Crab Cake (special, not on menu) for appetizer and Catfish St Peter and BBQ Shrimp Pasta (both special) for entree.  The two fish dishes were both excellent while the BBQ Shrimp Pasta had a certain flavor from the spices that we did not enjoy.  The crab cake in both the appetizer and Catfish St Peter fell flat.  Although to be honest I have never had good crab cake, ever.  I think I just don't agree with the flavor and much rather just have crabs.  The pasta is also unacceptable as it seems it was over boiled and fell into tiny pieces.

3-stars for being open for business and the fish dishes.

I am not one to posted on social media every time I have a poor experience with a company or a service l...but this has really ticked me off!
Prior it Christmas I purchased a Walmart gift card at the Kirkwood Walmart for a gift to my parents. My Dad called me this weekend and told me that when he went to check out at his local Walmart the gift card was not activated. He had to explain that the card was a gift and even then the store told him at first they wanted to take the card from him. He eventually was able to keep the card and the receipt showing the error message that the card was not activated.
Since this, I have searched for my original receipt but have not been able to locate it. I called the Kirkwood Walmart and was told there was no way I could have obtained a store gift card without purchasing it. After explaining several times the entire story I was told that Walmart can not look up receipts with credit card numbers and they do not review video camera for items under $250.
Today I called the Walmart nationwide customer service line. I was transferred twice and eventually received an automated response stating the hold time was 32 mins. I then hung up and called the Kirkwood Walmart again. After being transferred 4 times and being on hold 10 mins I spoke with a manager and was advised that they could in fact look up sales receipts with a credit card number as long as you have the exact date and time of your visit and the exact dollar amount. The manager then stated I would need to speak to customer service and transferred me to that department.
Now after speaking with 2 managers and 3 sales reps I finally thought I would get to the bottom of this. While speaking to the customer service department at the Kirkwood Walmart I heard a voice in the background say $50 was not worth the time and effort that it takes to look up receipts and then I was hung up on!!

I just moved back to the area after being gone for 4+ years and during my time away, finding a good nail shop was always hit or miss and even the hits sent you home with a miss now and then. I hadn't had a Mani/pedi since late August because I've been so turned off and skeptical of new shops. Well my nails looked extra WRONG so I decided to give Red Cat a go and I have NO regrets. 

Everyone was very nice and inviting. I like my gel manicure and my pedi and the girl who did them, Elisa (I believe), was amazing! She wasn't too extra and not too mute. I'm so satisfied! Man did I miss the east coast!!!

I'll definitely be back as a regular and I definitely recommend this place!

Update: although [I think] I will still recommend this place, I had to take away a star. I got my nails done on Saturday and they started chipping Monday, just like a lot of other reviewers mentioned. I've been getting gel manis for as long as I can remember and my nails NEVER chip. If they do, it's literally at least 2 weeks in when they need redone anyway. This is very disappointing. Another thing to note is that I went in for a gel manicure but my hands didn't take a single dip in any ounce of water & my cuticles are looking low key trashy. I'm annoyed. I'll go back once again and will deduct another star if this shit happens again.

Sadly, the prices have eclipsed the quality of the food. It's been a few months since we last visited, and this was always a good local option. On our visit today, I was surprised at the prices. Additionally, the servings are smaller. I'm afraid it just doesn't make sense, economically or gastronomically, anymore for me.

The service is usually pretty slow checking out, but the staff has been pretty knowledgable whenever I have asked a question.  They will run free battery diagnostics and if you purchase a battery from them, they will install it for free.  

Pro tip: If you have a smart phone, you can look on their website while in the store, sometimes the website has better deals.  I was able buy a battery on my phone while standing in the store so that I could use a 30% off internet coupon, it was "delivered" to the store within five minutes and they intalled it right there.

This place is cool, the tofu/vegetarian sliders are good, the egg biscuits are amazing, but the donuts are just...average. They look amazing but don't even taste good. The dough isn't sweet and tastes like bread, the only sweetness comes from the icing. Not sure if that's how they are supposed to be, but I'm not a big fan.staff is nice though!

We stopped in here for a drink after Sunday brunch.  I've been here a million times, but that was years ago.  Nothing has changed here...a bunch of South Tampa bros hitting on the 3 bartenders working there.  We did a one and done because it definitely wasn't our scene.
Be careful with this lender and Jason Hunter.   They promise big lender credits which disappear at closing.

Great Balls...I dont like to give out 1 star reviews but this place was pretty awful, almost everything we had was nasty. The only thing they didnt make totally awful was some broccoli in general tsaos sauce so maybe thats ok here. The fried rice also was manageable but its tough to mess up.

The soup was a watery oily mess, the mongolian beef was tough and stringy and the lo mein had no seasoning. 

I also wasnt feeling too good after I ate all this after a bad decision to get lunch here post hangover. Stick to the other chinese food place thats in close vicinity on canal street.

Came through Chinatown Philly and since this seems to be a gathering of all Asian cuisines we thought we would give the ramen a try. Highly rated in Yelp.... BUT we had to look carefully because highly rated in PA doesn't mean anything for New Yorkers. Lol.... Sorry PA people... Just a fact and you know it.

Anyways... We came in for a quick bowl of ramen. In Japan this is typical street food and people go in and out like a bank. We ordered the Mayu and Shoyu bowls. The Mayu ramen came with thin noodles and the Shoyu ramen came with thicker more traditional ramen noodles. Both had aldente noodles, but the Mayu had a much richer flavor due to the garlic oil. So depending if you are feeling like a clear broth or a thicker broth both are quite good.

Very efficiently run establishment. Waitresses are loud but it's for efficiency. Even though the restaurant was full things came out quickly and we were off to the next stop.. nice job!

It's a great concept, but it's membership based, and the more members the less availability.  I thought it was going to be a fun and different way to meet people and experience interesting food but that has not been the case. 

How it works is that you pay an initial membership fee, and that fee allows you to pay for "events" that are typically a few weeks later.  Unfortunately, the "events" sell out within a few minutes after they are posted (on Wednesdays), so not only do you need to know your schedule a few weeks out, but you need to act quickly...within minutes sometimes...or the event will be sold out.  I've tried to book two hours after an event was posted and it was already sold out!

I expressed my concerns with the people running the events, but there was little they could do.  They offered to get me a reservation for the event I wanted, but in the end that would only solve my issue once, and not provide relief for everyone else that I am sure are experiencing the same issue.  They seem to be growing too fast and don't have the capacity to offer reservations for all their members.  

I thought it was going to be a fun and relaxing way to experience food, but so far it has not been...just one more thing I need to stress about.  I won't renew my membership.  There are other dinner events that are available in the city and I think I will give those a shot.  If you know of any give good ones give me a holler.

Might be a good spot if you're already drunk, but don't start your night out here. The food is just bar food and the pirogies we had were cold in the center. Food came out relatively quick though. Young and loud crowd.

This place has gone downhill. Started as a good place, buffet/a la carte, but of late, with this new management and the experience we had recently with one of the staff ( if you want to be rude...dont work in the hospitality industry).  We have been going here for over a decade....not anymore
Went to the farmer's market  for some fresh local produce -- NOT.  Might have well just gone to Megamart & saved the hour long drive.
A bit over rated and pretentious.  The food was good, rich and tasted somewhat processed (in an okay way).  Glad we went but not sure I would like to return often.

Had a most unpleasant experience...have been going to Harry's for over 20 years off and on...service not friendly, wine list is non-existent, food not good.  Last time had a tough steak, this time a tuna sandwich.....they go on their drinks, not food.  Amazed that they have such a loyal clientele.....used to be good old SB place, but no more.

My boyfriend have been here several times before and the service was usually decent. We decided to visit again for Halloween with our friend, where we ordered several drinks, and my boyfriend started a tab under my name. When it was time to collect my card, a bartender donning a zipper mask asked my boyfriend extremely rudely what the card name was under, then yelled at us, then overcharged us my drink (it was 7 but she charged us 10). Obviously, she didn't get a tip, and we were both disgusted that we were abused by someone whose job is to give us respectable service.

My mother, who is disabled and uses a service dog, was turned away from this location after being asked illegal questions about her dog (asked for "paperwork," which places of business cannot require and indeed does not exist in the U.S. for service animals). After being informed politely of the ADA law regarding questions that employees may ask to handlers of service animals in public locations, the employee then forbade my mother to make an appointment with the doctor on the grounds that "she is allergic to dogs." Now, first of all, how would she know that? But let's say she does. Why wouldn't she mention it when my mother walked in, instead of requiring phony "paperwork?" It seems retaliatory and is definitely illegal. Why does this location hate people with disabilities?

Mixed feelings about this place which is very disappointing as I just moved in a few blocks away and was hoping this would be my "regular" bar.  Poor customer service last night particularly at the bar, as we were denied any food from the bar menu because of the time we came in (9pm). I explained that I was interested in the bar menu because there were more vegetarian options and the bartender without compassion pointed out the whopping 3 items on other dinner menu that I could choose from (one had an egg in it, so really only 2 options for some vegs). Not vegetarian friendly at all.  I had checked the website in advance and it is not clear what time the bar menu is available. It's a shame because the drinks were good but I will not be back.

The food was really amazing but even more then that was the owner coming out and chatting with us about fun things to do with our daughters. Italian is my favorite and I was so happy to find this place!!! Apparently Florida doesn't have water Haha

Small but really well done--a relaxing and beautiful space for families and pretty much anyone!!

Restrooms are pristine, wildlife (bunnies, dragonflies, hummingbirds, etc) abound, and sculptures pique interest

If the actual service could match the food then it would be a 10/10 but sadly our experience was poor. 

As soon as we walked in we kind of just stood there because there was no host stand or host! So it felt odd just kinda standing in the middle of the walkway waiting for someone to attend us. When they finally ask us how many we are and if we had a reservation, we said no. They kinda looked around and made it seem like there wasn't enough tables, when funny enough there was only 3 tables seated outside and only one inside..

Anyways we get seated and about 5 minutes later the waitress finally comes and just offers water, doesn't ask to take a drink order just asked if still, sparkling or house water would be fine and walked away. Ok? When she finally returns to take our drink order I tell her that we are also ready to order our food, she walks away again this time not saying anything? I thought made she just needed to grab something but no she walks away and starts filling up water jugs and even goes outside. She returns about 5 minutes later and acts like nothing. I understand how it is to work in a restaurant, I've been a hostess, food expo, and a server. When I see the place is busy or people are flagging the server down, I get it! But when you keep walking away as we are trying to order that I don't understand. 

We get our food and before any of us even touch it she comes to ask how's it tasting. I let her know it literally just touched the table and we can use some ketchup. Also my boyfriends fork was dirty and covered with chocolate we had to ask for some different silverware. She never came back to ask how everything was tasting once we actually started eating. Never came back to ask if any of us wanted another Bellini or a different type of drink. Me as a server I would be trying to sell you as many drinks as possible or anything else to make that bill go up because I know that will most likely mean a bigger tip. I guess nowadays people assume just because they are waiting on someone the service doesn't matter and they will still be getting a tip. Sorry but that's not how a view it. You work for what you want and that is also a damn good tip! 

I will be back just because the food was so good. But maybe next time I can add a few stars, that is of course if the service gets any better.

So my friends and I thought we were going for brunch as their website lists their brunch menu. We each had our dishes picked out before we even walked in the door (we love brunch). However, we were advised that they stopped serving brunch in 2019.  Yet, it's 2021 and it's STILL on their website. Ok.  So we ordered from the dinner menu as that's what they serve at 3.  Shrimp cocktail was good, cheesesteak eggrolls were just ok.  They don't compare to Double Eagle's cheesesteak dumplings at all. There was a sauce on top which was the only flavorful component of the dish. The tomato and burrata salad was fresh, bright and delicious. The catch of the day branzino was very fishy in taste, and the skin was not crispy, which is the thing that makes fish skin palatable for me, so I had to send it back.  The salmon was completely bland. The sirloin was not cooked to the requested temperature, and like the eggrolls had no flavor on its own. The truffle butter on top made it edible. We ordered a round of VIP's but they were not as promised. When they were brought to the table the color was pale so we knew they wouldn't taste like Del's D.E. which they didn't.  The truffle mac n cheese was absolutely delicious and was probably the star of our lunchtime dinner. Nice & creamy with a truffle essence and was seasoned just right.  

Our server Kayla was knowledgeable and attentive. However, it was not enough for me to ever return. Unless maybe if they bring brunch back.  Afterward, we walked over to Double Eagle and had a couple rounds of true VIP's (yellow in color, and potent pineapple flavor) which were delicious as usual.  I will definitely stick with the original restaurant for sure.

This spot blew my mind, for brunch! I got the skillet with a side of avocado and eggs over easy! Gluten free  just the way I like it! You could tell the ingredients were so fresh and delicious. I would definitely head back there!

I was surprised how good it was.Didn't look so impressive on the outside but the price was cheap and the food was yummy.I liked how they didn't salt heavily on everything.I am so glad we found a local breakfast place we can go to on the weekends finally.

I'm kind of the odd man out for this longest running deli in Reno. In another review, I've stated I'm not big on sandwiches, but a place should be able to do a plain-er sandwich excellently. I've had handfuls of visits over the years and nothing has ever given me a "wow" impression. My last visit, enforced my opinion of this place as an A-OK deli. This visit, I had a basic, plain turkey and provolone sandwich, granted they were out of the Dutch crunch bread, but it was just so plain and bland... plain French roll, with plain mayo and mustard. I honestly make a better sandwich with more flavor, on open face grain bread at home. The few salads I have had are okay as well. Iceberg lettuce and toppings, while larger, nothing special for me. The soups are probably the best thing here (namely a Manhattan clam chowder), but they have a rotational schedule of the same few soups (lobster bisque, chicken noodle come to mind). 

I know others rave, but this just isn't my go-to lunch spot.
Not bad for 13th street fare. Have to say that the byo tequila margaritas top the meal which was a bit too salty. Something to consider in the future.

Had a couple of hours to kill during dinnertime and asked someone where they would recommend.  I'm SO glad they told us about Ralph's!  Cozy interior, reminded me of my Grandmothers kitchen wallpaper. The food was delicious, loved the meatballs appetizer and the veal parmigiana.  Service was superb. No worry about where to park either, they have a valet option for $10 flat fee.  Can't wait to go back!

I ignored the bad reviews because eco-friendly dry cleaning is hard to come by, but I am starting to wonder if they are "eco-friendly" because they don't clean your clothes at all. I've had the usual experiences where they return clothes that have clearly not been cleaned, but I thought I would give them another chance. This time, I picked up a dress with new grease and grime stains after "they cleaned it twice," even though one of the stains even got better by just scratching it with my fingernail.

Each time the owner has been totally nasty, for lack of a better word. I will never be back to this cleaner.

Easy location to get to. Room was really comfortable and clean (great shower) with a great breakfast and attentive staff. The saline/ salt water pool is on the small side and 3' deep but was nice to relax in. There isn't a whirlpool or hot tub. Large fluffy towels are provided. The workout room was large with a variety of machines and exercise balls. Parking is iffy. Large trucks and people taking up 2 spaces can make it precarious.

I was in town for business and I found this great restaurant.  The place has a great atmosphere and I liked it from the first step. My dinner was delicious and I had enough for lunch the next day.  I would definitely recommend it.

Maybe it's because I had higher expectations of this place coming in--
I just wasn't impressed. I just wasn't. I ordered the two most mentioned dish and both were mediocre at best; the service was brisk but at the expense of seeming rude.

The roti canai wasn't anything special. It's supposed to be an Indian pancake and the pancake itself was an interesting dish just because of the fact that it's crispy and flaky but chewy at the same time. The side sauce is the curry sauce, which I personally thought was uninteresting.

The mee siam was a disappointment. Maybe I have a bias because I'm not a fan of rice noodles. 

It's very reasonably priced, however so it may be worth checking out once.

We LOVE This Place.  Best Pizza in all of Tampa!  So excited they are close to our neighborhood, so they deliver.  They care of about their quality of food and services and it's apparent. Their delivery services, is one of the best.  Besides, their Pizza we have also, had the calzone, super cheesy, YUM and the Chicken Carbonera, and Beef Lasagna, is really good.

This place was a quick stop to get my gel manicure done for the weekend.  It was $25 which wasn't too expensive.  I didn't have to wait long.  There were no boards or samples to look through to select colors; you have to pick from the actual bottles which for gel means unscrewing the bottle to see the color.  They work it like an assembly line, which means that I had multiple people working on my nails throughout the process.  They did a decent job with shaping and painting my nails but apologized that they had no hot towels so I missed out on part of the service and paid the same fee.  I was a little put off with being asked to pay for the service before it was complete when getting gel; I completely understand if it was regular polish but I have never been asked that for gel manicures before.  The plus is that you can add a tip to credit card purchase, which most places I have been to do not have that option.

The absolute worst customer service I've ever experienced. After signing in they told me there was 1 person ahead of me, there were 4 employees & only 1 other customer besides me, the one employee helped the other customer while the other 3 employees stood around & did nothing. After waiting a half hour I asked how much longer I'd have to wait & I was told the same thing, there's 1 person ahead of you, I asked why I couldn't be helped by one of the other 3 employees and I was told they were busy when they were clearly doing nothing. In the end I waited 45 minutes for nothing and was treated very rudely during my wait. I will never step foot back in that store again & am ending my contract with Verizon immediately.

I went to this salon to get my hair and makeup done for a wedding I was in.  The price we were quoted and the price we were charged varied significantly.  We were told updos start at $50, and after my hair and makeup was done I was charged $145.  $100 for an updo is a bit much, especially considering I have extremely thin, straight, medium length hair.  Everyone had varying hair and we were still all chargd the same price.  I was happy with the end result, but I have been to other places that have charged much less, and more importantly that were upfront from the beginning about the cost!  To make matters worse, I heard them whispering about how much to charge us, so it just made me feel like we were being taken advantage of.

How fun is this place? They bring you a scorching hot "stone" that looks like a paver of sorts.  Then they have your selection of meat on the stone. You slice off what you'd like to eat, and lay the meat on the stone as long as you'd like until it is cooked to your preference. I had the filet mignon, and it was wonderful, especially w the compote butter. It's a unique spot with delicious sides, steaks and salads. Be warned the "wedge" isn't iceberg lettuce but romaine, so if that is important to you order something else. Nice date place if you don't mind TV's hanging around here and there. Not too fancy, jeans or shorts are fine, but prices are a bit upscale.

Had a wonderful dinner at this recently reopened BBQ place in Unionville.  The restaurant is updated  with a nice bar area and the food was outstanding.  Definitely best BBQ around and worth the ride. The ride down 82 is beautiful and you will enjoy the ribs, brisket and all the BBQ at Hood's.  Service was great and staff was very friendly , too.  We will be back!

Across from the Pageant and in the loop, this beauty bar gave me an up-do and eye-makeup/lashes. The owner and the only everything woman here does hair, makeup, extensions, and there is someone else for nails. I think they are looking to hire others. But for now, Ley is the only person I would trust. 

I was really really please, actually more than please. I was looking for a beauty bar that can work for ethic woman in general, but Ley can just do about anyone's hair and make-up. If you know how to do a black woman's hair - you can do it all. Ley also spent her training/time in LA- so there are a lot of influences there.  Ley was really professional and also just down to earth/friendly. 

In addition, I received so many compliments for my hair/makeup that it was worth the 2 hours in the chair. This place is almost a secret to Vegaz (which truthfully I could not get an appointment to - so decided to stumble about this gem here). But- I am glad I did. 

So my experience was on-time, friendly, and professional. So - totally worth it. The services in general are really well priced, but shampooing, etc. are extra. They are a full service bar and can do it all. If you are from cities like NY, Boston, LA, etc, this place is very underpriced and cheaper than Vegaz. 

For my hair/eye make-up/extensions- I spent around $96 (which includes the discount). But they also have loyalty cards. And I will be back and try new services.

I also know they have make-up classes on Sundays. If I can fit it in, I completely need to give this a try and bring the girlfriends along.

Solo coman anticucho aqui!
La parihuela es el peor que he probado en toda mi vida!
El seco super salado!
This review is for the paddy on welsh rd. The bartender girl is more preoccupied with her texts ( she looks like a heroin addict too). Pls give a shit. Food: garlic parm wings is good though

Go someplace else or dont go.There is no maid service, but they charge you for maid service any way.  There is no valet parking so you may have to walk a distance in the heat to get to casino.  They will email you ahead of time with a phone number to call if you have any questions before staying....that has been discounted.  Says you can register on line the day before, not true.  Try to find a manager.  I dont think there is one.  No room service until 6 pm.  Buffet is $33 per person which is outrageous.  Cant find an email address.

Honestly should've listened to the reviews but I didn't. Not a very good experience in my opinion. I got the burrito with beef. It was not edible the flavor was off on everything. Had it a month ago but remember not even being able to eat it and grabbing something else not long after. Not sure what happened but the reviews are definitely true to taste.

Beer selection is magnificent, food was forgettable. Ordered a burger medium, came out well done. Mussels we ordered were cooked well, but the broth they were served in tasted like the wine hadnt been cooked at all. The fries were incredible though. Loved how crunch and flavorful they were. Dipping sauces were bangin as well. Come for the beer and snack on the fries, eat your meal somewhere else though.

Always make a trip here when I go visit my brother! I'm a huge donut fan and this place has never disappointed. Their apple fritter and boston cream donut is to die for! They have a huge assortment of bagels and breakfast items as well. Highly recommend to all donut lovers like me :)

A place for the convention-goers to get what they will leave believing is authentic home-style Creole.  I suppose it really IS authentic, but Colonel Sanders' recipe may also have sufficed in Kentucky six decades ago.  Lines of course are ridiculous at any point in the day (though only at lunchtime peaks is it usually out the door and wrapped around the building's perimeter).  Service is predictably sassy.

The red beans and rice here are fine, but absolutely no surprises, and shrimp creole and etoufee are a watery mess.  Cafe Maspero in the Quarter has an awfully similar menu and is equally touristy, but you'd have a lot more reliable meal at a better value.  And many locals will defend Maspero, which I don't hear them doing these days about Mother's.  Don't let the signed photographs of luminaries over the past eight decades fool you; this place is resting on its laurels.  It remains completely vegetarian unfriendly: the collard greens ) are cooked in a cork base, which is as they should be, but if you are looking for meatless eating at Mother's, you're probably taking a risk even with the cornbread.

Despite the fact that they have no culinary similarities whatsoever, you actually can get a more interesting meal at Mother Clucker's down the road.  Give it a try and leave this place for the guileless convention-goers.
Went there for dinner and it was terrible.  Food arrived cold and low quality.  The food is just trash, don't go here.

Margarita Tuesday!!! It was a coworkers birthday and we chose to go there after work to party. The atmosphere was boring and no juke box to play music.   There was only one bartender so I didn't expect service to be there. When the bill came and I read my receipt. She charged me full price on margarita that are half off and add a few more drinks to my tab.   I called her out on the mistake so she can fix it. Instead of just fixing her mistake and call the manager.  She said she can't fix the mistake and would give me free drinks.  I have to drive.  I can't drink additional 4 drinks and drive home.  I can't afford dui and it was snowing.   I honestly don't think it's free drinks if I'm being over charged from the beginning.   I ordered 4 more drinks and I tipped her.  Instead of saying thank you and apologizing, she took the money and walked away as if she deserved it.  I'm never coming again and I want everyone to check their receipt.

Found this salon in my neighborhood and love that it is only a few blocks away. I do feel the people here are not that friendly and never really engage in conversation which is different from what I usually experience. The dip nail here is ok and it falls off in like 3-4 weeks which NEVER happens to me and any other place Ive gotten it - it stays for like 4weeks plus and never chips. Otherwise, an OK place and prices are kinda overpriced.

Great food and waitresses but why are the bartenders always so miserable?! They don't even crack a smile or ask how you're doing when you sit down. My glass stays empty unless I ask for another by getting their attention. I don't know, this used to be one of my favorite places. What's happened?!

I was here for a baby shower on a Saturday, and although the entire group, including myself, was really happy with the food, I couldn't help but feel very disappointed with the customer service. 

A private room was booked for the shower, and the menu was preselected. For my primi, I chose to get the lobster bisque which was fantastic, to say the least. It was rich and creamy, and loaded with lobster. I really enjoyed every last spoonful!

For my entree, I selected the grilled salmon Fresca which the menu said came with pesto vinaigrette, feta, diced tomatoes, and balsamic glaze. What also came with the dish, but was not mentioned on the menu, were sweet potatoes and asparagus. Not sure why it was left off the menu, but no complaints on my end, because I was happy to see them on my plate! The diced sweet potatoes were, by far, my favorite part about the entire dish. My friend who got the salmon as well said the same thing!

Unlike the food, the customer service was a huge letdown. The staff assigned to our party were extremely disorganized, and even seemed inattentive and negligent at times. There were many instances when they didn't ask people for drink orders or refills, and I can note a couple times when they brought out the wrong drinks and dishes to the table. 

Since this was an organized party that had been planned in advance, it was also disappointing that the entire lunch ran late. The room had been booked for two hours, but the event ended up going almost three hours because of how long it took for the food to be served. We ended up not having time to play one of the games we had planned, and many of the attendees had to leave before the event actually ended due to it running late. 

While Brio impressed me with the food, I would really be hesitant about organizing a party here myself.

I'm updating, and slightly upgrading, my review because I'm now starting to understand this place.  It's not fair for me to rate it as a pure Americanized Chinese food play because that's really not what they are or possibly even care to be.  I'd probably hit them with a 1 out of 5 star review if I were comparing them to this brand of Chinese I crave every Sunday evening.

They're a bakery and roastery, specifically duck and pork.  That's what your expectations should be set to, and almost literally nothing more.  But I upgraded my review because there is in fact an order to be had if you tread carefully.

From the menu, go ahead and order potstickers, the roast duck and chicken lomein...and stop there!  I see you looking around, I said stop!  Now walk over to the counter, treat yo'self to a few orders of the pork buns, let's go with as many as you can carry, and pick up a pack or two of the pork dumpling dim sum things to round out the spread.  

That compilation is the first time in 4 years of trying to make it work with this place, where at least half of our collective family order was willingly consumed.  For that order, I'd send someone their way, because those items are tasty.

Ordered for pickup and tipped 20% in advance as I normally do for any restaurant. Order was ready 10 mins late. When I got home, I saw a container labeled "ran out of tots" and found they gave me a "side salad" (just lettuce, no other vegetables in sight) as a substitute for tater tots! Really?! And they didn't even tell me in advance so I could just not pay for a side. Mac and cheese was very bland. I called to complain and they said they'd refund me money. A week later and still no refund. I'm not surprised they failed at that, too.

Pi Indy is hands down the best pizza that I've had in the Indianapolis Area. They feature so many unique combinations. My personal favorite is the Pineapple Express. And guess what? It doesn't have marinara. They have options even on the sauces. A regular size specialty pizza is roughly $12. The crust is hand tossed but closer to the thin side. They toss it into the wood-fire oven and soon your toasty but still soft pizza is on it's way to you. 

They had an issue with the online ordering not going through to the printer this last time I went in, but they quickly got my order into the oven and brought it out to me as I waited at a table. They have always had great service whenever I have stopped by, which is at least twice a month.

Will only go here for piercings. We had a pierced here several times that was great. However, the tattoo artist we had today seemed like she didn't know what she was doing, and I had to basically walk her through sterile technique as she was forgetting a bunch of simple steps (change gloves, clean the tattoo gun, etc). She would touch things such as the biowaste garbage with her gloved hand and then touch my friend, and even put on the plastic protective sheet barehanded. These all scream infection. The tattoos she did for my friends have blowout as well. I know this is for a good cause, but you get what you pay for. And sadly, potential disease and ink needing a redo in the future are on that list. Also, they use tape instead of plastic to cover the tattoo gun, which would not prevent blood from soaking through in the event it got on it.

Food was definitely average.  My quinoa burger was rubber, the onion rings were great.  Service was slow and our server referred us to a QRC for the list of beers of the day.  A server should have a printed list or be able to recite the beers of the day. 

But the worst problem was the incredible noise on an early Saturday night:  other customers making lots of noise.  Could not hear people sitting next to me.
Wow the food is delicious!  Don't judge this book by  its cover.  This is a great little family restaurant. Full bar. Eager servers. The Fajitas are awesome!

It's a nature preserve, lots of turtles every where. They're so adorable! Can I just add, it's a park-ish it's not suppose to be fancy or anything... There's trails to follow, a little play area for the kids. You can go canoeing, have a picnic, and just enjoy the outdoors.
Restaurant is clean, bathroom is clean, food is hot and delicious.  Service was fast and pleasant.

Breaux bridge Benedict & corn beef hash , exellent! With a mimosa and a Stanley screamer that will have you slurring the rest of the afternoon ! Food and drinks came out fast and it's continuously moving .

Whatcha wanna be Five Bistro?

I live in Los Angeles. We research areas before traveling checking out restaurants. I love the hill and St Louis restaurants, but this is not a 5 star restaurant.

Here's my short take on the night:

We had a 7:30 reservation which was confirmed a few hours before.  I love getting a confirmation as it shows resepect for the guests. We (4 of us ) are seated at the first table in the middle of the first room.  So here's how the dialog went:

Me - I'd like a different table I don't want to be in the middle of the room.

Crabby Pants Seating Man - (who I later found out was the chef's Dad) -
You don't want to sit here.???..silence...Well go look in there then (bar area) and points to the left. Which we did ( by ourselves) and picked our own nice table between the bar and the windows.

The bar area room is so much nicer than the room you enter.  It's almost like two different restaurants with the wall taken out.  It looks very unsophisticated. 

Water was brought to the table immediately along with the wine list and menus. We noticed that there was bread on other tables and decided to wait to see how long it would be before we were served bread.  We finally had to ask for it. 

The waitress came over asking if there were any questions about the menu. She started telling us about EVERYTHING in detail on the menu until our heads were spinning.  My sis had to tell her to stop so we could disgest the info.  The menu isn't that big, but I guess they feel a need to tell you in detail every item on the menu. 

Five Bistro puts a big push on local farm food and grass fed beef.  It always annoys me when the staff thinks nobody has ever eaten grass fed meat or local farm food. Surprise..we don't need a big explanation.

The wine list is exceptional. We were impressed being from California.   There are lots of varietal choices that go well with the menu.  We chose a nice Villa Creek (we are familiar with the winery) and a Mendocino, CA syrah. Both delicious.

The pork and salmon entrees were very tasty. The pot roast (which two of us had) was okay.  The meat was dry and bland.  When I told the waitress she said well it's grass fed and it's lean and tastes different.  I wanted to say, I eat grass fed beef and I've never had dry cooked tasteless meat before.   She told us she would let the chef know.

 In other restaurants when you complain about the main dish the chef always comes out. We were just ignored. Especially, since I had taken only one bite of meat. I was really surprised.  I didn't eat it.  The squash/gnocchi hash under the meat was not tasty. It needed a carrot, potato and some seasoning.

We ordered the cheese plate letting the chef pick the cheeses.  Very tasty, but again we had to ask for bread to eat the cheeses. The dipping olive oil was very good. 

The jamoca ice cream desert and sweet potato torte were  good. The pear tart was burnt on the top and bottom and tasted burnt. The sorbet plate had weird consistentcy and wasn't very tasty. 

One last thing...what's with wooden chairs in restaurants lately.  Your behind gets numb and achy.  Can't we just have a comfortable chair for a 2.5 hour meal?

Overall, the restaurant needs to define itself.

Spent the past weekend here and the place was pretty nice.  I don't know why some reviewers said parking was free... it was $10.  It's alright... it was a vacation anyways.  This place is right in front of the beach and not too far away from the pier, which was great.  

Reserved a room with 2 queen beds but eventually upgraded to a partial ocean view. It was nice and the room was very well decorated. We enjoyed our stay here.  Checking in was a breeze and the manager was very professional and pleasant.  No real complaints.  The price was a bit high but, oh well... it was right across the beach and the hotel was very well maintained.

We also ate at Eladio's.  Food was great albeit a bit pricey.  Guests received a 10% discount.  Free wifi!!

I would recommend this place!  

Just an FYI - they offer a 10% for AAA members! Don't forget to use it!

We were driving from Baton Rouge to pick up my wife at the NOLA airport so we stopped at N&O for lunch. Unfortunately- the owners had decided to randomly close the restaurant during a time that they advertised to be open. In fact - their yelp entry STILL says "Open until 9 pm". 

They are not.

We will NOT go back.

I'm breaking up with you Minato.  And it's not me, it's you.

We were regulars at the old location and frequently and insistently recommended them (If you are local, you know there is a plethora of sushi places and everyone has their favorites they champion).  We loved the menu, the service, we were even comfortable with the small space.  

With their move to the new location it's like they are a completely different restaurant.  What happened?  They added new items to the menu and took off some old ones.  A lot of the new rolls are similar with the imitation krab mix and lots of sweet sauce, not very innovative. I can't really fault them for that since they are probably very popular so they are filling a demand.  They also have "special" nigiri they offer (limit one order per person) but everytime I went they were out so I never had the opportunity to try.  

The main reason however that we are done is the service.  Back when they were in a smaller space they staffed 2 or 3 girls on the floor, now that they are in a higher capacity they didn't raise the number of floor staff, although it appears they have an entirely new staff.  The familiar faces are gone.  

Our waitress was flailing and I'm guessing very inexperienced?  She stopped frequently to ask how things were and then walked away while we were answering. She dropped of items and was puzzled when we would ask what it was and floated from table to table trying to find a home for dishes she didn't seem to know who ordered. She never once picked up our order sheet, we had to flag down the other staff to submit it.  We never received requested condiments or refill on drinks which was bad because dinner spanned over 2.5 hours due to her slowness and confusion. They were not busy that night.  Our waitress had 2 other tables aside from us. a 2 top and a table of 3.   The other 2 girls were very attentive despite not being our waitress (I think they were actually bussers). We were patient throughout until we asked for our check from the waitress and waited.  20 mins and no check, although she came to the table next to us 3 times to check on them.  My kids were getting restless so I packed them up while my husband waited.  On my way out I reminded the waitress that we were still waiting for our check and that my husband would be paying (I didn't want her to think we were leaving without paying). He sat and waited at our table.  I took the kids out to the parking lot to walk around and look at the mini golf course until my husband came back out.  10 minutes goes by. 20 minutes goes by.  We've paced the entire parking lot like 10 times, so I go back to peek inside and my husband is still patiently waiting.  I go in to see what's taking so long.  It's been 40 minutes now since we've requested our check and my husband has reminded her an additional 3 times whenever she stopped by the table.  Finally he had to get up and walk over to the cash register where one of the bussers had to check with the manager if it was okay for her to close out our check so we can pay.  We spent almost 4 hours trying to eat dinner and pay.  We normally are out of there in an hour or less when we eat sushi.  We didn't eat more than we usually do, we just spent about 75% of the time waiting and waiting and waiting.  It was a very frustrating experience.

Positive: This used to be Vesuvio, but they had to change the vibe after complaints from neighbors (used to be sporty/clubby ... now they're gonna start hosting jazz nights ... which is all good).  Owner is a cool cat.  Hummus was fairly generous portion (though not cheap).

Negative: On a single visit they intentional mislead my party into ordering something other than what we intended at a higher price TWICE (though to our credit we caught it both times).  

Example: 
Q: the beer and shot special, does that mean $6 for a beer AND shot?  any beer? 
A: Yes
Q: I'd like a xxx beer and the shot
A: That will be $10

Q: Could I order the hummus
A: Sure no problem ... OK hummus and bruschetta coming right up
Q: I know that some toast comes with the humuus, but you're talking about a whole seperate bruschetta order?
A: Yes

Solid mediocre in terms of food. Their salsa tasted like watery tomatoes (no flavor or spice) and I got a chicken quesadilla and wasn't super impressed. The chicken was dry, which is strange because typically the way the shredded chicken is prepared seals in moisture. 3 stars for OK food, but really more for the cool modern touch on western interior decor- galvanized metal meets bright colors and wood. Also, their bar is HUGE and well decorated to look inviting. I'd come back for a few drinks here, but I'd skip the food.

Stopped in at 7:00 pm for a hot n ready thin crust pepperoni pizza. The place was packed with people waiting while a guy was doing chin ups. I ordered and he said they didnt have any ready so I turned to leave and he loudly and smart ellicly says "bye" and waves at me in front of everyone. Which was embarrassing and unnecessary.  And to top it all off the place was dirty.

This is a SCAM! They charge a deposit and continuously "reschedule" your "appointment" then when you call or text them, you NEVER get a response back. And the voicemail is conveniently full. Then on the last "appointment" date, they never show up. Ron and this so called "company" are scam artists. And I truly believe that all the 5 star ratings are from buddies of theirs to help sell the scam. Don't waist your time.

Went for my boys birthday lunches. It took us over an hour And a half to get our food. Our ticket was started at 12:28 according to the receipt. We got there at 12. We were there until 2. We didnt have time for them to get their birthday desserts because I had to go to work. They were not busy at all, I'm talking 3 tables in the whole place. My son asked for onion petals on his steak and she charged us 4.99 for a bowl of cold petals.

Some dishes have a good value and make you want to come back. To remain relevant some spit and polish is needed desperately. The place looks shabby and is. Clutter on the walls and everywhere make the place dark and contribute to the  appearance of shabby and almost dirty Even though it probably isn't. Display cases are so high you can't even see the people behind it.Dining room had many tables with a un-bussed dishes. To be relevant time is now to "sandblast" the interior n  some fresh  paint and clean the place up. Doesn't necessarily have to be a lot of money. It's a shame what the place has turned into. Go for it spring cleaning!

I'm so torn on the rating of this place so I'm going with 3 for now. If I was judging on Pupusas I would give it a five, but yesterday for my main dish I tried to go with a meal that looked straight out of a Nicaraguan restaurant, but clearly I made a big mistake after I took my first bite. 
From the outside they advertise a Nica flag and even have some decor in the inside, but I don't think anything on the menu is Nicaraguan whether it appears to be or not. 
Nothing on the plate had any flavor to it. I would not recommend the carne Asada to anyone, you will be disappointed! 
Everything on the plate tasted the same. No distinct flavors, just grill and salt. It was bad, and the cabbage salad that accompanies this meal tasted like it had lemon instead of lime, and not fresh lemon, the one out of the bottle, which I hate because it has a soap detergent taste. 
That was the first time ever I didn't like the Queso frito, it was slightly rubbery like, so salty and tasted like I said, the grill. Thumbs down. 
Now, we also ordered pupusas. The chicken&cheese, and the pork. Both awesome ! Loved them. Highly recommend! Passion Fruit Juice is house made and FANTASTIC! 
Now when I come here again, I will just order more from the food items made out of corn because they clearly excel in that area. The tamales, tacos dorados, pupusa loca I want to try because it's a mix of all the meats, enchiladas, pollo con tajadas looked good. 
The people next to me ordered that, and looked like they were really enjoying it. I wondered how it tasted. Plantains w/crema also looked great as a side, and I've read a lot of good things about the soup. Combinacion guanaca hmmm maybe. Probably not. 
This place was packed when I walked in so I know it's good, I may have just had bad luck the first time, and I'm not giving up on this place yet. I was happy about the fact that I did find Bags of these little Nicaraguan corn treats called rosaquillas. They are not for everyone, but I love them

Delicious food, pretty little place, friendly and courteous staff. This will be one of the best Thai food you'll have. So if you're in town be here. Don't make excuses!

When you're at The Smiling Elephant you order Pad Thai. That's what you do. If you want to save 15% or more on car insurance you switch to geico, that's.. Wait. You get the point. We also ordered Gai Yang (chicken cilantro dish) which was pretty kick ass. The honey lime tea (not really a tea) was refreshing. And the best part, when you order the ice cream you get two scoops( try the coconut lycee)!  Everything tastes good here. Service is nice and quick as well. Totally recommended.

Secret tip : This place gets crowded. Parking lot isn't the largest real estate in the country either. Get here around 8:30pm and you'll beat the rush hour. You're welcome.

Was staying at the aloft, right around the corner and decided to get a takeout breakfast.  Service was fast, although not very knowledgeable about menu items (could not tell me if pepper and eggs on a long roll was Green peppers or just black pepper). Got a sausage egg and cheese on a hard roll.  The eggs were cooked perfectly fluffy and moist; the sausage was two links splint lengthwise so they covered the entire roll; decent amount of cheese; the roll was a bit stale and although toasted (briefly) on the grill could have benefitted from more toasting to overcome its ripe old age.

We tried this place after seeing it on a Tucson Foodie piece on a recent United flight. We went on a Sunday, which is all-day happy hour and the HH prices weren't bad. I got the beef rolled tacos with rojo sauce. It comes topped with pickled onions and cabbage. My husband got a burro, cochi style. It was pretty big and again topped with pickled onions and cabbage. Overall, the food was good. It was nothing super special and a little greasy.  They provide you options for salsas, which is nice. Their queso was tasty. The outdoor patio was clean and it was a nice temperature.  It was good, but not sure I'd drive back across town just to eat there regularly.

NOPE! Stayed at the B with some friends this past weekend. We were all excited, seeing as though the online photos looked awesome and posh, but the reality is very disappointing. 

* The lobby smells like the sewer with perfume poured in it. I don't see how the front desk staff could stand it. 
* Elevator was very slow. Seemed to be only one out of 2 working
* Rooms are small and the furniture looks chipped and cheap. 
* I shared a one bedroom with living room and there was no mirror in the living area, so it was tough for both of us ladies to get ready every day.
* Bathroom is VERY small. Hardly any room to close the door from the inside while you're in there. No counter space to put your makeup or toiletries. 
* Took the bathroom shower 10-15 minutes to warm up. 
* No iron or ironing boards. Only a steamer. No thank you!
* The room phone did not work when I tried to call about the iron situation. No dial tone or anything.
* Room TV advertises a Netflix connection option, but when you click it, it says no internet connection. Lots of the TV channels would not play because of this as well
* Hotel is a long walk to everything and is in an area that gets a little sketchy at night. I would not walk back at night if I were you. 
* The hotel lobby doors lock after a certain time, probably because of the neighborhood. The key card box didn't work one night when I came back. 

On a good note, the canal street trolley runs right by the place so it's easy to get back and forth that way. Also, the hotel staff seemed nice enough and the housekeeping came by every day and did a nice job freshening up the room. I visit NOLA a few times a year and have tried many hotels. This is the only one I would never return to.

We grabbed a seat at a bar table for dinner. Our server was slow and we never got a bread basket (not sure if the bread is something you pay for but for their prices it should be included for free). Our dinner seemed to take a long while to come out (40+ minutes).

We had two steaks, two drinks, lobster Mac n cheese ($10 extra for little to no lobster) and our bill was $130 before tip. 

The steaks were cooked perfectly and were high quality and full of flavor but our $20 "lobster" mac was not worth it's $20 price tag.

I would say the steaks are in line with the pricing / a good value compared with what the other resort restaurants are charging for the less fancy food they are serving. Just don't expect 5 star service along with it (at least in the bar area).

Umm... HELLO??? Does anyone know what they're eating...? How this place receives any good reviews for food or service is beyond me. Ca' Dario, you're doing EVERYTHING wrong.

The food was so bad that it became a joke among our table, and our snobby waitress didn't help our experience either. I'm going to use quotes from the night as my review so you get everyone's perspective. I was dining with three others, so let's call them T, K, and M.

M (eggplant parm): "This is awful... The eggplant is undercooked and the dish doesn't have any flavor. This is just awful."

T (salmon penne): "This tastes like airplane food."

K (ravioli): "That's a lot of butter... I don't think I want to eat this." (There was so much butter it might as well have been ravioli in butter soup. Raviolis swimming in butter with a dollop of tomato sauce in the middle -- the whole table had a good laugh when we saw it.)

B (ravioli): "This tastes like Trader Joe's ravioli that was burned in a frying pan."

We sent back the butter soup and ordered the ossobuco since we were afraid of the pastas. The ossobuco had a bone so small in it that I had to dig through the mountains of tomato sauce to find it. Come on, Ca' Dario, I order ossobuco and that's what you give me? To top it off, the risotto was so undercooked that I think it's safe for me to say that it was raw. No, it was not al dente -- I know the difference, and I'm sticking to the risotto being raw.

We received the bill along with some candy, and T tried one.

T (candy): "Even the candy is bad..."

That's it -- we were done. I literally have never had such a bad meal in my life, and I'm forever scarred from this experience. After this awful and unfulfilling dinner, I snuck over to In-N-Out which was much more fulfilling than whatever this pretend Italian joint's food is pretending to be.

Ok so Tony Luke's is pretty legendary no doubt. But this location needs to get its act together before it tarnishes the legendary brand. 
Very simple order, cheesesteak American light fried O and hot pepper side with a bottle water. Pretty easy,  right?  Nope; along with my order were fries I didn't order but paid for and a kinda sorry ass cheesesteak. What happened to the cheesesteak?  Well the light FO was located on a very small portion of the sandwich and it was the "knots" of the onion.  Chunks of onion that no one in there right mind would want on their sandwich, but here they were on mine. So...I had to remove the crap onions and try and spread out what remained with my fingers as I did not grab utensils as I thought I would not need them. The meat also was not leveled properly on the roll. C'mon man you gotta do better than this!  
So...to the folks actually making the sandwiches at this particular time and day...pay attention to what you are doing because you are ruining the legendary brand. Simply not up to standard.

Don't know why this place is still liked. I used to love their paella. I recently went again. The paella sucked. Not flavorful. And rice was undercooked. For the price I expected something that would blow other places with paella out of the water! Like before when I used to come.The service was incredibly horrible. On the good note the bread pudding was delish . BUT I am hopping it was just a bad night
Fantastic! Beautiful modern atmosphere, delicious food. I highly recommend the dates and the avocado blt.

We ordered online for an office lunch meeting. The order was accurate and packaged well for delivery. They had added extra sauces which was nice. Excellent service and we will order our office lunches from them again.

Not half bad for fast Mexican! Had the green chicken enchilada plate and was surprised at the amount off chicken. Beans were very tasty but rice was very bland. All you can eat chips and a fairly large salsa bar. Left very satisfied.

Okay, first of all, I'm an engineer , with a college degree. So I went into Cody's in Palm Harbor, I had one drink and my buddy accidentally spilled it. We ordered dinner and ate our dinner, and then the manager came over and said we were not allowed to have another drink. No idea why, I'm not allowed to have one drink at Cody's I guess. I would not recommend this place to anybody. And the manager was a probably 22 year old. I'd like to see her college degree, and treat me like that. Stay away from this place!

Although the food was good (and deserving of more stars), the service was terrible. 

Almost immediately after we received our menus, the server asked if we were ready to order. We asked for a couple of minutes and didn't see him again for at least 10 minutes. During this time, he cleared tables and ate lunch, but didn't notice us waiting until his boss pointed us out. 

The food came out very quickly, but the server managed to tilt one of the plates, allowing a portion of the food to slip off onto the table. He apologized but didn't offer to get a new dish or even clean the table off - we were just left with some noodles on the table. He also forgot to bring out water until reminded. 

We had to ask for the check and apparently the credit card machine is down now and we've been waiting in uncertainty for the past 20 minutes.

The set up of the restaurant isn't great. The tables are so close together that when trying to navigate to your table you're left with the age-old question: "As I pass, do I give you ass or crotch?" 

Maybe this place is best for take-out?
This is a "must do" every time we come to Gulfport.  We love their food and atmosphere.

I live closer to another Winn-Dixie, but come to this store for the customer service. The employees are nice, courteous, and helpful. I have never had a bad experience here. The parking lot is in severe need of repair and complete resurface. If not for the parking lot I would give this store 5 stars.

I have only been here to drink so I don't know anything about the restaurant part.  I was here on an idle sunday funday afternoon. My group of 3 friends and myself were the only few people at the bar.  We all enjoyed the absinth "experience."  The bartender was very knowledgeable and friendly.  He knew everything there was to know about cocktails, alcohols and their history.  It was a great time!

What a wonderful meal! We ordered the Turista menu (which you should definitely do) and found the portion sizes just right, and the variety of flavors quite memorable. Excellent atmosphere. Service was friendly and attentive without being annoying or overbearing. 

I'm giving 4 stars instead of 5 because even though I loved my meal, they only had one vegetarian option per section, and I would have loved to be able to choose. Sometimes, when you know there's only one thing you are able to eat at a place, even if it's something totally delicious, it makes you reluctant to go back there. 

(For the record, my fiance gives it 5 stars and loved the porchetta, the lasagna, and the sausage appetizer.) 

I heartily recommend Modo Mio for an excellent experience.

I wanted to like this place more. It was my first visit. My friend's Caesar salad looked great -  full plate with plump shrimp. My Smashed Meatball sandwich was less impressive. The meatballs, cheese, and sauce on the sandwich were skimpy for $12. This could have been much more generous given the price. I will probably try again, but am not eager to.
The food was delicious!! The bowwow fruit punch was on hit!! The chicken was outstanding!! Old school like bigmama just salt and pepper.

I come here every other week. It's a godsend for people who want to eat late but don't want po-boys or burgers. The service is slow but the food comes out fast. The Chinese food isn't the greatest but it is definitely above your local fast food delivery. The sushi is good and fresh - if you order in season. And it's a nice relaxing atmosphere to unwind after a long day of work. Also, sit at the bar or in the bar area when George is working. He's awesome and very, very cool.

Decided to try Ruby Tuesday's before going to movies.  Just looking for a burger with the salad bar.  Food was as I expected but I had a problem ordering beer.  I was there during happy hour.  My wife and I were sitting in a booth 10 feet from the bar...the empty bar I might add.  When our waitress came to take our drink order, I asked her what they had going for happy hour.  The waitress said that happy hour is only for drinks ordered at the bar.  So I looked at her, pointed to the bar and said that maybe I'll order my beer at the bar and bring it to my table.  The waitress said that can't be done.  I'd have to drink it at the bar.  I ordered an ice tea, had my burger & salad but I  resolved that no more Ruby Tuesday's for me.  What a ridiculous policy.  Happy Hour is happy hour and should apply to the drink regardless of where it is ordered!

So I have to say it's not as amazing as everyone says. Yes, they have a great bottled beer selection but their tap beer selection isn't amazing. I did enjoy a few beers I wouldn't have tried otherwise but I wasn't horrible impressed. I was amused to find out that a SEPTA bus crashed into it only in August which explained the rather unfinished look to the hallway leading into the restaurant. I might go back for a beer but I don't think I'd eat here since I wasn't digging the atmosphere.

We really wanted to like this place!
We both ordered the cone meal which comes with a sauce, side, and a drink.  

While at the counter, we were informed there was no ice for the soda... BUT it was cold! Fine, we were walking two blocks back home, we could deal.  
When we got to the sodas, they were HOT and FLAT. They weren't salvageable so we opted for drinks at home.  
Unfortunately, it didn't get better from there. 

We unwrapped the cones which was covered with the sweet cinnamon sauce. The cone fell apart as I lifted it from the foil. I dumped out all of the chicken and proceeded to try to eat that. The sauce made the breading on the chicken gummy and sticky. I opted to just finish the cone, letting my SO eat the chicken. 

The only decent quality was the Cajun fries. They had good seasoning, and were decently crispy. Unfortunately, they were cold. 

Overall, I would not go back. The novelty idea is what sells and that hasn't been perfected.

I was interested in going to Iceland for my birthday in October 2017.
Gate 1 Travel came recommended to me. In May 2017 I contacted Gate 1 and booked my trip for me and my husband including daily excursions.
One and half week before our trip, my healthy, never smoked or drinks husband suffered 3 consecutive strokes and was hospitalized.
I contacted Gate 1 Travel and communicated the unfortunate 
Circumstance. I suggested they credit my account for I hoped to reschedule after medical clearance was given to my husband.
Very coldly the reps answer was that I didn't purchase "their" insurance therefore I will not receive refund nor credit. My choices were to take trip (which we couldn't) or lose the money - Her words!
I am extremely disappointed in the fashion this company treats people and conducts business. No understanding, kindness or consideration was shown. I hope to visit Iceland, but will never use Gate 1 Travel. I give zero stars actually.

I remember when this theater was shiny and new. Now it is shabby and old. The fact that it sits out all by itself just east of the huge bulldozed area where Parklane Mall used to be doesn't help at all. 

It has an outdoor ticket office with is a huge negative. When you walk back towards the theaters, it seems dark and spooky. 

On the plus side there is plenty of parking. 

The last time I went, I saw a movie with a group of friends and I am glad I was not there by myself. Now I am not usually a timid person and I seldom hesitate to go places by myself, but this theater just seems a little dicey.
