Probably will not be back unless there is something really spectacular showing that is not available at my neighborhood Cineplex and I have friends to go with.

Our group stayed here for a conference last week and we all enjoyed our stay. First of all Tori and Jose are amazing individuals and always there to assist. Our rooms were nice and clean with each room having a microwave and small fridge was a bonus. This hotel is newly built and is located a block from the Country Music Hall of Fame Museum and about 2 blocks from Broadway where all the action is. The breakfast was good and my only suggestion would be to have an omelette station since they already have a grill there. The happy hour menu was also decent and the 3 drinks were more than enough. The only reason I didn't give it 5 stars because I ordered towels and it never showed up. Overall experience was good and I would definitely stay here again.

Okay... Grumpy's was alright... I believe there's better spots for burgers though. I had the open face burger, but instead of bread I opted for lettuce. Let's just say, I woke up last night feeling like I ate a pound of msg haha. I think it was the green chili that they served. I mean, really? 

Anyways, we came here on a Monday night. It was slow, only one table. We sat down and ordered. What felt like 30 minutes pass before we get our food, and mind you, it's not like the food was very difficult to prepare. Burgers mostly. Nothing too crazy.

As I said previously, I ordered Grumpy's open faced burger. It was umm not as good as I thought it could be. I felt as if the chili came out of a can. And it just wasn't satisfying. This place is rated as 4 stars and maybe I just ordered the wrong thing, but I'm sure there are better things around.

I probably won't come back here, even if I was visiting. It didn't last as a lasting impression on me, but hey, try it out and if you disagree let me know what you got!

DO NOT EXPECT Mattress Firm delivery will reach out to you when they arrive if you live in an apartment building!

I'm so disappointed with​ their delivery service. I was waiting at home all the time after I got their automatic voice mail. When it was approaching​ the last minute of their delivery window, I called the retail store. The only thing that the manager repeated was THIS IS YOUR RESPONSIBILITY. He said he told me at the store they wouldn't call, which I DIDN'T KNOW AT ALL. He said the delivery man called my apartment, which I DIDN'T RECEIVE EITHER. He said my apartment building lobby was locked and they took a picture. Of course,​ it would be locked because of this late delivery time on Sunday and the property management office was closed then (5:10pm according to the manager), but what's the picture for, to prove I'm not waving a ​hand at them at lobby??

I was so angry at the manager repeatedly saying or indicating this was my responsibility, and not answering my question on how to solve this problem. The manager also kept repeating that the delivery team wouldn't call me because it is against law. I'm not an expert at the law and don't want to argue if it is against law, but I strongly recommend Mattress Firm to improve the delivery service. Instead of a ​phone call, you can text me or let me choose if I would like to be called at. I live in a large apartment building, and we have a loading area at the back of the building. If you don't call me and use a third-party vendor which you can't control at all, how can I tell them they need to go to the loading area instead of the ​lobby? I understand not every company could have their local delivery team like IKEA who is familiar with the local building, but please try to reach out to your customer rather than just wait there, took a picture and blamed your customer not showing up.

I decided to cancel this order after I heard from the manager that they would process my order and begin to reschedule as early as Tuesday. My second suggestion here for the miss delivery is, please try your best to help your customer get their mattress on the second day when you don't make it for the first time. I don't know how many people would need their mattress as soon as possible due to relocation like me, but this slow process indeed pushed me even further to cancel this order and turn to other retailers.  The disappointment, the blame and more waiting days, all of them are too much for your customer after they sweep the floor and make everything ready for the new mattress with an exciting mood.

This is my go to place for coffee and snack 3-4 times per week for over two years. The staff in the bakery are each as good as they come.  And...they never forget a name. We have catered events through AJs, bought lots of flower arrangements, unusual gifts, enjoyed sushi, used the back wine room and more.  Love this place!

If you are turning in your lease vehicle and is not purchasing a new vehicle  make to tell them that you want to sell them your vehicle, so your able to get money back based off of the Blue book value and after they pay off the remaining cost of your lease. If you do not say you are selling your car to them they will take advantage of that and ground your vehicle and your basically giving them your car for free while they're able to resell your vehicle and make a big profit off it. Michael Smith and his staff is very conniving and they will take advantage of customers lack of knowledge. Michael told me it's their job to not mention anything so they're able to get your cat for as low as possible so they can make more profit.. so they are okay with scamming people out of money that would be owed to them

I'm am not going give a long review here because I only tried the ribs. 
Appearance: they looked great
Taste: left a after taste that stayed with me all night..and not a good after-taste. Ribs were very fatty and by no means even close to ribs I do at home.. I would not repeat.

If u aren't used to good BBQ, it will probably be fair to you. Sorry Jimmy's, major miss on the ribs!!

I really like this place! The parsley potatoes are on point. I would give this place 5 stars, but I felt the ribs were not as tender as they should be, in my opinion. Prices are great. The food comes almost immediately, service is great too. For dessert, I got the apple cake, absolutely delicious! I'm a fan!

I'm so happy to have this restaurant in our neighbourhood. I've been several times now, the food is always great. The chef has taken time to come out & talk about various dishes. Prices are reasonable & good sized portions. Great naan! Highly recommended.

Besides the little communication between Frank and us, the work performed was mediocre. There are lots of visible seams in the artificial turf in a high traffic walking area. We had to clean up an area from the old grass they pulled up! The workers left our rock work scraped hap hazardly so we had to replace with more river rock. We also had to add base to one side of the driveway ( where there was none ) and had to tamp down the base ( which I requested them to get) in the front of the driveway. The paver work is o.k with minimal unevenness, nothing to say, "WOW" to.  When we showed these to the project manager, his words were, "We are finished with this project." It seemed the workers had very little direction as WE had to tell them about the drains and dimensions and what style of pavers were to be put down!! Basically, we had higher expectations from this company.

They changed their wings!  I have been a loyal patron (once a week) since I moved here two years ago but recently they changed the size of their wings to normal size. I have to say the beer is great, but this is the reason my family loves this restaurant  the most and go back every time. Very upset. I have challenged others from out of town and when they visit they can attest--these are the best wings ever. Not anymore. I would pay more if these large wings aren't profitable. But the fact that they sold out makes me feel like they are not in touch with their loyal patrons.  Used to be five stars and now I would say three. Sorry.

I was so excited to try this place. Whiskey AND doughnuts?! C'mon!!!!

Given, I did go in and order mule at a whiskey bar but they were completely out of vodka... BUT the bartender was very friendly and offered to make me a mule with orange vodka because that is all he had left (and let me tell you it tasted like an orange dream-sicle). So the service, the environment, and the drinks are great. And if you are into whiskey, they have a HUGE list.

The doughnuts however weren't amazing. They are very basic and were very dry. For a place advertised as a whiskey/donut place I expected more. But overall I was happy with my experience.

The fries were delicious. Perfect crunch and crisp. The veggie burger with the pomegranate sauce was really good! Substituted lettuce bun for the bread. For weight watchers followers this is an awesome place with low point options! 
The Diet Coke (from New Zealand) no points!! And super good!!

I think the staff was great.  We enjoyed our experience at the hotel bars and at RumFish grill.  The service was excellent.  I suspect this would b a great hotel if you have kids.  We paid up for the beach front rooms and found in the hopes of some tranquil sunsets.  We found the constant noise to be unbearable.   Between the bands and the parties at the fire pit on the gulf side, then the rowdy adults and kids on the east side we found it difficult to relax or sleep.  The noise continues well into the night.  Our room was full of mosquitos every night though we took care to close out door quickly when entering.  The gap between the door jam and door allowed the buggers in.   When we checked out the bill had more than $60 of long distance calls, though we never used the phone once while there.  While it was corrected, it made me wonder who was in our room and what else they may have used.  

I had a better experience in the Sirata beach front rooms up the beach some.

This place is great. Lot of variety on the menu. Good clean decor and has a small bar and a good all you can eat salsa bar which I find amazing. Food came out hot and fresh tasting. Prices are normal to slightly high but the quantity of food you get is good.

Worst Starbucks ever, staff ignored me when I had a problem with my order. In fact worse looked at me at the register and turned their back on me and continued to talk. All I wanted was to buy the banana nut bread I had asked for and they did not add to my order.

I really like this place, cool concept, very tasty.  Unfortunately there are so many drawbacks.  The line can get very long making your meal a very long night.  Service is hit and miss as the staff here seems to turnover weekly.  There are so many options in the same area, so make sure you have a good 45 to 60 minutes if you are coming here.

First and last time at Mo's. service was great, unfortunately steaks and food was not even near the price. I regrettably asked for the 8oz. Waygu... Cold, unseasoned, bland, not tender... The worst spent $73.00 bucks in my life. My husband's NY was not right temperature and lacked flavor ....starter bruschetta was horrible.. Strawberry shortcake was just ok, not worth it.  We came home feeling ripped-off, we don't mind spending money if the food is parallel... We visit steakhouses often, don't  waste your palate... Ted's, at a walking distance from Mo's has awesome cuts at less than half!
Great place for drinks and food. I visit here when traveling for work and they have the best happy hour special. Food is good too and so is the service.

Overpriced. Def not friendly even when you are looking to buy! If you are a woman, be prepared to be ignored at first then be ripped off. Thank goodness I didn't purchase from there! Go to another local dealer at least the salesman won't say "Well I am worth something aren't I?" when you try to baragain.

It's been almost nine years since I last wrote a review for Shere-E-Punjab and since I visit this restaurant twice a week, it's time for an update. My husband and I came here for their New Year's Eve buffet and it was SPECTACULAR! All of my favorite dishes were made: goat curry, lamb, chicken bhuna, vegetable korma, three kinds of tandoori, their marvelous basmati rice (better than any other Indian restaurant), okra, cauliflower, vegetable pakora, saag paneer, a mixture of different naans, salads, fruits, condiments, and a large assortment of desserts. Everything was so delicious (I went up twice and for my husband, it was three times). The lights were dimmed which made it more relaxing. There wasn't a large crowd and it was actually pretty quiet. They have some incredible chef in the kitchen. The servers were very attentive by coming by to pour more water in our glasses and to take the finished plates away. More people should come here to savor their wonderful food. They have a lunch buffet every day including the weekends. The food is replenished promptly and for sure, you will not walk out hungry. During the week, Diptesh keeps the place running quite well. He is attentive, respectful, and will answer any question you have about the food, especially all of the ingredients. Diptesh did a great job on New Year's Eve, too, along with the two other servers. So, if you have an urge to eat Indian food that is of high quality, stop in and enjoy yourself!

The three stars are strictly for the atmosphere. The food is absolutely horrible. We both ordered the chicken Cobb salad which was presented to us by our waiter as having delicious fried chicken on top.. it came with deli sized, cold, cubed fried chicken. It was disgusting. We both said we would've been better off getting a Wendy's salad because at least a Wendy's salad isn't $13 and it tastes better. Then we ordered the Hot Chick sandwich which sounds so good on the menu. Again, majorly disappointed. It was a thin, small chicken patty that gave me flashbacks to the middle school cafeteria. The bread was falling apart as if it was stale. Again, we wished we had gotten fast food because then at least the price and food quality would've matched up. Anyone who says they enjoy the food is lying.

Our usual butcher was full during hunting season so I frantically called around looking for anyone to take the two deer in the bed of our truck on a warm day. I found The Meat Shop had openings. I should have trusted my instincts, gross. But we were out of luck. I requested the ground have no added fat of any kind, since I personally only eat venison. Immediately after opening a MASSIVE portion of ground, I see added fat. I started to make into patties for my family, since I won't eat it, I see what looks like cartilage and tendons. Extremely hard pieces, that do not crumble with hard force. NOT JUST BEEF/PORK FAT. So all the ground is garbage!!! Now today after thawing a "roast" I uncovered a piece of meat I feel bad feeding to my dog. Absolutely inedible. Covered in hair, dirt, thick layer of fat and what looks like hide. It literally looks like a pair of tar filled lungs. A roast shouldn't be able to unfold. Disgusting! An entire year of meat for my family is trash. Out the money we paid to have it butchered and now we have to buy meat from the store AND EXTREMELY ANGRY I won't be able to eat meat until next fall. BEWARE!

stay away from this place!
service is HORRIBLE  for leased and car owners. 
I had a bad experience with both and it is not worth your energy.
They write nice answers to your bad review but the reality is - they do not care! therefore, nothing changes after their " nice response to your complaints." This is the type of place where they charge for something that you do not even need. Complete rip off! Stay away from this place. Save yourself the energy and money.

Having been away from Philly for awhile, I don't know all the good local places, so my family picked Bistro Romano for my birthday. I'm glad they did. I can't imagine how there are any bad reviews of this place- to start, its old city charm and coziness perfectly match the charm of the surrounding neighborhood. We had outstanding service- not rushed but attentive. The artichoke appetizer was excellent, and we all enjoyed the tableside-prepared Caesar salad. The table had a mix of veal and pasta for entrees and everyone enjoyed their dinners. My veal saltimbocca wasn't the best veal i've ever tasted, but it was very good- simple but flavorful and well cooked. We also enjoyed our desserts, and it's always nice when things are made in house. I would give Bistro Romano an extra half star if I could. It was classically good fresh food in a nice atmosphere with good service. I'd definitely go back if I were in town again.

Walk me in here blindfolded and when you yank it off this could be ANY BAR USA.  Wait a minute; I see A Villanova banner on the wall.  I must be near Philly.  Wait a minute again; last year I was in Alaska and saw a Villanova banner so who the hell knows now days.

The bar/restaurant design folks have their act together and they know a lot of folks like this style so they give it to us.

* You got your slightly dim lighting.
* Plenty of wood just slightly beat up.
* A very long bar in this case topped with copper.
* Zillions of TVs.
* Sports and college memorabilia everywhere.
* A line of cozy booths.
* Lots of taps and lots of bottles.  Over all a very decent selection of brews.
*  A pub grub menu that won't break the bank.

Like I said, it's ANY BAR USA.  So is there anything that makes this place different?  Yes.

* Unbelievably great wings. Six will set you back $6.99 and twelve for $11.99.  Go for the dozen and try the Peanut Thai variety.  Awesome!

* Head downstairs to the Bottle Shop and peruse over 200 varieties of suds.  If you like, mix and match your own six pack.

Special note- Some folks either visit here or avoid visiting here because they think the name and logo denotes just a seafood restaurant.  Yes there is seafood here but the menu has many choices for carnivores and other folks.

I can see where a beer aficionado might go four stars here but I'm going for a solid three.

This place does not wear gloves! The manager does not appreciate your business and is beyond rude instead of being grateful at a time like this. Food was delivered leaking and the delivery guy did not have gloves on. This was my LAST order placed to these ungrateful "business". Be aware people!

I hate to rate this too harshly, but I was really underwhelmed. We had dinner which cost about the same price for 2 as ordering takeout with leftovers. I like the idea of variety, but the quality of food here just didn't compare to our normal takeout place. Also, they didn't have some favorites of ours like orange chicken or plain white rice. They did, however, have the Mongolian BBQ. But, they didn't open it until 5 which I didn't know so I had already grabbed a plate of food that seemed like it had been sitting out for awhile. The BBQ is good, but I wouldn't go here as my first choice for the BBQ. I will say it was pretty busy so maybe others really like this place.

The lunch buffet is just really bland and lackluster. The saag (spinach) was watery and the basmati was overcooked. Luckily it was only 8.95 or I'd be complaining. Seem like nice folks; not trying to knock them. Just being honest. Could get some basmati and paneer pouches from the grocery store, microwave them and get the same effect.  Meh.
I've been here a few times but always forget to write a review. Even though I love wings, the waiting time was pretty long for a not-so-busy restaurant at the time.

I feel sorry for this place because the owner is a hot head and felt the need to degrade me for returning a purchase. Saying "never return here" "it cost me money to return your items" . Clearly sexist and racist owner. If you are unhappy with your return Policy then you should change it, not yell and disrespect your costumers for simply following your policy. Sad.

I loved the environment when I first walked in. The staff was so nice and helpful. My brother tried almost every flavor because of his indecisiveness haha but the staff was pleased to help him find the right flavor for him. 

This place has the best ice team Ive ever had. The cones were delicious and the service was quick.
I love the silver taste testing spoons.

A decent spot for tacos, burritos, and bowls just steps away from all the theatres on Broad Street / Avenue of the Arts.

If you choose a taco plate, you can mix and match certain meats, so I chose the carnitas, pastor, and barbacoa. All these meats had a good amount of flavor to them and came reasonably portioned. You can also choose from a variety of add-ons like lettuce, cheese, and sauces. 

It's sort of like Chipotle, but locally run and reasonably fresh. Seating is communal, and even though the staffers appeared to be dissing patrons right in front of them, I didn't have the energy to summon my college-era Spanish-language skills and listen closely to what they were really saying.

Great stop for a souvenir for that cigar lover in your life.. the old guys are rolling them right in front of you here. ( see pic)
We were celebrating so my husband was happy

It was raining in the city -- not a hard rain -- a soft rain that looks like tears, if the city had a heart. But it never does. The mist just wells up over the shingles until it spills onto the sidewalk, slowly washing the dreams of the desperate into the sewer. 

It was that kind of day.

"How's tricks?" I imagine she asked, as my companion and I entered. She was worth a stare. She was trouble. She led us to a table in back, illuminated only by a low-wattage wall sconce.

I came to TM for the lunch special, a chicken wrap with a side of slaw, a wrap so large I couldn't eat it all, a wrap so big I boxed half to feed the hunger that would gnaw at my stomach later that day.  

Why didn't I order the BBQ pulled pork? Some things you do, and some you don't. My business associate liked it. If it was good enough for him, then it was good enough for me. But I didn't order it, see?

Three Monkeys was a dry place, a warm place, a place where I could forget the stinging chill and the endless gray outside. A place where I could grab a bite, share a story, and know that its dimly lit walls would forgive: would see no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil.

Then I had to go. Whatever life there was for me, it was waiting out there. I wasn't much for long goodbyes. So I left, I beat it, I scrammed.

-jh, after genre "film noir": not proud, not my best work

The current manager here, a young girl named Brittany, is an extremely inappropriate nuisance and is downright harassing.  She calls approximately 3 days in advance to confirm scheduled appointments. Completely reasonable and understandable. We always politely confirm.  She then proceeds to call several times a day to re-confirm, including leaving multiple nasty voicemails telling her you NEED to call her back and threatening to cancel your appointment if you do not call her back and confirm AGAIN. I received 3 of these voicemails in a 2-day time span, which I have saved on my phone. The exact same thing happened either last February with my other daughter's photo shoot, or last October for this daughter's last shoot. Brittany called me about 4 or 5 times, asking for re-confirmation each time. I finally called the 1-800-345-6019 to describe again this girl's behavior and request to have the district manager call me so I can relay this information to him.  He/she has yet to call me back. We have been customers for years, coming twice a year for both of our daughters' annual birthday photo shoots, and we have never once been a no-show.  Nick was a wonderful manager who never treated customers this way. This is just crazy, unprofessional behavior and needs to be stopped immediately, as it has been going on for months now.

Was told that i could wait at bar during my 20 minute table wait and have a drink by hostess.  I made my beer order and watched 4 tables get drink in front of me.  Then told that i can only get a drink when my table is assigned.  Ibwalked out!

I had a coupon for a 2 for 1 deal for lunch.  Usually we don't eat lunch out but I had a bit of shopping to do at Safeway so it seemed like a good opportunity to get the deal.  I must say the system they have is really good.  The manager is the one who took our order.  I have seen him many times before and he has always been very pleasant.  It came within 5 min. and was tasty.  I had the lox, cream cheese with fresh capers, red onion on a toasted bagel.  Hubby had the turkey, bacon, mayo. Both were good.  For me the  prices are high for a lunch.  It would have been over 15 bucks for lunch but with the coupon we both enjoyed our lunch for 7 bucks.  Can't beat this deal.  Was so full, we had no dinner that night.

I love this stand. 
It may be small. 
It may be hard to find. 
It has a Ton of flavors annd its open from 1-9. 
Score.

While the work that Bright Now Dental has done with my teeth has been exemplary, and the staff are incredibly friendly, their customer service regarding wait times and cost is horrible. During my first appointment, the hygienist disappeared at the end of my appointment for at least 20 minutes without telling me where she went. Prior to my root canal, I was advised of one price for the root canal and crown. When I returned for the temporary crown, which I was told would take one hour, I was charged an additional amount for the crown. At this time it was too late so I had to pay. Granted they changed my loan to same as cash but I was there for almost TWO hours and had to rush back to work. Today I arrived 15 minutes early in hopes to get done quickly and sat unacknowledged in the waiting room for almost an hour. Unfortunately I have paid for my crown so I have to go back to get it completed. I called and spoke with the manager, who said he'd return my call right away...that was over 30 minutes ago. So while everyone is nice and they do good dental work, you had better schedule at least 3 hours to get your appointment completed.
I love the food, the service is wonderful, and the atmosphere is great for every and all ages. Great place!

Great and fast. They use cotton thread which is a plus so it doesn't feel like the thread is slicing your brow. 

Unfortunately I don't remember what girl k had but she did an amazing job. Even brows :). And she was super fast which is a bonus because I don't like sitting in a chair for 10 mins holding my eyebrow. The girl I went to say in the first chair when you walk in if that helps.

Food is overpriced for mediocre at best food. Sad excuse for Italian food. The only reason I even bother coming back here is because even with two stars it's better then half the food in Deptford. This town needs to clean house and get real food. Honestly an Olive Garden is just as appetizing as this at half the price. Seriously. Just go to an Olive Garden. You won't tell the difference. 50 dollars for two apps (soggy mushrooms) a beer and a cocktail. Gtfo

Food is great but I won't be back.   I was there with my son and it was crowded.   We took the only open table which could seat six.    There were three young people waiting for a table.   The hostess was incredibly rude and asked us to sit at the bar, which had three open seats, so they could have the table.   They were more important than us how????   I'll never go back!!

I would give zero if I could. My sisters and I all had appointments and two of three of us were helped right away. I waited almost 30 minutes until someone came over to the chair. My sister had someone start on her and then without saying anything just started cleaning up and then went over to her appointment to help someone else. My sister sat there for 15 minutes without anyone saying anything. No one apologized or explained the situation. They're pretending they don't speak English to get out of it and have zero responsibility for serving their customers. RUDE

1.5 stars

SW is wrong. Bad customer service is THE standard.

I've been to Innisbrook before, and I attended meetings there. This time I was a so-called "guest."

I just left this property today, after spending five days there for a conference. Be warned: if you are considering other properties, take it from a former Tampa Bay resident of eight years, please go to those. There are far superior properties in the area (Saddlebrook, for starters). 

A few bullet points:

*Innisbrook is located off the extremely unattractive US 19. The actual grounds are lovely, but that's where it ends. It's an ugly drive in, and an ugly drive out.

*The resort is extremely dated. They put about $26 million dollars into the common areas - two restaurants and a lobby area. Yes, attractive. They should have put that into the lodges. The suites are something straight out of a Golden Girls episode. The rooms are a bit musty as well.

*Getting anywhere you need to go and requiring a shuttle to get there-very inconvenient. (However, this isn't a complaint as this is a resort and they are set up that way. Rather, it's a warning to those who don't want to have to rely on shuttles to get anywhere).

*The largest pool, The Loch Ness, was closed early on a weekend day for a private party. It was nice, definitely NOT quiet nor peaceful. Very child-friendly. So we went to a smaller pool because of the early closing.  Eh. It was ok. Small, indeed. The service was horrible at the cafe' located at the Loch Ness pool. I asked the guy for a smoothie and he looked at me liked I asked him to go to China for sushi. We had brought our towels with us from Loch Ness to the Copperhead pool and everyone asked us where we got them. Apparently they weren't providing towels at other pools.

*The one highlight of the entire resort is the Osprey restaurant and our server Daniel. Each night the meals were quite good and Daniel was excellent. Packard's, the steak restaurant was also good. A bit stuffier, but good all the same. There was an unfortunate incident with one of the meeting attendees having glass in her meal. 

*The overall service was extremely poor. Despite knowing our group was coming, the staff was completely unprepared. Approximately fifty of our attendees waited hours for their room keys - after normal check-in time and were offered no refreshments or words other than "it'll be a few minutes and housekeeping doesn't have your room ready yet." Another attendee got to his room and it hadn't even been made up. The bed linens were not even changed.

*The meeting space is ample, however, it's dank and musty. 

Overall the only reason you'd go there is to golf. And eat. And golf some more. So I suppose that for people who actually golf this place could work. But if you actually like good customer service and nice accommodations - go elsewhere.

This place deserves zero stars. It's a shame because they used to have really great service and many in my family and significant other's family purchased vehicles from them. Well, not any more.
My boyfriend's truck was having trouble for a few weeks. It was a bit scary and we were driving together and it would switch gears real quick. My boyfriend gave John Kennedy Ford a call and they said to bring it in, saying we would need to drop if off for a few days. His transmission was messed up and he told them we would need a rental car if the truck needed to be there overnight. The truck is under warranty, and should not be having transmission troubles under 38K miles. He had the phone on speaker to take down any helpful information and I could hear the first person he spoke with was  a real nice older gentleman. He was told us he would need to bring the VIN # .  We found the # and called back and were placed on the phone with another staff, Jim Mullen. He told us that we would not be getting a rental car free of charge regardless if the truck was covered under warranty. We went back and a forth a bit, so my boyfriend figured he would come in as it's much easier to talk in person.

He went  in and spoke to another gentlemen at a computer. Told him the story and he was very polite and kind and said sure he would look in to it. Not a few seconds after that, Jim Mullen came charging out of his office proclaiming "No, I talked to you on the phone and told you that you cannot get a rental car"  He had absolutely no sense of customer service and I am utterly shocked that he not only works there but manages the store. He came charging out as if he was stopping a thief. When simply, we were trying to obtain a rental car for a few days while the car we had that had under 38,000 miles had a bad transmission. We were then told by another gentleman that we could get the rental car reimbursed if it was the transmission. Odd, because Jim never mentioned this once, yet he pompously raised his voice and proclaimed we would not get one.
It's not the fact that we did not receive the rental car, which we also find frustrating as the car is under warranty,  but the way Jim presented himself; an utter lack of communication, interpersonal, and customer service skills. 
 Shockingly he is the supervisor as well and kept on informing him of this over and over he was saying " I am the boss. And after me is John Kennedy himself and I know him really well"  
I am not sure if Jim was having a bad day, or if he regularly treats customers this way. I have worked in the customer service industry for a long time, and rule of thumb is that customers are always correct. You work with your customers to make them happy...especially if they and their family have purchased many products from you (we were at about 6 cars in 6 years)
They've lost not only my business but my family's. ...oh and very recent business. We bought a new truck today from another Ford dealership. One that was much more accommodating and agreed the truck had transmission troubles.

I don't see why this place is so poorly rated. I wouldn't compare this place to the small mom and pop authentic Italian restaurants as it's a chain restaurant but the food is pretty good. Our first visit in December wasn't terrible but wasn't great either. Mainly service related issues. I filled out an online survey stated on the receipt about my experience and the manager contacted me to make it right (which is more than I can say about restaurants that actually really do suck really bad!). My husband and I returned last night for our anniversary. Everything was excellent. We had made reservations and were seated immediately. The restaurant was steady busy at 7 pm yet we did not have to wait on our server to come and greet us. He was prompt and friendly. His name is William and he is an excellent waiter. The manager, Jason, is also excellent. Greeting customers and making sure things are running smoothly. We didn't wait long at all for our appetizer, drinks, main courses and dessert to be served. Everything tasted great. I ordered the same appetizer and meal as the first visit and it was even better this time around. I recommend the Crispy Shrimp Napoli appetizer, the Petite Filet & Grilled Shrimp steak which is served on a romano crusted tomato and bed of lightly sauteed FRESH spinach (sooo goood) and their DELICIOUS cheesecake. The Rustica salad is really good also. My husband and I will be returning!

Definatelt the best sushi, salad, soup, lo mein and pad Thai I've ever had in my life. Great prices, fantastic food, it's never crowded (can't figure out why!) Staff is friendly....nothing bad to say. This place is awesome!!!

I am floored to see the abysmal reviews considering I have found this location quite decent. I had sworn off Dunkin' about a year ago when I started to see various locations continuously get my orders wrong. This one was different. Today I went to this location, and they got the order correct.

I ordered a egg white croissant sandwich, with maple bacon and white cheddar, and a chai latte; the sandwich was on-point. The chai latte was almost on-point had the hot chai latte had been hotter than warm; If the drink isn't almost burning my esophagus at first sip, it isn't hot enough. By the time I was done, it was room temperature--not good. I deducted a star for that, but I'm sure it was a one-time fluke.

Service is prompt and efficient, if a tad cold and indifferent. It is very obvious that they (Dunkin') are not as picky about the types they hire as Starbucks, Dutch Bros, and the like--hence the lower price and lesser perceived overall quality.

I ordered vegetable fried rice and told on the phone no meat specifically. The person who answered the call did not have patience to listen and she immediately hung up after getting my phone number when i was stressing i want NO meat. When i got the food, i was shocked to see bits of meat in my order. I threw it out and did not like it this time. When your customer requests something and you cannot make it don't commit. Atleast wash the freakin dishes you cook with. I am not going there anymore.

It's OK. Some people can't stop talking about how great this place is but that just shows an extreme lack of intelligence. The chicken salad is mediocre. Nothing compared to homemade. The mac and cheese had little to no flavor.
food was very good,specially the grilled Mackarel,im a big fan now...cheers and more power...

Here's my feedback regarding the recent 2 night stay at this resort.

1) I had called your hotel on 4/19/2020 and spoke with someone (did not get the person's name, but it was male) about whether your pool was going to be open, he specifically said that the pool at the hotel is open. This is mis-information that was given to customers, as upon arrival, I was informed that the pool had been closed for 1.5 months. I chose your hotel because I thought the pool would be open, but in fact, it was closed. What a disappointment.
2) Upon check in on 4/28/2020 morning, Michael assisted us with finding an ocean view room. When we arrived at room 30X, the ocean view is really NOT an ocean view because right outside our balcony - is the largest tree ever that blocks our entire view of the ocean. Please also note that when we arrived to our room, the floor was covered with at least 5-6 pieces of dog kibble. Makes me feel like the room was unkept and unclean. Is this how a 4-star hotel should be?
3) I had to contact the front desk and spoke with Anna to request a room change. This was not an easy request to satisfy either, because she gave me some options such as moving over the to Eucalyptus building 2nd floor, and would not provide a specific room number. I had to personally walk over to the 2nd floor of that building, and look out at the view and realized that there were again multiple trees blocking the ocean. I had to go meet her at the front desk to request for another 3rd floor room near room 30X, and she gave me some excuses such as your hotel doesn't allow people to move rooms during their stay, and that she can't find any other rooms available on the 3rd floor, etc. Honestly, it's just a bunch of excuses and you guys are making it MORE DIFFICULT THAN IT SHOULD BE. Customers are here to enjoy, relax and spend time in a soothing environment, but all I get is a room with a partial ocean view, and NOT what I had paid for. I informed Anna to please note in my reservation that for the next morning, I would like the front desk to find me a room near 30X that doesn't have an obstructed view. I was hoping that I could at least have 1 day where my view was truly unobstructed.
One hour later, Anna calls me back and offers Room 30Y, which she said is open and available now. I had to go downstairs and get the keys from her so that I can FINALLY move into a true ocean view room around 4 pm. What makes this situation so odd is that Michael told me that you guys are at 20% capacity, so how can there be no rooms for me to move into with a proper ocean view room?!
4) On 4/29/2020 morning, I am told by the front desk lady (and also Michael said the same thing at check-in) that I am able to redeem my free wine for a juice / milk at the Java corner. The Hispanic lady at the java corner (did not catch her name) refused to give me a simple milk carton, because she said she can only do "coffee". Then she proceeded to say that I did not have a mask on and cannot serve me. I told her that I only needed her to point out where the milk was located, and that I am NOT needing any "serving" by her. She was quite curt, rude, and just very poor service. I finally retrieved the milk carton on my own, and placed the wine card at the counter. This leaves a distaste in my mouth after interacting with her. Why does every interaction with your staff have to be so difficult?
5) I am currently a Gold member with Hilton Honors, but because I used Capital One points to book this reservation, I am not awarded any Hilton Honors points, and I don't get credit for staying here for 2 nights, which don't count towards my Gold status nights accrued. This is rather disappointing. I also offered to NOT have my room serviced during my 2 nights, so that I can earn 1,000 Hilton Honors points for being "eco-friendly" but was told by Anna that I am not eligible. Again, a disappointment. I feel no sense of appreciation from Hilton, even though I have a Gold member status. What a joke.

I really wanted to enjoy this hotel and gave it a chance, even though your reviews on Yelp are so low. But now I feel like I understand why the reviews are only 2.5 stars overall on Yelp. 

Some things I did enjoy about your hotel are:
- 2 hour bike rental, but this was basically covered by my resort fee
- the fact that your location is across from the beach with easy access, but your hotel does not offer any beach umbrellas
- there are charging ports for electric vehicles, which is essentially paid for by parking fees

The food at Cafe Alessio is very tasty but we have been here many times over the years where the service is terrible. This weekend was another one of those times. My DH and I ordered salads with extra lettuce and he ordered his with bacon and dressing on side. None of these requests was honored. I wonder if it was because the server was "too good" to write down our order. Granted, there was an event in Doylestown which attracted a lot of people but they have been in business a long time and certainly could have planned for a larger crowd.

They remade DH's salad without the bacon and dressing on side. Then, when we repeated that we would like extra lettuce, they said they are almost out of lettuce and couldn't accomodate this request. Really? On a summer Sunday evening they had no more lettuce? There are plenty of supermarkets in Doylestown who would have been happy to sell boxes of organic lettuce to them.

Each time we've been here, there has been more of an issue with the servers than the kitchen, although this time it was a combination. It's too bad because it is one of the few restaurants in the area with pleasant outdoor seating and an extensive menu.
Excellent service, cute restaurant, the food was fantastic and not over priced.  Short rib ragu was the best. Fresh baked garlic rolls.

I love ramen. I get so excited when I find new places to have ramen. So I was super excited when a friend told me that St Louis had some ramen shops and he dropped this name. When I arrived and saw the menu my first thought was, if you're going to have ramen in your name, how are you going to only offer 4 ramen choices?

No matter, I ordered the seafood and my wife ordered the Vista ramen (pork).

Let me say the atmosphere was nice and the service was great, my only complaints were the food.

Also ordered the empanada appetizer. Many different flavors that simply didn't mesh well together. It was an A for effort and a C or maybe even a C- for execution.

The seafood ramen was soy/salty....not savory, not delicious, not "Omg I can't wait to come back and eat this again!" and unfortunately not very flavorful, the smoked shrimp that it advertised are ground up and laid on top which makes them unrecognizable as shrimp of any kind and the shrimp cakes that were added were also not really flavorful either. I could have made better seafood ramen for $16 than what I was served. My number 1 pet peeve is going out for food that I could have cooked better and for less. 

My wife's broth was pork flavored but not in a good way, sadly.

I've eaten at some amazing ramen places and always get excited about tying new ones, some hit home runs others don't.

This is a ramen shop that I'll not return to as there's nothing left to try when 50% of the ramen menu falls as flat on your palette as this did.

I ordered $700 worth of blinds from the Riverview home on Bloomingdale Rd.   They promised me that they would be and in 2 weeks when they actually took about 6 weeks to come in. I had one special order that was correct the remainder of the blinds were supposed to be cut to size.  They had the measurements and when I came in to get them the 1st time..  They gave me uncut  blinds.  I did not know they were the  wrong size until their installer told  me so.  I had to haul all of them back.

a.kitchen was very good this time around. The space is beautiful and the service is excellent. I came with a friend for restaurant week. This is one of the few restaurants in which restaurant week is a GOOD deal. Our dishes, if purchased on a "regular" night, would have cost upwards of $50 per person, plus alcohol. So the restaurant week price of $35 was excellent. The food was quite tasty - started with the shrimp, then the fluke, then the hen of the woods mushrooms, and finished with the olive oil cake. My friend did the shrimp, chorizo meatballs, roasted chicken, and olive oil cake. The flavors of all the dishes were very good, and everything was cooked perfectly. But I must say, I don't know if I would've been thrilled had I paid full price. As many others have said, it's expensive and the portions are small. The wines and cocktails are expensive (though I did learn last night that they are all organic). The only time I am okay with expensive and small is if the flavors are truly mindblowing, and a.kitchen's just weren't. I am excited to see the changes made after Eli Kulp gets his hands on the menu...given that High Street on Market is our favorite restaurant, I will definitely revisit a.kitchen after Chef Kulp makes modifications. This place has huge potential - gorgeous decor, excellent servers, and perfect location!
Stand in line for 30 mins while the manager gets his head out of his ass, just to told only room for leaguers. Don't waste your time, you probably won't get a lane. This place really sucks!

I'm really shocked to see some of the rave reviews, or maybe I came on a bad night? Went for dinner one night and I had fried shrimp and my friend had turkey, both were just o.k. but the side dishes that came with them were really my issue, yuck! Maybe breakfast is better! Seeing the crowds and the lines at this place made me think it was something really special, so disappointing, I guess a diner is a diner after all.  Maybe I'll try coming for breakfast sometime.
This stuff is true Thai food cuisine. My grandparents use to live in Thailand and my grandfather gave his approval. Yummy food, good service and it really quaint and homey.

This place is horrible! I've had problems with the lenses and frames the past several months and have had to go back there several times to have them fixed. I was assured by the People there it was the glass company. They also are closed a lot of the  time. I can't tell you how many times I've gone there and they are closed for the day or early or in the middle of the day. So unprofessional. 

But today my husband who bought his glasses 6 months ago from this place brought them back to drop them off because he was on military leave for the weekend. They were falling apart ...the screws on the hinges that hold the temples on the frames kept coming out and they fell off his face and hit the pavement and got scratched. So when we walked in we explained to the new lady behind the counter that my husband was on leave and we wanted to leave them here and he wouldn't be taking them with him ( he was being sent over seas until May of next year and had been gone since August and this was his first leave home since August) so now that that was explained to her she automatically said she didn't think the lenses would be replaced and frames fixed. So I said to her that we just got them and it should be covered under warranty and i also explained that my glasses got scratched up within the year I had them and as long as it was in the one year I got brand new lenses. That's how our insurance works. So she then said that that's not how it works and the insurance company and lense makers will try and not replace them. So I asked her to please look up his information. So she proceeded to give us more excuses. 

So my husband who had to get ready to leave to go back to his base within the hour said to her "ma'am I really don't have time for this if you just look up the policy and my information and see that it should be covered and I'll leave them here. Don't tell me what they are going to say or do or not do ....just send them back". We were so irritated that we weren't being accommodated .....they were trying to not do any leg work to send them back and trying to find any excuse to not send them back. That's how it appeared. No if, ands or buts about it. 

Well as soon as the owner Dr. Savochka heard the lady behind the counter get annoyed with my husband not wanting a run a round and rightfully frustrated. He came over and reprimanded my husband like a child. Told him that irritating talk or raising of voices were not allowed in his store. I was like WTH?! Well that pissed us off even more. He said this is my store and my business and He won't tolerate us getting upset or angry with him or his workers. So my husband said that well sir that's not acceptable. I bought a pair of glasses from you six months ago and they are falling apart. I shouldn't be hearing excuses from you or your workers. And he also told him that his attitude was not acceptable as a business owner. Which was absolutely true. So then Dr. Savochka told my husband to leave the store or he was going to call the police. Lol Dr. S was rude and so disrespectful I couldn't believe what I was hearing  and seeing. My husband was upset and pissed off but he didn't threaten the guy, didn't touch him or even say he was or even do anything that was remotely threatening to him or his store. I was like is this guy for real?  

 So I told my husband to wait outside. This guy wasn't worth him being upset. And that I would handle it. So the doctor and I had some words and at one point he threatened to call the cops on me. I said give me a break. I said to him I can't believe you treat military service members like that and calling the cops over something like this especially on an active duty military person was ridiculous and he said so what and who cares. So this guy has zero respect for military people. I then calmly asked him a question and  guess what? He yelled at me. So i said to him let me get this straight ....you can yell and raise your voice but I can't as the customer? He said nothing. I asked him 3 more times and he didn't answer ...So I then called him a hypocrite for yelling and he said sarcastically "you're right. " I know he didn't mean it. 

I will never ever go there again to purchases glasses. And always remember those who seem so nice and accommodating ....you're not always seeing who they really are all the time. I truly saw Dr. Savochka's true colors  today and they were down right awful. I cannot stress enough that if there's a problem and you are not happy with the service or product you purchase here.... These people will give you attitude, excuses and God forbid you get upset or angry. He will call the cops on you. Totally pathetic. I give their business another year of that.

This place is mediocre, at best. I have been here twice and was honestly not impressed with the food either time. You might be drawn to this place purely by the decorations, if you are huge anime fan. I mean, they give you your receipt inside a volume of Naruto manga. That would totally tickle the fancy of certain demographics. 

All the drinks are named after Naruto characters and the ramen is decent for the price. You won't be blown away by it but you sort of get what you pay for. Their sushi rolls aren't anything amazing and not rolled perfectly. I've had the pork buns twice and once it came out watery. The sauce was watery and had spread all over the plate and made the bun soggy. Not very satisfying at all. 

There are cheaper and much better tasting food in Chinatown, PA. If you want good Japanese food, I would suggest you try your luck at Bubble Fish.

When I'm looking for a burger fix, Burgerhaus doesn't come to mind. I've only been once, but from that experience alone, I haven't felt the need to go again. My burger was dry and unseasoned. No salt, pepper, onion powder, herbs, nothing. I ordered it medium but it was served well done. The fries were decent, to their credit. Burgers are so simple to execute that I really don't give a lot of leeway to restaurants I order them from.

I enjoy the uniqueness of their menu. I love Mexican food but get bored by the sameness offered at most restaurants. I usually get takeout and the food easily makes it to my house. I love their street corn as a side. The food is made fresh as ordered. Service is quick even during busy times.

I went out to dinner it was a little busy and didn't mind waiting, but it took 40 minutes from when we sat down to get appetizers. So then 20 minutes later we get our entrees which the sandwich part was delicious I was highly disappointed by the French fries. They were the softest excuse for fries ever, like barely attempting to make them suitable, no salt, no crunch, or crispiness to them at all. We literally couldn't eat them, as seen In the pics. I assume they were understaffed or just giving lousy service that night because we were only seen like three times about drinks and order. I hope you see this review and improve your service experience and revamp how you make your fires because it really ruined the whole experience.
I ordered a large jerk chicken platter jerk and two juices and they charged my card $28.00 the menu says $14.00 for a large
I had a wonderful experience there,the donoughts were incredible and the customer service was absolutely amazing!! I will definitely go again

I go to Hopsmith with friends often and it has always been amazing. I chose this location for a business gathering of friends and clients recently and I had a mixed opinion for the first time. 

I attempted to announce how many ppl would be attempting so the staff that I love could be prepared. Getting a hold of the event planner was very challenging.  I was eventually left a message after several attempts, informing me that Hopsmith does not take reservations for brunch. 

I arrived early in preparation for the party I had planned. The hostess did the very best she good with the policy they have in place. She could only seat us for who was there. So coming early did nothing. Our party grew as expected. I truly feel if the event planner could have at least given her a heads up it would have meant a lot to her and her colleagues.  The best part is coming..

Our bartender, Brian, was beyond wonderful. He was solo in the basement area. That is where I told the establishment many times I was looking to be. I am sure none of the staff was told this. Brian was amazing, I can't stress this enough. Our large party felt catered to. The other party of 14 and parties of 6 did too. From beginning to end, Brian made everyones event special. 

This story, is just one story. I love this location. They all work hard. I would only recommend communication being an area of growth. The food is always amazing! The drinks are consistent. Please try  them.

First time eating here or I might even give it 5 stars. I will return and update this rating.

Very good diner style food with their own homemade recipes. They have a number of specialities especially on the breakfast menu which they serve until closing at 2pm. I had a Wild West scramble with their own homemade salsa and a side of hot cakes. Everything was terrific. The staff is friendly with very attentive service. 

Been at least a dozen times now for breakfast. All their scrambles are good. Huevos Rancheros are excellent. And yes, if you like hotcakes, not pancakes, they are terrific

I am visiting from California and needed my shellac redone. The girl was very informative and taught me a lot about the best shellac products. I am very pleased and will definitely return when I come back to safety harbor!! A++ Polished!!

No complaints though I thought the food would have been better. I had the "La Completa Cubana," which offers a taste of a lot of Cuban dishes. Pork was good though I thought the Empanada de Picadillo was overcooked. I love the black beans, yellow rice, and plantains. But I could have done without the yucca. I had my first mojito. It was recommended by my server but it was nothing great. Stick to margaritas. We also had the Mahi Mahi "Veradero." I thought it looked OK if you like meatier types of fish. Also had Florida's Cigar City IPA. OK for beer in a can. No dessert. The view at the top of The Pier in St. Petersburg. was very nice.

I use this park as part of my outdoor fitness rotation (mostly for jogging). Cooper River  runs through Pennsauken, Cherry Hill, Collingswood and Haddon Township. The park is bounded by North and South Park Drives, Route 130 and Grove Street. 

Fellow Yelpers (Christopher R) review on  8/31/2010 sums up the park very well. 

I have noticed additional porta-potties throughout the park. A centralized bathroom is also available at the Jack Curtis Stadium (next to the cinder track). 

I appreciate the monuments located at the  Memorial Grove  (Medal of Honor, Fallen Officer, etc.)

As a note, if you cross over the intersection/corner  of Cuthbert Blvd and N. Park Dr. (where  Parkway Miniature golf is located), you will find a less traveled section of the  park. (this is where I usually go).  Pass the Parkway Miniature golf on the right & continue down to the far end of North Park drive; you will come to a dog park ('Pooch Park'). Continue on the dirt path and you will see the back of the Cherry Hill National Guard Armory. (area is a  little sketchy late at night, be careful).  Continue on the paved path and then cross over Grove Street. You will then enter a long track of woods that has cool trails for trail running. (goes all the way to Kings Highway, connects to Maria Barnaby Greenwald Memorial Park and Hopkins Pond).


Overall, I like this park. Camden County just announced a proposal for a $23 million dollar upgrade to the park. Here is the architectural vision plan 
http://www.camdencounty.com/sites/default/files/files/1132-cooper%20river%20booklet.pdf.  for the park. (.pdf file).  It is actually a pretty cool read. 

Hopefully this upgrade  comes to fruition and helps to better the park. We shall see..........

ps: Don't forget to visit Moore Brothers Wine Company on Park Drive http://www.yelp.com/biz/moore-brothers-wine-company-pennsauken

I'm so frustrated!  I normally would not shop at Dierbergs b/c they are so expensive.  However, I noticed that they had more to choose from during this Covid situation, so I decided to try an online order.  One problem is that, while shopping online, they only provide ingredient information for half of their items.  This information is extremely important to see for people with food allergies.  The next problem is that, after you spend an hour and half doing your 2 week online shopping AND input your credit card information, your order is not allowed to go through b/c delivery nor pick up is available.  

I completely understand that this is a time of unusual circumstances.  I would've been happy to have been placed in a cue, or able to complete my order and then have them phone me with the upcoming availability.  I would even be understanding about having to wait a few days.  I have no problem shopping ahead of time.  But none of these were options.  My shopping cart, that I worked so hard on, was in limbo.  I used the contact support link and was taken to a chat with Shipt, the service they use.  After a complete 2 hours of waiting for an assistance to attend me, I gave up.  It continually gave the messages: "Hello, we're experiencing a high volume of chats, with delays up to 40 minutes. Thanks for your patience.  Connection Lost Waiting for agent..."  

I was trying to do the right thing by obeying recommendations and staying out of the stores.  Oh, well.  I guess I'll go to Schnucks now.

Not pleased at all with the curb side service for 2 weeks in a roll I have called multiple times with no one answering the phone.  Called Smyrna location they always answer right away and will continue to get my service.

On any other day I would've given this place 5 stars because I am a regular and the food is delicious so this review is solely on my experience tonight. I placed my order over the phone per usual and they said it would take 30-45 mins for my food to arrive. It took about an hour and I live less than 5 mins from the location. When I received my food my fries were soggy and my sandwich was cold and my chocolate cake was practically melted. This never happens when I order from here my food is usually super fresh and arrives in a timely manner. I called the store to express my disappointment and to get the situation rectified. Danny proceeds to tell me that "no matter where I get my fries from I will always receive them cold or soggy". I told him that I beg to differ because I order food from them once maybe twice a week and my fries are always hot and crispy. He put me on hold several times during our phone call which only led to me becoming more frustrated because there was an easy solution to my problem and Danny made it seem like it was impossible to do. He finally agreed to remake my food but I still had to wait another 30-45 mins which I think is completely unacceptable. I hope in the future they will train their employees on proper phone etiquette and how to rectify situations such as this so that regular customers such as my self can continue to support the business.

Absolutely love this place! The salsa is delicious and the tacos al pastor is my absolute favorite thing! I have loved every single thing I have ever ordered and the staff is always so nice. My toddler who is a picky eater loves it too!

A little greasy but full of flavor!  Jewel's noodle kitchen is a unique Chinese restaurant in Tucson.  They have a very small menu and there is not much to choose from when it comes to veggies.  The chef makes all their noodles from scratch, but there are only 3 to 4 noodle dishes on the menu.  It seems odd to have such a small noodle selection for a noodle house.  For 60 dollars we got noodles with pork bean sauce, beef with scallion pie, triple dumplings, 2 Chinese pork buns, 1 Chinese beef bun, and 1 green onion pancake delivered to my home.  I didn't know how big their portions were.  Lesson learned, this was way too much food for 2 people. My favorite dish was the beef scallion pie.  The buns and green onion pancake are unique to Tucson, normally you would have to travel overseas to eat something similar.  You will need a nice beer or red wine to pair with this greasy food.  Cheers!

When you come here - expect to wait and wait and wait and wait.  I had an 11:15 appointment and i wasn't seen until 11:50 - i literally had to go up to the nurse and be like "hi did you forget about me?" the time before when i had an apt for my annual i also had to wait 30 mins after my appointment.  

either way my doctor rules and she def helped me.  the staff is nice. i guess patience is a virtue.

This place is so steeped in history that you almost can't help but get caught up in the nostalgia. I had no interest in going here but my wife insisted. She really got this one right. 
This building is over 100 years old and anybody who's somebody has played here or wished they had. The list of singers, actors, shows, etc. who have occupied the stage is amazing. The Grand Ole Opy is only one of many who called this the ultimate stage.
The acoustics here are incredible. The only thing I have seen like it is the Hollywood Bowl. You can barely see the person and yet you hear them very well. A photographer was barking instructions to her subjects at stage front and we could hear her clear as a bell walking above the balcony seats.
We tried to book a show after our tour of the theater, but they were all sold out. So book ahead and rest assured it will be a treat!
Service bad and the food order was incomplete sent back twice and vegetables on the burgers not fresh. Unacceptable in my book

This is an Bavarian food, not a German restaurant and it's not very good. I'm German, and I cook waaaay better than this. Bad beer, bad wurst, and barely passable schnitzel. And no rouladen. Also, NO brotchen for the bratwurst! I went here when it first opened, and swore I'd not be back. But some friends wanted to go, so this summer (2020) I went back. Nope, still bad, not sure how they're still open.

I recently went back to Alibabas (on another groupon), and we had a great server who knew the menu well (a first). The food came out quickly and halfway through my meal, the owner came to our table with a pitcher of water(another first), asking how our meal was. Food is amazing, as always.

The plates can get expensive but if you're going out for lunch without some groupon-like discount, then get the Gyro Sandwich! Costs 4.99$ and is a full meal. 

Rating 4/5 stars because this is the first time I've had a good server, so I will raise it to 5 stars if it is the same next time I go.

I was mesmerized about how quick and efficient they are.  But the food quality wasn't great, not real Mexican in my mind.  I wasn't expecting gourmet.  Maybe something more along the lines of Chipotle or Qdoba.  But it just wasn't there.  However there is a wide variety to pick from.  Overall, it's somewhere I would go to again (for food only) if I was in the area but wouldn't go out of my way for.

Another issue...the bar.  This is my real issue with the place.  We had a party of 6 and I understand we are expected to wait on ourselves.  But the female bartender (white, probably in her 30's) was horrible!  She pretty much hates her job and lets everyone around her know.  She wasn't only rude to my party of people (who were eager to tip), she was rude to another gentleman at the bar while we were waiting to place our order.

This is going to sound nuts, but I had the best mushroom Swiss burger at this pancake place today. My coworker wanted breakfast, but I was craving a burger. Fortunately, the giant menu at Christina's had us both covered. Unfortunately, I left my phone at the office so you'll have to take my word for it, as I have no photographic evidence of my well-portioned burger. The platter came with fries or Mac-n-cheese and soup. I didn't want the soup, so asked for a side salad instead. No dice, unless I wanted to pay extra for it. That side salad would have likely bumped this review to 5 stars. Anywhoooo... our server Brenda was great. Very attentive. Quick on the drink refills without hovering. She really seemed to enjoy her job. The decor is homey without being kitschy and the environment was clean. I told my coworker we should put this back in the regular rotation.

Worst nail experience ever. 
I paid for a premium mani - pedi with regular polish on Sun, Aug 2nd. Three hours after leaving the salon my nails were not dry and smudged. 
I called on Wed evening, 8/5 and was told to come back to have them fixed. 
The salon manager I spoke with on the phone was not there when I arrived. I had to explain the entire situation again. The nail technician was rude, defensive and combative to the point of threatening to call the police. 
The manager was called and they agreed to re-do my nails with gel polish. Less than three days later, today, Sat 8/8 while scratching my leg, the gel polish lifted. 
I am not a nail expert however, there is something sub-par at this salon in either the application or poor products that they use.
I advise any potential patron to avoid this nail salon. Your time and money can be well spent at a better performing salon.

The worst hotel ever. The hotel amenities listed a bar and restuarant but that was false. It also listed a refrigerator and microwave in each room, again false. We had requested a double with 2 queen sized beds but both beds were full sized.The room was very small. The bathroom was dirty. The towels were old and thin. We had to go to the front desk for additional towels and toilet paper. The free breakfast was horrible. The coffee was lukewarm and the microwave was by the ice machine down the hall. The elevator got stuck several times. NEVER STAY AT THIS HOTEL!!!!

As an SPG lifetime gold and platinum I stay more nights than I'd like away from home. Checked in today and waiting in the lobby for 2 hours. At 6pm, room is still not ready and told it will be 7pm. Asked for another room then and checked into a very dark, tired (scuffs on walls, missing tile in bathroom, scratched up furniture). While waiting at lobby bar for room, they never entered my order.... waited then finally asked. Asked for water twice and still never got it. Finally asked for the check. Seems like the whole operation is drained of attn to detail or service. I will say, that Evan at the front desk was super, trying to help but issues seem more a lack of management. Next time I will try the le meridian a short distance away.
The worst publix deli Experience I've ever had. They are so slow and they skimp with the veggies. I hate when I don't pick up my lunch in Brandon before coming to work.

If you like craft beer and creative food this is a godsend for the area. Great environment and great service!

As far as food, the consensus seems to be that their sweet potato fries are good and tasty. You can pair them with a Buttermilk Fried Chicken/Seitan Sandwich, or a strong and meaty Bison Burger and more. They also have a tantalizing Italian Grilled Cheese and some Mussel dishes!

As far as seating, you have three choices; sidewalk seating, restaurant seating, and bar seating. I love the environment, it reminds me of a tiny castle. I mean this place was on the boob-tube partially because it's a nice and cozy place to film, I'm sure. They got that aged wood look going on; candles, antique lights, gutted pianos. 

*Tues open mic   *Wed quizzo 

*Only criticism would be the curry dipping sauce (it's apparently Tabasco dipping sauce but tastes like curry). I love Indian food, and I love this sauce, but it doesn't go with errrything. Another dipping sauce option would be a huge slam-dunkaroo.

we invited all family for dinner! And it took loooooong time to chili's prepare our food.They forgot one order!!!we were almost done when ther brought it!That was terrible!!! And i hope employees smile more!

Just left here for lunch. Ordered the stuffed jalapeños that were swimming in grease and tasted like a ball of salt. Asked the waitress if I could get something else instead. She responded that they don't let you get something else just because you don't like what you ordered. I said well it's greasy and doesn't taste good and she said, "That's the policy." Safe to say I won't be back  I used to come here all the time and get the pork vermicelli. Their not so stellar customer service has lost them a patron.

I wouldn't recommend ordering delivery from here - have no idea how dining there would be. We ordered the Vegan tofu scramble and seitan wings, and both were cold and hard.

Everything was separate in the scramble for some reason? The potatoes and tofu and peppers were all in their own corners of the delivery box. The potatoes seemed like they'd been taken from the freezer and then defrosted on the ride over.

The most edible thing was the bread we didn't realize we were getting. Have no idea how any of this was cooked, it's kind of fascinating, but also a waste of $25.
Great selection of beers from some of the best local breweries, helpful bartenders, and a cool outdoor patio. It's great having this place on the south end of town!

My family and I have been going here ONLY in the AM, better  Dim sum than others, we did try the other one down the street, not as good, if you are looking for typical Chinese food, there are others. The siomai, the chicken feet (yes!) and new dish: the calamari here is okay, eggplant is good,  our kids love it here and the nice fish in tank for .. Well, you know, lunch  you know what I mean-

Well 5 months later i tried them again.  I ordered my lunch take out with no onions or garlic as i am alergic.  I get back to work and of course it was in there.  When i looked at the receipt to see if it was atleast written and perhaps just a chef mistake, but it was not.  So the woman owner who took my order chose to ignore my diet request and in turn i pay her and not eat lunch  if the owner does not care to do the right thing the most important part the order, then why would anybody want to patronize such a place.  There are many places in the city who care about their customers.

While my experience was awful, it doesn't mean yours will be too. A couple things to keep in mind.

1- Get here early. Lines are crazy for non VIP. We arrived at 9:30pm. Lines for everything are over 30 minutes.

2- Drive slow leading up the the place. The roads are bumpy and your car will thank you for it. 

3- Wear sneakers, or boots. Standing in wet grass is not fun in flip flops.

-----------

Attraction wise, it was well thought out and the acting was authentic. Less hair touching is recommended and putting foreign objects down peoples shirts. 

Like everyone says, the Ghost Hunt is not worth it. Skip it.

I am new to town and stopped in here for lunch since it is near my work. What a lovely, eclectic place. It has such a modern and clean vibe to it, not a romantic place but a great place for lunch and to hang out with coworkers.

I had the grilled cheese sammie and I much say that it was amazing, perfectly cooked cripsy on the outside and gooey warm on the inside.

Gotta love the mural on the inside to, graphically clean. crisp and captivating stark in it's design.

My first place to eat at during my New Orleans Trip. Staff was very helpful and friendly. Carbs galore if you're into that sorta thing.Ordered a delicious breakfast and the Beignets with Mimosas! I would recommend this place but be advised it is quite small. Does have a nice small outdoor area too.

Nice big place with high roofs and 2 bars. It is a great place for a big group to hang out. You can also go up to the second floor bar and watch a game when it is on (I went there to watch Usain Bolt win his 6th gold medal)

Came here for a Wedding :) So this review will mainly focus on the place as an event location. We did get to try their Vodka, and whiskey. Both very good. Inside is a nice full bar with a couple of medium size rooms(our Buffett was set up in one) but the main focus is the huge patio. The crew setup a seating section for the ceremony and in maybe ten minutes after the ceremony was done they took it down and set up the dinning area on the same patio very efficient. My only complaint would be we actually couldn't hear the ceremony, not sure if they didn't provide a mic or the brides didn't want one but because of the traffic noise we couldn't hear the ceremony :( if I go back I may up my review because it's really a wonderful venue

17 Souls ...

     Where were you when you learned of Nine Eleven? 

     Some things we can never forget.  Nor should we ever want to. 

     The Garden of Reflection is a work of love, loss and remembrance.  The families of those who perished in that horrific attack, along with some very talented and dedicated people, came together to create a one of a kind setting for the somber, yet uplifting, task of bringing us together to Reflect on and Remember what happened on that day. 

      We feel a shared emptiness in our hearts from the loss of loved ones many of us knew.  We appreciate the powerful yet gentle flow of the park-like design that guides us along its paths ever closer to its center, the twin fountains in a pool made of concrete, stone and reflections of trees and sky.  We count the trees planted around the low walls of concrete and glass with so many names...

     This is a profoundly beautiful and haunting place. It echoes within, and calls out for love, peace, understanding, and never forget determination.

9.11.2013 - The Memorial Ceremony held here today was poignant and sad.  Finding and giving comfort was the message of the day from all the spiritual leaders asked to speak, pray, play music, and stand silently.  From the size of the crowd it is clear that we will always remember the national and personal losses we suffered that day 12 years ago. 

Teach your children well.

Dancers on the bar on saturday
Cover bands or live music

The city tap house is my favorite restaurant in university city. I love the burger but the service was even better. Deja served us and she was very quick at getting us what we needed while being very friendly.

Not impressed. Mediocre at best.  Service was extremely slow even though there were only 3 tables in the entire restaurant.  Had to ask for salsa and more chips.  Wasn't offered another sangria until the bill was presented.  Had to ask for silverware.  Our quest for a good Mexican restaurant will continue.
Best Sandwiches EVER. Get the #6!!!! The service is always good and nice. I've never had a better sandwich in my entire life

4/13/2019 (evening service). This was my first time sitting in this restaurant. I may have ordered car side before, but will no longer. This is the worst Chili's I have EVER been too. Quite frankly it's the worst looking restaurant I've been to in a while. It is so dirty looking, dirty windows and it even has an old smell. There were dishes everywhere on tables and the staff took their time taking care of it. Someone actually sat dishes on the median separating my table from another and it took them forever to notice this and remove it. I immediately wanted to walk out, but our kid wanted to eat there for their birthday. Then they actually forgot what I ordered and when it was received the fries were lukewarm and the skillet queso was watery. Quite shameful. Also the people bussing tables preferred to stand around and talk instead of clean. I will never be back to this location in any capacity. I sure wish I had stuck to Applebee's this evening. Go there, the food is better and it's clean.

Shrimp and grits
Good a little pricey 
But was real good 
Would look to get it on rice. 
The fried deviled egg. Are the boom 

Nice place to take some one special

Staff. Was nice and Fast 

This is 
better then
 a lot of the other high end place in town
Price gouging gas prices in a pandemic...its a fact and a shame...Stop it!!!!!!!!!!! You're a gas station that's why??????????????

Spent 4 days at a hotel next to this location. I like the atmosphere and food, But this location sorry to say sucks!. Friday night there were empty tables and nobody at the wait stand.
