Many staff passed us and would never acknowledge us. We finally seated ourselves, and waited more. (dates 1/10/17-1/12/17)
  While waiting I sat and watched of duty servers running around drinking yelling and making a total ass of themselves. We did flag someone down who reluctantly waited on us, We had the same type of wait for the bill.
We tried again Saturday with the same results this time we didn't bother, we left and went to another nearby restaurant. I ate every meal out  for 4 days only 1 at this Hooters location because it is a joke.. Spent the night in Macon Ga on the way home and went to Hooters on I believe Riverside dr. It was what you wold expect, Great food, Hot, the server was fast and attentive.. Hooters would do themselves a favor and close the Rivergate store as it gives the company a bad name.

Dave

More event than meal! 

Hanging out with friends one friday in Largo, we laughed about how there's a lack of interesting places around town to grab a bite. THEN, this showed up in our Yelp search. We were ecstatic since none of us had ever tried hot pot or conveyor belt sushi and especially not in the unexpected location of a Largo strip mall.  

Overall I'd say the experience is a MUST when it comes to entertainment level. If you're doing hot pots, you'll need a whole lot of time, creativity, and be with a group of open minded dining partners. You're essentially choosing a broth, noodle, meat and sides that will make up your "soup," and cooking it up in real time. There was boiling over, splashing, overcooking, slurping, egg cracking. Basically, a whole lot of mess for what seemed to be a mediocre meal. 

Overall, our server was very informative, the business was modern and clean, and we loved that they also had boba tea on the menu. But, because it's such a novel idea, I don't foresee us going back anytime soon.

Great place to get hammered but I went there for oilers game and food and they had reserved signs on all the decent tables.  By reserved I mean I'm there at 7pm and the tables were reserved for 8:30... leaving tables unoccupied for hours is a great business strategy.  ;)

OMG!! Great food bursting with flavor and texture and  presentation. Appetizer and Entree were beautiful! Outstanding service from Dustin.  Luscious libations!! Ahi tuna was succulent with subtle flavors that compliment the food, lamb shank was tender with rich, bold flavors.... Yummy!!

Love this place; style, service, ambiance, yummy food.  A little pricey but if you know what to order it's totally worth it.  The octopus was amazing and the pitcher of drinks we ordered was delicious and a fun way to enjoy cocktails as a group!

For starters, we had chips and salsa. The chips were a bit chewy, and the salsa tasted canned. The restroom stall didn't have a lock on it, and the toilet holder was loose so when I pulled the roll, it fell on the floor. Tables and chairs need tightening up before someone breaks a chair and falls. The restaurant is also poorly lit. Now the food: The flour tortillas were very good, and the vegetables were delicious! Unfortunately, I cannot say much about the chicken. It was bland and watery. I'm not a fan of chicken stew in my tacos; I prefer grilled chicken. In addition, I chewed on a bone in one of my tacos. I enjoyed the rice, but the refried beans were way too runny! Our server was very nice and pleasant, but I cannot give this place more than 3 stars.

I had been having problems with my Galaxy J7 phone.  Yes, I know its an older phone.  An employee at T-Mobile at Clearwater Mall recommend CPR to see if my phone could be fixed or if it needed to be replaced.

So today hours before going to buy a new phone I took my phone to CPR.   Within about 10 minutes they fixed my phone and it's working like a charm. 

I highly recommend CPR.

I ordered some food from here thru Uber Eats the Chicken & Shrimp Steak and the Tilapia platter with asparagus an Mac n Cheese. The food was very fresh an still warm considering it came from Germantown Ave to West Philly. Now as for my food the steak was lacking substance very little in my roll for $14.00 an the shrimp were small an it didn't have my mushrooms. As for the fish platter they called an said they didn't have anymore Mac n cheese bummer  settled for the rice then called back an got fries. The fish was good, asparagus alittle overcooked for me but I loved the fries very good. Will try other selections.

Very disappointed with Noah's. We ordered a Santa Fe salad which is supposed to come with corn, salsa, cheddar cheese, tomatoes, and tortilla chips. We received a small sandwich size container with just ice burg lettuce and grilled chicken. None of the other toppings! The cheese was a congealed nacho cheese sauce on the side. Worse than Taco Bell cheese whizz. And the "salsa" was marinara sauce! Laughable! Also, we ordered chicken fingers which melted through the styrofoam container and had a nice side of soggy fries. And, when we went to pick up the food the credit card machine was down. Unprofessional and irresponsible food establishment. Bummer because we live nearby and were excited about this new place close by. Won't be ordering from Noah's again.

I had the great opportunity to go here after our Yelp event for a meeting with my boss. 
It was a business breakfast of sorts. 

The service here is wonderful. The staff really go the extra mile to make sure you are satisfied with your food and drink. I ordered the bacon and avocado sandwich...It is one of the best breakfast sandwiches I have ever had. I am a HUGE fan of breakfast sandwiches! It has egg, bacon, avocado, and pepper jack cheese! It had me at bacon and egg but the avocado and pepper jack are what make this sandwich the king of breakfast sandwiches. 

Now, maybe the king of breakfast sandwiches is a bit extreme.  I have yet to try the other options and need to give them a fair shot at the title.  I must come back and experiment with them. I did not try any coffee but I did get hot tea. They served it to you with the little pot (so cute). 

This seems like a great place to go for lunch. I need to come back for every meal! I saw a sign that they do wine & cheese tastings, yummm....If they keep up with what they have going, this place is going to be very popular.

Seriously authentic. If you can, stop here. Every time I'm in Tucson I pick up a bag of the Anita's chips and red salsa, and a carne asada burro... Unless it's Tuesday when it's .99cent taco day. Some of the best red tamales in Tucson, and their tortillas can't be  beat. Support Anita's.

Went there today for father's day early dinner (i.e. the place was not that busy). Some of the food was very good, some not so much. I ordered their bacon bbq burger, two double patties. The bacon look old and unappetizing and the burgers were overcooked, flavorless and the uniformity of frozen beef. For $19.50 I would expect a fantastic burger. Also service was just OK. After we finished up, got some to go boxes after had to wait to get the bills and once given, after waiting about 10 minutes, had to go to the bar and get a manger to finish the transaction. Overall not a thrilling experience.
Sat for over an hour no food, no manager, wrong items on the menus, skunked tap, got up and left

We have been there twice for breakfast.
The wait staff is very friendly and the food is fresh  and very good.  Go early !!
This Brazilian hurt so bad. Great price, got the job done but man. It was sore for a day or two afterwords .

If you want some BBH but you don't wanna drive over to philly to cafe diem, this is a great alternative. It's in a small strip mall with plenty parking out front. I came here with my family after wedding venue shopping I normally don't get bbh unless it at cafe diem but my hairdresser recommended it and I went for it. I'm so glad I did because it was so delicious. I might even say it's better than cafe diems  

The interior is not cute but your standard pho place. They sell a bunch of lime trees, ripe limes and Serrano peppers. Random but interesting. The pricing aligns with the surround pho places in the suburbs. I would say it's slight cheaper with bigger portions. For example usually when u order summer rolls you get 2 for like 5 bucks, you get three here. My family all loved their food and they got a mix of pho, chicken n rice and bbh. If I was ever in the area I would deff stop over here!

Two stars only for the cheap drinks. Bartender upstairs was very friendly too. But the wings were ehhh, the music is really loud and there is nothing special or unique here. 

Just cheap drinks.

Oh yeah, and the bouncer was not that pleasant to one of my companions. Not cool.

The bartender smokes.

There was a group of 20-somethings vaporizing tobacco (or some such) and being generally boisterous, but you must keep in mind it IS a sports bar. Also explains the deafening NFL broadcast to a nearly empty pub.

Nice selection of beer, the polish dog I had was surprisingly OK for a mini mall pub.

I saw (bar) pool tables but did not opt to play.

I -think the place is OK, but nothing special. They need to be smoke-fee, though
$12.00 for a sandwich is one thing, but it needs to be the sandwich you at least ordered!  Poor at best.....will never go back or recommend

What a wonderful way to start a beautiful day in Santa Barbra , my family and I stopped in to our favorite lil bakery cafe on state street . Always excited about their exquisite menu , after trying to figure out what to eat we enjoyed the social climate and beautiful  ambience. The staff was super helpful and nice perfectly sweet to my dog & welcomed an  LGBTrans family Perfect . I will always come back when visiting family .

Came here with a Groupon.  I really wasn't a huge fan.  We came in, and were not greeted at all.  Actually it didn't seem like there was anyone attending anything.  We decided to sit down and a man popped up with menus.  He never brought us drinks.  We waited for 20 minutes before he came over to take our orders.  We ordered everything at once.  He was not very personable.  People came in and waited to get seated. He asked if they were ordering out, then if they weren't just told them to sit wherever.  It probably would be better if they had a sign that said "Please Seat Yourself". 

We ordered a pork spring roll and pho, with a couple of boba teas.  The spring rolls were good. I thought there was a tad too much fish sauce.  The pho was bland and small. The boba teas were delicious and looked like they used fresh fruit for the strawberry one.

We finished eating and no one came to clear our table or ask us if we needed our check.  I got up and paid.  We left.  It wasn't the best experience. Miss Saigon is definitely better. I wouldnt go out of my way to come here. To be honest if I were in the area looking for pho, I would just go to Pho 88 or Miss Saigon.

Kinda out of the way of the airport and you need to drive through a residential 25mph neighborhood if your coming in off of Essington.    The service is very friendly and they are there immediately when you arrive to drop your car off and when you call on return.   However our driver made numerous lot stops and he was in slow motion which is OK if you leave yourself plenty of time to get to the terminal.

I have been there twice and each time was better then the previous. The avocado toast is a must and if lunch is in order get the tomato basil soup. 
Staff is alway friendly and helpful  while the atmosphere warm and inviting. Making the best of these times for a meal  includes a trio to Marathon.

Food was spectacular so fresh made to order like 10 different soups to choose from it was amazing cost wasn't their daily special was wonderful the people were so nice to give you samples of the soup I will be going back
Today I had beef stroganoff soup so yummy they're only open from 11 to 2 weekdays
I can't wait to go back
this pizzeria is very good.  try the cheese steak pizza ...it is delicious.  have tried a few other special pizzas and were all good.  best pizza in Riverview .

Delicious foods with interesting flavor combinations that will tickle your taste buds! The French dip was very good.  The cheeseburger with their strawberry and thyme cocktail was wonderful. Definitely going to this restaurant again!

They have one vegan entree. I've come to expect disaster when ordering from steak houses and they did not disappoint. The blend of acid and sweet dressing on the veggies missed the mark big time. My entree arrived 10 minutes after everyone else's. They took it off the bill and I went home for dinner. I just have to learn to say "no" more often when friends want to take me out to dinner. If your party includes people who don't eat meat, look elsewhere.

The vegan entree does not include any sides. Nada. After everyone else was served their salad and waited a few minutes uncomfortably for my food to come, someone asked "Aren't you going to eat anything? Didn't you order?". "I guess not." I replied, "Go ahead and eat.". Us vegans just love standing out in a crowd and being the center of attention (not!). Charlie is charging me the same as a steak for $2.50 worth of veggies and can't spring for a salad like every other entree on the menu? Come on. Step up to the table and serve us a salad like everyone else - we vegans love salads. Trust me on this one. You can even leave off the bacon bits and grated cheese and make my day.

Also a note to oblivious carnivores everywhere: anyone calling themselves a "vegan" should be warned that this establishment is a Steakhouse, not just a restaurant. It's sometimes difficult to get the idea across that the experience of watching people eating dead animals invokes emotions ranging from disgust to nausea, or worse. It's the gastronomic equivalent of second-hand smoke. Of course getting my food on time would have allowed me to focus on my own dish instead of everyone else's.

Michelle and her team made me feel so comfortable during their plus size WToo event. I found the perfect dress for my big day this October. The dress selection is modern, and up to date in style. The prices are very good as well. Would definitely recommend for anyone in Reno!
Even though they were busy, the food came quickly. The roast beef sandwich was amazing and what I expected. We all enjoyed our food and service.
This Pho restaurant is okay.  Positives the portion size was HUGE!!  Okay customer service.  Variety of bowls excellent!!! The taste was average.

I've seen some reviews complain about customer service and price. I just want to say I came in here one morning and was greeted by three different women, all very nice and welcoming. I actually had to get the OK from my girlfriend to spend $50 on a stuffed animal and she didn't answer the phone so I said I'd be back later. I mean if they're so terrible wouldn't that be a prime opportunity to scoff at me for leaving after standing next to this damn rainbow bird for 5 minutes on my phone like a weirdo? No they were nice and said goodbye and we're all smiles. Then guess what I came back and bought that bird. Yeah $50 is a lot but this is rittenhouse square and they have some really unique stuff. They were very nice and bagged it up with a bow, gift receipt and blacked out the price. We shared a laugh and they all wished me a great day. Couldn't ask for more!

They have rows and rows of shoes.  From trendy to sexy to classy!  The staff is friendly and helpful.  

Don't forget to check out their clearance racks in the back.  They usually have good deals there!  If you find shoes you like at your size, you better grab them quick because they won't last.

Tried this new Mexican restaurant today for lunch. There were only about a dozen tables of diners in the place so we didn't have to wait for a table. The service was good, but the food arrived lukewarm. We both had the "Texas Fajitas" and they were good and would have been excellent had they been hot. The tortillas were wrapped in foil & were cold. When we checked out there were a couple of issues. I was billed for a drink that I didn't have and my friend had a problem using a gift certificate. It took an excessive amount of time & the man handling the register seemed irritated with us. I doubt I'll return with all of the other options available in our area.

While I was here, I had the propitious opportunity to visit many of the spring training ball parks in this area, everywhere from Port Charlotte to Lakeland to Tampa.  And by far, the best-maintained, most appealing, and loveliest one of them all is Legends Field.  I was sorry there wasnt time to stop and watch a game, as I would have loved to been able to explore the park a little more in detail, as well as take part in one of my favourite pastimes, which is watch handsome, young baseball players run around, haha.  Great job Yanks!

I walked in for lunch today at 11:50 a.m.  I ordered the #22 lunch special and a Diet Coke.  

40 minutes later, I noticed a woman at a table not far from mine staring at her plate of food -- which had the same things I had ordered on it -- and trying to flag a waitress down.  The waitress eventually went over, picked up her plate, and came to my table saying "this is yours".

I told her I didn't want a plate that had been sitting on someone else's table for ten minutes, so the waitress went back to have the cook do another one.

By 12:50, with no food delivered, I walked out.

The owners should fire the chef (40 minutes to get a lunch special out, really?) and the waitress and start over.

Found it one day, and came back three more than in same week.  It was gooood.  So glad there is finally a really authentic chinese food in the area.  

Thanks yelp
Terrible food. Bad waiting area. Don't seat for movies when the board says they will. And bring appetizers out at the same time as your entree. Trashy.
My experience was great. I did the deluxe mani and pedicure and my nails feel and look great!

Incredible service!  Had been going to Fountain Tire and got completely fed up with the lack of friendly timely service.  I decided to try Big Kev's and couldn't believe how Friendly the service was and the amazing quality of work.  Bonny provides service the good ol fashioned way and goes the extra mile - said he'd check in to something and call us right back and he did!  You feel they appreciate your business and want to make you happy.

I just love this pizza! The ingredients are always fresh. Everything is always delicious. Portions are definitely filling. And service is always good. 

It's also always super busy when I go. So parking can be a challenge and sometimes there is a wait to be seated. 

That's not necessarily a bad thing though, right? That means thatsa GOOD pizza pie!! 

I did use the Yelp wait list option on my last visit. That helped get us seated a bit faster, I think, as we were able to get on the list before arriving and were seated within 5 minutes. Coincidence? I *think* not. High five for Yelp wait list!

Greetings,
I visited this office site three weeks ago. The worst mistake,  I arrived at 12pm and fell asleep in the chair. Then I woke a up from extreme pain I was in at 1:00pm. I told the medical assistant to cancel my visit and I will drive to the hospital, due to I couldn't wait any longer, she wasn't to pleasant about the cancellation. "Luckily I drove to hospital, I could of past away, they keey me.This place need to be professional "front desk staff"

So I'm in Philly with family and, since I went to college here 10 years ago, they asked whether I could recommend a good cheesesteak place that was off the beaten track.  I'd been here once before - again, 10 years ago - and had good memories, so I recommended this place and we went.  The cheesesteaks were fine - not the greatest I've ever had, but certainly very solid and better than the cheesesteaks in Richmond.  Unfortunately, getting the cheesesteaks was a hassle. There was a line out the door, but it was pure madness inside - no real line, just people taking orders, other people milling around waiting for their cheesesteaks, and a general sense of anarchy.  My sister had to step out because she found it too claustrophobic, and I would've followed her if I didn't have to wait for the cheesesteaks.   All in all, it took us somewhere between 30-40 minutes to get the food. This was at about 2 PM on a Saturday, so not even during the lunch rush.  I'd come back again for the food, but you should probably come at a less busy time if you value your sanity and want your food in a reasonable amount of time - if my memories from college still serve, random Tuesday nights in January seem to be a good time.
Great location,  cool place to go for dinner,  and drinks to avoid downtown.  Best part.... eat on floating docks,   service is great,  and fresh foods.  I recommend it!

My friend thought that Nora's would be an excellent alternative to our Jim's cheesesteaks since she is a vegetarian. The tacos are extremely cheap and the food looked amazing. My friend asked to get a quesadilla, even though it is not an option on any of their menus, with a small side of rice and beans which is $2. With her quesadilla, she wanted cheese, lettuce, sour cream, and pico de gallo inside. After THIRTY minutes of waiting for her food, we took her food to-go, since the rest of us were eating cheesesteaks, so that we can eat at Jim's. 

Once she opened her box to eat, she realized that her quesadilla had nothing but cheese. On top of that, the cheese looked like it had been thrown on the stove for about 10 seconds. The cheese wasn't melted and the tortilla was barely cooked. My friend is not problematic so she just tried to make the best out of it and eat it anyway but since the cheese wasn't even melted, it wasn't appetizing. For $7 (price of the quesadilla), that was not acceptable. Especially when we had ordered, there had only been a couple behind us, so they were not at all busy or should have had trouble with her order, since it did take 30 minutes to make.

We decided to return so that we can work some kind of solution out since my friend had spent money on food, yet was still hungry. We politely told the woman who had took our order that the order had been messed up and asked if she could either get a refund or an entirely new one done correctly. The first thing the woman had said was, "Well, you ate half of it." She did not care AT ALL that they had initially messed up. We were very shocked that she reacted this way since we didn't want any problems at all but she had acted as if it was our fault. We continue to tell her that we would either like a refund or an entirely new one because of this and after she talked to the only other worker behind her, she said they will make her a new one and took her box. Before she walked away, I had asked her if she needed to write down what my friend had initially wanted in her quesadilla since she just took her box and was about to walk away. She then RUDELY and sarcastically said, "Yeah I know, you already told me, remember." ???? I was so confused by this since if she already knew, why wasn't it done right in the first place?!

I completely understand when people make mistakes, we are all human. However, the way this woman handled the situation was absolutely unacceptable. I don't understand why she had an attitude with us when we just wanted to fix a mistake they had made for our friend. It was so difficult without it having to be at all.

I did not get the name of the only woman we had encountered, who took our order, but she does not know anything about customer service. I'm really upset that this had happened too, since this was my first time coming here, I thought it had a lot of potential. 
I would love to give this place 5 stars, however, after this experience, I will never return here again.
This place is a lot of fun. I just love that it's lit only with candles and they have a piano planning covers. Always a good time.

Honestly, just okay. It's nothing to write home about. My family eats 90% vegetarian so this place used to be our regular joint. But recently, I tried the chicken from here and it was.. bad. It tasted like the freezer. It was funky and didn't taste fresh.

I would say if you're vegetarian, their potato enchiladas are yummy. But if you like the meats and appreciate freshly cooked meat, you may be in for a surprise.

The salsa is damn good though. The chips can be really hit or miss, sometimes great and other times oily and stale. 

I don't think I will be back, but I will miss the choriqueso. That shit is bomb.

Food was OK....very plain, no seasonings. Not bad but not great.
 Customer Service is great and delivery is on time but could do better with flavor but thats preference

This used to be my breakfast green smoothie home. Last school year I'd call almost every morning. They knew me by name and my order. Always had it ready to go on time. Never had any problems using my rewards. The 3 stars are for the great service I received in times past. 

Now it's a mess. They have new staff and more of them but somehow service has gotten worst. On my birthday I was so disappointed. First of all I tried to order using the app like I've done several times in the past however the app for some reason wasn't working so I called. The woman who answered the phone was rude. I explained to her that the app wasn't allowing me to order. She then told me she do it over the phone one time as a courtesy but next time I need to use the app. As if I can control the app not working. I walk to the counter and I go to get my birthday reward and the person at the register has no idea what order I'm talking about or how to redeem my birthday reward. Then to top it off my drink isn't even made! 

I wasn't the only person having a bad day there. This other woman was asking for her food to be prepared for a third time and requested that one of the girls not touch her food. I'm assuming it was because of something has happened before I entered.

We love hanging out at Miss Vicki's on the weekends. They have a beautiful view and great entertainment. A fun local secret to bring friends when they come to town! Seating is limited, so we have to go early to get parking and a table.

Great veggie burger even though I really meant to get the black bean veggie burger they had no full menu? Which is how I ended up with a normal veggie burger because I had no idea they had more than 1. ... Needless to say it was delish ... My friends say I shouldn't rate food anymore unless it's something sweet because I put mustard on everything .. I disagree ... Obviously .. Suffice it to say, I'll be back for that black bean burger!

I decided to go here because I was throwing up to the point of passing out, so they put me in a small cramped a** janitor closet basically with a tiny bed and a tv to match for well over 7 hours. The nurses were all short with me and rude as you expect from a hospital, but the worst part comes in the fact that the doctor rolled in and said that he thinks I have stomach cancer or Crohn's disease, then he hands me a fat stack of papers which you may think had a good deal of info on it but instead was just a safety Manuel for how to not get COVID. After all that the very next day I get a fat bill, I talked to my insurance and they said they would cover even more because of how much was charged so I get in contact with this hospital to tell them what I was told, with screenshots of a statement my insurance sent me in the mail. Customer service as you'd expect from a company that takes from the dying to line their own pockets told me to completely f*** off. TLDR: come here if you want to get treated like human trash by staff or instead you could just go to your job and get that same treatment but getting paid instead.

Not a pleasant experience. I do NOT recommend this place at all. We had hot pot, and they didn't even cut off the root of the spinach! I don't know about other dishes, but for hot pot the price is not appropriate to the portion size at all. They claimed on the menu the base was bone soup, but when we asked to add soup they said they only got hot water to add. What? The waiter was very inexperienced and he didn't add any soup at the end. He didn't even clean up the empty plates. Btw, for my taste, all sauces have been added water to it. The sesame sauce does not taste like traditional Chinese sesame sauce at all. The beef in the picture is about 2/3 of the portion ($6)
This place doesn't deserve the 1 star I gave, the owner is very mean, the food is outdated and bland, place smells and price is not affordable!!!!

We stopped in because my buddy wanted to buy me a powerful drink for my bachelor party. The drink set him back $18. Sure Absinthe is a specialty drink but we both felt that was a little steep.

Beyond the prices, the music they played was decent (but seriously, shouldn't they have LIVE music anywhere on Bourbon Street? I think that should be the rule going forward, who's on board?), the bartenders were pleasant, and the decor was pretty nice.

We really just stopped in to grab a few drinks and say we drank at the Old Absinthe House. Worth the visit but I would not say it's a place to frequent while in NOLA.

Cheers!
Love Thai Sweet Basil! Great options and amazing food. Very friendly and courteous staff.

Their food was good but I ate from the place where they sell wings and they were undercooked. I had asked for grilled wings and they were very chewy and blood inside. I am not feeling good and pretty sure I got food poisoning from here. I'm pregnant so this is a huge deal to me. I didn't finish my food and I wish I had taken pictures but I was with a huge group on a time limit. The potato salad was good but I just feel completely nauseous.

When we got to the Restaurant the first sign we saw was for marina parking only.  So needless to say the parking is limited so get here early. 
The restaurant is definitely catered on the bar scene with good food rather then a restaurant with a bar. They also have live "loud" music inside, so keep that in mind if you are wanting conversation rather then a dance party. We sat out side so it did not bother us. 
There is a nicely listed drink menu, I had the sea hags brew, it was very tasty. The first one went down real easy as a sweet drink, however after starting my second the alcohol started to kick it and it hit me how strong the drink is. 
The food was all very good, they have a special you can add 2 hush puppies to any order for $1. The out side is a little to crispy but the inside was sweet and tender so it balanced out. 
The Chicken fajita quesadilla was well prepared, the only thing I questioned is why the tomatoes and scallions were on the side rather then in the quesadilla itself. 
The fish tacos. The whole chicken  shrimp bucket and the cuban all of it taste great we enjoyed the huge servings and everyone had to take left overs home. Great food for a great price.

Cafe Poca Cosa is unique all around. The food is magnificently delicious. The owner, Susana, is the older sister of the Lil' Cafe Poca Cosa owner, and she also greets you with a warm and welcoming hug. 

The food is considered "nuevo Mexico" not as in New Mexico the state but literally a newer version of traditional Mexican entrees. The dessert is also one of a kind and they have vegan options as well. 

They are open Tuesday-Saturday for lunch and dinner. Lunch often requires no reservations but for dinner it is a MUST. They are often booked a week in advance as they are busy most evenings, regardless of the season. Speaking of seasons, they are often closed for Christmas, New Years and Easter for extended days (7+ days). 

The ambiance is soft and gentle with Latin music as background noise. The bathrooms are immaculate and have one of those nice sinks where the water seductively flows down the horizontal drain. The decor throughout the restaurant is unique in that it is colored with deep reds, bronze, and other early tones. The lighting is semi-romantic and put anyone in a relaxed, pleasant mood.

Looking for a steak dinner and was referred here. Steak was great and the service was attentive.  Enjoyed the potato skins and green chilis appetizers and I got the blue cheese salad and ribeye, which was med well done and it melted in my mouth. GREAT place to visit and eat dinner.

We ordered veggie burgers being vegetarians like we have done many times. What we received was burgers with bacon in them and no bun!!!! Can't believe they messed up this bad. Don't think we'll be ordering ever again!

Had a great (and affordable) lunch. We ate off the specials list and off menu - French dip sandwich and meatloaf. Both were awesome. Service was great and we got to eat upstairs in the dining room so we had a nice view of the streets of Lambertville! Try the blood mary - we had the fender bender (garnished with shrimp, pepper jack & olives) and the spicy bourbon bay. Delicious!

Only went because my group wanted to go. I didn't appreciate the "sarcastic humor" because it wasn't humor, just rudeness. Please don't go here for the food because it was terrible! As was the service! Even our hats they made for us were lame! If you have to eat lunch room quality food, I at least want good entertainment!

Very high quality stuff, but usually out of budget range, even for this devoted foodie.  Very source-conscious, which I appreciate.  Sandwich prep a little slow but friendly.  Good place to survey passers-by on Baltimore.

Very cute little establishment in the middle of Doylestown with some great food options! I'm an avid carnivore but the vegetarian portobello wrap caught my eye and it was pretty good. The four choice barbecue sauces offered really brought the wrap to a whole other level. My sister throughly enjoyed her California avocado burger that had a bun that was to die for. I'll have to try their signature BBQ options the next time I stop in!

Jones is hit or miss when it comes to the food. I've had things that were overly salty or under-seasoned. From salads to home classics...at best, it is average. The decor and vibe of the place are what's fun. You feel like you stepped right into The Brady Bunch.

Stay away from the coffee grinder shop. Owner is exremely rude when it comes to customer service. Asked her for 1/4 pound of coffee and because her helper did not believe there was enough for a 1/4 pound of coffee beans she refused to weigh them or sell me what was left without even looking for herself. I requested that she at least weigh them to see if it was close to a 1/4 pound but she refused to even look. A grown woman rolling her eyes at me like  13 year old that doesn't want to haer what u have to say. Saying she was to busy with other things to weigh the beans even though i was only customer in the store at the time. Very disapointing experience! The only place worthwhile going to at qmart is Fredricks meats otherwise stay clear and spend your money somewhere were the owner appreciates your business.

The AT&T store has come a long way ... it appears that they are trying to mimic the Apple experience ("A" for effort, but don't really see that happening).  We were politely greeted at the door by someone whose sole function it seems was to do so, to take our names and to invite us to have a seat. When we got to speak to an associate (Ivan), he was very polite, pleasant and seemed genuinely interested in helping us. When we were done with our transaction, he walked us to the door, opened it and shook our hands on the way out. Overall conclusion: while not the "Apple experience" definitely a better AT&T experience than I remember!

I very much dislike posting unfavorable reviews.  So, Joey K's, this is going to hurt me more than it hurts you.

Very unremarkable overall. Dim lighting and kitschy signs all by the same dude and perfectly straightened with a level lead to a surprisingly sterile and vaguely unsettling environment.  My fries were fresh, but my shrimp platter tasted thawed and/or reheated. Clientele is cops, old folks, people who tuck their tshirts into their jeans, and sad looking couples. My most concise description would be New Orleans' answer to Bob Evans.  I see literally no compelling reason to come here regularly.
Thank you Towner for literally catering to our particular taste with the Charbroiled Oysters!! The atmosphere was quiet and enjoyable, but the food was AMAZING! and so was the service!

Nice variety of items. Some items didn't have tags so be sure to ask. Friendly service. A little more expensive than other big box stores, as expected, so we stuck to plants vs soil, decor, etc. Worth a trip for your next landscaping project!
We enjoyed our Reubens-  top quality ingredients. Their homemade chips are wonderful. We really enjoyed their breakfast choices too. Great biscuits!! Strongly recommend this place.

First off, let me say the 2 star rating has nothing to do with the food. The food was fantastic. The seafood is to die for. The sea bass is fantastic and I had the monk fish special which was fantastic. The 2 starts is for the service. My wife and I went there for our 6 year anniversary and also devour Indy. Our server was slow and inattentive. He gave my wife the devour Indy portion of the sea bass and not the dinner sea bass and we still charged for the dinner portion. My wife ordered the dinner portion but received the Devour Indy portion. How do I know this, our couple friends ordered from the devour Indy menu and the sea bass was the same portion even though the waiter stated it was smaller. When we addressed the server about it, he acknowledged it but did nothing about it. We went to Salt 2 years ago and the food and service was fantastic. I'm not sure what changed since then or if we had a bad experience last night, but the service was definitely not worth the price of the meal.

Came here with the family after browsing through all the positive reviews. This place is located right by Clearwater beach. You order inside and then take your food outside to a nicely shaded patio.

Most of us opted for the fish tacos. I thought they were great. The siricha sauce had the perfect amount of heat to it, and if you aren't a fan of siricha, they have about 4 other unique sauces to try at your table.

My wife had the cluck-it tacos (chicken) and they were equally as good.

Each taco plate came with some very addicting chips/salsa, I only wish they gave you another handful!

The service was fantastic. We let them know our daughter has a peanut allergy and the chef  personally walked out and assured us our food options were fine; it was a really nice gesture.

I would definitely recommend this place, especially for a quick bite to eat after spending a few hours on Clearwater beach.

I would like to say that Dr. Maxwell does a good job, but I can't because I never met her.  I made an appointment to address a specific issue, but ther office does not have the capability to address said issue.  I had communicated clearly with the office over the phone and e-mail; They had no qualms about wasting my time with an appointment for no reason.

Also, the appointment turned out to be with a technician only, rather than with the doctor followed by the technician as I was initially told.  The technician was not able to go over other questions that I had.

We had seen this restaurant on a food tv show and I wanted to try it just for the grilled cheese. So, that's what I got. The bread was great. The cheese mixture could have been better. Just not what I was looking for. And the tomato bisque wasn't as good as I expected. Not much flavor. But we had the pretzel with cheese as an appetizer and it was awesome! The cheese was really good. We went late lunch on a Saturday and it was not crowded at all so you think the service would have been a little better. Atmosphere was really neat though. Prices were right too. It was ok but we probably won't go back.

This place was honestly NASTY. We heard mixed reviews so we figured we'd try it out for ourself and boy was that a mistake on all counts. I'll break it down for you.

Ambiance: they were BLASTING early 2000s rap like it was so loud I felt like I was in a dive bar right out of high school again and I kept thinking it would end but it never did, bubba sparks, young joc, on and on.

Cleanliness: firstly our table was sticky like I peeled my forearms from the side of the table. I ordered a glass of wine and the glass literally had like a sticky splat on it and it was far from polished. Also I know this is a lot of restaurants but the water tasted like straight pool water.

Food and drink: so my fiancé ordered a modelo and it came completely flat, switched for a Pacifico and better... but I guess better is the standard lol. So we ordered cheese fries, wood fired cauliflower and wings (garlic bbq flavor). We were nervous right off the bat when the cheese fries came out like 2 min after we ordered. They were ehhh def fake cheese but whatever at least there was good ranch. I didn't know what to expect from garlic bbq but this sweet honey chemical taste was not it, so gross. And the cauliflower, dude this was not wood fired lol this was raw blanched for like 3 seconds in some weird oil and thrown in with the croutons you use for stuffing.

The waiter came by and asked how everything was and I was like "honestly it was gross but it's whatever" and he was just like "ok I'll get your check." Kinda acted like it happened all the time. Didn't offer to fix anything, didn't take anything off the check, didn't even offer a box for all our untouched food just "ok, I'll get your check." Needless to say we will NOT be going back.

This was a great lunchtime surprise! I wasn't expecting the level of fresh and flavorful food from a corner chain on Oracle, but that's what I found at Pei Wei. Since moving to Tucson from the northwest, I've found that teriyaki shops are almost non-existent here. Pei Wei comes very close to this tradition with tangy Asian sauces, generous rice bowls, and vegetables so fresh I scarcely believed my tastebuds (the pea pods are CRUNCHING!!). My favorite is the vegetarian rice bowl, which includes a ginormous helping of said fresh and crunchy vegetables, plus grilled and sauced tofu better than most I've ever had. Pei Wei has managed to cultivate flavor and texture from the tofu without relying on a deep-fry batter, which is unique and worth note. 

The interior is like the most Zen cafeteria you can imagine. It's not a fine dining place, but it's definitely nice enough to have a quiet lunch hour and read with your meal without feeling like you're cramped or in an unpleasant space.

One star deducted for the complete and utter lack of sriracha sauce available. This, my friends, is a teriyaki crime.
When I am in Nashville, this is one of my favorite places to go. The food is great and the staff is friendly. I like that the sushi bar is far removed from the hibachi area, as well.

I had 3 tacos, carne asada, chipotle chicken and a pork one. I was expecting a regular taco but the flavor and freshness of the each taco was extraordinary. I wanted to got back that same day for more. Also, it is not expensive.
More of a bar than a restaurant. Food was OK. Service was meh. Crab cake appetizer is pictured- pretty good.
Very creative dishes! Best veggie restaurant in town.

We got a groupon from a friend that she couldn't use, so we decided to go for the 7pm comedy show. The place is busy and sits in a slightly unassuming strip mall. There is the regular restaurant and them theres the comedy area where you can also eat.

The space is tight. I basically was a part of the conversation of the people next to me. The comedy itself was so so. The announcer and the first act did not measure up to the main act at all. 

The menu is extensive and prices reasonable. We only ordered the fried calamari and it wasn't too great. The calamari had an almost undercooked texture.

All in all, its not great, not bad. Its a night out.

This place is shit and so is their workers. F*ck this place. We went in to get a pizza and apparently they had a sign where the had no pizza's and I was like and this rude ass worker told us if we can read, like dummy shit up and do your work instead of sitting down and not not doing shit. Never again going here f* ck these workers in there. just straight up lazy.

Will never go here again.  Service was atrocious.  We were checking it out as a place to take family during holidays to try C.J. Hamm's cowboy burger from Tucson Foodie's best burgers in Tucson list.  We actually had to walk out due to horrible service that finally delivered cold and inedible nachos and wings. We will not be going back.  Good luck getting a glass of water with your $40 order to dine in.
Excellent tacos for a great price. Salsa needs some improvement for 5 stars

Happiest place in the city!! Everyone is so friendly!!! I got my hair done two times this week while visiting Philly. The service was great!! They were on time and worked fast. My hair looked great! I will come back again the next time I am in town.

I know that there's a Cracker Barrel somewhere in Cincinnati, OH, but I've never been to it. This is my first time at a Cracker Barrel, and although I was skeptical about it, I was won over by the time I left.

The storefront is really cool! They have everything from headache balm, to clothes, quilts, vintage candies and sodas, and candles. It reminds me of the old general stores, so I guess they nailed the concept. This location had rockers out in the front for people to chill out on, and you could buy them if you chose to. 

The breakfast menu has something like, "It's never too late for the most important meal of the day" to which I was definitely like "Amen." I got the Old Timer's Breakfast, which included: Two eggs, Grits, Sawmill Gravy, Two Homemade Buttermilk Biscuits, Hashbrown Casserole, and Bacon. Bacon was good (Nothing beats the sugar cured bacon at Clinton Street Baking Company in NYC), but I wasn't too fond of the grits (it was bland), nor the gravy. 

HOWEVER,

The biscuits are so, so, so, so, soooooooooo good. Make sure to request the apple butter, smother it on your biscuit, and revel in the taste of delicious, butter, warm-in-your-tum comfort. Oh my gosh, I wish I could just order 5 bajillion biscuits and a tub of apple butter. 

The hashbrown casserole was okay (kinda cheesy). 

I do have to say though, my boyfriend got the Chicken Fried Chicken with mashed potatoes and greenbeans, and his food was more delicious. I'm totally getting that next time. 

The woman who served us was really friendly, and it really felt like we were given a dose of friendly southern hospitality.

tl;dr southern hospitality forreals, biscuits with apple butter is a must. chicken fried chicken is amazeballs. Throwback Home on the Range storefront is pretty cool. Vintage candies!

Spending time in this restaurant was the nicest experience I've ever had at PHL. The seaweed salad was solid, tuna roll was fresh, and the ramen was much better than I was expecting to find in an airport. The decor and ambience are modern and relaxing. Yes, this is an airport restaurant, so it's pricier than what you'd get otherwise, but it's reasonable for what it is.

I'm all about the hot sauce at Buffalo Wild Wings because I can actually get the high level of heat that I desire.  The food itself is never anything special, but it's tasty fried junk food that occasionally I want to indulge in. I've visited this particular location on three occasions and unfortunately, all of them were a bit of a train wreck.

The first time was during the grand opening week.  My husband and I ordered the chicken tenders and fries.  After an hour we still didn't have any food.  Finally a manager comes out and explains that there was a disaster in the kitchen of epic proportions, and all the food was ruined.  Therefore, they would have to remake everyone's orders, and it would take a while.  We'd already waited an hour, so we decided to wait.  It took another half hour.  The food was warm, but not piping hot when it finally arrived.  We were given a coupon for a few free wings on a return visit.  My day was shot though because the places I needed to shop at in the area were closing soon by the time we go out of there.

The second time they just took forever again, but the food was okay.  I decided to give them a third chance the other day after a lot of time had passed thinking that the big disaster was during opening week, so they were probably still working the kinks out.  The third time when we arrived we were asked if we wanted to sit at the high top tables or the low regular ones.  I wanted the low ones because it's more comfortable to have a seat with a back.  The server walks us over to the high tops and tries to seat us there.  Then seeing the confused look on our faces, she asks where we wanted to sit again and moved us.  No biggie.  

Then we ordered the same thing as last time.  I had the hot sauce with mine and salt and vinegar on the fries.  The food took a reasonable amount of time.  When it came, they didn't bring any sauce and just had a big thing of the salt and vinegar powder on the side.  We told the server who soon brought out our sauces.  Then I start eating and turn over a chicken strip to notice it's all weird and wrinkly.  I look at the inside and sure enough, it is still raw inside.  Yuck!  I send it back, and soon I got a new cooked one. My husband's breading on one of his strips was undercooked as well on one side, but not totally raw.  I feel like this is unacceptable considering all they have to do is throw frozen chicken strips in the fryalator.  I guess they were throwing them in in one big frozen chunk and not separating them, so they don't get done on all sides.  This is just sad.

The servers were nice and tried to rectify the situation, and it's not their fault the kitchen is a wreck, so I'm giving two stars.  If you have a hankering for BDubs, I'd recommend going to another location. This one is not worth the trouble.  Even though it's a bit of a haul, I recommend the location on Limestone Road in Wilmington, Delaware.
Well maintained and clean, service was prompt , and kept on top of our needs. Food and selection was good, prices average for this type of lunch.  I would return again.

Stopped here several times for lunch, including a late lunch today.  Very dark inside, dark wood, dark floors, dark ceilings, and all the windowblinds closed.  But hey, it's supposed to be a cozy pub, right?
Typical pub/bar fare, not that great.  Had the traditional pub tenderloin, this one looked like it was fresh out of the deep freeze from the wholesaler.  No hand breaded tenderloin here, just some institutional stuff like you'd get at the high school cafeteria.  The bun looked like something I'd have leftover in a bag of buns smashed down in my bread drawer for a week.  Cole slaw was most likely from a tub as well.   But it was edible, not horrid, and the place was clean.  Must be the hangout for chain smokers, because the place smelled horribly of stale smoke, despite the exhaust fans running like crazy.  Came out with my clothing saturated with smoke smell, even though I only saw one person smoking on the far side of the room.

Awful just awful!!! Do not have any work finished by this place!  I went with my 12 year old daughter to get a rug pillow finished.  Since it was hers and her money we asked how much it would be ... around 50 dollars. Which was high for a rug craft but she wanted it ...so we said okay. Today I picked it up, when we signed the ticket I thought it said 60 dollars . It was not until we were in the car .... $160.00 yep 320 % More than a quote not 20% higher. She called and was told the person should not have quoted her a price !

I had back surgery and my dr. was great but the nursing staff at this hospital is the worst I laid in the bed and could not get help and when they did come they were rude and did the lest possible. I could not get my pain meds. and when I ask for water you would think I was asking for gold. One nurse named Grace was awful she let me know I got my pain meds when she decided not when they were due. If at all possible do not go to this hospital. My surgery was July 2013 and I still have bitter felling about the treatment I was given there.

We had to cancel our wedding due to covid-related issues in April of this year. Love City was on our list for a reception space initially - and when we contacted them in the beginning of May, I was so impressed by how quickly they were able to help us. 

Phoebe was our main point of contact throughout the planning process and she was WONDERFUL. She was so responsive and friendly in our emails, it really made the process easier. Day of (8/13!), the manager Mike was so pleasant and helpful. The staff working the outdoor bar were all so sweet and nice.

Almost every one of our guests mentioned something they liked about the beer, the space, or the staff. The tent made the heat and sun bearable and everyone working helped make our day special and perfect. Thank you so much!!

If you ever want a really cool, out of the way spot to host an event, Love City delivers, 10/10 recommend.

Came across this coffee stop while stopping by a friend's house- nice area; clean facility; friendly courteous staff; restrooms available; outdoor seating; free parking; coffee was piping hot and strong just how I like it. Bakery items offered. Some kitchen items available- after seeing someone else's order of scrumptious avocado toast, I regret not ordering any. Reasonable prices.
Used to be one of my favorite places. NOT ANYMORE! just spent $25 on food that tasted horrible. My fav was the Vegan Farmer but now the sandwich is something that belongs in a school lunch box.

I originally gave 4 stars about 6 months ago, and now I'm upgrading it to 5!

As a local dog walker, I often find coffee shops near my walking routes where I can sit down, relax, rehydrate and have a few nibbles of a snack.  I usually stop by in the early afternoon and this is a popular coffee shop, so if you see a table, grab it!  

My favorite items to order here are the jasmine pearl iced green tea, which is very flowery.  I tried out their light roast coffee it was the most delicious tasting coffee but I  have a sensitivity to lighter roasts, which are sometimes more acidic, but it tasted delicious.  I really enjoy the hot cider and it's very flavorful with the biscotti.  I like the Spindrift sparkling waters, especially good over ice. 

As far as the food offerings, I like the zucchini bread and other breads.  I enjoy the bagels, especially with fig jam, I love Dottie's Donuts, the biscotti and the polenta cake are some of my favorites.

It's very relaxed in this cute coffee shop.  The music is usually not loud, which is nice.  It is a sweet coffee shop, and at times is quiet but it's very popular and gets busy often.  A lot of people come here to work and I often do work on my phone or read.

I have found the baristas to be very friendly, knowledgeable about the different beverage and food options.  It has a welcoming atmosphere, especially with the plants along the windows.

Definitely one of my favorite coffee shops in the area!

Always "understaffed" and full of ghetto dwellers. Garbage wawa. Never go here unless you want to wait an hour for gas and have employees be rude except the one girl who pumps gas. Outrageous how "understaffed" they always are. Pathetic. I lived in the midwest where things were self serve and NEVER encountered a gas station with 8 pumps closes for no reason. Garbage store. The manager jeeds to get some stones and start clamping down on these lazy turds

Nice and friendly people. They offered same day delivery to me.  They even suggested what flowers to give and picked the flowers by themselves. All i did was call them and have them deliver at my place to surprise my wife.
One thing I didnt like was that they charged $7 for delivery fee. The shop is literally 2 mins from my house on the same street. I dont mind the fees, but there should have been proper disclosure of the fees up front than for me to find it directly in my credit card statement. Second thing, it was meant to be a surprise. When they could not find anyone, they called my wife even after explicitly telling them that in case of any issues call me not my wife. basically, gave away the surprise. 
Anyways, the flowers were beautiful and very well decorated. Loved it. Highly recommend this place.
This place is okay. not worth the price in my opinion.. BUT their ice cream sandwiches are too die for!

The doctor and nurses I have had experience with are amazing and caring. Unfortunately the front desk just ruins my experience completely. As soon as you walk in you greeted with a glare and the women's who name that vegans with a "V" is always so rude. She ruins and irritates my appointment every time. She's never nice, it seems like there's always an issue with her and I feel like she judges me. My boyfriend has noticed her attitude and how negatively she interacts with me. It's unfortunate and she may be the reason I no longer come back.

Came in for a dip manicure. Lasted about one hour so it was quick but Sophia did a good job! Definitely on the pricier side, with tip extensions, French nails, and special shape plus tip it was almost $100. What was a little frustrating was they used a communal dip pot and for that much I would definitely expect a nice salon like this to be using individual powder. I love the manicure, it turned out great! Don't know if I would return based on the fact that it didn't feel totally hygienic, especially for what I was paying. 

Pros: beautiful nails, nice atmosphere 
Cons: pricey, unhygienic dip

I ordered a room here from Booking.com . I asked for a non smoking room. When I got there the Manager Erin had already processed my credit card which Bookings said wouldn't happen .When I opened the door to my room the cigarette stench almost made me puke. The Manager Erin knowingly put me in a smoking room knowing I requested a non smoking room, when I asked for a refund and that I wouldn't be staying there she said " oh well take it or leave it" and because I booked in less then 24 hrs I wouldn't get my money back. I still have not received my money back and probably never will. Do not book here online they're shady as feck. I'll be reporting them to the Better Business Bureau.

I'm sorry to say we didn't have a very enjoyable evening. We walked in and it's a cute place the hostess was very friendly she said sit wherever you want. We went down and the very center table had a reserved tag on it. We sat down and ordered wine. My husband said it was very good. They brought bread and oil that to was very good. We're off to a great start. We order appetizers Caesar salad for me pasta fagioli for my husband. Meanwhile the reserved table guests come in. At this point the waitress starts spending more time at that table. We've waited a good 10-15 min for our appetizers.  Why such a long wait?  Mine came out of the frig because the bowl was cold and the croutons were soggy from sitting in a salad made previously the soup out of a pot. The soup instead of elbow past is made with spaghetti noodles broken up. It also looked green instead of red. We've had this soup in Boston and down here in Clearwater both places elbow pasta and red.  At this point the reserved table gets their food on their table clothed covered  table we're sitting at our sticky table still waiting for our dinner. Finally our food comes. It was so dim that we couldn't see what our meal looked like. My sauce was with olives and artichokes. There were 3 olives and 1 artichoke broken up. It was hot and was very good. The waitress keeps going over to the reserved table never asks us how our meal is. My husbands meal is usually made with spinach this was served with broccoli.  The reserved table got their check right away and left (hostess did talk with them as they left).   We wait for our check then wait for it to be picked up. We got to the restaurant at 535 1 couple was finishing their meal. We start the process and reserved table cones in and 1 other table soon after. I'm not sure who the reserved table was but it's pretty bad when such special treatment is so obvious to other diners. Once they left the tablecloth came off. we had a Groupon so the evening only cost $24 instead of $54

We were on vacation in Nashville. We went to Morton's for our 48th anniversary. I ordered a 12oz filet mignon and my wife ordered a 6 oz filet mignon. Both medium to medium well. Hers was very good, pink inside. Mine was butter flied and over done. Well done. Not good. The bread they brought out was covered with onions. I hate onions. I asked for something different and they said all they had were hamburger buns. We paid $194.00 for a average at best meal and drank water.  Way over priced for the quality.
Good luck if you eat at this location. I suggest you go to a local bar and get a good sandwich.
Ron from Lancaster, Ohio

Above average, but not by much. I had the Diablo burger, and while it brought the heat, it didn't have much flavor otherwise. I guess I expected better. Not bad, just not as good as it sounded or could have been. I feel a better cheese or flavorful beef could have added to the burger. Service was fine, again, above average but nothing extraordinary.

Went for dinner last night with four others and had high expectations. Ordered a chicken breast panini with mozzarella, tomato and basil. $13.00. Supposed to be served with a red pepper aioli. After digging in, my sandwich tasted very dry, so I flagged down the waitress and asked for some extra aioli. She came back and informed me that they don't have any red pepper aioli. That the menu is incorrect. Hmmm. After eating a few more bites, I realized something else was missing. The basil!  So I flagged down the waitress again and told her my sandwich was missing the basil. She said that she would check, but that they probably didn't have any. And she was right! What the heck?  I was not happy. She offered to get the manager to take it off the bill, but I felt bad. I hesitantly told her that it was fine. After finishing half of the sandwich and feeling very underwhelmed, I called her back and asked her to take half off. She was very apologetic, and said she would ask the manager. She came back later with the check, and told me that they took half off. After leaving and thinking about it, I felt like this was handled totally wrong. First of all, if someone orders something and ingredients are not available, you go back to the customer and ask them if they would like something else. Most importantly, the manager should have come to our table to apologize and insist that they take the entire sandwich off my bill. I took the other half of my sandwich home, and when I went to eat it, I realized there was just a tiny piece of mozzarella on the other half, that only covered 1/2 of the chicken. So disappointing!  I guess the major problem is the outdated menu. The waitress told me that they don't ever have the aioli, yet on the menu, under panini's, it says they are all served with the red pepper aioli. If you don't want to reprint the menus, either take a marker and put a line through it, or have your staff inform your customers that significant ingredients are no longer available.

I gave this place a try after passing by numerous times. The first thing I noticed when I sat down and looked at the menu was that it was pretty similar to Houston's menu (the previous restaurant). I was glad they kept most of the old favorites such as the House Smoked Salmon. We had the fried oysters with creamed spinach as a starter. The spinach was my favorite part because the oysters seems to be fried a little too long, shrinking the size and drying it out a bit.

For the main meal, I tried the herb rotisserie chicken and the bf had the prime rib sandwich. They were both pretty good, but I felt like the chicken was lacking something, but I couldn't figure out what. Maybe I'm just not the herb spice type.

I prefer Zeas rotisserie chicken more. 

Overall it was a good meal. I would try it again.

My friend recommended Perfectly Bare Laser to me because she had such a great experience here. I have been twice now and I really love it! I love that it's pay per session rather than buying an overpriced package deal like some other laser hair removal businesses. I was a bit nervous about doing laser hair removal because I expected it to be very painful, but it's not at all! To me it feels like if you put pop rock candy on the areas that they're doing. Lol. The staff is friendly and professional and they make you feel very comfortable. The location is a bit tucked away in what looks like a pretty barren office building but once you get to their suite it is decorated beautifully and is very clean. On their website, they have a blog that answers FAQS and explains the services that they offer. They do charge an extra fee if you pay with a credit card so make sure to either bring cash or make sure that you pay with either Venmo, cashapp, or zelle. 10/10 so far, I enjoy going here!
Great no frills Chinese food with a family atmosphere. In my opinion their egg rolls are the best around! Great lunch buffet m-f 11-2, as well as a full menu. Staff is friendly and attentive.

Giving 5 stars for quick service, HUGE portions and prices. I'm not comparing this place to other Mediterranean places I've been too. I'm looking at it in terms of value and it taste good. I got the chunky chicken salad.  Comes with pita and hummus and I had enough to take home. Don't need a lot of dressing since variety of flavors in veggies.  My man got the Tex Mex wrap and it was huge and looked exactly like the picture. Well done! The only negative was the smell of Fabalouso but glad the restaurant is clean!

All the 5 star reviews here are right! These meatballs are amazing. I've been to this truck a few times now and they are always consistent. You know you are going to get a great meatball no matter what. They are beyond fresh and you can taste it. The ones that are stuffed with cheese have the perfect amount in it that it doesn't take away from the meat itself. The other flavors are also on point. Seasoned with just the right amount of ingredients and they blend well together.

I first had them at the Manayunk Streat food festival and have been hooked ever since. Service is amazing and pricing is right. I can come, get the 3 meatball sandwich and walk away feeling full for a great price. Trust me on this....don't split it with someone. You will regret!

Overall: I will keep coming back as long as they keep up with the quality and service they provide! AMAZING!

WOW.  I don't have to drive to Oro Valley for chiropractic care anymore.  I'm so relieved to have found such a wonderful chiropractor, quick, painless and competent.  You don't find that in Tucson with any regularity.  THANKS Dr Tory.

I've given them more than one chance and it doesn't seem to matter.. They will not fix what they do wrong without an attitude.. I got all the way home all my carne asada frie meals were made with chips instead of french fries and the sour cream I paid for it was missing.. My favorite thing to get is a chile relleno I make them at home so I know how they're made but here they leave the seeds and they don't even clean the peppers.. My husband went back to get our food fixed and they were extremely rude and still left out the sour cream so all my food was cold while waiting to eat with my family and I didn't even get to enjoy it..

We took some clients to POD and had a fantastic meal.  We chose the Chef's tasting menu which afforded 5 of us an amazing spread of sushi, dim sum, fresh fish and meats that more than satisfied the team.  Add in a gigantic bottle of Sake and you've got all the makings for a great night.  Yes the ambiance is a little 'Tokyo 1998' with plastic and rounded edges coming at you from every angle but it works. Favorite dishes include the Sea Bass, Calamari Salad and Crab Fried Rice.

I am torn between giving this place 2 or 3 stars. 

I've been meaning to visit this place for months as I keep hearing about it and we finally decided to stop by on a lazy Saturday morning.

Food: The donuts were pretty good with the exception of the regular cake donut I had. It was pretty dry like it had been sitting around for awhile. The red velvet donut though made up for it. It was exceptionally good! I also had a lemon donut that reminded me of a Hubig's pie. YUM!

Decor: Meh. Kinda bland and dingy on the inside though its in a cute little house.

Service: This is where they are severely lacking. Maybe it's just me and my expectations but I would think that employees at a donut shop would be a little more cheerful and happy to see you. Mostly I felt like they could have cared less that we were there. And they weren't busy. Literally had four customers in the 40 minute span that we were there. We got a curt "What can I get for you?" upon entry and were basically ignored while we perused the offerings to decide. I think the lady who rang us up may have said 10 words to us total. They weren't really rude more just a sense that they couldn't be bothered and didn't really care that we were there.

All of that being said I may give this place one more try just to see if it was a fluke or not.

Thai Chef & Noodle Fusion is located on a dreary strip of Rt. 1 (Lincoln Highway) in Penndel.  The effort to bring new life is taking shape with a few new restaurants in a two block area.  This place is one of its anchors, and is a pioneer there over several years.  

The shop has a casual air, much more laid back and cozy than it appears from outside.  The tables on one side are separated by privacy screens made up of thin branches and curtains.  The outer wall has carved wooden folding screens that can be opened to extend for more separation.  Nice touch.  The staff is friendly, informative and prompt.  This is primarily a Thai house, although the name and menu offer alternatives in Japanese and Chinese cuisine.  I join the other reviewers in skepticism that "fusion" is a reality as opposed to a marketing gimmick.  I have only tried the Thai dishes as the owners and chef are Thai, and they come by this style honestly - they live it. 

I had a lunch special which is a combination of a choice of cup of soup, app, and entre.  I chose the Tum Yom Soup, Moon Dumplings, and Drunken Noodles with Chicken.  The soup had the strong taste of lemongrass and mild chili with ample slices of chicken and veggies.  I enjoyed it.  The Moon Dumplings were a pleasant surprise.  While small and lightly fried, they were loaded with ground mushrooms and possessed a nice earthy flavor, and absorbed the hot chili oil/sauce well.  The Drunken Noodles were very good, with the right ratio of broad noodles to veggies and chicken and a decent sauce.  It was a little bland so I asked for chili flakes to liven it up.  I was brought a shaker of finely ground orange pepper that was a perfect complement to the dish and brought it to the heat level I wanted.  Some joints don't have the right spice and offer only Sriracha, which, while hot, does not blend well and distribute evenly over most foods.  Thai Chef had what was needed.  

I can't comment on the "fusion" aspects, but I was very satisfied with my Thai lunch here.  I know that I look forward to more visits, and that makes me a fan.

August 10, 2014 - I have had dinner here a couple of times recently.  If anything, the dinners are superior to the lunch deals.  Last night I sampled their "Jungle King" and "Cashew".  The names themselves were unique. Both were nice portions of fresh veggies, tofu, chicken, with a nice mix of spices, including ginger and a sweetish curry, and large cashews on one.  An order of House Fried Rice rounded out the meal, while leaving enough to take home for today's lunch.  

The vibe continues to relax and encourage lingering.  The fact that the owners are Thai helps bring the authenticity home.

I had been anticipating trying Nada since it had opened just a short while ago - I'm a sucker for Mexican food, the endless instagrams and yelp pics of the queso had my mouth-watering, and it always seems poppin' on a weekend night. 

I'm not gonna lie, after a long day trip to Kentucky and back, in the rain, I was NOT feeling like doing but being in my bed, but after a few more looks at the Yelp pics of Nada, I convinced myself to make it to Nada in time for my dinner reservation. I was not disappointed. 

On 8:30 on a Saturday night, the place was packed, with all the tables and booths filled, and people crowding around the bar waiting for drinks. Nada has a very young + lively feel to it, and seems to be a great place to celebrate a birthday, or before a night out on the town. 

Despite the business, we were promptly seated at our reservation time. Our server, Cayman (sp.), was an A+ employee. Throughout the evening he was attentive, constantly refilling our drinks and checking up on us, and making loads of friendly conversation. He was very knowledgeable of the menu and was happy to answer any questions we had. 

We started off with two cocktails to start: 

NADARITA // The house margarita, which was very refreshing. 
It tasted fresh, without any of that syrupy-mixer taste to it, and they were pretty strong. After my 2nd one I was definitely feeling it. 

CHILE-MANGO // A fun play on the margarita! This 'rita had habanero and mango flavors, and a chili salted rim. I didn't care for it (I don't like mango), but the boyfriend really enjoyed this.

To start, of course we had to get the queso - it's basically why I insisted we go here. It was just as glorious as I expected it to be. The QUESO GRINGO was brought to our table in a copper pot, and poured into the serving bowl over a mixture of black bean puree, pico de gallo, a habanero salsa, and some peppers. The queso is not made with traditional Mexican cheese, but instead a smoked gouda - very unique!! The mixture at the bottom of the bowl added texture to an otherwise one-dimensional dish, and tons of flavor from the peppers. We mixed it all up in the bowl, and each bite was full of flavors - the smokey and creamy gouda, the spicy peppers, and the black bean puree. The chips were thin and crispy fried tortilla chips, and were the perfect salty accompaniment to the queso. GET THE QUESO. I repeat, get the queso. And don't be embarrassed to take a boomerang of that cheeeeesy pour.

After the queso, we each decided on two tacos (2 baja fish, carne asada, pork belly), and then two sides to share. Cayman told us that the elote was really good, but that the cracked potatoes were heavenly, their best seller, and being referred to as "nada fries". He could not have supported these potatoes more.

The tacos are small, like many other reviewers are saying, but not too small I don't think. The perfect size to try many things. The tortillas were good, and we really loved the carne asada tacos & and the baja fish tacos.

BAJA FISH // Beer-battered mahi-mahi with cabbage, pickled onions, pico de gallo, and a chipotle crema. The fish was perfectly fried and crispy, and the crema added a smooth component to the dish, while the tomatoes and onions added acidity. Great taco.

MU SHU PORK BELLY // This was our least favorite taco. While the pork belly had good flavor from the hoison sauce, it was way overcooked. This taco wasn't even finished. 

CARNE ASADA // I don't normally order steak tacos, but I was feeling kooky, and I'm glad I went rogue on this one. The slices of skirt steak were topped with a tomato salsa and a crema, and were delicious!!! I loved this taco.

WOOD-GRILLED CORN ELOTE // Our favorite of the two sides. The corn is served on a layer of chili mayo and topped with cotija cheese. The corn has a nice grilled flavor, and the mayo is easy to mix around into the bowl to give every bite that fun punch! Licked this bowl clean.

SWEET CHILI-GLAZED CRACKED POTATOES // These potatoes were alright. I had the highest of expectations for these after hearing our server talk about them like Garth Brooks had harvested the potatoes himself. The flavor was definitely there. The sweet chili-glaze was everything I wanted, and was complimented well by the cooling crema. But the potato pieces were too big, leaving crispy exteriors, but loads of potatoey interiors. The best parts were the little pieces in the bowl that were crispy throughout. These would be a lot better if the potatoes were cut smaller, to get rid of that middle-of-a-potato bland texture and taste. 

Will I go back? Yes. Nada is a fun, lively spot for happy hours or weekend dinner plans. The food is all really good, and so are the drinks. A great spot to go on a date or with girlfriends.

The drinks are a bit expensive but delicious. I had the Tiger Lily & Pineapple Express. Both were great. It's fun sitting at the bar and watching the bartender make the drinks.

This bar is located in a basement. Very different from other bars in I've been to in Indy. Nice & relaxed. Went midweek so it wasn't crowded.

I was also there on Saturday night 9/24/11 (see Michael T's comment).  They were out of lamb only as far as I know.  They were short-handed - just the husband and wife owners were there to cook and serve.  The place was full and people were even eating on the patio outside.  We try any Mediterranean/Middle Eastern restaurant we can find in our area and have the same experience in each one: great food, poor service.  The Mediterreanean Cafe was the exception.  Even with as busy as they were our service from both the husband and wife was excellent.  The food was excellent also.  We finally found a restaurant we want to go back to.  Loved it.

I sent in an inquiry through their website and received an automated response that they would get back to me within 24 hours on 8/22.  It's 9/5 and I never heard back.  It's okay if my request wasn't something they could accommodate but no follow-up at all is kind of unprofessional.  Fortunately, this wasn't urgent.
Love the chicken here!! Super crispy and tender and the sauce is awesome! Love their honey garlic and soy garlic and their spicy is not too spicy which makes it pleasant to eat!

Always a must after the club or bar when we visit NOLA.   Don't let the bodega/simple corner store set up fool you. The hidden gem is in the back: the chef.  Go for the shrimp or oyster po' boy....dressed (sauce, lettuce and tomato on a sesame seed hero). It's soooooo good and it's big, but not overwhelming. There's a little bit of a wait, but definitely worth it. Food is cooked to order, so eat it while it's hot. Sorry....no pics to post...I ate it too fast!!

Great place just west of Saint Louis by Olivette. Very casual, amusing service from behind the counter and very good BBQ. When we arrived, the line was almost out to the door, but we were eating within 10 minutes. Good pulled pork, good sides and great sauces. A great place for a BBQ fix if you are near St. Louis.

The atmosphere, experience, and food were all delicious. Granted, there's little to compare it to, but excellent nonetheless. Get the Sambusa for an appetizer. Go ahead and order 2. Great value and friendly service.

We came here with our friend who is local. We only had to wait 5 minutes for a table and the place was packed! They do not have a huge menu, but it seems like everything on there is good!
