I had the half roast beef po boy and a cup of gumbo. The po boy was good, but the gumbo was amazing!! It had the right amount of spice. The chicken in it was shredded up and the sausage was tender. I wish I would of gotten the bowl! My friend had the Reuben. He said he never gets Reubens, except for here. This is because we are from where the Reuben was invented- Nebraska! So it must be a good one if he only gets it here outside of home! My husband had the house special BBQ shrimp. He said it was really good. The thing is it isn't a regular bbq sauce, it more like a garlic and butter sauce. Unfortunately for my husband not much after we left he said he didn't feel good and then yelled for our friend to stop the car. Well the all bbq shrimp came back up... We are prettier sure that he was just not used to all the butter. Besides that we had a great time!
I recently moved in here and I love it. The management team is helpful and knowledgeable. The complex is very quiet and the apartments are extremely clean.

Worth the calories? Yes! Si! Oui! Ja!

Found this place on Yelp, and with the awesome reviews it got; decided to try it! 

Thank you fellow Yelpers you have not failed me once again! 

I have a very bad habit when it comes to unique food places that have so much variety! I want to try it all, every last bit of everything! Haha. 
So we decided to share two burgers. Jumping on the band wagon; we had to try the PBJ burger, and then picked out the Sweet Hot burger as well. Got a side of fries with cheese sauce, cookies and cream milk shake and limoncella adult beverage. Feeling like we covered our bases we waited. 

Super excited when we saw our food come out. Well... Everything was "mini me" version. The burgers were small and so was the milkshake. Oh well, size doesn't matter right? ;-) In this case... Nope! The burgers were beyond delicious!  Such interesting combinations that were perfect, so amazing. I love goat cheese, so the PBJ (NOT a peanut butter and jelly burger... that also sounds good though..) was naturally amazing. And the Sweet Hot was surprisingly delicious as well. The fries, sauce and milkshake were good. The lemoncella adult lemonade I got was great, and strong. The potions left me satisfied, not still hungry and not overly stuffed cause I couldn't control myself with all that goodness!. Thanks :-)

Service was ok. Guy was stressed out. But did his job adequately. 

And the decor was diner/industrial/shabby chic. The ambiance defiantly indicated that Baileys Range was "the spot" for hipsters, business professionals, family's.. In fact everyone. Great vibe!

My complaint; that there isn't one in downtown San Diego!

My Review : 

Upon entrance to Somerset, one is taken back .... 

"ah hem" ; 

as to where am I ? A contemporary wonderland ? Inception level 2 aesthetics ? oh do proceed ....

Perusing the drink menu leaves one in a state of tickle ; as amusing & unusual concoctions dot the pages ....

Shall we acquiesce to a "duck fat-washed bourbon" or dabble in "apple 3 ways" ? yes & well, yes .... 

Matrix-esque servers fill water @ sublime levels, while tending to frothy libations, which ultimately leads you to the question : "Will I Return ?"

Some say it is so ......

MVG gets a 5* for variety and selection. A 2* for value and a 0* for customer service.  They are rude and unhelpful.  In late February I stopped by and asked about the roses and was told rudely "They won't be available until the first weekend in April."  I tried to ask about a specific Rose I was interested in and the young man at the info counter ignored me and started talking to another customer.  In mid-April I stopped by to buy a planter.  The one I selected was quite heavy and one of the workers did help me move it to my cart but it didn't fit on the small cart I had so the worker told me I needed a flat cart.  The worker drove off in his cart so I thought he was leaving to get me a cart.  When he didn't return in 5 minutes it was clear that I was on my own and expected to go get my own cart which I did.  But I still needed help getting the large heavy pot onto the cart but there was no one to be seen.  I just left the dang pot on the ground where the worker had placed it and left.  Finally, today May 2 I stopped by to look for a butterfly Bush.  I told a worker what I was looking for and the worker asked, "What kind?"  I said I wasn't sure, what do you have.  Rather than take the time to help me select a plant she just said "they're over there past the cactus" and walked away.  That's it for me.  There are just too many nurseries in this city that treat their customers better.

I was in town visiting family and we were looking for an adult night out. Eventually came upon this place and it was a night to remember. Great crowd, live music, large dance floor. At the bar, Sarah was AWESOME, even helped me carry multiple shots over to our table. 
I don't see myself making the 11 hour drive to Boise very often, but I know where I'm going to get a drink when I do!

My second trip to Opal left a bad taste in my mouth.  

We were seated next to the server station and several times I heard the waitstaff making snide comments about customers.  This should definitely not be happening in front of other customers.  Period.

The bay scallop salad was fantastic!  Seriously tasty stuff.  

I honestly don't know what happened to the steak.  My first review was a mouth-watering 5 stars, but this time there were NO seasonings that I could tell.  Also, I asked for it medium-rare and portions of it were cooked into oblivion.  I could have sent it back to the kitchen, but for the cash money I was throwing down it should have been right the first time.

Well they were out of most food options when we went it was a Saturday night. 
Staff was friendly. 
No mashed no Mac 
Almost no chicken

Wedge potatoes were cold and dry

Better to go to the bob Evans down the street

This place was SO good. They let us place a reservation 50 minutes in advance, plenty of parking spots. The decoration is amazing! Even though is indoors, you don't feel unsafe and tables are social distanced. Service was 10/10. Food was AMAZING. The pizza was my favorite part. 100% coming back. Love it

Oldish feel to the hotel and it's more in the commecial area of downtown, rather than being smack in the middle of downtown.  But I'd say it's one of the better midscale hotels in the area.

Fitness room is a really good size with plenty of equipment.  Good for people on business.  Free airport shuttle too.  But other than that, nothing stood out.

This was a COVID visit and they handled it very well. 

You call your order in advance, then when you arrive you call again to let them know you've arrived. When I arrived, the young lady running orders out was already making deliveries to other patrons and just stopped by car to ask which order was mine. When she returned with my order, she used the passenger side, so we did not have to touch each other passing the bag of food. The lady was friendly, detailed and was hustling to take care of all the cars waiting for carry-out. 

Being new to the neighborhood, this was my first visit. I ordered, an Egg Roll, Crab Rangoon and General Chicken with Fried Rice. I was not impressed by any of the items. My Egg Roll was over cooked. The pockets on the Crab Rangoon's were small and contained a very small portion of crab. My entree, General Chicken was very dry and was more breading then chicken. The Fried Rice was, just nasty. 

I'm still searching for a good Chinese restaurant in my new hometown. I may circle back to China Wok, to give them a second chance, but until then, I'll continue my search. 

When: December 14, 2020
Why: Dinner - Curbside
They screw up my order every single time. Never coming here again I gave them too many chances...
The place is clean and spacious. Some of the employees have been really helpful. For a post office, it's convenient. I find the wait times better than most post offices.

I know Chinese...this is NOT very good.

Yeas, the deliver aspect is very handy, but the food was...just...BAD. Hey, if a few people liked this place....good for them. But I can tell you this: There are WAY BETTER places in the area (aka: China Bistro). The issue is...you have to pick it up.

Hey, as a fellow Hoosier, I know that many of us have not experienced really good Chinese...but...ya know...whatever fills your belly.

Always has a great stock of records coming in, both new and used.  Keith does an excellent job of cleaning up the second hand vinyl which is greatly appreciated by a collector.  The only downside would be that there's always something that I want, which will eventually put me in finical ruin.
Had a good meal with friends and a GREAT Waitress - Alexa you were awesome! Thanks for such good service.

My main experience is around lunch time and the pretzels are always warm and fresh.  It seems like they are making them all day when you are in the store.  I like that every time I go in, there is not really much of a line and you are in and out very quickly.

I was so excited to visit this place! I had seen the picture and great reviews! Being Indian I had to try this place out. First of all our waiter didn't seem to very much interested in serving us after we were shocked by the prices and needed more time to decide. Definitely expensive Especially for a Chat place! We order chai (tea). It had definitely been sitting around or burnt because I couldn't put in enough sugar for it to taste good. They did offer to replace it but at this point I just wanted our over priced food. Chai Came out Luke warm, 5 minute later food came out and our chai was cold. We ordered the Maggi, it was ok. Definitely not enough spices or flavor. The Samosas chat was delicious and warm. Small portions. The waiter was very weird towards my friend and never responded to her saying thank you. If he did it was more of "uh huh". I think he heard me offering to pay for our meal and was only polite to me after that point. If your looking for good Indian food with a great price, leftovers and great service. This isn't your place. This was definitely a disappointment. For my wallet and my belly
Came to Al's late on a Saturday night with my buddy who has also never been. Anthony was great and made us feel right at home. Ps. The urinal is awesome!

Where to start. Probably the best meal I've eaten anywhere in the world. First we started with the green tomatoes, Ceasar salad, NY strip steak cooked perfectly with mashed potatoes and spinach finished off with a liter of a Chardonnay. The desert was too die for. I only wish something like this was in Atlanta simply amazing. To eat a meal like this on my birthday was awesome.

Soooo...i went for the first time last week for my birthday. I was super stoked after reading the reviews and watching the pictures on Yelp....

Ambiance: very quaint and intimate which i loved. There are probably only 8 tables in the restaurant so i would call ahead just to get an idea about wait times. I went on a Thursday evening around 6.30pm and there was no wait time. 

Food: this was the problem for me...oh how i wish it weren't so. We had the Sake pulled pork buns (which was delicious!!!) but when it came to the ramen (Tan Tan Men)...that fell flat for me personally. I did not like the flavor...it was too bitter!!! I had no problem with the simplicity of the dish but i really couldn't get over the flavor palate. If only I could erase the flavor from my tastebuds! My husband and I were truly disappointed. I can't talk for anything else on the menu but i will surely not have that ramen ever again.

We met friends of ours that were staying in the hotel for drinks there, and at that time we liked it so much we said we'd return for dinner sometime.  Well today dinner ended up being a late lunch but we went for it anyway.  You walk into the resturant seating area through the lounge, which has a very modern feel to it.  We ordered drinks and proceeded to seat ourselves in the resturant dinning room, also decorated with very modern finishings.  The seating area also features an expansive view of the Santa Catalina mountains to the North.  We started with an order of fried calamari for an appetizer.  It came out sizzling hot with a couple of lemon wedges and a red dipping sauce.  The calamari itself was enjoyable, very tender with a light touch on the batter.  The only drawback I had with it was that the sauce was a bit to much on the ketchupy side for me, so we  just used the lemon slices instead.  I ordered the fettuccine with grilled chicken, my spouse ordered the the slow cooked rib panni sandwhich.  The fettuccine arrived with a light sauce and grilled, spiced chicken breast.  The fettuccine sauce had a nice flavor, but just a touch thiner than I prefer.  The noodles were cooked just right, slightly al dente.  The chicken breast was great, nicely spiced and very tender.  We both loved my spouses rib panni sandwhich!  The panni was very fresh, and the rib meat was very, very tender; with a subtle smoke flavor to it.  All in all, a very pleasent experiance.

What a yummy place for handmade icecream! Lots of flavors, 5 or 6 different cone choices, fair pricing, and a very pleasant staff. Be prepared to stand in a line, it's a full house (in a narrow space)!

Only reason I wouldn't say 5 stars is due to the location. There's no easy way to access this location unless you're already downtown. You could get lucky and find a parking spot, but with 2nd street becoming more popular, it's a bit difficult to get to.

I enjoyed a scoop of cake batter with sprinkles. It wasn't really all that, but my boyfriend got the red velvet cake, and that was to die for! ABSOLUTELY delicious!!

Review #102 - 2020

I had this spot bookmarked prior to our mini getaway because it was new and had a few good reviews, some of which were a little too good to be true so I should've seen the sign that it was probably written by friends or family of the owners.

The 7 of us showed up at around 6:20 PM on Saturday, June 13th.

The place is very cute, but that's probably about all there is.

We each ordered our drinks and I specifically asked if any of their drinks are made with fresh fruits and the guy told me that all their fruit drinks are made with fresh fruit, which was awesome! 

Much to my surprise, my avocado smoothie and my daughter's mango smoothie had a very strong powder taste so I told them that neither drinks tasted like fresh fruits & the lady, whom must have been the owner, told me that only their drinks on the smoothie list were made with fresh fruits.  Thanks for the clarification!

She told me if I wanted, I can tell her what I wanted the next time I come back, which will be a never-- since I only took two sips of my drink and tossed it at a gas station.  

My daughter hated her mango smoothie & refused to take a second sip of it so hers went in the trash as well!

My son got the house milk tea, which is basically a Thai iced tea, and it was ok.  You really can't mess up something that simple.

My 11-year old niece got an Okinawa milk tea, which she also said tasted like Thai iced tea, which was very odd.

My 15-year old niece got the cheese foam green tea & she said it was ok, but she wouldn't order it again.

My two nephews got two different types of lemonade drinks, and they were the only two who said their drinks tasted like what they ordered.

So, just as a mean joke, the next day before we left Reno, I asked the kids if anybody wanted a drink from Num Num for our drive back home and they all yelled, "Please no!  We're good with bottled water!"

Never coming back to this place!

Wow, I didn't even realize this was a chain! I guess it kind of has that look to it, especially from the outside, but it seems nicer than most chains to me. It has a pretty unromantic location--right by the Kroger, pretty much in a strip mall--but the food is good, and pitchers of beer are relatively cheap. The decor is a little nicer than the standard Mexican restaurant pictures of girls in 19th-century-looking traditional dresses baking bread or something.

I recommend either not ordering an appetizer, or sharing an entree with your dinner companion, because the portions are really big. Any kind of burrito smothered in sauce is a good bet. The only thing I really can't recommend ordering is the quesadilla with meat added--when mine arrived, it was almost all meat and very little cheese. Hardly a quesadilla in my book!

Another thing I really like about this restaurant is that there are two "sides" to it. When you walk in, you can either go to the right or to the left. To the right is a dining area which is mercifully free of TVs, and to the left is bar area (with tables) that does have a TV. I really appreciate the choices there, since TVs that can't be escaped are one of my biggest restaurant pet peeves!

When in New Orleans, go to Erin Rose to get a frozen Irish coffee. It doesn't matter the time of day, they'll have it and you'll be in good company because there will be other people that made the pilgrimage to get it who are there also. Splurge for the souvenir cup because it's bigger (and the drink is delicious) and it just so happens that their CC minimum ($10) is just $1 more than that of two smalls ($9), so buying the souvenir cup portion will save you from buying an extra drink you don't need...and your stomach prob can't handle more than one frozen Irish coffee.

As a bar outside of the frozen Irish coffee, it's a'ight.
Such a great restaurant. Wonderful staff, beautiful setting, and everything was so delicious. We got the scalibut, bluefin and sizzling oysters...all were amazing. A definite must!

It is good German cuisine restaurant. Considering this area is a popular restaurant. During this time, it is just like October fest. Many love to sit outside like beer garden. In the good weather, you may need to come early if you want to sit outside. The restaurant serve German food and gyro. Interesting. The chef can handle two types of food. 

We got the Jagerschnitzel. It is breaded pounded pork gravy with mushroom and bacon. The side is potatoes salad. This dish is good when you are hungry. Love the coating flavor on the pork. We also get the Doener Bowl. It is shave lamb with rice and one special sauce. This dish we added a knockwurst with sauerkraut and falafel, the wurst is good and brown finish outwardly. Falafel also tasty. 

Overall the food is good. Good thing we arrived before the big crowd. They only has one waitress. With their business they may need two. Many come here for the beer.

Got in there at 745. Wait for 15 minute to order (5 people ahead of me) and 823 the order is ready. Staff is loud to each other and yell back and forth for communication and I heard they call each other bixch. eyeroll to customer.  No table is clean. The star is for the food, better than the McDonald in kingshighway and Jefferson .

What happened here? I come here at least once a year and it's always been above average. Maybe today was just a huge miss...

As usual I sit at the bar and the staff is super friendly. I get what I always get, the seared scallops, pork belly, and foie gras (in that order, because the scallops and pork belly go perfect together!) Unfortunately they were out of pork belly :( so I got the carpaccio instead and it was good except they had rather large chunks of sea salt in certain areas which made me grab the water immediately. 

Next were the sea scallops; perfectly cooked, not chewy at all. It came with a light sauce that complimented the scallops through every bite. 

Finally the foie gras, saving the best for last! This was the biggest DISAPPOINTMENT!!! The foie gras itself was cooked excellent, however the pairings are just as important as the foie gras! Every single time I've got it there is always a small apple crumble or apple strudel (that you can tell was made in house) that offers the perfect texture balance for the the foie gras. Instead of the strudel, there were cooked Granny Smith apples. I can see why they chose that, because the crunchiness and tartness of them is a good balance, however there was no sweet element on the plate which the strudel took care of. The "sweet" element was the fig (I think it was fig) compote. I couldn't even tell what it was because it was so bland in flavor! Normally my foie gras plate is cleaned 100% but this time I finished half of the apples, none of the compote and forcefully ate the foie gras without the pairings. 

With such a bad experience already I was ready for dessert because that should save the night for me. I ordered the creme brûlée and I sincerely regretted it. The topping was torched for so long that the container was hot and the filling was melted! I thought I was eating ice cream. The topping was so hot that it even melted the chocolate they placed on top. 

This has always been my go to place for food in grand Sierra and after this I don't know if I'll be back. 

P.S. I'm going to post a picture of the foie gras I got tonight and the foie gras I had the last time I was here and you'll see the difference.
Very nice rooms , beautiful golf course, very good food and excellent staff.  Great place to stay.

I am so loving this place! Frothy Monkey serves fair trade and organic coffee, kombucha, juices, teas, and tasty sandwiches and baked goods from a local bakery. Throw in free wi-fi and you've got one sweet place to hang out in 12th South! The atmosphere in this converted house is very comfortable and mellow, with a mix of booth, table, and bar seating, plus an awesome outdoor patio and a glassed-in porch room. I think I want to get a job there just so I'm allowed to go up the spiral staircase.

Had the Etouffee and it was wonderful and I have eaten at least 20 versions of it.  The jambalaya was also very good, that I tasted off the plate of my boyfriend.  Saw the poorboy come out and asked the table next to us what they thought and they said excellent!  I will be ordering it next time.

Was there Sunday afternoon 11/8--Where do I start?? First, this has to be the only diner on the planet that does not offer full breakfast all day (only eggs). Also the only restaurant I've ever been to that does not take credit cards. But they do have an ATM machine in the lobby- so if you don't walk in the door with cash already in your pocket you effectively have to pay a surcharge to eat here. Is the owner that cheap that he or she cannot afford the small bank fee (which they can write off anyway)? Also the only diner I've ever been to that did not offer soup and/or salad and bread with a full dinner. Can you say CANNED green beans?? Lastly, the server seemed like she would rather be working elsewhere- barely spoke except to take our order.

The bartenders and other staff were very nice and the service was good every time we've been to Primebar. The atmosphere is also nice, particularly as a good place for happy hour drinks. Unfortunately, the food was pretty disappointing both times we've eaten there. I thought the first time might've been a fluke so we gave it a second try, but we both independently came to the same conclusion each time, and we're not the extremely picky type, so I'm confident we're not being unreasonable. That second time was a few weeks ago so I don't recall details well enough to mention specifically what we had and why it wasn't very good, but both of us only ate less than half of our meals and were hungry again in just a couple hours. It's actually not that the food is so extremely horrible to the point of being inedible ... we're just not used to what I call "bar food," so I think our disappointment stems from the fact that the food is priced as it would be in a decent restaurant (thus setting expectations), but it is really just bar quality food.  We'll still go back to Primebar for drinks, but we'll eat first next time.

I've been to this place since it opened and never reviewed it but if I had I would have given it 5 stars. I went tonight and the experience was just horrible. The waitress guided us to our table without even a hint of a smile, visibly annoyed by her job. Another waitress (that one at least smiled) brought us warm water ... supposedly what used to be the tea (it used to be amazing). When the food arrived it was tasteless. The rolls looked and tasted terrible (the tuna looked like left over pieces, the low quality smoked salmon was uber salty and the cream cheese was not cold). The soft shell crabs were not properly cleaned. The pad Thai had the most horrible unappetizing smell and was completely bland and piled in the plate without any effort of making it look plated. My date only ate half of it. They never asked us if we wanted dessert and just brought the check. What happened to this place? I never had a bad dinner there before. I used to go weekly but had not gone for a couple months as I had a busy summer. It seems that their succès finally got to their head and they are not willing to make any effort to keep their clients happy and live on their good reviews. It's a shame ... I have to find another place. I cannot believe how this place went from 5 stars to this ... I'm so disappointed with the whole experience.

I found a bug at the shower and I could barely sleep in my hotel room due to the high heat because hot rooms are uncomfortable for me. I really hope my family doesn't stay in this hotel again. I prefer the Wyndham in Trenton. Also, the noises that the people were making were loud and hotel staff aren't there to tell them to stop. So, I'll try my best to not come back to the hotel. What's bad is that this is near Trenton and Trenton isn't the best place to be in for that matter. Though the breakfast was good and the beds aren't too terrible. Just people making noises and it being dirty is the problem.

I have not dined off of the full menu, just in the bar upstairs (Black Duck Bar) from the bar menu. The food is straightforward but good quality. The drinks.... oh the drinks! I love a good craft cocktail and they got it right. The menu has some creative cocktails and also some of the classics- with a twist, as it is a rum bar so most of their cocktails are made with rum. I visited the Black Duck twice in a long weekend because as you can imagine so many of the alcoholic beverages you will find in the area are premixed and very sugary. This was the complete opposite. The Black Duck Down has a dehydrated orange sugar rim that they make in house and let me tell you, I may have to try it at home it was so delicious. The staff was great and happy hour is very popular. My only complaint is that not all of the cocktails were on happy hour special, but they were worth paying full price, so what can you say?

The burgers are fantastic! The food came out hot and fresh! Our waitress was really nice and prompt. The restaurant is an ideal place for watching anything because the place is filled with TV's. They have a full bar. I got the American Burger and it was cooked to perfection. The fries were good as well. Overall I will return whenever I am in the area.

The shop was literally right across the street from our hotel so I was excited to eat there for breakfast one morning. I even knew what I wanted it, an almond croissant. I ordered one with a bottle of apple juice. It was okay, really nothing special. I managed to eat it all. My parents ordered bacon, eggs, and a croissant. I tried my mom's bacon and it was way too smokey tasting. I wanted to try something else and I picked out a linzer tart because it was cute..haha.. The jam inside the two cookies was basically leather! I've never had one before so I don't know if it's supposed to be like that or if it was dried out. If I ever went there again I guess I'd have to keep trying stuff until I found something I liked. There was a pretty good looking cheesecake but I thought it was a little early for dessert lol.

I was running out of time to get a bridesmaid dress altered and decided to come here instead of my usual tailer because it is more convenient.  The dress only needed to be hemmed and that was fine.  A little more expensive than I anticipated ($55) but fine. Except that when I put the dress on for the wedding, I noticed a small hole towards the bottom that had been singed--almost like a cigarette burn. It didn't smell like cigarettes,  so my guess is that she cut it a little and then singed it to keep it from fraying. The fact that she never mentioned this to me when I picked up the dress feels dishonest to me. So next time I will be sure to leave plenty of time to go to my usual tailer.

While visiting Giant this Subway is located in I asked again about "Daily Special ". Staff said yes it was available. They were all very nice and made me a great sandwich. Told me at times when daily specials would be overridden by aSubway "special". Never heard of such a thing. Good meatball sub.

Went there with some colleagues a couple months back and had an excellent time. Staff is very friendly and accommodating. Great instruction on how it all works, the best way to make the axe stick. 
Overall a real good time. Highly recommended for some good fun.
Great little record shop in the French quarter. I found several things that had been on my list for a while. The owner, Scott, is very knowledgeable and friendly.

I have eaten a lot of mediterranean food in my life and coming from the bay area I have had quite a selection and naan n kabob definitely is on my top 3...when it comes to taste and portion size I was very pleased...this is man food...meat rice and veggies...I shared the family plate with one other person and I am a huge eater and I still couldn't finish it...I was very pleased to find this gem in reno!! The service was great I always love when the chef personally comes to each table and asks how the meal was and offers discounts for future visits :-)

I have not been to Nashville in a while and decided to go back to my old favorite sushi restaurant and get my Sushiyobi fix! They have the best sushi in Nashville! Hands down. The BEST! Taste the rice at any sushi place and compare it to Sushiyobi. Sushiyobi wins. The sushiya explained to me the difference in quality of rice that they use. The rice is not dry like most other sushi places. It's moist and has a hint of sweetness to it. Also, the fish is always really fresh here. You can actually taste the fish. Most places hide the fish with mayo and other sauces. Sushiyobi makes great rolls. The prices are pretty good too. My favorites are: Crunchy Mochi(probably the most popular), Spicy Scallop Nigiri, Rocky Mountain and you can't go wrong with the Rainbow or the Soft Shell Crab!

I've only been here once, but had such a great time.  The food was fresh and hot, the service was outstanding and the people who frequent Fish Tales are warm and funny.  I highly recommend it!  On a warm sunny day, you can't beat the views or the tiki bar on the dock. :)
Just had my first haircut w Laura. Great experience. Friendly, helpful and efficient. Will definitely be back.
Awesome place! Great sushi! Terrific Service! Excellent prices!

pho was good, flavorful. its a one size bowl, would be nice to have various sizes. honestly couldve had a 5 stars because broth was good and noodles were cooked perfect. only downfall was it took a while before someone came to take our orders, took awhile for soup to come out. but aside from that , good soup

On the day of QingMingJie(Chinese culture when loved ones visit cemetery of deceased), I was looking to buy white chrysanthemums or "Mums". Call flower shops around  Mayfair/Tacony/Holmesburg, and came across Torresdale Flower Shop. It was such a relief! The lady that answer the phone were very courteous and knowledgeable. Definitely recommend this shop! 

P. S. On the same day, I told another fellow family member that TFS got the chrysanthemums, she also stops by and got some.

Went to the new location in elmwood today! Had high hopes they had improved since moving...wrong! Ordered the Caesar salad with salmon and cup of tomato basil soup.  The soup is delicious... the salad had way too much dressing and (I know I should have ordered it on the side) but the salmon was either from a package or a can... I thought it would have been a piece of salmon... husband ordered French onion soup and French dip. His soup was not even warm... the cheese could not melt... he took it back to the counter and the girl said she could not do anything about it and to take it back to his seat! He then found a manager and she did get him a hot soup... but his French dip came out cold...and the employee who brought it out had pants on that were hanging down below his ass! Not a professional look! Because I was not thrilled with my salad I went and grabbed a slice of bread and a butter... the butter was rock hard cold... so that also disappointed me...I gave 3 stars because the new place is beautiful and the manager did bring a hot soup to hubby as well as offers us free desert... but we were so disappointed we declined and left... don't think we will be back

Stop at the little town Verdi right before Boomtown and try this place out. Got the Grill burger for $10.50 with a ministroni soup.  The soup tasted homemade and was full of a lot of veggies and noodles.  The burger had the initials marked into the bun and the cheese skirt was great!  The onion rings were more of a tempura style and were perfectly crispy.  Everything had a lot of flavor and the service was nice and friendly.  I will be back next time!

This place is definitely cute and it fits into the entire state street environment and vibe. I came in here with my boyfriend because he wanted an afternoon snack. I looked through the displays and everything looked good. He ended up getting a basic chocolate cream filled eclair and loved it. I thought it was okay and tasted like any other eclair I've ever had so I didnt care for it. The service here was definitely good though. Maybe I should have ordered a different items and tried something else.

Food is good - tasty with all that Cajun fried magic that'll make anyone happy and ready for a nap. Whatever you do, get the bread pudding. You won't be able to eat it all at once. Unless you have some superpower that'll make you the Ironman of bread pudding. It's filling and southern excellence. You know, grandma's house good!

I had sent my faulty drive and backup drive to this company. They gave me a free shipping label and I sent the two drives through that. The manager in the company acknowledged through phone that they received both the backup drive and faulty drive. Later I wanted them to send both the drives back as I did not want to go through their service. They sent me the faulty drive and did not sent me the backup drive. This three months since that incident and I am following up with them. They are now saying that they do not have evidence of getting my backup drive. Finally I spoke to them and said you guys are liars.

Please do not go through this company. I have the jobID and all the mail conversations with them. They stole my backup drive ( 1TB brand new Samsung EVO drive)

This company is a cheater company.

Cabana is a good spot for bachelor/bachelorette parties. They do a nice job accommodating large groups. Food is simple and good, and the menu has something for everyone. Our waiter, Bryant, almost ruined the experience for us though. He was very rude and talked back to almost all of our table. He scolded my friend and I for saying we would like to share two plates of food. He told us we shouldn't use the word share in the restaurant, and then proceeded to get very agitated and rude as we attempted to order dinner. I would not have given him a tip, but it was already included in our bill because we had 14 people. He made the experience very uncomfortable and really put a damper on the night. I hope this isn't how all the waiters at Cabana treat their patrons, because they will begin to lose business.

Ate there at lunch with friends for the first time today. I am gonna start doing my reviews in a slightly more orderly fashion so let me give this a try.

Atmosphere
From the outside I was expecting very simple furnishings and bare bones interior. I must say I was very surprised with the little details they have in there. The interior is a blend of middle eastern and egyptian themes with basic tables and chairs. There is modern middle eastern music playing. It does  feel slightly cramped and on a busy night I would wager you would hear the conversations of all your neighbors. The tables have the glass topper with some advertisements posted underneath.

Service
It was not very busy but our server was very attentive and helpful. She kept our glasses full and checked in on us the proper amount. She was not there too ofter but I wasn't left wondering when she would arrive. The food arrived a little later then I expected but it was fresh hot and well presented.

Food
I ordered the Lamb Shawarmas with hummus and a salad. The menu had a variety of good options as well as a large drink selection. I would almost say there were a few too many options as I was slightly overwhelmed at the number of selections but this is a mild complaint. 

The Lamb was delicious, tender, perfectly cooked, and seasoned. The Hummus was not the best I have had but was tasty and was a nice compliment to the meat. I didn't really enjoy the salad as the leaves were bitter I  ended up picking around them and eating the cucumber and tomato.  The dressing was flavorful and aside from the bitter leaves I had a stellar meal here. The Pita that came with my meal was very good and had a nice light flavor.

Was looking forward to trying but waited to be seated at the hostess stand for 5 mins, had three employees look at me and ignored us.

Left and went to Peggy's
There can be some real characters in here, but the convenience of this Walgreen's can't be beat.  Except maybe by the Schnuck's across the street.
I put a pizza box on the $250 table pad and now I have a big square on it. So, no it does not withstand heat. Their solution was to buy another one! Horrible customer service!

I've been here 3 times now.  Pleasant people, reasonable prices, and an education in the "world of beers".  I have discovered some new favorites thanks to this place. All in all, a good addition to the rejuvenation of downtown. Yayyyyyyy!

The cleaning job was mediocre.  They are much more expensive than other services I have used.  I bought a 5 session voucher and they came 3 times and now say they used up all the 5 sessions because they stayed longer on two occasions.  NOT TRUE.  Stay away from this company.  They want $120 for a bi-weekly cleaning of my 1,300 sq ft house with 1 1/2 tiny bathrooms and 2 bedrooms!  Another company who does better work is asking $75 for bi-weekly.
Very good breakfast, priced good. I will go back. Service excellent. They only serve breakfast and lunch though.

It's time for an update.  We went to the new location to check out just how well they did in the move.  We hit them right in the middle of a 12:30 lunch rush.  Michael T. was coming to join us at about 12:45 so we wanted to get there a little early to get our names in for the bar.  It didn't really help much, they only have like 8-10 seats at the bar.  I haven't heard of a restaurant reducing their size on a move to another location.  But these guys did just that.  Probably a reduction of up to 5 seats.  

So we walked in just as a group was being sat.  When we finally were seated...we sat in their seats!  Yep the bar is that small.  But that was ok...we didn't have any push for time.  When we sat, Michael T is well known in these parts and the Chef remembered my name too, we were started on a quick regimen of goodness.  And my goodness it was good.  Usually we focus on the raw because that is just what we like...but the new Menu has so much new baked stuff and cooked stuff we had to try as much as we could.  

The baked section was good, but these rolls might be harder to share for groups that aren't super comfortable.  The rolls are cooked and the stick together and fall apart.  This is NOT a complaint, it's just a warning.  They were all very good.  I especially liked the Pizza roll.  The best thing I had this time was the yooki, yuki, uke, I have no idea how he spelled it.  But it was salmon with some ginger and some sort of sauce on top.  Just wowfreakingawesomeamazing for my mouth.  I could just eat a whole batch of these.

I was able to get a nice bottle of cold unfiltered Sake.  Exactly what I needed and it hit the sport perfectly.

We walked out full and happy. 

So a few notes...
Don't go right at peak time and expect them to sit you at the bar.
We didn't sit at a table so we don't know how well the tables are served.
If you can wait til 2ish you can probably get in pretty easily.
When you do go...you will probably see 20 people you know.  We did.
Parking sucks compared to their old place.  This mall probably has the worst parking lot in the city.
Still easily top 3 sushi places.

See you there!  Yes, that's me sitting there waiting for the bar.
The flavorful food is only overshadowed by charming and attentive staff! Additionally, I was happy to hear that they are big supporters of the Pour House charity, which helps the homeless community.

I went twice: lunch with a friend and dinner with a group. Both were excellent. Food, drinks and service didn't disappoint. The dishes were unique and delicious. Some places put all their effort into serving aesthetically pleasing dishes but the taste is mediocre at best. Not here!
Excellent attention to detail - washer installed properly and carefully - on time - excellent customer service

This is a review ONLY for the their LUNCH time menu as I have never been there for Dinner.

I have been to this restaurant for lunch on several occasions, both on my own and with collegues. The food has always been excellent and the service very good. Their lunch time menu is well priced and the taste is as authentic as you can get.

I purchased a Jeep Wrangler from them in mid-June and did a trade-in with the transaction. Come July, the payment was still due in our name on the trade in. It took a week of chasing to finally hear back from someone that the car was going to be paid off. It was still paid off past the due date, obviously no concern for the potential impact on your credit. Then the temp registration expired, and no registration cards in the mail. After calling several times to get someone, we found out they still didn't have the title and cards for us a month later.  And don't bother trying the finance department, they can't be bothered to return a phone call. I feel dirty that I bought a car from them. Seek cars elsewhere, you can do better price and people wise.

This Kroger seems as good as any, and it's definitely better than some Krogers I've been in (but not as good as other Krogers farther north). There's a big wine and spirits section, and a lot of fresh, hot food, as well as a sushi bar. 

The Redbox is located outside the store, which is nice and convenient. 

Parking is abundant. Plus, this Kroger is in the same shopping center as a laundromat, so you get the sweet smell of detergent wafting toward you as you walk from your car to the store.

Me and my sister were very excited to try this restaurant. Unfortunately, even with reservations, it took 40+ minutes to receive our food, once receiving the food, the salad was fine, both plates of fries were cold and one of the burgers was cold. The cold burger was removed from the ticket, but not by our waitress. She had only seen us once to take our orders and never revisited our table. Other waiters came and helped us out once we had gotten our food. All and all and ok visit, probably won't return.

Seriously, I'm over these kinds of places - fast food mexican or tex mex....order at the counter........

Hey on Thursday afternoon, I guess it was slow enough, we were invited by the staff to sit wherever we wanted and get table service.  That was very cool.  And they were all really, really nice.  And not so nice that you thought they were trying too hard.  Just nice folks.  That's really nice to have!

I wanted to sit outside, but then the further back into the restaurant, the additional seating inside with all the window views of outside......we sat inside against the windows real close to the HOT SAUCE BAR.

So, we had some chips and many kinds of hot sauces served with our salsa, brought over to the table by our server.  I didn't care much for the salsa itself, but add a touch of the hot sauce flavor and WOW that was really yummy.
I liked best the pineapple hot one and one of the sweet mild ones....wish I could remember their names, but I liked from one end to the other!

It took us a really long time to figure out what we wanted.
But I finally opted for the NORITA thing....with carnitas (pork).
Luckily, I was able to exchange the guac for sour cream and dug right in.
The meat was plentiful on either end of the oval shaped plate.  Not as much in the middle, but it was fine - there was enough meat overall.
The flavors of everything mixed together tasted pretty good.
However, when picking out the meat alone with nothing else, it was a little bland.

Throughout the entire meal, our server was very attentive, refilled our drinks, all that good stuff you expect to have at any restaurant, but you don't expect it as much at a fast food type spot and am happy with this locations' offerings and most of all, service.  I will be back!!

Very tasty Chicken Parmesan and reasonably priced. House bottle wines a bit pricey considering home brand. One of our 6 guests had 2 of 3 ingredients of her entree uncooked and had to send back (no we did not demand free meal). Manager provided 2 large portion deserts for table as apology- not expected but appreciated. 
If nice outside, do the patio if able!
Would be 4 to 5 stars if entree was cooked right the 1st time and wine prices a bit more in line.

Went in an evening for couple dozen oysters and clam chowder... GREAT food as we have always have!! 
(Yes, this 'fresh' seafood is a little pricey, but look around folks, unless you are seeing a mirage of the ocean, I believe we are in landlocked, heart of the midwest, Indiana! SO the cost of shipping that 'fresh' meal in has to add up somewhere!)

Service was a bit better than our past couple experiences. A manager (I suppose) chatted with us (my young daughter) most every time she walked through - calling her by name. And had a very attentive bus boy keeping my table clean and water filled (he was also quite sweet and silly with our daughter). And our server checked in enough that I didn't feel abandoned. Hope this service improvement continues. Thank you!

Spent New Years Eve here. Wasn't packed to the seams and really enjoyed that. Only complaint is they need more than the two TV's (it was really hard to see either of them from where we were sitting).

Live singer was great really liked the music selection (Johnny cash, Tom Petty, etc).

It's such a great break from being on the beach without leaving the beach. You can still look at the wonderful scenic views and enjoy drinks and food in their outdoor seating area! People are allowed to walk in and out in their bathing suits. 

Our server was very friendly but seemed to be very busy so was missing a good part of the meal. 

We did observe a table filled with 4-5 servers who sat right next to the customers talking loudly with scowls on their faces.

It was packed but we were seated quickly. Everyone enjoyed their food. I had their mushroom wrap with fries. It was slightly over-dressed because the balsamic vinaigrette was leaking all over the place so definitely messing eating. 

We had to remind another server (our main server didn't bring out food) that we needed utensils and ketchup.

We have been using C&L for nearly 20 years and are very statisfied with their preformance and will be using them in the future to maintain our equipment.   We are glad to see Mitch carrying on the family tradition.

After all the hype and great reviews, we expected more.  Pricey and short wine pours, measured next to a sample pour kept on the service bar; overcooked crust on tomato pie; cold fried chicken and mashed potatoes, sent back to be heated and still cold. But mushroom side was delicious, interesting assortment. Bartenders Storm & Carolyn were a big plus; friendly and informative. Hostess was also very welcoming and outgoing.

Well I drove 14 miles to your bank on a Saturday morning to chat about your thoughtless error and to my surprise, your lobby was closed. Who knew?
So now you want to fix the problem?
Are you going to give me something for my gas expense there and back just to recieve the same checks i had? Or is this just an attempt to look good?
You know, Its very easy to see why i got the same checks, because nothing had to be done on your end. No type setting needed to be changed, no re-printing, etc. just pure profit.
The thing was, i had run low on checks and needed to pay a few bills. Sorry Hughes, but i didnt have another 10 days to wait. I would have been past due on those bills.
Furthermore, I asked for an ADDRESS CHANGE ON MY CHECKS AND NO CARBON COPY.
I was just sent the same thing. The laziest possible response to my request.
So disgusted in what youve become since this stupid pandemic started. I dont feel like a customer anymore, i feel like im under scrutiny when i walk in or like youre being told "ONLY OUR DRIVE THRU IS OPEN" we might all get a runny nose and die ahhh. Not to mention all these wierd looks your tellers give out.
Im just chalking it up as the messed up millinial world today.
As a business in which you run tv ads claiming you're so great of a bank, you should have gotten it right the first time.

Great spot, just North of 101 and 5 minutes to beach.  Fresh authentic Mexican food.  Great selection, though a bit pricey.  2 fish tacos $12.99!!!! Carnitas plate $12.99.  Anyway, deserving of your business.

I've been meaning to write this review for a very long time. I was in New Orleans for a wedding in June, and didn't want to deal with the struggle of styling my hair myself in the humidity! I decided to go with Buff Beauty Bar because of all of the great reviews. Yes they give you a mimosa, and the salon is cute, but the stylist I had was horrible.

When I met my stylist she wasn't too friendly. Walking up the stairs I asked her how she was and all she said was "good." She was not welcoming at all what so ever, and didn't even politely ask how I was doing in return. I showed her the pictures of the hairstyle I wanted, and she didn't seem too enthusiastic. 
 
I wanted to get a low side messy bun with some loose curls. I showed her some pictures on my phone for reference, and I have had my hair styled like this before from a previous hairdresser. She told me those hairstyles wouldn't look as good since I didn't have blonde hair! The pictures I showed her were actresses with blonde hair, but I've had my hair done like this before and this is what I was asking her to do. I am the client right? I should get what I am paying for. 

First she was going to just pull my hair back and put my hair in a clean side bun -- I could have done that myself! I stopped her, and told her I wanted a messy bun. She was reluctant to do it, but did it anyways. I told her I wanted my hair curled a little bit so the front was not tied back so tight in a pony tail. She said it would be a "waste of her time" because you wouldn't be able to tell the hair was curled. Mind you, I booked her for an up-do which is an hour long session.

My hair looked "okay" and they stylist literally took 15 minutes to do my hair -- the appointment was for an hour. My up-do was not worth the $55, and I will not be going back there if I visit NOLA again.
Never disappointed !! I have been here a few times and thoroughly enjoy their food but the brunch here is out of this world

I'm a New York transplant to the Philadelphia area and I've had the privilege of working with some of the finest trainers and attending the best boutique exercise classes while living in NYC. Due to personal circumstances when first moving to Philly, several months went by before I began to think about about exercising again. At this point I felt like I had lost much of my muscle definition and, honestly, I wasn't feeling good about myself.  I set out on a mission to find high-quality classes that would inspire me. From experience, I knew what I wanted and have always had very high expectations. 
And then I found RIPPEDPHL! If you want a serious "kick ass" workout and want to see fast results, then this is your place. Treadmill and HIIT kind of says it all. How can you not get a good workout? 
I am constantly amazed at the different types of exercises that are given in the classes. They are by far the most innovative and creative exercises that I've ever seen. The instructors circulate through the class, reminding you to engage your core muscles, and correcting your form if you're not doing something properly. 
I also had the opportunity of taking some private one-on-one personal training sessions. Again, off the charts amazing fast results and again, some of the most innovative exercises that I've ever done.   
 Katie and Charbal, the owners, are the most down to earth, authentic, and enthusiastic people that you will ever meet. This place has a really great energy.

I really wanted to like this place. I came to this place for dinner after yoga class next door (Dig Yoga: AMAZING!). Ordered dishes I classified safe: chicken sate and green curry. The sate came out neatly presented and appealing; the taste, however, is completely sub par. If it wasn't for the grill marks on the skewers, I would definitely say they were microwaved! They take the liberty of putting the peanut sauce for you, so regardless of whether or not you want any, you're gonna have to live with it or ask for it on the side. The green curry was bland and under seasoned. The service was exceptional.
Overpriced and parking sucks. We had to leave to feed the meter 3 times during our meal. We are locals but this restaurant is clearly for tourists that they know won't be back anyway!

Love this place. Wanted a little something different on my business trip so I went with this suite hotel. This place was brand new and the nightly rate was very reasonable. The hotel is has a contemporary ambiance and although I checked into my 1 BDRM suite a little after 12 AM, the front desk clerk was very friendly and even called 15 minutes after my check-in to see if I needed anything.  I asked for the ice machine location (there are none I learned), but the clerk arrived at my door with a full bucket in what seemed literally like 1 minute!  The bathroom was spotless; dishes, plates, pots, cups, silverware, and even a water/juice pitcher were all provided (and clean!). I was able to connect to the FREE WiFi without any hiccups. There were also 2 flat screen tv's (one in living room and one in bedroom), which was a nice touch. My only complaint was regarding the complimentary breakfast buffet. The eggs and coffee were a bit on the cold side and the bread/pastries were in that 'older to stale' range. The hotel is near numerous restaurants and shopping venues.  Overall a great experience and would recommend to anyone looking to stay at a newer, cost-effective, and clean hotel.

I have been hearing good things about this (T.H.I.S.) place --hah!  I decided to make a quick stop in here between appointments to grab something to go and, also, case out the place. 

The decor is minimalistic and modern. The space is nice, albeit small. The furniture is not the most comfortable, but there is still a cozy feel to the place. 

There is definitely a "mom and pop" vibe to this place that is appealing. The customer service is great, and the people who work here are very friendly. I was told that they specialize in gourmet hot chocolate. Certainly, the hot chocolate menu was extensive with some great options like orange, spicy and almond. The hot chocolate here is not made from syrup or powder, but, rather, actual chunks of chocolate. It really is a purist's hot chocolate! Verdict: delicious. Be warned that they may not always have soy milk available. 

I have not yet had the chance to try any of the espresso-based beverages, but I'm looking forward to revisiting T.H.I.S. place to try them out. 

There is parking at the rear of the building, accessible from the alley. However, there are only 6 spots available that are usually taken from the afternoon onwards.
Wow the older gentlemen who may be the owner is awesome.  They got my order wrong but he corrected it no prob.  Meat quality delicious.   And the food is dope.

Wonderful dinner! High points- really delicious Eggplant parmigiana, very big portion- enough for 2.  Delicious garlic bread, great Caesar salad.. And the most amazing cheesecake ever.  Service was a little slow.. And unfortunately the pace of the service wasn't great.. But the food was very good.

I was really excited to try LaScala after hearing about them and seeing all the amazing pictures on Instagram! The inside is absolutely gorgeous with light colors and black and white vintage photographs. The dining room overlooks the water and there is a nice outdoor space. 

The menu is not huge which is nice. They have a great selection of different pizzas, appetizers, and pasta dishes. The ricotta appetizer with honey was interesting and tasty! Extra bread was definitely needed for the portion size. The muscles were very delicious and served with herbed bread. 

The mac & cheese pizza was a little bit dry and under seasoned. I asked for salt and pepper but would've loved pepper flakes or even some old Bay. The burrata  pizza was great! There were generous portions of burrata and the arugula really took it to the next level!

Overall a great experience! Happy that they have a location in Philly as well!

I placed an order for delivery on a weekday afternoon. This was first time trying this place out. I ordered a burger, loaded mashed potatoes, and a slice of carrot cake. The burger was average. The mashed potatoes looked juvenile. The carrot cake was ALRIGHT. Super average food. It is extremely likely that I won't order from there again.
I like the food, I had a diker and it was cooked well. Service was good as well. Will be back in the future.

This coffee place is nice. Plenty of sitting places. It's also in a nice location by the river. My father and I ordered a latte and a chai latte. My chai was delicious and served in a cute mason jar. We ended up getting our drinks and walking along the river. Customer service was great as well.

If I could give ZERO stars I would! Usually love this place, however tonight we walked in and were treated so rudely! They close at 11 and we came in 45 minutes before closing time and were ignored by the host and three waiters, we stood at the door for about ten minutes without anyone giving us any attention, then finally a manager came over and said they would get a table ready for us, when he told the waiter you could see everyones annoyance,  we didnt walk in 5 minutes before close, but we certainly waited until 10:30 ... So disrespectful.. And while we continued to wait the waitress cleaned our table as slowly as possible, we ended up leaving of course..

Great atmosphere, fantastic food. The goat cheese tart is INCREDIBLE, and I occasionally visit just for the short ribs. Drinks are unique and delicious. Good service, and the wait is usually not terribly long. I just wish they were open for lunch!

A relative gave us a generous gift certificate for the holidays.  We used it a few months back and I have to say we had a very good experience.
--The place is beautiful and we appreciated the piano player to set a mood.  Next time we will request the bar area to hear even better.  We had reservations and were seated promptly even though they were very busy..
--Our waiter came right away, explained about the restaurant and was available to answer questions.  He was only one of many that tried to make our dining experience a good one.  I do agree that it did make it a little confusing for them and us at times.  We saw that they were prepping a large table very close to us, so I suggested that we get started with our order.  (been in the business)
--We had cocktails, Garlic Chicken Flatbread, Stuffed Mushrooms & Baby Spinach Salad.  For our entrees he had Grilled Chicken w/tamarind sauce, and I had the grilled lamb chops.  We had to try a wonderful mini indulgence.  --and of course a few to go boxes taking a little of everything home!  Everything was very good, the ambiance and service were good.  We splurged because we could!  (gift cert) & had a lunch to go!
--I so agree w/Jim T. and I'm proud to have Seasons 52 here in Indy because of what they stand for.  I love their nutritional chart and their portions make eating there a no brainer to enjoy your food without worries.   
We plan to go back and take a relative that will be coming in town for the holiday.  This is her cup of tea!

Solid middle eastern food.

The place is in the Reading Terminal, and definitely has a presence. A curated menu that packs a lot of food per item. The main draws are the platter options - either the preplanned ones or they have a make your own option. 

I ordered half grape leaf/half hummus platter and felt it did the job to satiate my craving. The grape leaves were well packed and you get a good amount of them. The platter also comes with a salad and pita bread that I used for the tabouleh and hummus. The food was good, but a bit foodcourt-y. 

They also have a nice list of smoothies that can be ordered, which I found very refreshing for a middle eastern place. I ordered their banana, strawberry, and blueberry option! There's a host of add ons that you can throw in (protein, peanut butter, etc.) but I kept it simple.

The workers were friendly and were able to explain the menu options well. There was hardly any wait to order when we went, and once adding in our order, it was quick and fresh when it came out. 

Overall - would likely go for just a smoothie if I came back here, I thought the food was alright enough, but nothing stood out that was crave inducing.

Check out their weekly specials-- Chris's offers a $6 lunch special every day.

I just got back from enjoying a chicken salad club with bacon, french fries, and a drinks... with tax, a very palatable $6.50.   I've been equally happy with other $6 specials, which have included an asian tuna salad, and a fried fish sandwich.

Note that there is not live jazz during lunch, but it is a nice atmosphere and good service, if not the fastest.

If you like Chris's and are looking for good deals, you should link up with them on Facebook.  They often have happy hour freebies for their Facebook followers-- I've enjoyed some great crawdads on the house.

Not the best, but certainly not bad. Went here with my parents a while back & it took quite a bit for us to get our food. Our pizza wasn't even super hot when we got it...huh?

Since I've discovered Bellagio, I probably will stick with that instead.
Ummm was brought to my job by a vendor. Ice cream had no taste and toppings were stale and not much selection.
Poor service after taking 15 minutes for a milkshake. Friend next to me just ordered a small fry and that took 10 minutes as well. At least the food tasted semi decent.
Food was good, friendly staff, beers were tasty.

Amazing! Super knowledgeable staff. We were first timers and we had a blast. Maria was patient and super nice and they roll the cigars in house! I highly recommend for novices and it seamed like the regulars were having a good time too.

Not what I expected, at all.

I ordered hot tea... it was flavored? Mango or peach, not sure, but I was expecting plain green tea. I wanted plain green tea, they should have let me know it was flavored.

I ordered Tom yum soup with chicken, should be pretty basic but the chicken chunks were so large I had to fish them out and chop them into bite size pieces. I was not expecting a some assembly required meal.

I ordered the chicken pad thai with egg fried rice. Both were cold. Like not even the center was warm... been sitting out for quite some time cold. Since I was one of the first ordered, I was not expecting the food to be cold.

 Also the pad thai was orange? I've never seen orange pad thai before. It left an orange film on the plate, utensils, my mouth and napkin. Pretty disgusting really.

I feel like I had a pretty good sampling of their foods and I'm not impressed. Will avoid in the future.

Very dirty hotel.  The carpet and the skuffs in hallway are extremely gross. Cumon a little touch up paint goes along way. Then I get to the 1st room they gave me and it was unacceptable. Gross partied on a million times couch and the weird layout of room was very odd and very used. To say this hotel needs a face lift is an understatement! They charge u a crazy amount to park even more to valet. Then they charge for wifi. 12 dollars for 24hrs. 

So the first room was extremely dirty. Carpet, walls, curtains, vent, bathroom, all dirty. Clean the room, its your job!. I've stayed at many Sheratons and never seen a property so in disarray. So they give me another room. A little better but by no means what I'd expect from Sheraton. The long walk to the second room was more of the same. Trash, puke stains, spills everywhere.  So gross!

So I'm 60+ dollars into parking and WiFi, unsatisfactory room dirty windows on the inside. I mean forehead prints on the windows. It just seems like nobody cares. The first night it was pretty cold outside and so was the room. The heater barley works. It didn't turn off all night and it never reaches the temp I set it at. Which was only set to 76 degrees.  I really feel like I got bent over and it keeps getting worse. 

So the second nite. There was a fight or some extreme violence in the room close to mine. I tried to stay out of it. But after 45 mins I couldn't ignore it anymore. So I walk down to convey my concern and the front desk attendant says "oh this happens all the time" I will send security over there. Obviously I didn't want to get involved,  police did come and it quieted down around 2am 

 I got 1 more night here and I would do anything to get out of it. But I fear that this is probably a little better than the other old hotels nearby.  So since I'm already paid I have to stay. But I will never stay here again. And it makes me a little leary of Sheraton altogether.  Up until now I've never seen a hotel this nasty. Dust, filth, stains everywhere. Believe it or not I could say more but I think I've said enough. The ratty couch and dusty cushions seem so uninviting.  The trash up and down the hallway, numerous stains, drug bags or what looks like paraphernalia everywhere. The violence and the front desk non caring attitude was a real bummer. I plan on saying something to someone @ hotel. But I really needed to vent. And I hope this might inspire some changes. So thanks for checking out my review. I don't dwell on negative so this is probably my first negative review. But I had to get it off my chest.

Front desk staff is rude, specifically LeRon.

I am currently on tour with my job and we are staying here. I got stuck in one of their elevators for 10 minutes, where it completely shut off on me and was unresponsive to anything I did. I luckily had my phone with my and connected to the wifi to call my coworker to call the front desk. I was let out eventually, but very shaken. I approached this LeRon and his handling of the situation was 100% percent unapologetic and brusque. His response verbatim? "Well what do you want from us?" and to contact my company manager instead of going to the front desk. 

So instead of putting on the lens of an actual customer service worker, I get "too bad." No solution, no reparation, no sincerity. Not sure about any of you reading this, but do you think it's ok to be stuck in an elevator at a place you are visiting, to be met with a "Well what do you want from us?"

I am rating this place a 5 star for the food alone. Everything we ate here was cooked perfectly and the dishes are extremely authentic to what you would find in England. Something that is very hard to find here in the US.

For starters we tried the sausage roll and welsh rarebit. On the entrees I had the large fish and chips with cod. They also have a haddock option. Easily the best fish and chips I've had in the US. My fiance had the curry chicken salad which she also loved.

If I were to take off stars it would be for two things. The first was the slow service. It wasn't horrible, but it could have been better considering the place only had 4-5 tables with customers.

The other complaint was the lack of low tabletop seating. The majority of their seating is high tables, sans 3-4 tables off on the corner. Everyone that came in after us also wanted low top seating so I know we aren't the only ones. As a disabled person I cannot sit in a booth or high top table. Something worth considering if the place is busy.

I'm not really sure what the St. Louis style BBQ is other than the style of ribs, but the sauce on everything here is sweet.  Too sweet. The potato salad was even sweet. I gave them 2 stars because they were so friendly. Give it a try if you want because they were on the tv show bizarre foods, but you've been warned.
I would say very beautiful complex. Went to apply on Thursday. Didn't have a good reply to my application, felt a tad bit misled and am in hopes of a call back on Tuesday from email submitted.

I have tried several desserts, my favorites include the chocolate mousse tart and chocolate cupcakes. Brunch is good also, they use fresh ingredients and the dishes are creative. I enjoy the waffle with peaches, steak & eggs, and sausage omelette. I plan to try dinner which is only offered Fri. & Sat. It's byob.

About 12 years ago we stayed here while looking for a home to buy. It's definitely not the same place that it was then. The good:  no bugs, roof didn't leak, quiet, surface clean (except for the tub.. The "interesting" features:  several holes in the walkway ceilings (some covered with black plastic and duct tape), enough mildew in the sauna tub to worry me that something might crawl out of one of the jets while I was using the tub, garbage left on the private balcony that we had to clean up, and the  blackout liner behind the curtains in the bedroom actually had several holes. And this was one of the presidential suites. We would not have stayed here had we not gotten in so late and been so dog tired that we didn't notice much other than the mildew in the tub. I would have given it only one star except we did stay in a worse BW years ago. All of our other BW stays have been very good experiences.

There are two lines -- one for your meal, and one for add-ons such as fries and drinks, which I think does cut down the length of time you have to wait in line. 

I ordered the chicken tenders sandwich and was disappointed that it was basically a long loaf of bread with dry breaded chicken and some melted cheese. The bread was overwhelming the chicken. Adding tomatoes, lettuce, and several different kinds of sauce would have improved the experience because it was so dry! Try eating it without a soda or water--nope! The fries were good but not amazing, which is the erroneous impression I got. The fries were also served on baking paper, so they spilled everywhere. I mean 500 paper plates cost around $4 or something at the grocery store. Really, baking paper?

What I noticed is that the employees were all tough-looking macho men with tattoos and hairy arms (where are the female employees?). Regardless, they're friendly and attentive, which is a plus.

This is definitely a place where local employees go to grab lunch--it was certainly busy. And what I love about Philly, and this place too, is the diversity. It's great to sit down and eat a meal (albeit subpar) and see a range of shapes, sizes, colors, and ethnicities. 

Editors, designers, and English-language mavens might find this place's menu jarring--inconsistent capitalization, poor syntax, misplaced punctuation, haphazard color scheme, and extraneous italics.

Some of the better bread I've had from a sub shop! I got the Italian sub and it was average, not a whole lot of flavor. My husband got the chipotle steak and his was pretty darn good, so I have failed.

Very well put-together hot dogs. They have a Mac and cheese dog any kid or teen would love. As far as the buns go, not my favorite but ok. 

If you're a dog person I'd recommend a stop-in for sure.

Let me start off by saying I had a promo that expired which I knew about but the price I paid would still be good. So before I ordered I was trying to explain that and the owner maybe didn't understand or I didn't explain it correctly but he started giving me a bit of attitude and said it was no good and contact deal chicken as they were responsible for refunds, ok finally he said he would give me the value of what I paid and I said that's all I wanted. Ok so that was solved. They brought me the chips which were fresh and tasted good but the more I ate they had an aftertaste. Not sure if it was the oil the chips were fryed in.i ordered the carne asada enchiladas and the steak was good with some nice chunks but the sauce had a wang to it. Overall the service was ok but I was a little disappointed in the food and have had better in other Reno Mexican restaurants

This was my first visit.  I had heard how great the food was and I wanted to try something new for my birthday.  We ordered lettuce wraps and pan fried wontons as appetizers and they were very good but before we could finish enjoying those our entrees were already out. It felt very rushed. Unfortunately my entree had no flavor.  I had my husband try it and he said the same. His dish on the other hand was very good. We did have dessert and it was very good as well but we had to ask for dessert. It was not offered. Don't know that I would go again.

I had been meaning to try Bing Bing Dim Sum for a long time, so when I finally got a chance a few weeks back I was so excited. I actually didn't realize it was a fusion spot until getting there (fusion gets dangerous because it's hard to do well), but I figured I'd give it a shot since I was already there. Overall I was disappointed with the dishes. The price makes it even more disappointing. 

Ordered the
--Roast Pork Bao - a roast pork sandwich in different packaging. I would take any other bao over this one. It was just a bad combo. 
--Spicy Cucumber w/tofu - really great! Light, refreshing, and a little tangy. 
--Pork Soup Dumplings - the pork was fine but the skin was a bit too thick (making the dumpling somewhat bland). 
--Soy Sauce Pork Noodles - more expensive and a little less flavorful than ones you'd get in Chinatown.

If you like really like Chinese food, then I suggest you stick to Chinatown

If you are looking for a farmers market, look elsewhere -- this place is something, alright, but a farmers market it is not. Just a bunch of overpriced "artisan" food vendors. If you buy into the whole main-line elitist attitude, you might enjoy paying the premium and eating a fillet of beef from a cow that was raised on ancient grains hand-grown by the Dalai Lama. However, if you want fresh produce, there are a few REAL farmers markets around. Or if you want quality food without the jacked-up cost or main-line attitude, you need only look across the parking lot

Tate's was good about 15 years ago. Now, the service is absolutely terrible. Very slow, and they don't even give you menus when you sit down. 
The food itself is no better. God forbid you order a fried egg. I guarantee the yoke will come out broken. The home fries Tate's was once famous for are now greasy as hell!
I refuse to eat breakfast there, but I decided to give lunch a try today... Same bad service along with a very dry, very bland cheesesteak. 
I'll never go back

Average Chinese food.
Very Informal.

Not much of a dining area, I noted as I came in for carry out.

The rice was good. 
I had Orange Chicken and it was average. There was more fried gunk wrapped around the chicken chunks then chicken as most average Chinese food places. 

If you are hungry or have a craving this will do but it won't bowl you over in any sense.

Note: There is limited parking in this strip mall. There are many restaurants in this small strip mall so when it is lunch or dinner time you might be hard pressed to find a spot.  Plan your time accordingly.

Tonight was my very first time going to see music at The Basement East!!! I don't know why it has taken me so long to check out a band here...I was the lucky winner of 2 free tickets to see Austin Meade (opening act) & the Read Southall Band - THANK YOU @NowPlayingNashville

The music was great and the venue is simple and easy to navigate.  I highly recommended coming early to grab a seat at the high bar tables... (most of the venue is standing room only). There is a free parking lot and nearby street parking.  I'm not sure If everyone was at LOTG but it wasn't that busy tonight.  I'm not sure how the venue picks the lineup but I thought both bands were really good! Austin is from Texas - favorite song was Savannah! Read Southall is from Oklahoma and describes themselves as Southern Rock & Crunchy Country . My favorite song was "Why?" - Loved belting out the lyrics!  The drinks were great and the bartender was really nice.

Worst experience with this theater everrrr. Me and my
grandma have been coming to this theater for a long time and since they been under new management, it has turned into a shit storm. Also, my boyfriend left his wallet there on accident a month ago. Called the SAME night and said they had it. They said they would hold it until it was picked up and didn't have it there when i went to pick it up (we live 2 hours away) Also when he called and asked if there was cash (supposed to be $400) they said there was cash in it when it was found but it was no longer there. This company has turned into nothing but crooks and unprofessional employees. Stop hiring ignorant high schoolers.
Best cheesesteaks on either side of the Mississippi. Highly highly recommend. Bring cash and go at an odd hour to secure a spot at the bar.

Been wanting to try this place because there were so many great reviews.

Atmosphere: hipster, modern 

Menu is different. I ordered the build your own breakfast: toast, 3 slices of bacon, 2 over medium eggs and avocado. Was pretty good. Also ordered hot cocoa with whipped cream and their homemade marshmallows. It was okay, didn't finish my cup. The homemade marshmallows were pretty good though. 

Husband ordered the build your own breakfast: 1 eggs over medium, arugula salad, avocado, poblano peppers and toast. He enjoyed it. He also ordered their dirty chai latte. 

Location is near downtown. Noticed that there were a lot of homeless people right outside of the building in the grass area.
I almost don't want to review this for fear that it'll grow busy with people. This bar is quiet, warm on a cold night, and perfect fit an after dinner cocktail.

For the price this is one of the better fine dinning experiences I've encountered.  If you are interested in trying delicious food, multiple courses and artistic plating then look no further in Nashville.
