The cuisson was absolutely on point, I couldn't find much to fault during the meal - outside of subtle balancing of some courses.  The atmosphere and intimacy with the crew working on your food was what I savored most.  Essentially the entire dinning room is sitting at what is traditional considered 'the chef's table.'  They are excited to talk to you about their food, inspiration and most anything else.  We felt so incredible welcomed in this warm environment - a special shout out to Will for taking time during the meal to make a special 'suckling pork skin sandwich' for us between dessert courses, as he easily picked up on my gushing excitement to be eating such a delicious meat.  The pairings were mostly well done as well.

If we are ever back in Nashville you better believe we will be making another stop here.  Really delightful and absolutely on par with 1 star michelin food I have tasted in Chicago, NY or Europe.

don't waste your money - hole through the cigar after barely getting a few puffs - brought it back for them to see - and was told... that's how they are rolled... right - I said it is rolled incorrectly... got into an argument and told I didn't know what I was talking about. Um no my friend - you don't roll a cigar so it has a HOLE in it!

don't waste your money - there are better cigar stores in New Orleans with QUALITY merchandise - this stuff is just crap

I've been going here for about 5 years! Two happy pregnancies and babies later! I originally wanted a female dr but Dr.Garol was so awesome and same for Ruby his Medical Assistant. They know exactly who I am, ask about my kids, job etc. One time I showed up 2 hrs late for my appt thinking it was later than actually scheduled. I was told that if I wanted to be seen that day I would have to see a RN or reschedule with Dr. garol. I said RN was okay and apologized for my unintentional tardiness. And who shows up in my room? Ruby and Dr. Garol! Love love love them. Yes it's a busy office. But I'm not complaining about taking the rest of the day off.

I used to get my Brazilian's done by Kim when she was at Better Health & Tanning Spa. I stopped going to her for a while and found her again at this salon. Long story short? She burned my vag twice with wax that was too hot, and the second time couldn't be  bothered to answer the phone despite multiple attempts to contact her. 
DO NOT GO TO HER FOR YOUR BRAZILIAN. YOU WILL FEEL LIKE YOU HAD INTERCOURSE WITH A CACTUS IF YOU DO.

This was my second time here and it was no better than the first. 

The first time I ordered the paella that was mediocre with very little meat. 

This time around, I ordered the mango frozen margarita that wasn't to par and I didn't even drink half of it. 

My friends and I also ordered don Julio shots. The waitress came back with them and we took the shots. We realized quickly that it was definitely not don Julio. After talking to our waitress she said she gave us another brand b/c Don Julio was out, but tried telling us their both the same price. 

Pretty disappointed we weren't told of this switch up and wish it would've been comped for. 

And you get cut off at 4 drinks... Not even buzzed!

They also forgot my friends burro and he had to wait an extra 15 minutes after all of our dishes came out.

This place looks awesome, service is just not there.

My first review, and of course it's because I was so disgusted I had to warn others.  If you like to take health risks and be ignored, then this place is for you.

I ignored the recommendations of others and decided to give this place a try.   The place is very dirty and the service/staff is awful.  I'm a huge fan of eclectic places and no-b/s service/staff, but dirty taps, mold and a don't-want-to-work-here attitude on top of bad hygiene is inexcusable.

The food?  How anyone could award this place anything had to come from friends and family.  No one with any prior pizza eating experience would ever vote for this place for any reason.

Once again, I need that half star that Yelp does not allow us to have. 
The food here is a solid 4 Stars. The service is 3 Stars, so this rating is actually a 3 1/2 Stars. 

My go to's here are the Baby Spinach Salad, Grilled Salmon Filet and the Grilled Shrimp. The BSS is topped with crumbled Goat Cheese, Candied Walnuts and tossed in a Wild Berry Vinaigrette. I'm typically not a fan of walnuts and will pick them out, but not here. They have given me an appreciation of walnuts that I did not know I had. The dressing is also one of those restaurant vinaigrettes that I wish was sold in the bottle. Being a fan of Goat Cheese, I am pleased that they are generous with their crumbles. 

The Grilled Shrimp is on the menu as an appetizer, but I order it as an entree. After my Baby Spinach Salad, these beautifully grilled shrimp are just enough to satisfy my hunger. I'd say in my humble opinion that they are better then some seafood restaurants. 

My other favorite entree is the Grilled Salmon which is topped with a dill sauce. The filet is a large portion and is served with vegetable rice. I'd prefer a side of steamed vegetables, but the rice is still an except-able side dish. Again, it is a quality that you'd expect from a seafood restaurant. 

The service can be hit or miss here. To be honest, if you have a male server, it is a hit. If your being served by a female, it is definitely going to be a miss. The male servers here are more friendly, attentive to details and make it a pleasant dining experience. The lady servers are less friendly, are slow, forget things and are more interested in turning the table then creating an enjoyable dining experience. I've been here enough, to be able to make this comparison. 

Will I return? - Yes, with hopes of a male server

When: December 19, 2017
Why: Christmas with friends

Sorry, not great.  Ordered an overpriced salad and had a bunch of romain lettuce with a side of dressing.  Sure, the caesar dressing was vegan which is a rarity, but $7 could have bought me a lot of lettuce.  The pizza tastes fine.  I wouldn't give the flavor more than a "fine."  Speciatly pizzas are quite costly- $19.  I'm interested in knowing why Blackbird charges $3 for mushrooms on top of a pizza.  I suppose if you are a true vegan you go here for more than the taste or value/price point of their food.  That's gotta be why they've received such high ratings.

Do not use this DR. He prescribes addictive medicines then cannot get the prescriptions right and the prescriptions run out leaving a patient in withdrawal. They do not respond to urgent voicemails, ignore faxes and calls from the pharmacy, and this happens every cycle. He requires a visit every 60 days, but they schedule past the 60 days. I believe this practice of ignoring patients who are having to stop taking medicines that warnings say DO NOT suddenly stop taking medicine is malpractice and a call to our lawyer is next after writing reviews to let people know to never ever use this DR. He is bad news, period. Or his office staff, but the buck stops at the top, and hard to blame a horrible team if the DR does not care. The past few years with Dr. Ashtok Patel has been a repeating nightmare from day one.

The space is the clear winner! An excellent way to be the neighborhood coffee shop is to provide space for all your clients. Shaded patio in back was fantastic, small area for kiddos (snack cups were fab too). Agree service could be quicker but the $1 refills on coffee encourage you to settle in and relax. They aren't promising quick so if you can be patient and relax and read or chat the wait is no biggie. Everything salmon bagel was bland and the vanilla Latte ran a bit sweet. Staff were friendly. Decor was eclectic.

The food is wonderful but comes in small portions considering the bloated prices.  

I had the okra and collard green sides, both of which tasted really good.  The fried chicken was very good as well but for $20 I got a tiny drum stick and smaller than average thigh piece.  

The chicken pieces were so small they were more like quail pieces or perhaps hummingbird pieces.  The drum stick was more like a dwarf stick.  I'm exaggerating but you get the idea.  That is my gripe, give me more chicken in my main entrée.  

The service was okay albeit a little disorganized.  My food took forever to arrive.  

Very modern and well appointed interior.  The restaurant is clearly going for a cutting edge hip hop ambiance and succeeds to that end. 

 Ms. Tootsie's serves up tasty food but much sure you bring a full wallet if you want a full stomach.

So I love Target...I spend way too much time and money shopping at targets. I love the specific items Target has. But unfortunately this target has a serious problem with keeping items in stock, especially in the beauty and home decor sections. If you walk through the store a lot of the shelves are empty. The selection in the grocery is good, but it seems as though that has come with the cost of less selection everywhere else. What is especially frustrating is that the app will say this location has items in stock but then once I get to the store the item is no where to be found.

Why I will never return to the Sage Diner in Mount Laurel, NJ:

While having breakfast this morning, a friend stopped by to show me a proof of a marketing piece. He told the waiter he would not be eating and would simply like a glass of iced tea. The waiter politely informed him there was a $2.50 minimum for him to sit in the booth whether he purchased anything or not. My friend and I were both... embarassed and humiliated ... and instead of causing a stir ... he simply left.

When checking out, I "attempted" to explain to the cashier what had happened and why I would never return. She was determined to justify the policy ... which I could literally care less why they had such a policy ... and simply wanted to explain why I would never return ... and let everyone I know why I would never return.

So ... if you ever go to Sage Diner ... understand there is a $2.50 minimum PER PERSON, whether you order anything or not, if you choose to sit in one of their booths.

I understand this is their business and their right to have any policy they choose. It is also my right to refuse to enter their business again.

Yummy food
Went on a sat no line maybe because it
Was after lunch Went here around 3pm Oysters were so good Poboy is Amazing Soft Shell crab is amazeballs service was excellent
Food came fast. We had gumbo 
Chargrilled oysters and a soft shell crab poboy

Food was excellent once they got my order right. The place was a little dirty and the waitstaff was helpful but snooty and did not divulge the policy of their entertainment coupon discounts and daily specials. My coupon says "buy one get one free of equal or less value dinner". The menu said "kids eat free with purchase of one adult dinner". It did not say no sandwiches or apps or mention that it is only good for entrees for adults and children. I presented my coupon and the waitress said nothing when I ordered. She gave me my check and said "oh yeah, I can't give ya the discount, because you ordered burgers." WHAT???? It is an 11 dollar burger so isn't that the same price as a friggin entree? Read this fine print chickie...It is a shame this happened because the food was good. The meals are huge and tasty. I order the gyro and they forgot the Tzatziki sauce which put a damper on things because like I said the meat was really good. The waitress went to grab my plate and I shrieked with horror of the thought of a chef getting scolded for forgetting Tzatziki sauce and dumping a load of jizz on my gyro. I asked for a cup of sauce and I got about 1 tablespoon of sauce. YAY! Now that made it taste like a Gyro...NOT!!! The onion rings were off the hook and my friend's burger looked delish. It's just a shame that "little thing" get in the way and ruin a good time...and ruin a good review for great food as well. MEH.

As I get prepared for yet another Tenderness Tour event in March, I'm finding myself hitting the Indy trail system more often including this particular trail, Fall Creek Greenway, which is actually the closest trail system to my home and one of Indy's more underrated trails. 

The Fall Creek Trail/Greenway starts at the Skiles Test Nature Park trailhead, a small park that is also one of my favorite spots in Indy. As is true of most of Indy's trails, The Fall Creek Greenway has some fun detour options, ranging from a 1.5 mile limestone loop trail that is a great spot for jogging as it passes the Fall Creek overflow pond to a 10.5 mile pedestrian trail that follows the George Kessler Boulevard plan that travels along a waterway and some really awesome bridges. 

While the Fall Creek Greenway "feels" less accessible than the far more popular Monon Trail, the truth is that the Fall Creek Greenway is easily accessible at several points along the trail and offers several parking options in safe areas. The Fall Creek Greenway has, at times, had a reputation for being a less populated, less safe option but if you check out the stats you'll learn there's actually more crime along the Monon Trail and it's for the most part considered to be a safe trail. I'm a disabled wheelchair user who typically wheels alone and I've never had any trouble on either trail. That said, I'm a big believer in practicing trail safety.

The Fall Creek Greenway has some delicate wetlands along the north side of the trail, though you should probably take note that these areas can also flood during times of excessive rains. While I've never seen the trail not able to be traveled, there are times you should definitely plan for a wetter trail. The south section of the trail ties into the Monon Trail, while you can also catch the White River Wapahani Trail directly off the Fall Creek. 

The majority of the Fall Creek Greenway is nicely paved and easily wheelchair friendly. The upkeep on the trail seems to be a notch below that of the more populated Monon - I've seen storm damage take a few days to clear and the trail can occasionally get a little muddy in spots from the rains. Personally? I like the less tailored feeling of the Fall Creek Greenway along with the fact that it offers more natural sights and sounds than the Monon Trail. You get to walk/run/bike/wheel along water quite a bit including a stretch near Fall Creek Parkway that  is truly right next to it much like a similar trail up on South Bend. If you're a wheelchair user who is prone to spasticity or you have difficulty steering, you should be aware that the waterside sections of the trail do not offer railing and you should plan to ensure your safety. 

The Fall Creek Greenway Trail has the usual educational stations along the route, especially near the Fort Harrison State Park area but they exist throughout the trail. The trail is mostly flat with some minor gradual inclines that, once again, are more frequent around Fort Harrison. It's relatively easy to spy fish, birds, deer and other animals along the trail and in the small but beautiful Skiles Test Nature Park. 

If you're needing some time alone with nature, Fall Creek Greenway is likely a better option than Monon Trail. While people certainly utilize this trail, it's an awful lot easier to use the trail without the forced social interactions and every two minute exclamations of "on your left" that are part of life on the Monon Trail. However, if you love the social experience of the trails then this one likely won't satisfy you. 

I'm knocking off a star primarily due to what I'd consider to be slightly lesser upkeep than is offered on the Monon and those occasional days when the north section is a little more challenging to use due to flooding and weather factors. However, these really are minor considerations. Indy's trail system is awesome and disability friendly and Fall Creek Greenway is a terrific trail for walkers, runners, bikers, wheelers and those who have disabilities that include sensory issues (though if these individuals gravitate toward water, I'd exercise caution). While the Indy Parks website doesn't talk about it much, there's also a section that goes north into the Geist area. Again, it offers far more nature and beauty than the Monon with a few extra inclines.

This is where I go to pick up my ingredients to make my famous Bibimbap.    
The store has a good variety of fresh greens and meat selection.  The aisles are just a tad small, but that's the case with most Asian grocers.

Basic, inexpensive diner food. It's quite good if you keep your expectations at 'reasonable'. That being said, I would go here again. 

I went here in the morning while all the drunkards were passed out. It was very relaxing.

Went to the store shopping for a 20th anniversary engagement ring "upgrade."  Found a beautiful setting.  The diamond selection they showed me wasn't to my liking, so I told them I'd think about it.  Came back a few days later ready to purchase the ring, but the setting wasn't in the case and they couldn't find it for several minutes.  They didn't like that I had gone ahead and purchased my own diamond for the ring, and asked me to give them a chance to be competitive with the diamond I had bought (after saying that I hadn't told them my true budget when I came in initially, which was not true).  Since the diamond I had purchased hadn't arrived yet, I said sure.  

Came back this Saturday with my diamond, again ready to purchase.  Again, they couldn't find the setting, and after 5 minutes of looking for it, they showed me the comparable diamonds.  They were lovely, but they couldn't match what I had.  Then they "found" what they said was my setting -- only it was not the same one at all!  They were all set to order me the totally wrong ring!  We had to convince them we were right about it (we had written down all the information).  So they started searching again and I told them I'd come back.  At that point I had honestly had enough -- I don't like the idea that things disappear from a jewelry store -- are they so disorganized that they lose $2200+ items all the time?  Do they not care enough about customer service to keep a note to themselves about where they put things?  

I then went across the street to Bernie Robbins and had an excellent discussion with their salesperson Ellen.  While I was there I did get a voicemail from Jay Roberts saying they had found the setting....but too little too late.  I tried to call their store Saturday night to leave a message letting them know I'd purchased another ring -- but NONE of their phone numbers works properly.  Every number listed sends you to this automated message that won't allow you to leave a voicemail....it's not even a "You have reached Jay Roberts jewelers..." type message.  Well hopefully this review will find them and they will "get the message."

Closes pretty early and no real place to eat it in besides the benches for people waiting for their orders. Hot and fresh with large portions. I particularly enjoyed the fried chicken necks which were very lightly battered. The jumbo wings really are jumbo sized and a meal by themselves. Crunchy with no spice if people are expecting Popeye's. Collard greens stewed with bacon and pretty tasty as well.

They also have po'boys and other things on the menu but going by their name I thought I would do the chicken in a box.

I went to this location with the BF a few weeks ago and was sadly disappointed.   The food was just okay, but the service was definitely questionable.  We waited for our drinks (soda, no alcohol) for over 15 minutes.  When our food arrived, it took our waitress 25 minutes to ask if everything was okay.  When we asked for fresh parmesan cheese, she told us they don't have it.  Ummm.  Really?  Since when?  I think she was just too lazy to get it and bring it over.   Then, it took another 10 minutes to receive the check.  FAIL.

Our dishes were mediocre at best, but the lack of customer service is my deterrent for ever returning.
Neat place to stop for dessert after a nice meal. Dessert were fantastic and drinks very good. Very creative

Attractive setting for what is essentially fast food. However, the food is prepared fresh to order, and it was quite good. But don't think that because it's a fast food-type setting that the food will be inexpensive -- expect to spend $12-16 per person depending on what you order.  And yeah, the soft drink machine is pretty cool.

Unfortunately, the Echelon Mall has been on the decline for a decade.  About half of the stores are currently empty, or display only.  As of 2011, there are a few stores worth stopping by for.  But most of the other area malls are better.

But there is a light on the horizon.  Echelon has been transformed into "Voorhees Town Center" which is a combination of condos, the Mall, and a series of small specialty stores which mostly are set to "open in Fall 2011".  There is a good chance Echelon will be a much more fun place to visit next summer when we're back in the area.
I love the big rink, friendly knowledgeable and always helpful staff.. Great place to skate

Vintage Stock has one large floor almost entirely devoted to video games, most of which are retro.

I saw plenty of decent prices on games and accessories. Along with a couple of SNES games, I picked up an SMS controller for a very fair price.

I suggested to my wife that we live in this store. Sadly, she shot that idea down.
Wonderful hotel & the best complimentary breakfast I've ever had. Thank you for treating us great!

Pizza ordered at 6:24pm on Tues, 3/17.  Expected delivery time at 6:54.  pizza arrived at 7:23pm, lukewarm at best.  Crust had cold, uncooked spots.  Delivery driver could care less if I left ANY tip.  We order pizza once a week usually. If you are loking at pizza, look at Jet's at the corner of Concord and Nolensville, or Pie in the Sky in Lenox village. The old saying of "ya get what ya pay for" seems to ring true.  Just for reference on pizza delivery time, we live exactly 3.5mi from store. 59 minutes for a lukewarm pizza...I think I could have made one in that time frame.

No backbone. You can't move tables for families to sit together? Since when did we become scared of our government and suffer for it?

Food was ok.
Help america find a backbone.

I just tried to return some things that I had bought for various projects and either not used all of or didn't use. I was denied the return because I didn't have a receipt. I am very frustrated as I did a lot of business there over the years. It frustrates me because they simply give you a little piece of the receipt from the register and say I can't help you. I looked it up on line and it makes it seem like I am stealing things and then trying to return them. This is what happens when companies get so large that they loose touch with the little guy and don't give a crap about them any more.

The first time I saw BJ's BBQ I was driving down the street and I thought I saw a car fire up ahead due to all of the smoke.  Nope, it was the sweet smelling smoker of BJ's.  Unfortunately, they had run out of BBQ due to it being a football game day, so I had to plan a return visit.  This weekend, my son and I were in the area at lunchtime, so we stopped in a BJ's.  I had the two meat plate, beef and pork, and he had the special, which was a pork sandwich.  The meats were good, but I prefer brisket and pulled pork to be moister.  Instead, BJ's chops their meats and although my plate was flavorful, it was a bit on the dry side.  As for the side dishes, we both had the baked beans, which were good, and I had the cole slaw, which was ok.  Overall, BJ's is an authentic BBQ joint as can be attested to by the hard working smoker right there on the street, it's just not the style of BBQ that I prefer.

I really enjoy this bar. The bartenders are always friendly and they have a nice assortment of beers. However, I have one complaint. I wish they would list the alcohol % on their beer list. I was drinking beer all night with a 10% alcohol percentage and had no idea until I looked it up. As some one who appreciates a large variety of beers, I like to know what abv I am drinking because it makes a big difference in the number of beers I purchase. This is a bigger concern for someone who plans to be a designated driver. Obviously buying one beer with a 5.4 abv is much different than a 10 %. But overall, a nice experience.

I came here a couple times for a special catered dinner talk in the back room.  They usually gave us a small wine flight.  I guess they've opened it as a full restaurant now.
Also came a few times to buy wine and they have been helpful.  Alternately, you can try the wine merchant.

I had a great experience at this diner from the service, food, and everything in between! I came here on a Sunday morning so it was fairly busy and loud but to be expected just like any other typical diner. We were seated immediately because they had plentiful tables. As soon as we sat down our server offered us coffee to sip on as we explored the menu. A disclaimer - the coffee was awful but thankfully everything else made up for it. 

Their menu had a plethora of breakfast options ranging from sweet to savory crepes, omelets, variations of eggs Benedict, and so much more! I ordered the spinach eggs Benedict which came with potatoes and fruit. It also came with a side of meat but as a vegetarian I substituted it with more potatoes. It was served fairly quickly. The only issue I had with my meal was that it came with literally three pieces of fruit. The menu makes it seem like it was a side portion - not a decorative piece. BUT the eggs Benedict and potatoes were absolutely phenomenal. The eggs were poached to perfection, the spinach was savory and cheesy, and the potatoes had such delicious seasoning.

On top of the food being phenomenal, the service was great too! Our servers name was Don and she checked on us regularly, was super attentive, and extremely friendly. What more can you ask from a server, am I right? 

I definitely recommend this diner to anyone who is looking for a good breakfast place.
Service is friendly, but why don't they salt their fries?  I thought the first time was a fluke, but now twice.  Horrible.

Hands down my favorite breakfast sandwich EVER! Love the old school mom and pop feel of this place. We call ahead almost every time we order for fast pick-up and the price is right. Try the pepper, egg, and cheese on a long roll, it's to die for! I've also had their waterice many, many times...the super pineapple and super peach are amazing!

I can't help but express confusion whilst dining at the Cheesecake Factory.  There's a million items on the menu, making it extremely difficult to narrow your options.  And what the heck is with the decor?!  It's like Candyland, meets Egyptian goddess, meets Italian styled ceiling art?  However, I have been twice and have left pleasantly plump and happy both times.

If you are a dieter...forget it.  Nothing on the menu is calorie friendly.  Even the "Weight Management Salads" are packed with 590 calories!  The Cheesecake Factory is meant for everyone to forget about the fat/calorie intake, and splurge a little. 

I have indulged in the Asian Chicken Salad, which was fantastic.  I've tried the herb crusted salmon as well, which was also fabulous.  However, I do have to say the Red Velvet Cheesecake is worth writing home about times 10!!  YUM YUM YUM.

After babysitting my grand babies all day I decided to get my hair cut and went to Fantastic Sams in Wentzville, MO. I typically have my hair done close to home in Illinois. From the moment I walked in I should have turned and walked out. The only stylist there was upset that another employee had not came in to work. It was about 6pm and they were supposed to be open until 9. Constant phone calls from the missing employee "making sure Sa.... wasn't upset". She would say "NO", then complain when she hung up. Calls to and from the manager were abundant too. My turn, I show her in a book what I want. She says my hair is too short, but it isn't, it is curly and looks short when not blow dried. I do not frequent these type of salon's, but there was no water applied, no shampoo, not asked if I wanted that, which I did. She cut my hair dry while on the phone 80% of the time. I was turned away from the mirror and she used a razor to thin my hair, which makes my type of hair frizz. I've worked for years on the length of my hair and she cut it in layers!!  She explained to me she is only working there until she gets into a high dollar salon. Nice. Whoever hires her at a high dollar salon better teach her etiquette, manners, customer service, a listening ear and training for different types of hair!!
I hate this, I never write bad reviews. I have stewed over this for close to two weeks, hoping I would figure out how to live with this cut over the Holidays. Not happening.

Due to its smaller parking lot, it's a lot harder to find a parking spot here after 8:00 AM if you are a commuter who needs to get into Philly before 9:00 AM.  If you get turned away, your next bet is to run over to Ferry Station. Much bigger lot. 

During rush hour in the morning, trains tend to stop every 8 minutes or so.

No bathrooms, similar to other PATCO stations.

A bit closer to Philadelphia, so a quick 12 minute hop

This place was close to our hotel, rated 4 1/2 stars, so you can't go wrong, right? The food wasn't bad, it just wasn't very good either. We got Calamari as an appetizer, and I have had better at a Chinese restaurant. Not tender at all, but not quite eraser texture. For our meals, my g/f got crabs cakes and I got the Grouper. The crab cakes looked like small baked potatoes, which is not a good thing. Apparently the flavor was good, but we eat with our eyes first and it didn't look like crab cakes that I am used to eating. The Grouper was good, but the side items were edible, not good. I'm sure this place has been opened a long time, but the food hasn't changed since The ThunderBird Hotel opened in 1957. I was hoping for so much better based but n other reviews.

Looking for an ice cream. .. daughter needed restroom. . We could not use it ... not for public use... my daughter is 7.  Did not stay for ice cream.   Can't wash our hands ..  can't eat ..  I thought all resturants had to have restroom. .

We have actually been avoiding this place because the reviews said the service wasn't great/takes forever but we decided to stop on a Monday late afternoon. We thought the tacos were great! Fresh tacos, are they full blown authentic..mostly not but they were good fresh tacos and good margaritas. We've been looking for a place downtown st Pete with fresh tacos and margaritas and are all a bust. I think this is a great addition to the area and will definitely be a regular stop after a beach day so I hope service continues to keep up. My only negative thing to say is that our chips were mostly over cooked with the queso. Half were chewy chips but not big enough of a deal to not come back. Winston was our server and was very attentive, checked on us just enough but not too much. He was great :)

Dr. Curcio and her team are great! Dr. Curcio is one of the most highly trained physicians for cosmetics - why pay for a non-MD or a lesser trained physician to take care of the way you look?  The offices are beautiful too.
This is a chain and the food is of that type of quality.   I would go again and have been here a few times but I don't think it compares to some of the full service restaurants like dinosaur bbq.

Extremely disappointed, went there for the crawfish and seafood. Despite what photos show , this is not their expertise . It's Chinese food and some gumbo and jambalaya, but mostly Chinese food.food looks like it was sitting their for a long time, did not look fresh .  Based on reviews in search of seafood , was excited , took a $15 uber all to be beyond greatly disappointed .

I am in shock that the average for Pappy's as of today is 4.5 stars, perhaps my experience is biased because we just ordered takeout.

Sweet potato fries were the absolute worst I've ever had in my life. Not only were they soggy, but they weren't even fully cooked. It was consistently recommended by folks on Yelp ... is that because the Lou doesn't have lots of sweet potato fry offerings?

Potato salad was just OK. I've never seen carrots in potato salad, and am not sure what they added.

Baked beans were only marginally better than what you can get in a can.

Pulled pork looked a lot like chicken to me. Order taking fail? Probably -- since we ordered 2 baked beans, 1 sweet potato fries, and 1 potato salad and got 2 baked beans, 3 sweet potato fries, and 2 potato salads. Usually, I'm grateful for an overload, but they also overcharged us and well, the food was not good.

Only decent offering we had was the brisket. Good for them to serve sauce on the side. That said, I wouldn't go back. Brisket was good, but not good enough to counter all the bad with this place. There's gotta be better BBQ in the Lou.
Sooo bad.  Awful and not cheap.  Why is there no Chipotle's here?  Seriously?

I expected a bad experience based on the reviews, I was wrong. We were seated quickly, our server was pleasant, food came out very fast, and the place was clean. 

The bad reviews are very misleading.

Our server, Sara/h, was really sweet and just the right amount of attentive. The food was good, and reasonably priced.  However I'm confused. Really confused.

The place is called Pub 'n Grub, however they call themselves a bar and ristorante. There is nothing italian about this place.

The menus are paper and look like take out menus, yet they have cloth napkins.

I think they need to decide which direction they want to go in, because its just complicated at this point.

I wouldn't seek pub 'n grub out, but I wouldn't refuse it if the opportunity presented itself to go again.

Once you've been to one Denny's you've practically been to them all. This one is no exception. Your typical 24 hour diner style franchise right here. 

Me and my wife went in after a movie screening and we were seated right away. That's about where the fast service ended though. There were only a few other parties there before us but fifteen to twenty minutes after being seated, those other groups were being served. It just took a really long time (almost 30 minutes) for them to make a breakfast sandwich and pancake special with hash browns. 

Don't get me wrong, the food was hot when it came out and it tasted good. The hash browns had a crunchy outside and soft inner layer that mixed well with the sausage and bacon. Everything mixed well with the fluff pancakes.

If you're looking for that diner style breakfast you can't go wrong with Denny's and if you don't mind the wait you can check this one out.

I've been once for lunch before so I thought I'd get some take out this time for lunch: the food is good but the service is lacking. As I walked up to the serving table the waitress acknowledged the person that entered right after me. The owner came out and asked what I wanted. Without being told about the choices I picked the first one with various pasta shapes and was told it's the most popular. He offered me "salad" which is just lettuce with Italian dressing. I had to ask for the bread before I left or I wouldn't have gotten any. The waitress asked me if I was paying card without even telling me the total owed.  After I paid I thanked them but wasn't acknowledged once payment was received. Hopefully their dinner service makes up for the crappy lunch service.

The food was ok, I order the crawfish, shrimp and crab, everything was so small the it was a lot of work to eat it and very little food after finish cleaning it. the potatoes In my food wasn't cooked,the service wasn't that great either, I have to ask for all the esencial to eat my food.

So so. Nothing special. Interesting that everyone that works at an asian place is not asian but Mexican. 
Quality of all you can eat is always a joke so can't expect a lot.  Staff/ manager strange group. Spanish being talked the entire time on floor and from the cooks. 
Bad atmosphere.
Pizza was disappointing. It looks better than it tastes. The breadsticks were decent. I honestly wouldn't go here again.

The most relaxing tiny bookshop café ever. The book collection is fantastic, and they also carry some fantastic stuff. This was my third visit, and the barista was really pleasant and kind. 

They have a wonderful selection of lattes, including one named "Kobe"! I wish I lived in the area so I could make it my regular hangout, but I'll definitely be back. The atmosphere is fantastic. There is a decent assortment of literature for all ages. In my view, this is one of the most friendly spaces in Philadelphia! 

The size of the space isn't awful, but I wish it were bigger for one reason: so that more people could experience the positive mood and inspiring atmosphere.

White people LOVE IKEA.  I guess since I'm not really white, I'm only kinda like it.  

I can tell you one thing.  Walking around the showrooms makes me WANT TO STAB EVERYONE REPEATEDLY.  

I'm just trying to get the hell out and the signs sending me all over the place and looping me over myself are just not helping me do it.

I wound up not getting everything i set out to get because I felt as if I couldn't move freely.

I do have hang ups though.

I went here with a friend for brunch one morning. I would say there are definitely other places were you get better food for a better price, for sure. My food was OK, but I had ordered a coffee, which was FULL of coffee grinds. I don't mean one or two; it was literally swimming with coffee grinds. When I pointed it out to the waiter, he replied, "Well, I can get you a new one, but I have to charge you for it." WHAT? He should have brought a new one and taken it off my bill completely. It was almost $4 for just a plain coffee alone! So, that was my first (and only) time there, and I won't be going back. Hope that was worth the $4 to them.

"It's a lot more fun to eat in a bar than it is to drink in a restaurant", is their motto and for those of you who are not familiar with Chickie's & Pete's, they have won the title of Best Sports Bar on the East coast by ESPN, plus countless other awards for their wings, crabs and "crab fries".Crab fries have no crab meat, but are a huge portion of crinkle cut fries seasoned with their secret spices, similar to Old Bay if you ask me and served with two sides of a thick white cheese dipping sauce. Even though they are not mind blowing, they are addictive once you start to dip those puppies and I tend to eat them like a robot until the last of the sauce is gone. They are a must have when visiting any Chickie's & Pete's. 

"Everyone's favorite crab house and sports bar" at the Parx Casino is huge with well over fifty giant screened TV's, this and some of their other locations feature Play2 where you can golf on a wall sized screen with Tiger Woods or interact playing many other games. The set up is unique with a large centered open bar on the main floor, there are actually three levels and it's cool to watch from your own private high backed booth literally being able to see all the action. 

We started with the crab fries, ordering them along with our drinks, I had a watermelontini which was okay, very well chilled just had that artificial after taste to it and hubby had a large coke. Our server, Nikki was so knowledgeable of the menu and space, she was so friendly and personable and on the ball. The food is cooked to order and does take awhile, but it comes out hot and she keeps you informed as to how your meal is progressing. She really added to our dining experience and I will not hesitate to ask for her in the future. 

We ordered Chickie's Riverboat clams which are clams casino, theirs have whole clams with spicy seasoning, red and green peppers and onions and just enough bread crumbs to bring it all together, we enjoyed these, but they came out pretty well after our meals. Bon Jovi goes to Chickie's & Pete's for one thing only and that is their "Lisa's Blonde lobster pie", so I had to order this. It is a white flatbread pizza loaded with cheese, herbs and all lobster tail meat and I do mean huge chunks of lobster tail on each slice, unless you can really eat, you will be taking some home, because it is by no means a personal sized pie, cost $20.00.and worth every penny! 

Hubby had a cheesesteak, what can I say and it was wit onions and provolone, served on a good roll and he liked it, I snitched a bite, twas good indeed. I really am so excited they have opened this location close to me, even though passing those one armed bandits is virtually impossible! I need to grab a few girlfriends and or coworkers to check out sharing the hot and spicy bowl of shrimp, they have won awards for their red or white mussels for eight straight years, the jumbo lump crab cakes, their crab flatbread with red sauce and aioli, oh I could go on, it all looks and sounds good. 

This is a crab house folks and they have crabs galore, king, snow crabs, blue crab, dungeness crab and lump crab meat.

I love their restrooms! Black wall and floor tiles, even the stalls with colorful tiles scattered here and there and their sinks are huge crab pots with matching metal strainers inserted on top. Chickie's & Pete's opened their eighth location inside the Parx Casino last Friday, but they will be celebrating their grand opening this coming Friday, August 6. Do go and check them out. Wheelchair accessible.

Chips from a bag, chunky tasteless salsa, margaritas (more like gnargaritas) and toxic blue salt on the rim.  

We tried desperately to get a seat on the patio but our efforts failed even though there were many open tables!

Philly has a plethora of excellent Mexican restaurants.  Ditch this place and try somewhere new.

My husband and I were given a gift certificate to go to Firebirds Restaurant for Christmas..we heard such great raves about the place and was looking forward to going..we are in our 60's and we go earlier to beat the crowds and waiting for a table...we arrived around 3:45...nobody in the place other than the bar. we were seated in the dining room area...we were the ONLY people seated in the dining room..first thing we hated was the loud music blasting in our ears.. and the unusual dark dim dining room.. hard to see.. the lights were down very low.. the waitress  greeted us right away and went into a big shpeel about the "wood fired" food, etc..it was quite lengthy and quite boring to us.. I like stuffed shrimp and nothing much is on their menu with shrimp..very disappointing..anyway, I ordered the prime rib, baked potato, and was shocked that they have the nerve to charge you 4.99 to add a salad, for their high prices , u should get that included!!!I opted to pay extra and get  shrimp on a skewer added to my order..another 6 or 7.95 extra ..my husband ordered the salmon...I had the lemonade and they boasted how good it was, but I wasnt impressed, and I hated the sugar they put on the rim of the glass, too sticky ,and the lemonade is quite sweet too.. I prefer Paneras lemonade much more..the lemonade was 3.95...at Firebirds...not worth it..we put our order in and waited and waited and waited for our dinner...in the meantime they give us 1 measley roll to split between the 2 of us!! and 1 melon ball scoop of butter for the 2 of us too???? Hellooo, how chincey is that?? I had to constantly ask for butter throughout the meal!! VERY ANNOYING!! They didnt even give me any for my baked potato!! Anyway, the worst is yet to come!!!! We waited and waited and waited for our food till well after 5pm!!!!!They finally came out with some cockamamie story that the cook had to go and cut the piece of salmon off of fish and it thats what was taking sooo long?? WTF??????? We joked and said, did he go to ALaska and fish for it?? The food FINALLY arrived and the primed rib piece I had was cold and over 60% fat!!! IT was AWFUL!!!! The shrimp on the skewer was warm to cold...no cocktail sauce, had to ask for that too..I couldnt eat the prime rib and told the waitress how terrible it was...the manager came up and said we wouldnt have to pay for the meals except the for the shrimp, since we had waited so long and the prime rib sucked so bad!!! Then the waitress asked me if I wanted to get something different,  I wasnt sure what she meant, I thought that they were just gonna replace  the lousey prime rib for something else, but I didnt know they were gonna charge me!! I ordered the NY strip!.. Well when that came it was like shoe leather ,no flavor, and most of it was FAT TOO? WTFFFFFFF????  This food is quite expensive, the prime rib was around 33.00 and this was NOT NY Strip, what NY strip has tons of fat..and it was 28.99!!!WHAT A TOTAL RIPOFFFF!!! We hated this restaurant to say the least!!!! WE were exhausted with the whole experience and so fed up and tired, we couldnt wait to get  out of there!! My husband said his salmon was ok, but a little too dry..and certainly not worth the 24.00 they charged tho!! The waitress was very nice, but too talkative for us, but we would rather have somebody personable than some ignorant unattentive one like we had at "Bucks", earlier in the week...she was the worst we ever had anywhere!! We would never go back to Firebirds,  quite frankly, it was THE WORST restaurant experience we ever had at any restaurant ever!!!! The food was AWFUL and waaaay overpriced for what you got!! We dont get how anybody can pay this much for this terrible food.. We still ended up having to pay 39.00 for the shrimp skewer and the NY strip..so nothing was really free for putting up with this awful experience!! We do not recommend this restaurant at allll!! A total rip off!! Steer clear!!! Overpriced garbage!!!
Last three times I've visited this place they have been out of Boba!? That's like McDonald's being out of fries? No explanation just out.

TJ Maxx's can be hit or miss.  Yes, there are some great bargains, but sometimes you'll need to put away your OCDness for a hot minute and try not to be distracted by how messy it can be. And it's not always the store's fault - shoppers are damn lazy and will pick up merchandise in one area and then get rid of it by dumping it in another section if they change their mind.

I like that that they have different sizes for women available, and I once lucked out and found a bunch of their cardboard cat scratchers for $6.99 that are usually closer to $10 each, so I grabbed a few of those.

As always for a store of this kind, grab what you like now as more likely than not, it will not be there three days later.

Used to be my "go to" breakfast place. New ownership, noticed that they altered the once superb pancake recipe. The Head Chef who was a family member was great. New chefs now. I couldn't eat the pancakes, haven't been back since, and I notice the once full parking lot on weekends is sparsely populated.

I so much wanted to love this l place. I love biscuits and gravy and I followed them on Instagram.  When I saw they had a place in Reno I had to make it a point to stop on our annual trip. Vibe was good but I didnt  like other patrons hoovering over us for our table. I felt rushed to eat and free up space.

Our table ordered a variety of items. Biscuits and gravy, hashbrowns, chicken biscuit sandwich and a breakfast sandwich.  My biscuit and gravy with hashbrowns where a disappointment.  They were covered in paprika and hashbrowns are on the greasy side. Other people in my party enjoyed their biscuit sandwiches.
Took 2 hours to change 1 tire WITH a reservation. This is ridiculous. Worst customer service too. Never going to come back. Do not come here unless you want to waste your time.

We're from Los Angeles and came here because it's close to the Nashville airport.  We had a few beers and also had 3 burgers. Everything was so damn good the burger were amazing!!!!! Must try if your in the area!

Very nice dealership they took the time to show me the car that I wanted explained financial options even though my credit wasn't so hot and came up with a solution so I can get my dream truck. Really had a great experience here

the girls here are very nice and do a very good job.  Its the first nail salon I have stayed with for over a year.  I do not wear fake nails, I usually get a regular manicure and a regular pedicure and I am very satisfied.  I have had the gel polish a few times and have had very good results with it.  Gel polish lasted more than 2 weeks with no lifiting.  I cant comments above with lifting.  And yes ... they are very friendly to the regulars, thats why they keep coming back

Northern Liberties hot spot - get there while you can still get a table!

I had dinner there Thursday with two friends.  We ordered and shared:
- Octopus
- Caesar salad (lightly grilled)
- Brussel sprouts in bacon jam
- Salmon
- Sirloin
- Monkfish

The food was amazing - the sauces and accompaniments brought out unique flavors that made each dish come alive. The place was hopping.  Get there while you can still get a table.  

Tony and his team have put together a delightful creation!
Food is decent, lazy cooking. Got an omelette put the filling on top of the food. My hash browns were only cooked on one side

One of my new fav spots in the Gulch (North Gulch tbh, but right by Chauhan) and is a great cocktail spot, Duckpin Bowling attraction, and retro game aficionado bar. What more can you ask?! Also, it's dog friendly and they rotate food trucks daily that you can check on their app. This is a killer spot and will be a staple of mine in Nash. Not to mention, their Maple Nut Old Fashioned is one of my favs on Nash.

3.5 stars overall. 

This place kinda makes me think of McAllister's and Panera, but a bit cheaper. Sandwiches, salads, Mac and cheese, and pizza options + tea!

The best part of it is the small bar (Newk's Roundtable) on the side with extra toppings for your meal - oregono, pickles, garlic, cheese, oil & vinegar, and more. When I went here in Dec or so, the pickles were delicious. Now they are the bread and butter pickles (disgusting) so be warned! 

My pesto & chicken sandwich was good - and better when I visited the Newk's Roundtable and added lots of oregono, cheese, oil & vinegar. The 5-cheese Mac and cheese was good too. 

It was all "good" but not great. And the portion sizes were SO small. I got the 1/2 pesto sandwich and 1/2 Mac and cheese for $9.29 and it was tiiiiny. So I don't feel good about my bank account after eating here - it seemed like not a great use of money for quantity and quality. 

Also, they do have cake, and that is pretty good. So, overall I don't hate this place but won't come often.

Oops, did we close the bar ?

Wanting a night out with friends, this was actually my backup. It turned out to be a fabulous spot. 

This fairly new wine bar is located to the right of Simon's in a strip mall. Plenty of parking. A large patio that I can't wait to sip on next summer. Several seating choices inside. Plus..........a large selection of reds on display and a huge walk in fridge for whites and bubbles. Not a wine drinker?  Why not ? Are you crazy?  
If not, there are two large coolers with canned and bottled beer selections.  The snack menu offers a nice nibble selection. Pâté, cheese board, charcuterie, salmon and some dipped to enjoy with your wine. 

I didn't notice any background noice. Once we were sipping and talking, it felt like we were the only ones there. But all the tables were full. 

I thought the red choices were pretty good and varied. I was surprised to see a couple from the Languedoc area of France, a Greek wine and many Italians. Also present were several familiar Californians. The prices were great !!  Check all around the bar before making a decision. There were a couple of mix and match sales that came to $10 a bottle when purchasing just 3 bottles. 

 Excited to see a wine bar back in this neighborhood location. Will be be very soon.

Somehow I keep coming back to this place, because it's so darn convenient--I walk by it on my way to work.  And then I somehow end up inside. I blame it on the electric projectors I teach with, which project my fingers, GIANT, in front of the class when I point to a line in a handout. And then walking home...sigh. Today was the most recent time I was suckered in.

Relaxing? Not so much. The last few times I've been here, they've been blasting "Keeping up with the Kardashians," in the main area, so I've had to listen to the Kardashians fighting with each other. I think today's argument (very dramatic) was about one sister not wanting a mayonnaise mask. 

All the people that work here are very sweet, however. There's one older woman in particular who is very kind.

You get a (small) discount on a pedicure if you have a Cat Card, so $20 total, pre-tip.  Today I stopped in just for polish on my toes, and was charged $10.

Make sure to bring cash, because they charge you extra for using a credit card.

We stumbled upon this place late night b/c it was within walking distance of our hotel.

We found 2 spots right at the bar and ordered a bottle of wine and an appetizer. 

While both were good, the service was meh....

It looks like it's a place locals go for a nice evening out in the area, but since we were obviously not locals I feel like the bartender wasn't nearly as interested in taking care of us b/c he knew we wouldn't be back.

One of my favorite places in Nashville.  My boyfriend and I dressed up as Jay Gatsby and Daisy Buchanan for Halloween so we figured this would be a sweet place to have a couple drink.  We only waited around 10 minutes and then we were seated at the bar.  The atmosphere was dark, music was perfect and everyone was great.

They have a menu that's divided by base liquors and the drinks are listed below.  We both started with a whiskey cocktail and loved watching the bartender craft our drinks. Most drinks were around $12 each so a little pricy but so worth it.  They were great so we had a second round.

I would definitely recommend!

Finally, after driving by everyday for about 2 years on-and-off from/to work, decided to try Back To Cuba. My first Cuban culinary experience was earlier this year in Miami, so I was hooked. Going in, I already knew not to compare to Miami, I kept an open-mind and this review is solely based on Back to Cuba. The place is small, 8 small tables, plus some bistro seating outdoors. Its in a small shopping strip attached to a gas station. Getting parking and going in-and-out can be challenging. We went in around 11:30, luckily not too busy. The menu is displayed on their wall, and can be a little confusing/overwhelming. We just asked what they recommended and we went with that. I had the pork roast plate and my husband has the cuban sandwich with fries. We also tried their chicken empanada. The roast pork could have used a bit more flavor, and maybe some spice/acidic. I really wished I could've substitute the yuca for plantains. The rice and beans were just average. The cubano was extra crispy. And the pork was chewy and dry, I couldn't even eat it. Also lacked some spice/acidic. Fries were good, and the empanada was as well. I definitely want to give it another try, perhaps try something different. Service was good, food came out fast.

Long-time fan of Ralph's here.  I adore sweetbreads, which are not on every Italian restaurant's menu.  So i go to Ralph's for sweetbreads.  I think the mushrooms in the dish are canned, but the sweetbreads are perfect, with a light sauce and are a huge portion.  I'm also crazy about their Fettucini Alfredo, which is the real thing, made with pasta, butter, parm and cream and nothing else.  My partner always orders spaghetti and meatballs - he's not a foodie and that's what he likes.  Since I order 2 entrees (so I'll have leftovers tomorrow), we don't get out of there cheaply, but I love the place.  And yes, it's a good place to bring visiting firemen - old-time ambience, attentive service, and Sarcone's bread.

The new buzzfeed spot to crown the king of cheesesteaks. I've been going here for years and always enjoyed the quality of cheesesteak. The quality is still there, but it's just not worth going anymore. 

Go at any time and the small interior is packed to the fullest. Just recently I ordered 4 steaks over the phone so I did not have to wait in the crowded space. Upon entering they told me my order was coming right up and made me wait, in the packed space, for another 40 minutes. Upon confronting them about my forgotten order, they realized their mistake and offered to make the steaks for me right then. But, it would take another 30 minutes. No thanks. Terrible experience. 

Do yourself a huge favor and go across the street to Chubbies.

This place is great. I love how close it is to Reno. Literally a 20 minute drive max from Reno. There's never any traffic on Mt Rose Hwy...makes the exhausting drive after a ski trip less exhausting. Also, the road to the resort isn't too bad, not too many winding roads.

There's a bundle of blue and black runs, with their infamous "chutes" run. There's a couple lifts for beginners, but if I could recommend anything, I'd recommend some more green runs. Overall, I think they have a great variety of runs for beginners to experts. 

Customer service is always great---the staff there always have a smile on their face and are willing to assist you. 

A lot of locals come here because not a lot of people know about this ski resort--so it doesn't get too crowded!

Get your tickets off liptopia to save a few bucks!

Really good delivery. We went back the next night just because we were craving more. The dumplings were amazing and the fried rice isn't filled with veggies (which i prefer). Sushi looked really good, I didn't order any and all the food was still steaming hot once it got to our house. I'm decibels going to try some different things next time because I was happy with everything I've had so far.

" Nothing Special "

We went to have a drink and to have a breakfast at Blake's On Poydras. We started our meal off with 2 Bloody Mary's which were very good. I had The Breakfast Bowl with Fried Green Tomatoes, Fried Eggs, Bacon, Feta Cheese on top of Grits. Sounds like it would have a lot of flavor but it did not have any flavors. My wife had the Crab Benedict with a side of Grits. She said it was delicious.

Amazing food and a personable owner make up for subpar service and cramped dining conditions. Everything is delicious; from the roasted beet salad, the Palermo pizza, Chicken Gremolata and the to die for Godfather Chocolate Cake.  Just go in with lowered service expectations and you will thoroughly enjoy your meal.

When I was there in Nov, 2009, there was a sign saying No Gelato. The case was completely empty. When the business closed in December, he listed equipment failures (and the high cost of replacements) as one of the reasons. It closed without fanfare in mid-December, 2009.

I bought a Groupon for an oil change with an expiration date of 90 days.... Called to make an appointment 10 days before the expiration date and was told that they are fully booked until two weeks from today.... I asked if the Groupon would be honored and he said no... I feel this is highly deceitful sice it wasn't my fault they can't accommodate me...and now i have to pay the full price for an oil change??? C'mon... It is an oil change that takes 20 minutes and they have my money already... Will not buy another Groupon for them or use them in the future!

First, I must say the women here were very nice and friendly and had every intention of being helpful. However, the fit specialist measured me and guessed 28D, which is just crazy. I've always worn 32B, and no one in their right mind would look at me and think a D would be a better fit. I suspended judgement, as I've often heard that a lot of women wear the wrong size. So I tried on what she suggested, and it totally did not fit. The right size? Yep - 32B, just as I thought. 

I was looking for a strapless bra to wear under a wedding dress. She brought a corset-style bra, which sounded like a good idea. When I tried it on under my dress, the ribs in it totally showed through. The ladies assured me that it "wasn't bad" and that I could just make some small cuts and remove the ribs. In retrospect, I really question the wisdom of spending $68 on a bra I would have to perform surgery on. Still, I bought the bra, and when I got it home and tried it on again, I realized not just the ribs, but the whole shape, was going to show through my dress. I also realized I would never wear it with anything else, that I could not get into it by myself, etc.

I went to Nordstrom and found a much better choice for the same price. I've heard good things about Ann's for those who need a size most stores don't stock, but I didn't find it very helpful, despite the very kind staff.

I was really excited to get my frozen yogurt fix when I walked into U-Swirl. The place looked really nice and had a lot of sitting areas. I also noticed that they have more flavors and a lot more toppings to choose from. 
What I didn't LOVE was that most of the fruit flavors were not all that creamy. The texture was more like a sorbet. All the flavors are really good though, and my all time favorite is the original/tart.
The service is really good, they offer as many samples as you want. Thaw reason why they didn't get more stars is because of the texture, but it is a nice place to sit down and enjoy good frozen yogurt.

We had a family emergency so our trip wasn't planned.  The guy at the counter at the airport and his manager treated us awesomely. An otherwise traumatic trip was made the better by Enterprise, thank you.

Mt. Laurel New Jersey 

Good products, a notch above SAMs , they literally have everything. They're meat department is awesome. The rotisserie chickens unreal, juicy, for 5 dollars ,can't beat it. I also have insurance thru them, Ameriprise which is excellent.
Dirty bar, and expensive drinks assembled with little care. There is absolutely nothing unique about this place whatsoever.
Not impressed. I think it must be a good place if you are a regular.....

I Really really want to like this place. It's a great new location and the patio isn't too bad.. but the food is absolutely awful. It's all fried and frozen.. you can tell it comes in plastic freezer bags. 

There is a full bar and it's great for sports games.. if you're not hungry.

I'm not sure why so many people were raving about the Thursday dinner buffets here. It's rather overpriced given what is offered. Also, is it just me or am I the only one that consistently gets very poor service each time I dine here? It always takes at least 40 minutes to get the food, even with multiple inquiries on its status once I am able to hunt down some eye contact with the staff... Of course, that's after at least 10 minutes of waiting for the staff member to come to you after they first seat you!!! 
There have been multiple times when the food came out wrong, but after waiting a good part of your afternoon or evening, we give in and eat what the incorrectly served us. If you are part of a large party, be aware that they will serve each person at different times, so some are left eating while the others stare for a good 20-30 minutes.
As I complete this review, I believe I've decided that I won't be dining in here. Instead, I'll go with ordering take out after giving them ample time and reviewing what I am given before I leave. The things people do for good Thai food in middle America...

Our experience with Ashley Furniture was a nightmare! Our salesman was Douglas Diedrich and probably couldn't spell customer service.  I have called him twice, sent 3 emails and even went to the store wherein he promised a phone call within a week....that was a month and 1/2 ago. We purchased over 4k worth of furniture and the deliveries continued to get pushed out week after week. I get that it is not Doug's fault however, he promised us that they did not do business that way. That if it took longer than quoted he/they would step in. He sat on his hands, did nothing and could not even reply to an email. I still have not received a call from Ashley Furniture- I have always had to call them to check the status and the hold time is anywhere from 15-30 minutes. Our chairs were set to be delivered in 2029-but still no follow up from the store. By looking at the recent reviews, it's a miracle they are still in business. Again Doug- a reply email would have sufficed. Treating your customers like you actually care about the situation would go a long way-Doug.

The place is nice but I didn't like the chairs. I'm short and my feet couldn't reach the water if I sat back to to feel the massage. Also I wanted hot stone but no one even asked me for an extras. I felt rushed to pick a color and sit down. For the price the foot massage a joke, also I'm use to getting a shoulder massage with my gel manicure and I barely received a hand massage. I will not be going back.

It was a 30% off sale that brought me in, but the staff and events keep me coming back. The staff here is incredibly knowledgable when it comes to wine and the stock they carry. They were the only place I could find in Edmonton that carried a brand from Santorini, Greece that I fell in love with years ago. Ever staff member I have run into there has been helpful and kind. 

The wine testers they bring in are always friendly and have given me fantastic suggestions, even when it isn't their brand. They also carry some of the best stock of beer from Alberta breweries such as Hell's Basement, Blindman, and Citizen. If there's something unique you are looking for make sure this is your first stop!

1) ordered food around 11am still waiting for the delivery, and it's 2pm. Was told 30 min ago that it will be there in 5-10 min.
2) Not going to review the food since I have not tried it

Came here on an open mic night on a recent Friday, and what made the night for me was the amazing company and the fun atmosphere around performances, not the drinks unfortunately. I had high expectations especially for their boba since it's even in the name, but their boba was more like bullets which made me suspect they might not have been cooked long enough. 

The jasmine green tea I ordered was even more disappointing because it was unbelievably bitter and impossible to take more than a couple tiny sips. A couple of us asked if our jasmine green boba tea could be replaced with something else, and we each got a smoothie and specifically asked for no boba. My chocolate almond banana had such an unpleasant taste that I finished no more than 10% of it, but the chocolate coconut was a lot better. 

There are dozens of seats and the vibe is casual and alternative, but I doubt their drinks will ever draw me back to this place.
I loved it. Steak fajitas were delicious. We got there close to closing and service was great. Looking forward to my next time there.

We went during restaurant week... Not sure if that means it could get better or worse..?
 If had better at NJ diners to be honest. But in fairness it was better than some of the reviews i read here....stuffed mushrooms were rubbery, mussels ok, pasta ok, red sauce ok, shrimp seemed fresh....
..the decor inside much different from what i imagined from the outside and their website...very outdated 1980ish...
.the help was friendly but don't expect anymore than adaquate...waitress obviously had someone uncork our wine bottle in the kitchen (at least I hope...ahem) and yelled "i said caaaaaaannoli" so loud I almost dropped my teeensy wine glass. meh...typical so. philly fare!
My main complaint is the dishes are not the healthiest.  Should be at least a few healthy choices without the rich sauces.

I'm not aware of a S. McCarran store. I've only shopped at the Mt. Rose Ave. store near Junkee. Because it's a chain, the website doesn't even tell you where the store is, or anything else that's really pertinent to shopping there, except a 'we're a teen to twenty-something store' statement. A bad website is also a turn-off for me, but the statement is correct. If you're over 50 ( a forty-something friend of mine likes Plato's), you will NOT find very much interesting here. I can wear tight jeans, but they're almost all tight. The shirts and tops are Cute, not a word I use about myself at my age. These are only party clothes for the over 50's crowd, and you know those are never in a thrift price range for the barely middle class. As far as selling gently used clothing, it needs to be close to designer brands or don't waste your time and get looks from the staff member who says "sorry". Shop on, young ones! I wore these clothes back in the day, but not now.

Mmmmm... if you go early, the bagels are crazy fresh and delish! They are busy in the morning, for sure but I've never waited long. I buy giant orders of bagels for work 2-3 times a year and though pricey, soooo much better than grocery store versions.

Well let's see before I went to Annunciation I was really excited.. I saw on their facebook page, Description  Fine Dining without feeling fussy...
Reservations recommended to avoid disappointment, especially on Friday and Saturday evenings.

Had two friends in town, one all the way from Italy so we wanted to have a good dinner, the four of us had a 9 pm reservation which is not to bad.  We arrived a few minutes early and they had us seated by 9:45 pm.

I did not remember all of the menu but I know we started with the Veal Sweetbreads with Gnocchi, Pancetta and Mushrooms, the fried oysters topped with brie and the crab claws, which were all nice.. but nothing amazing.

Then the turn downward started, the four of us wanted to try alot of things on the menu because we have heard alot of good things about Steven Manning and since he came from Clancy's we just expected it to be good.

We told the waiter that we wanted to try a few different things and to bring them in stages so we could all sample them and we ordered the, (2 ) Fried Soft Shell Crab with Crabmeat Meuniere Sauce. Crabmeat, Lobster and Shrimp Risotto
Angel Hair Pasta with fried oysters, Sauteed Black Drum with Crabmeat, Coconut Milk and Jasmine Rice, Veal Oscar, Veal Annunciation and the Slow roasted Pork Shoulder with Sweet Onion Relish. We had one more but I cannot remember what it was.

We told him we wanted to start with a nice white wine for the first dishes they would send out and it came out quickly and was nice.

A few minutes later he came out and told us they were out of soft shell crabs : (  so we just added another dish.

Five minutes later all 8 entrees came out at the SAME time.. they were nice to give us each a 6 inch plate so we could smash all the items together like at a buffet. 

The risotto was hard, a few of the dishes were cold and some just did not have much flavor, or it could have just blended into all the other dishes that were crammed onto my plate.

And of course only had that one bottle of wine, not the 3 that we were hoping to have over a nice 2 hour dinner.

I have to admit that the Maitre d and the GM were both great about the situation and I would have to guess that the waiter just was overwhelmed with the order.

So... maybe it might we worth another chance in the future... not sure : (

Crispy's was a pleasant surprise.  I am a big fan of fish and chips and this is the best I have found so far in the area.

This place is nothing fancy at all.  The dining furniture consists of plastic picnic table/benches all in ones.  The menu is very basic and they have fish, chicken, wings, and shrimp.  The sides are fries, coleslaw, and potato salad and a few others.  The drinks are all canned sodas.

When we arrived most of the tables were occupied with people waiting for to go orders and was fairly busy.  Busy usually means good and with the prior reviews we had high hopes.  We were not disappointed.  

I went with the four piece cod combo that had fish, fries, coleslaw, and a canned soda.  My wife went with the three piece chicken combo.  We split an order of hush puppies that was served in a basket that had several in it (largest portion we have ever seen).  The food is cooked to order so it tastes fresh.  Even the fries were a winner here.  It might take a little bit longer but it's worth the wait.  As I watched my food prepared I could see they were doing a little extra in the preparation of it and they were taking pride in what they were doing.  

The cost was 20 bucks for two and we were full, satisfied, and totally enjoyed our meal.  The owner took the time to ask us how we liked it.  I would rate this as the second best fish and chips I have had in the state of Arizona and the best in Tucson.
  
We will eat here again.  As the saying goes don't judge a book by its cover.  Well don't go here looking for a fine dining restaurant.  The food is really good and if you want a great tasting fish and chips this is the place!

We were walking with our stroller, our 2 year old was asleep so we decided to stop in for a meal. My wife went in and asked the manager if it would be ok for us to come in with the stroller. He was very nice, said it would be no problem and even helped us in. He brought us to a table and showed us where to park the stroller so we wouldn't be in anyone's way. As we were reading the menu the hostess caught sight of us, stared at the stroller and at my wife then stormed off to the back of the restaurant. She returned in a minute and very rudely (and loudly) instructed us to move the stroller so we wouldn't be in the way. My wife told her that the manager had sat us down and told us where to put the stroller and we would prefer not to move and risk waking up our child. She again, rudely and loudly, told us we had to move as the stroller was "a hazard". With this we got up, said thank you, and told her we would dine somewhere else. No apology, just "OK, goodbye." It's a shame, food looked good but needless to say we won't be returning.

Come to Mother's restaurant to get some real home-cooked Southern dishes! After watching Adam Richman from Man vs. Food pay a visit and eat a juicy Po-Boy - I just had to try it! YES - there is ALWAYS a line and its always LONG. But suck it up - because it goes pretty fast. I'd say we had at least 30 people in front of us and we were in within 15 min. Why? The restaurant inside is HUGE (self-seating) and the line to order your food inside is basically the only bottle neck.

--Famous Fredi's Special ($11.75)--
MUST TRY! This is what Mother's is known for! This sandwich is basically a huge pile of meat all placed between a french roll (which is also dipped in meat juice)! SO hearty and satisfying! What kinds of meat you ask? A variety: black ham, thick slices of roast beef, and of course the DEBRIS! When they described it on Man vs. Food - I was a little disturbed. The bits and pieces of left over meat - how can that be good? IT IS! TRUST! Its like a juicier version of pulled pork - soft and slow cooked! Make sure you get a stack of napkins - because this sandwich can get MESSY. With the meat juice, shredded cabbage, globs of mayo + mustard, gravy, and of course the layers of meat, you will have to wipe with each bite. All the meat went really well with the tangy and yet creamy sauces - I went to carnivore heaven!

--Jerry's Omelete ($11)--
After feeling a little sickly from all the heavy meals in NOLA - I ordered an egg white omelete in hopes of giving my poor stomach a break. The omelete was filled with bell pepper, turkey, red onions, and mushrooms. This was the blandest plate of food ever! I had to salt + pepper with each bite! Not only that, the topping to egg proportion was too little and the entire plate was covered in grease! I did enjoy the taste of grits - which I'm not used to! Felt like home in my belly! :) I also got to try Mother's famous jam with my toast.. but all in all, this is a dish to avoid.

--Combination Platter ($19)--
Yes we're tourists and we want to taste everything. So what better way than to order the combo platter which comes with Jerry's Jambalaya, etouffee, red beans, vegetables, and potato salad!? This plate is a blessing because you do get to try so many dishes - but after tasting these same dishes elsewhere in NOLA, I can tell you this combination platter may not be worth it. A lot of the food @ Mother's just tasted really mushy, goopy, and a little bland. The etouffee was one shining star on the dish - which was very flavorful. I think one downfall of this dish is that all these dishes are plopped onto the same tiny plate and the flavors get lost in the mix!

--Mae's Gumbo Cup ($6)--
A small little cup of gumbo - which tasted homey and less salty than other places. But I honestly had a hard time differentiating the different gumbos at the variety of NOLA restaurants. Meh - nothing special.

Tips
1) Self seating area is much bigger in Mother's next door
2) Soft drink ($2.25) - unlimited refills
3) FREDI'S SPECIAL!
This is the best fast pizza in town. Perfect when you're in a rush. Staff is amazing! Super clean place!

Wow, so not a friendly welcoming feeling. She was so busy on her phone. We were not welcomed when we walked in or told about anything like specials. My wife asked what flavor were the cakes, no answer. I had to ask for flavors specials. Would I Go back, NO. I get better costumer service at circle k. If I could give a minus star, I would.

I judge cafes on their coffee and Java is not high quality from what i tasted. I asked for a latte and got an under extracted coffee topped with mostly foam. Java needs to teach its "barista's" how to make espresso/lattes etc.
I dont think Java wants to be defined by its coffee but by being a nice place to hang out which it is. I thought the staff were nice enough, the food ok without being fantastic. Head a few blocks to The Crux if you want better coffee.
I wish I could give this place stars. They take advantage of people in an emergency. I'll suffer until my dentist opens next time!

Maybe it's that we came in the last hour they were open, but I have to say that I had higher expectations for the muffaletta based on Yelp reviews.  Didn't realize they were pre-made or how long they had been sitting.  I guess I was just expecting something warm...These weren't the most fresh things in the world and super oily.  Maybe an off day?

Tough steak & the best part of it was the bread.  I know it's a tourist joint but come on I was at least expecting decent food.  Not impressed.  Pat's was better.  Don't let the lights, glitz and glam fool you.  But I encourage you to play in the rivalry and do try both.

Wanted to put a better review.. the staff was nice but it took over 2 hours to do a oil change with no line.... that's just insane and when I went to complain the guy just had my paperwork sitting there

while I wouldn't say that this is a lousy restaurant, I think that more may be raving based on it's lineage.
Mrs Hays Stuffed wings are good, but nothing of note.
The caprese salad suffered from a lackluster tomato, prolly hard to produce in this environment.
The grilled pork chop was a little too sweet and a bit chewy.
The almond tart was low on sweet (good) but also low on flavor.
Cappuccino was excellent.
But the star of the night was the glass of red that the waiter recommended.  I wish I could remember what it was, but it was excellent, inexpensive, a little spicy and oaky and not fruit forward.
